days.title: Il congiuntivo presente, la forma
days.objective: You can build the present subjunctive of regular verbs and of the most common irregular ones, and you know why the subject pronoun is usually kept.
days.paretoFocus: The subjunctive is the single grammatical step that separates a confident A2 speaker from a B1 one, and every trigger in the next lessons needs these forms first.
drills: Write out the present subjunctive of parlare, prendere and partire on paper, all six persons.
Say aloud five sentences that start with Penso che, using a different verb each time.
List the six irregular subjunctives you meet today and cover the column to test yourself.

items: What are the subjunctive endings of an -are verb?
Why do the three singular persons need a subject pronoun?
What is the present subjunctive of essere and of avere?

activities.title: Le forme del congiuntivo presente
activities.intro: The present subjunctive swaps the vowels of the indicative: -are verbs take -i endings, while -ere and -ire verbs take -a endings. All three singular persons are identical, so Italian normally keeps the subject pronoun here even though it drops it everywhere else. The noi and voi forms are the same as the indicative for noi, and -iate for voi.

items.hr: Penso che tu parli molto bene l'italiano.
items.en: I think you speak Italian very well.
items.note: parlare: che io parli, che tu parli, che lui parli, che noi parliamo, che voi parliate, che loro parlino

items.hr: Credo che Marco prenda il treno delle otto.
items.en: I think Marco takes the eight o'clock train.
items.note: prendere: che io prenda, che noi prendiamo, che voi prendiate, che loro prendano

items.hr: Non è sicuro che i ragazzi partano domani mattina.
items.en: It is not certain that the boys are leaving tomorrow morning.
items.note: partire: che io parta, che loro partano

items.hr: Penso che lui sia stanco e che abbia bisogno di riposo.
items.en: I think he is tired and needs rest.
items.note: essere: sia, siamo, siate, siano. avere: abbia, abbiamo, abbiate, abbiano

items.hr: Credo che io capisca il problema, ma che tu capisca meglio di me.
items.en: I think I understand the problem, but that you understand it better than me.
items.note: the -isc- verbs keep it: che io capisca, che loro capiscano. The identical singular forms are why io and tu stay in the sentence

activities.title: Costruire il congiuntivo presente
activities.intro: Practise producing subjunctive forms, both the predictable ones and the frequent exceptions.

questions.prompt: Choose the correct form: Credo che Anna ___ in un ufficio in centro.
questions.explanation: Lavorare is an -are verb, so the subjunctive singular ending is -i. Lavora is the indicative.

questions.prompt: Which is the present subjunctive of fare for the person lui?
questions.explanation: Fare is irregular: faccia, facciamo, facciate, facciano. Facesse is the imperfect subjunctive.

questions.prompt: Complete with the present subjunctive of essere: Penso che voi ___ in ritardo.
questions.explanation: The voi form of essere in the present subjunctive is siate, with the typical -iate ending.

questions.prompt: Complete with the present subjunctive of andare: Non credo che loro ___ al mare questo fine settimana.
questions.explanation: Andare is irregular: vada, vada, vada, andiamo, andiate, vadano.

questions.prompt: Build the Italian for: 'I think you are right.'
questions.explanation: Avere ragione is the Italian for to be right, and after penso che the verb goes into the subjunctive.

activities.title: Un collega nuovo
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. You are talking about a new colleague and you keep framing everything as an impression, so every opinion needs the present subjunctive.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Hai conosciuto il collega nuovo?
lines.en: Have you met the new colleague?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Sì, e penso che sia una persona molto simpatica.
lines.en: Yes, and I think he is a very nice person.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Parla italiano?
lines.en: Does he speak Italian?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Credo che lui parli tre lingue, ma non ne sono sicuro.
lines.en: I think he speaks three languages, but I am not sure.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Da dove viene?
lines.en: Where does he come from?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Mi pare che venga da Bologna e che abiti qui da poco.
lines.en: I gather he comes from Bologna and has been living here for a short time.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Lavora nel nostro gruppo?
lines.en: Does he work in our group?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Penso che lui lavori con noi e che cominci lunedì.
lines.en: I think he works with us and starts on Monday.

days.title: Il congiuntivo dopo le opinioni
days.objective: You can express opinions, impressions and doubts with penso che, credo che, mi sembra che and dubito che followed by the subjunctive.
days.paretoFocus: Almost every real conversation contains an opinion, and in Italian an opinion that is not a stated fact pulls the subjunctive automatically.
drills: Write five opinions about your city, each with a different opinion trigger.
Turn three facts you know into doubts using dubito che.
Say aloud what you believe the weather will be like, starting with Credo che.

items: Which four verbs of opinion did you meet today?
What follows mi sembra che?
How do you say I doubt that he will come?

activities.title: Le opinioni e il dubbio
activities.intro: Verbs that frame something as a personal view rather than a fact take che plus the subjunctive. The common triggers are penso che and credo che for beliefs, mi sembra che and mi pare che for impressions, and dubito che or non sono sicuro che for doubt. The negative forms behave the same way, and non penso che is if anything an even stronger trigger.

items.hr: Penso che questo film sia troppo lungo.
items.en: I think this film is too long.
items.note: penso che, the basic opinion trigger

items.hr: Credo che i negozi chiudano alle otto.
items.en: I think the shops close at eight.

items.hr: Mi sembra che tu abbia bisogno di una vacanza.
items.en: It seems to me that you need a holiday.
items.note: mi sembra che and mi pare che work the same way

items.hr: Dubito che lo zio arrivi in orario.
items.en: I doubt that our uncle will arrive on time.
items.note: lo zio, masculine noun with lo before z

items.hr: Non sono sicura che questa strada sia quella giusta.
items.en: I am not sure this road is the right one.
items.note: the speaker is a woman, so sicura agrees

activities.title: Esprimere un'opinione
activities.intro: Practise turning opinions and doubts into correct subjunctive sentences.

questions.prompt: Choose the correct continuation: Mi sembra che il tuo amico ___ molto stanco oggi.
questions.explanation: Mi sembra che frames an impression, so the verb after che goes into the present subjunctive: sia.

questions.prompt: Which sentence expresses doubt correctly?
questions.explanation: Dubitare expresses doubt, so it takes the subjunctive venga, not the indicative viene.

questions.prompt: Complete with the present subjunctive of costare: Credo che questi stivali ___ troppo.
questions.explanation: Costare is an -are verb, so the loro form of the subjunctive is costino.

questions.prompt: Complete with the present subjunctive of sapere: Non penso che Luca ___ la verità.
questions.explanation: Sapere is irregular in the subjunctive: sappia, sappiamo, sappiate, sappiano.

questions.prompt: Build the Italian for: 'It seems to me that this restaurant is closed.'
questions.explanation: Mi sembra che is an impression trigger, so essere becomes sia and the adjective chiuso agrees with ristorante.

activities.title: Discutere di un film
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. You are giving opinions about a film you have just seen, so use opinion triggers plus the subjunctive.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Allora, ti è piaciuto il film?
lines.en: So, did you like the film?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Credo che sia un bel film, ma che duri troppo.
lines.en: I think it is a good film, but that it lasts too long.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: E gli attori?
lines.en: And the actors?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Mi pare che recitino benissimo, soprattutto la protagonista.
lines.en: I think they act very well, especially the lead actress.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Secondo te vincerà qualche premio?
lines.en: Do you think it will win any prizes?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Dubito che vinca, perché la concorrenza è forte.
lines.en: I doubt it will win, because the competition is strong.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Lo consiglieresti?
lines.en: Would you recommend it?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Non sono sicuro che piaccia a tutti, ma vale la pena vederlo.
lines.en: I am not sure everyone will like it, but it is worth seeing.

days.title: Il congiuntivo dopo i verbi di volontà e sentimento
days.objective: You can say what you want other people to do and how you feel about what they do, using voglio che, spero che, ho paura che and mi dispiace che.
days.paretoFocus: Wanting something from someone else and reacting emotionally to their actions are daily needs, and English uses an infinitive here where Italian must use che plus the subjunctive.
drills: Write three sentences with voglio che asking three different people to do something.
Write two hopes for next month starting with Spero che.
Say aloud two worries using Ho paura che.

items: Which trigger do you use to say I hope that?
How do you say I am sorry that you cannot come?
What happens after preferisco che?

activities.title: La volontà e il sentimento
activities.intro: Verbs of will (volere, preferire, desiderare) and of emotion (sperare, temere, avere paura, dispiacere, essere contento) take che plus the subjunctive when the two halves have different subjects. English says I want you to leave with an infinitive, but Italian has no such structure and must say voglio che tu parta.

items.hr: Voglio che tu venga con noi al mare.
items.en: I want you to come to the seaside with us.
items.note: never voglio te venire, the che clause is obligatory

items.hr: Spero che i miei genitori stiano bene.
items.en: I hope my parents are well.
items.note: stare: stia, stiamo, stiate, stiano

items.hr: Ho paura che l'esame sia difficile.
items.en: I am afraid the exam is difficult.
items.note: l'esame, masculine noun starting with a vowel

items.hr: Mi dispiace che voi non possiate restare a cena.
items.en: I am sorry that you cannot stay for dinner.
items.note: potere: possa, possiamo, possiate, possano

items.hr: Sono contenta che mia sorella abbia trovato una casa nuova.
items.en: I am glad that my sister has found a new house.
items.note: contenta agrees with a female speaker

activities.title: Volere e sperare
activities.intro: Practise expressing wishes and feelings about other people's actions.

questions.prompt: Choose the correct sentence for 'I want you to help me'.
questions.explanation: With two different subjects Italian needs che plus the subjunctive, and aiutare gives aiuti.

questions.prompt: Complete: Preferisco che ___ tu a parlare con il direttore.
questions.explanation: Preferire is a verb of will, so it takes the subjunctive sia.

questions.prompt: Complete with the present subjunctive of piovere: Ho paura che domani ___ tutto il giorno.
questions.explanation: Piovere is used impersonally and its present subjunctive form is piova.

questions.prompt: Complete with the present subjunctive of dire: Spero che loro ci ___ la verità.
questions.explanation: Dire is irregular: dica, diciamo, diciate, dicano.

questions.prompt: Build the Italian for: 'I want you to call me tonight.'
questions.explanation: Voglio che needs a subjunctive, and the object pronoun mi sits directly before the verb chiami.

activities.title: Organizzare una cena
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. You are organising a dinner and telling a friend what you want and hope, so use will and emotion triggers.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Chi cucina domani sera?
lines.en: Who is cooking tomorrow evening?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Voglio che tu prepari il primo e io penso al dolce.
lines.en: I want you to prepare the first course and I will take care of the dessert.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Va bene. Invitiamo anche Giulia?
lines.en: All right. Shall we invite Giulia too?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Spero che venga, ma ho paura che debba lavorare.
lines.en: I hope she comes, but I am afraid she has to work.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: E tuo fratello?
lines.en: And your brother?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Mi dispiace che lui non possa esserci, è fuori città.
lines.en: I am sorry that he cannot be there, he is out of town.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: A che ora ci vediamo?
lines.en: What time shall we meet?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Preferisco che arriviate verso le otto, così abbiamo tempo.
lines.en: I would rather you arrived around eight, so we have time.

days.title: Il congiuntivo dopo le congiunzioni
days.objective: You can use benché, sebbene, affinché, prima che, a meno che and purché with the subjunctive to link two ideas.
days.paretoFocus: These conjunctions carry concession, purpose and condition, the connectors that turn simple statements into the kind of structured speech expected at this level.
drills: Write three concessive sentences about your week using benché or sebbene.
Write one purpose sentence with affinché and one condition with purché.
Say aloud two things you must do prima che something else happens.

items: Which conjunctions mean although?
What does purché mean and what follows it?
Why does prima che need a subjunctive?

activities.title: Le congiunzioni con il congiuntivo
activities.intro: A fixed group of conjunctions always takes the subjunctive because the clause they introduce is not presented as a plain fact. Benché, sebbene and nonostante mean although, affinché and perché with the sense of so that express purpose, prima che means before, a meno che means unless, and purché means as long as. Learn them as a closed list and the choice becomes automatic.

items.hr: Benché piova, esco lo stesso.
items.en: Although it is raining, I am going out anyway.
items.note: benché and sebbene are interchangeable

items.hr: Sebbene sia tardi, vorrei finire questo lavoro.
items.en: Although it is late, I would like to finish this work.

items.hr: Ti scrivo tutto affinché tu non dimentichi niente.
items.en: I am writing everything down so that you do not forget anything.
items.note: affinché expresses purpose

items.hr: Voglio parlarti prima che tu parta.
items.en: I want to talk to you before you leave.
items.note: prima che plus subjunctive, but prima di plus infinitive if the subject is the same

items.hr: Vengo con voi, purché non si faccia troppo tardi.
items.en: I will come with you, as long as we do not stay out too late.
items.note: a meno che works the same way: a meno che non piova

activities.title: Collegare le idee
activities.intro: Practise linking two clauses with conjunctions that require the subjunctive.

questions.prompt: Choose the correct form: Sebbene lui ___ giovane, ha molta esperienza.
questions.explanation: Sebbene always takes the subjunctive, so essere becomes sia.

questions.prompt: Which conjunction fits: Ti presto la macchina ___ tu guidi con prudenza.
questions.explanation: Purché means as long as and sets a condition, and it is followed by the subjunctive.

questions.prompt: Complete with the present subjunctive of arrivare: Prepariamo la tavola prima che ___ gli ospiti.
questions.explanation: Prima che always takes the subjunctive, and the subject gli ospiti is plural.

questions.prompt: Complete with the present subjunctive of avere: Nonostante ___ molti impegni, mio padre ci aiuta sempre.
questions.explanation: Nonostante is a concessive conjunction and needs the subjunctive form abbia.

questions.prompt: Build the Italian for: 'Although it is late, we are going out.'
questions.explanation: Benché introduces a concession and takes the subjunctive sia, and the main clause stays in the indicative.

activities.title: Un viaggio in montagna
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. You are planning a trip and using conjunctions that trigger the subjunctive.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Partiamo comunque sabato?
lines.en: Are we leaving on Saturday anyway?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Sì, benché le previsioni non siano buone.
lines.en: Yes, although the forecast is not good.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Portiamo le catene per la neve?
lines.en: Shall we take snow chains?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Le porto io, affinché non abbiamo problemi in salita.
lines.en: I will bring them, so that we do not have trouble on the climb.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: A che ora dovremmo partire?
lines.en: What time should we leave?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Prima che ci sia traffico, quindi verso le sei.
lines.en: Before there is traffic, so around six.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: E se nevica troppo?
lines.en: And if it snows too much?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Andiamo lo stesso, a meno che non chiudano la strada.
lines.en: We are going anyway, unless they close the road.

days.title: Il congiuntivo dopo le espressioni impersonali
days.objective: You can use impersonal expressions such as è importante che, bisogna che, è possibile che and può darsi che with the subjunctive.
days.paretoFocus: Impersonal expressions let you state needs, rules and probabilities without naming who thinks so, which is exactly the tone used in offices, forms and public notices.
drills: Write three house rules starting with È importante che.
Turn three things you must do into sentences with Bisogna che.
Say aloud two guesses about tomorrow with Può darsi che.

items: What follows bisogna che?
How do you say it is better that you go now?
Which impersonal expression means it may be that?

activities.title: Le espressioni impersonali
activities.intro: Expressions built on è plus an adjective, or on bisogna and può darsi, take the subjunctive whenever they are followed by che plus a named subject. If there is no named subject, Italian uses the plain infinitive instead: è importante studiare, but è importante che tu studi.

items.hr: È importante che tu arrivi in orario.
items.en: It is important that you arrive on time.
items.note: compare è importante arrivare in orario, with no subject and no che

items.hr: Bisogna che qualcuno chiuda la finestra.
items.en: Somebody needs to close the window.
items.note: bisogna che plus subjunctive, or bisogna plus infinitive

items.hr: È possibile che l'ufficio sia già chiuso.
items.en: It is possible that the office is already closed.
items.note: l'ufficio, masculine noun starting with a vowel

items.hr: Può darsi che domani facciano sciopero.
items.en: It may be that they go on strike tomorrow.
items.note: può darsi che is very common in speech

items.hr: È meglio che voi prendiate lo psicologo sul serio.
items.en: It is better that you take the psychologist seriously.
items.note: lo psicologo, because the noun starts with ps

activities.title: Le espressioni impersonali con che
activities.intro: Practise impersonal expressions followed by a subject and the subjunctive.

questions.prompt: Choose the correct form: Bisogna che noi ___ subito.
questions.explanation: The noi form of the present subjunctive of partire is partiamo, identical to the indicative.

questions.prompt: Which sentence uses the impersonal expression correctly?
options: È meglio che tu vai a casa.
È meglio che tu vada a casa.
È meglio tu andare a casa.
È meglio che tu andare a casa.
questions.answer: È meglio che tu vada a casa.
questions.explanation: When a subject follows che, the impersonal expression requires the subjunctive vada.

questions.prompt: Complete with the present subjunctive of finire: È necessario che il lavoro ___ entro venerdì.
questions.explanation: Finire takes -isc- in the singular, giving finisca in the present subjunctive.

questions.prompt: Complete with the present subjunctive of esserci for a plural subject: Può darsi che ___ molte persone alla riunione.
questions.explanation: Esserci in the subjunctive gives ci sia in the singular and ci siano with a plural subject.

questions.prompt: Build the Italian for: 'It is important that everyone arrives on time.'
questions.explanation: The impersonal expression plus che needs the subjunctive, and tutti is plural so the verb is arrivino.

activities.title: Prima di un colloquio
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. You are advising a friend before a job interview, using impersonal expressions plus the subjunctive.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Domani ho il colloquio. Cosa mi consigli?
lines.en: I have the interview tomorrow. What do you advise me?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: È importante che tu arrivi almeno dieci minuti prima.
lines.en: It is important that you arrive at least ten minutes early.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Devo portare qualcosa?
lines.en: Should I bring anything?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Bisogna che tu porti una copia del curriculum.
lines.en: You need to bring a copy of your CV.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: E se mi fanno domande difficili?
lines.en: And if they ask me difficult questions?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: È normale che ne facciano, quindi rispondi con calma.
lines.en: It is normal for them to ask some, so answer calmly.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Secondo te mi chiameranno?
lines.en: Do you think they will call me?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Può darsi che ti richiamino già la settimana prossima.
lines.en: It may be that they call you back as early as next week.

days.title: Indicativo o congiuntivo, come scegliere
days.objective: You can decide correctly between the indicative and the subjunctive, and you know to use di plus the infinitive when both halves share the same subject.
days.paretoFocus: Over-applying the subjunctive is the most common error once learners discover it, and this lesson gives the two rules that stop it.
drills: Write three sentences with so che and three with penso che, and mark which verb form each takes.
Rewrite three penso che sentences with the same subject as penso di plus infinitive.
Say aloud one thing you are certain of and one thing you doubt about tomorrow.

items: Why does so che take the indicative?
What do you use when both verbs have the same subject?
Which is correct, spero di partire or spero che io parta?

activities.title: Fatto o opinione
activities.intro: The mood follows the meaning. If the main verb presents the clause as a known fact, use the indicative: so che, è vero che, sono sicuro che, ho visto che. If it frames it as an opinion, doubt, wish or emotion, use the subjunctive. There is a second rule that matters even more: when both halves have the same subject, Italian avoids che altogether and uses di plus the infinitive.

items.hr: So che Luca abita a Milano.
items.en: I know that Luca lives in Milan.
items.note: sapere states a fact, so the indicative abita is required

items.hr: Penso che Luca abiti a Milano.
items.en: I think Luca lives in Milan.
items.note: same content, but framed as an opinion, so abiti

items.hr: È vero che i prezzi sono aumentati.
items.en: It is true that prices have gone up.
items.note: è vero che states a fact, unlike è possibile che

items.hr: Penso di andare al mare quest'estate.
items.en: I am thinking of going to the seaside this summer.
items.note: same subject in both halves, so di plus infinitive, never penso che io vada

items.hr: Spero di rivederti presto, e spero che anche tu sia libero.
items.en: I hope to see you again soon, and I hope you are free too.
items.note: first half same subject, second half different subject

activities.title: Scegliere il modo giusto
activities.intro: Practise choosing between the indicative, the subjunctive and di plus the infinitive.

questions.prompt: Choose the correct form: So che tuo fratello ___ in Germania da tre anni.
questions.explanation: Sapere presents the clause as a fact, so the indicative vive is correct.

questions.prompt: Which sentence is correct Italian?
questions.explanation: When both halves share the subject io, Italian uses di plus the infinitive instead of a che clause.

questions.prompt: Complete with the correct form of essere after a verb of certainty: Sono sicuro che questo treno ___ quello giusto.
questions.explanation: Essere sicuro che states certainty, so the indicative form is used here.

questions.prompt: Complete with the correct form of avere: Mi sembra che questo appartamento ___ due bagni.
questions.explanation: Mi sembra che frames an impression rather than a fact, so the subjunctive is required.

questions.prompt: Build the Italian for: 'I am thinking of going home.'
questions.explanation: The subject of thinking and of going is the same person, so Italian uses penso di plus the infinitive.

activities.title: Notizie sul trasloco
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. A friend asks about a move, and you switch between facts, opinions and same-subject plans.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: È vero che Sara cambia casa?
lines.en: Is it true that Sara is moving house?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Sì, so che ha già firmato il contratto.
lines.en: Yes, I know she has already signed the contract.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: E dove va?
lines.en: And where is she going?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Credo che vada in un quartiere vicino al centro.
lines.en: I think she is going to a neighbourhood near the centre.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Tu la aiuterai?
lines.en: Will you help her?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Penso di darle una mano sabato mattina.
lines.en: I am thinking of giving her a hand on Saturday morning.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Serve un furgone?
lines.en: Is a van needed?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Sono sicuro che ne ha già prenotato uno.
lines.en: I am sure she has already booked one.

days.title: Il congiuntivo passato
days.objective: You can talk about a completed action inside a subjunctive clause, choosing the right auxiliary and making the participle agree.
days.paretoFocus: Opinions are usually about what has already happened, so without this tense half of your subjunctive sentences would be impossible.
drills: Write three sentences with Penso che about things that happened yesterday.
Convert three passato prossimo statements into Credo che sentences.
Say aloud two sentences with essere as the auxiliary and check the participle agreement.

items: How is the congiuntivo passato formed?
Which auxiliary do movement verbs take?
How do you say I am glad you arrived?

activities.title: Il congiuntivo passato
activities.intro: The congiuntivo passato is the present subjunctive of avere or essere plus the past participle. It replaces the passato prossimo inside a clause that needs the subjunctive. The rules for choosing the auxiliary and for agreeing the participle are exactly the ones you already know.

items.hr: Penso che loro abbiano già mangiato.
items.en: I think they have already eaten.
items.note: abbia, abbiamo, abbiate, abbiano plus the participle

items.hr: Credo che Anna sia partita ieri sera.
items.en: I think Anna left yesterday evening.
items.note: partire takes essere, so the participle agrees: partita

items.hr: Mi dispiace che voi non siate venuti alla festa.
items.en: I am sorry that you did not come to the party.
items.note: venire takes essere, and venuti agrees with a mixed or male plural

items.hr: Non è sicuro che il pacco sia arrivato.
items.en: It is not certain that the parcel has arrived.
items.note: il pacco, masculine singular, so arrivato

items.hr: Sono contento che tu abbia superato l'esame.
items.en: I am glad that you passed the exam.
items.note: superare takes avere, so no agreement with the subject

activities.title: Parlare del passato con il congiuntivo
activities.intro: Practise the past subjunctive with both auxiliaries and correct participle agreement.

questions.prompt: Choose the correct form: Credo che Maria ___ tornata a casa presto.
questions.explanation: Tornare takes essere, so the auxiliary in the past subjunctive is sia and the participle agrees with Maria.

questions.prompt: Which sentence is correct with the past subjunctive?
questions.explanation: Finire takes avere here, and the loro form of the auxiliary in the subjunctive is abbiano.

questions.prompt: Complete with the auxiliary needed by uscire: Mi sembra che i bambini ___ usciti senza giacca.
questions.explanation: Uscire is a verb of movement and takes essere, so the past subjunctive auxiliary is siano.

questions.prompt: Complete with the past participle of leggere: Dubito che lui abbia ___ tutto il libro.
questions.explanation: Leggere has the irregular participle letto, and with avere it does not agree with the subject.

questions.prompt: Build the Italian for: 'I am glad that you have found work.'
questions.explanation: An emotion trigger plus a completed action needs the past subjunctive, formed with abbia plus trovato.

activities.title: Dopo il fine settimana
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. You are guessing about what other people did over the weekend, so use the past subjunctive.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Hai visto Paolo e Chiara ieri?
lines.en: Did you see Paolo and Chiara yesterday?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: No, penso che siano andati in campagna.
lines.en: No, I think they went to the countryside.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: E hanno portato i cani?
lines.en: And did they take the dogs?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Credo che li abbiano lasciati a mia zia.
lines.en: I think they left them with my aunt.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Sono tornati tardi?
lines.en: Did they come back late?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Mi pare che siano rientrati verso mezzanotte.
lines.en: I gather they got back around midnight.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Ti hanno scritto?
lines.en: Did they write to you?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Sono contento che mi abbiano mandato due foto.
lines.en: I am glad they sent me two photos.

days.title: Il congiuntivo imperfetto
days.objective: You can use the imperfect subjunctive after a main verb in a past tense and after expressions of wish and hypothesis.
days.paretoFocus: Once the main verb moves into the past, every subjunctive trigger you have learned needs this tense, and it is also the engine of the hypothetical sentences ahead.
drills: Write out the imperfect subjunctive of essere and avere on paper, all six persons.
Rewrite three Penso che sentences as Pensavo che sentences.
Say aloud two wishes starting with Vorrei che.

items: What are the endings of the imperfect subjunctive?
Which tense follows Pensavo che?
What is the imperfect subjunctive of fare?

activities.title: Il congiuntivo imperfetto
activities.intro: The imperfect subjunctive is built on the infinitive stem plus -assi, -essi, -issi and their endings, and it is beautifully regular: parlassi, prendessi, partissi. It appears whenever the main clause sits in the past, and also after vorrei che. The main irregulars are fossi from essere, facessi from fare, dessi from dare, stessi from stare and bevessi from bere.

items.hr: Pensavo che tu fossi in vacanza.
items.en: I thought you were on holiday.
items.note: essere: fossi, fossi, fosse, fossimo, foste, fossero

items.hr: Credevo che loro parlassero anche il tedesco.
items.en: I thought they also spoke German.
items.note: parlare: parlassi, parlassi, parlasse, parlassimo, parlaste, parlassero

items.hr: Volevo che voi prendeste una decisione insieme.
items.en: I wanted you to make a decision together.

items.hr: Vorrei che tu avessi più tempo per te.
items.en: I wish you had more time for yourself.
items.note: vorrei che always takes this tense, even about the present

items.hr: Non sapevo che lui facesse il medico.
items.en: I did not know he was a doctor.
items.note: fare: facessi, facesse, facessero. Fare il medico means to work as a doctor

activities.title: Il passato nella frase principale
activities.intro: Practise the imperfect subjunctive after past-tense triggers and after vorrei che.

questions.prompt: Choose the correct form: Pensavo che il negozio ___ aperto la domenica.
questions.explanation: The main verb pensavo is in the past, so the subordinate verb takes the imperfect subjunctive fosse.

questions.prompt: Which is the imperfect subjunctive of dare for the person noi?
questions.explanation: Dare is irregular: dessi, dessi, desse, dessimo, deste, dessero.

questions.prompt: Complete with the imperfect subjunctive of venire: Speravo che anche i tuoi amici ___ alla riunione.
questions.explanation: Venire is regular in this tense: venissi, venisse, venissero.

questions.prompt: Complete with the imperfect subjunctive of avere: Vorrei che questa casa ___ un balcone più grande.
questions.explanation: Vorrei che introduces a wish and requires the imperfect subjunctive avesse.

questions.prompt: Build the Italian for: 'I thought you were at home.'
questions.explanation: A past main verb pulls the subordinate clause into the imperfect subjunctive fossi.

activities.title: Un malinteso
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. You explain a misunderstanding about a meeting, using past triggers plus the imperfect subjunctive.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Perché non sei venuto alla riunione?
lines.en: Why did you not come to the meeting?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Pensavo che fosse annullata, mi dispiace.
lines.en: I thought it was cancelled, I am sorry.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Chi te lo ha detto?
lines.en: Who told you that?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Nessuno, credevo che ci avvisassero per messaggio.
lines.en: Nobody, I thought they would let us know by message.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Avevamo bisogno di te.
lines.en: We needed you.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Vorrei che mi aveste chiamato, sarei arrivato subito.
lines.en: I wish you had called me, I would have come straight away.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: La prossima volta ti scrivo io.
lines.en: Next time I will write to you myself.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Preferirei che lo facessi, così non sbaglio più.
lines.en: I would prefer you to do that, so I do not get it wrong again.

days.title: Il congiuntivo trapassato
days.objective: You can express an action completed before another past action inside a subjunctive clause.
days.paretoFocus: This tense closes the subjunctive system and is the first half of every sentence of regret, which is the most emotionally useful structure in the language.
drills: Write three sentences with Pensavo che about something that had already happened.
Practise the auxiliaries: three sentences with avessi and three with fossi.
Say aloud one regret about last year using Se plus this tense.

items: How is the congiuntivo trapassato formed?
Which auxiliary does andare take?
How do you say I did not know they had already left?

activities.title: Il congiuntivo trapassato
activities.intro: The pluperfect subjunctive is the imperfect subjunctive of avere or essere plus the past participle: avessi parlato, fossi andato. It marks an action finished before another past action, and it is the tense that appears after se in sentences about things that did not happen.

items.hr: Non sapevo che voi aveste già pagato il conto.
items.en: I did not know you had already paid the bill.
items.note: avessi, avessi, avesse, avessimo, aveste, avessero plus the participle

items.hr: Pensavo che lei fosse partita la settimana prima.
items.en: I thought she had left the week before.
items.note: partire takes essere, so partita agrees with lei

items.hr: Mi sembrava che tu avessi capito tutto.
items.en: It seemed to me that you had understood everything.

items.hr: Era strano che i nostri amici non fossero arrivati.
items.en: It was strange that our friends had not arrived.
items.note: arrivati agrees with a masculine plural subject

items.hr: Se avessi saputo la verità, avrei parlato subito.
items.en: If I had known the truth, I would have spoken straight away.
items.note: a first look at the regret pattern you will build later

activities.title: Un'azione anteriore nel passato
activities.intro: Practise the pluperfect subjunctive with both auxiliaries.

questions.prompt: Choose the correct form: Credevo che tu ___ già finito il progetto.
questions.explanation: After a past main verb, an earlier completed action takes the pluperfect subjunctive avessi finito.

questions.prompt: Which sentence is correct with the pluperfect subjunctive?
questions.explanation: Uscire takes essere, so the auxiliary is fossero and the participle uscite agrees with a feminine plural subject.

questions.prompt: Complete with the auxiliary required by dormire: Mi sembrava che i bambini non ___ dormito bene.
questions.explanation: Dormire takes avere, so the pluperfect subjunctive auxiliary for loro is avessero.

questions.prompt: Complete with the past participle of nascere: Non sapevo che tuo nonno fosse ___ in Sicilia.
questions.explanation: Nascere takes essere and its participle is nato, agreeing with the masculine subject.

questions.prompt: Build the Italian for: 'I did not think they had already left.'
questions.explanation: Partire takes essere in the pluperfect subjunctive, so fossero partiti, with the participle in the masculine plural.

activities.title: Una brutta sorpresa
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. You explain what you had wrongly assumed about a booking, using the pluperfect subjunctive.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Perché non abbiamo trovato posto?
lines.en: Why did we not find a seat?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Credevo che tu avessi prenotato online.
lines.en: I thought you had booked online.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Io pensavo che l'avessi fatto tu.
lines.en: I thought you had done it.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Era strano che non ci fosse arrivata nessuna conferma.
lines.en: It was strange that no confirmation had arrived.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: E adesso?
lines.en: And now?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Se avessimo controllato prima, avremmo evitato tutto questo.
lines.en: If we had checked earlier, we would have avoided all this.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Proviamo con il ristorante accanto.
lines.en: Let us try the restaurant next door.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Non sapevo che avessero aperto anche lì, andiamo.
lines.en: I did not know they had opened there too, let us go.

days.title: Il periodo ipotetico della realtà
days.objective: You can build real conditional sentences with se plus the present indicative, followed by the present or the future.
days.paretoFocus: This is the pattern for rules, plans and warnings, the everyday conditional that covers most real situations.
drills: Write three real-condition sentences about your daily routine, each opening with Se.
Write two warnings that pair a present-tense Se clause with a future main clause.
Say aloud three consequences of tomorrow's weather.

items: Which mood follows se in a real condition?
Can the main clause be in the future?
How do you say if you study, you will pass?

activities.title: Il periodo ipotetico della realtà
activities.intro: The first type of conditional presents something as genuinely possible or automatic. Se takes the present indicative, while the main clause offers a choice of three: present indicative, future or imperative. Note that Italian does not use the subjunctive after se here, and unlike English it can also put the future in both halves.

items.hr: Se piove, resto a casa.
items.en: If it rains, I stay at home.
items.note: present in both halves, an automatic consequence

items.hr: Se studi ogni giorno, supererai l'esame.
items.en: If you study every day, you will pass the exam.
items.note: present after se, future in the main clause

items.hr: Se hai fame, apri il frigorifero.
items.en: If you are hungry, open the fridge.
items.note: the main clause can be an imperative

items.hr: Se domani farà bel tempo, andremo in bicicletta.
items.en: If the weather is nice tomorrow, we will go cycling.
items.note: Italian allows the future after se as well, unlike English

items.hr: Se non trovi il biglietto, devi comprarne un altro.
items.en: If you cannot find the ticket, you have to buy another one.
items.note: ne refers back to the ticket

activities.title: Condizioni reali
activities.intro: Practise real conditional sentences with the indicative.

questions.prompt: Choose the correct form: Se ___ tempo, ti accompagno alla stazione.
questions.explanation: A real condition takes the present indicative after se, so ho is correct.

questions.prompt: Which sentence is a correct real conditional?
options: Se parti presto, arrivi prima di pranzo.
Se partissi presto, arrivi prima di pranzo.
Se parta presto, arrivi prima di pranzo.
Se sei partito presto, arriveresti prima.
questions.answer: Se parti presto, arrivi prima di pranzo.
questions.explanation: Both halves stay in the present indicative because the condition is presented as genuinely possible.

questions.prompt: Complete with the future of chiamare: Se non ti vedo domani, ti ___ nel pomeriggio.
questions.explanation: After a present-tense condition the main clause can take the simple future, giving chiamerò.

questions.prompt: Complete with the present indicative of volere: Se ___ venire con noi, dimmelo entro stasera.
questions.explanation: The condition is real, so se is followed by the present indicative vuoi.

questions.prompt: Build the Italian for: 'If it rains, I stay at home.'
questions.explanation: The real conditional keeps the indicative in both halves, with no subjunctive after se.

activities.title: Piani per il fine settimana
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. You are making weekend plans that depend on real conditions.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Che facciamo sabato?
lines.en: What shall we do on Saturday?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Se c'è il sole, andiamo al lago.
lines.en: If it is sunny, we will go to the lake.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: E se piove?
lines.en: And if it rains?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Se piove, restiamo in città e visitiamo il museo.
lines.en: If it rains, we stay in town and visit the museum.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Devo prenotare qualcosa?
lines.en: Should I book anything?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Se prenoti oggi, paghi di meno.
lines.en: If you book today, you pay less.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: E per il pranzo?
lines.en: And for lunch?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Se partiamo presto, mangeremo in un agriturismo.
lines.en: If we leave early, we will eat at a farmhouse restaurant.

days.title: Il periodo ipotetico della possibilità
days.objective: You can build hypothetical sentences with se plus the imperfect subjunctive and the present conditional.
days.paretoFocus: This is how you talk about what you would do, the structure behind advice, daydreams and polite suggestions.
drills: Write three sentences about what you would do with more free time.
Give a friend two pieces of advice starting with Se fossi in te.
Say aloud two hypotheses about winning a prize.

items: Which tense follows se in this pattern?
Which tense goes in the main clause?
How do you say if I were you, I would wait?

activities.title: Il periodo ipotetico della possibilità
activities.intro: The second type describes something possible but not real at the moment. The pattern is fixed: after se comes the imperfect subjunctive, while the main clause takes the present conditional. Never put the conditional after se. The set phrase se fossi in te means if I were you and introduces most spoken advice.

items.hr: Se avessi più tempo, studierei il pianoforte.
items.en: If I had more time, I would study the piano.
items.note: se plus imperfect subjunctive, then present conditional

items.hr: Se fossi in te, aspetterei ancora un po'.
items.en: If I were you, I would wait a bit longer.
items.note: se fossi in te is the standard way to give advice

items.hr: Se vincessi alla lotteria, comprerei una casa al mare.
items.en: If I won the lottery, I would buy a house by the sea.

items.hr: Se lui parlasse più lentamente, capiremmo tutto.
items.en: If he spoke more slowly, we would understand everything.

items.hr: Verrei volentieri, se non dovessi lavorare.
items.en: I would gladly come, if I did not have to work.
items.note: the two halves can also come in this order

activities.title: Ipotesi possibili
activities.intro: Practise the second conditional, keeping each half of the sentence in its own mood.

questions.prompt: Choose the correct form: Se ___ ricca, viaggerei tutto l'anno.
questions.explanation: This pattern needs the imperfect subjunctive after se, and the conditional stays in the main clause.

questions.prompt: Which hypothetical sentence is correct?
questions.explanation: The conditional never follows se: the condition takes avessi and the result takes verrei.

questions.prompt: Complete with the present conditional of andare: Se non facesse così caldo, ___ a fare una passeggiata.
questions.explanation: The main clause of a possible hypothesis takes the present conditional, giving andrei.

questions.prompt: Complete with the imperfect subjunctive of potere: Se ___ scegliere, lavorerei quattro giorni alla settimana.
questions.explanation: The condition after se requires the imperfect subjunctive form potessi.

questions.prompt: Build the Italian for: 'If I had more time, I would travel more.'
questions.explanation: The imperfect subjunctive avessi sets the condition and the conditional viaggerei gives the result.

activities.title: Consigli sul lavoro
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. A friend is unhappy at work and you give advice with hypothetical sentences.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Non so se cambiare lavoro.
lines.en: I do not know whether to change jobs.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Se fossi in te, parlerei prima con il tuo capo.
lines.en: If I were you, I would talk to your boss first.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: E se non cambiasse niente?
lines.en: And if nothing changed?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Se non cambiasse niente, cercherei un'altra azienda.
lines.en: If nothing changed, I would look for another company.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Ho paura di sbagliare.
lines.en: I am afraid of making a mistake.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Se avessi paura anch'io, chiederei consiglio a un collega.
lines.en: If I were afraid too, I would ask a colleague for advice.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Tu cosa faresti al posto mio?
lines.en: What would you do in my place?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Se ricevessi un'offerta migliore, la accetterei senza dubbi.
lines.en: If I got a better offer, I would accept it without hesitation.

days.title: Il periodo ipotetico dell'irrealtà
days.objective: You can talk about things that did not happen and express regret with se plus the pluperfect subjunctive and the past conditional, and you can see all three conditional patterns side by side.
days.paretoFocus: Regret and hindsight come up constantly in real conversation, and this lesson also gives you the full ladder of the three conditional types in one place.
drills: Write three regrets about last year using this pattern.
Copy the three conditional patterns onto one card and add an example to each.
Say aloud two things you would have done differently at school.

items: Which tense follows se when the situation is impossible?
Which tense goes in the main clause?
What are the three conditional patterns in order?

activities.title: Il periodo ipotetico dell'irrealtà
activities.intro: The third type talks about what did not happen, so it is the language of regret. After se comes the pluperfect subjunctive, while the main clause carries the past conditional. Keep the whole ladder in mind: reality pairs the present indicative with the indicative, possibility pairs the imperfect subjunctive with the present conditional, and impossibility pairs the pluperfect subjunctive with the past conditional.

items.hr: Se avessi studiato di più, avrei superato l'esame.
items.en: If I had studied more, I would have passed the exam.
items.note: the classic regret pattern

items.hr: Se fossimo partiti prima, non avremmo perso il treno.
items.en: If we had left earlier, we would not have missed the train.
items.note: partire takes essere, so fossimo partiti

items.hr: Se me lo avessi detto, ti avrei aiutato volentieri.
items.en: If you had told me, I would have gladly helped you.

items.hr: Se avesse fatto bel tempo, saremmo andati in montagna.
items.en: If the weather had been nice, we would have gone to the mountains.
items.note: andare takes essere in the past conditional, so saremmo andati

items.hr: Se piove resto a casa, se piovesse resterei a casa, se fosse piovuto sarei restato a casa.
items.en: If it rains I stay at home, if it rained I would stay at home, if it had rained I would have stayed at home.
items.note: the three patterns in one line, reality, possibility, impossibility

activities.title: Ipotesi impossibili e rimpianti
activities.intro: Practise the third conditional and compare it with the other two patterns.

questions.prompt: Choose the correct form: Se ___ saputo prima, sarei venuto a prenderti.
questions.explanation: The impossible condition takes the pluperfect subjunctive avessi saputo, and the conditional stays in the other half.

questions.prompt: Which sentence expresses regret correctly?
options: Se avessi prenotato, avremmo trovato posto.
Se avrei prenotato, avremmo trovato posto.
Se ho prenotato, avremmo trovato posto.
questions.answer: Se avessi prenotato, avremmo trovato posto.
questions.explanation: Regret about the past pairs the pluperfect subjunctive after se with the past conditional in the main clause.

questions.prompt: Complete with the past conditional of dire: Se lo avessi immaginato, te lo ___ subito.
questions.explanation: The result half of an impossible condition takes the past conditional, formed with avrei plus detto.

questions.prompt: Complete with the pluperfect subjunctive of alzarsi: Se ti ___ prima, non avresti perso l'autobus.
questions.explanation: Reflexive verbs take essere, so the pluperfect subjunctive is fossi alzato, with the participle agreeing with the subject.

questions.prompt: Build the Italian for: 'If you had told me, I would have helped you.'
questions.explanation: The combined pronouns me lo come before the auxiliary, and the pattern pairs avessi detto with ti avrei aiutato.

activities.title: Rimpianti dopo il viaggio
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. You are looking back at a trip that went badly, using the third conditional.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Che viaggio disastroso.
lines.en: What a disastrous trip.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Se avessimo controllato il meteo, non saremmo partiti.
lines.en: If we had checked the weather, we would not have left.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: E l'albergo era terribile.
lines.en: And the hotel was terrible.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Se avessi letto le recensioni, avrei scelto un altro posto.
lines.en: If I had read the reviews, I would have chosen another place.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Almeno il cibo era buono.
lines.en: At least the food was good.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Se fossimo rimasti un giorno in più, avremmo visto anche il centro storico.
lines.en: If we had stayed one more day, we would have seen the old town too.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Ci torneresti?
lines.en: Would you go back?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Se ci tornassi, organizzerei tutto con più calma.
lines.en: If I went back, I would organise everything more calmly.

days.title: Il condizionale passato
days.objective: You can use the past conditional for unrealised intentions, polite regret and the future seen from the past.
days.paretoFocus: It covers two very different everyday needs, saying what you would have done and reporting what somebody said they would do.
drills: Write three things you would have liked to do last weekend.
Report three promises people made to you, using this tense.
Say aloud two polite apologies with Avrei dovuto.

items: How is the past conditional formed?
Which tense does Italian use for the future in the past?
How do you say I would have liked to come?

activities.title: Il condizionale passato
activities.intro: The past conditional is the present conditional of avere or essere plus the past participle: avrei fatto, sarei andato. Besides unrealised intentions and regret, it has a use that surprises English speakers, since Italian uses it where English uses would plus the infinitive after a past reporting verb: ha detto che sarebbe venuto.

items.hr: Avrei voluto restare, ma dovevo lavorare.
items.en: I would have liked to stay, but I had to work.
items.note: avrei voluto, avrei dovuto and avrei potuto are extremely common

items.hr: Sarei venuto volentieri alla tua festa.
items.en: I would have gladly come to your party.
items.note: venire takes essere, so sarei venuto agrees with the speaker

items.hr: Avresti dovuto avvisarmi prima.
items.en: You should have told me earlier.
items.note: the standard way to say should have

items.hr: Mi ha detto che sarebbe arrivato alle sette.
items.en: He told me he would arrive at seven.
items.note: the future in the past, where English uses would arrive

items.hr: Pensavamo che sarebbero partiti il giorno dopo.
items.en: We thought they would leave the next day.
items.note: partiti agrees with the masculine plural subject

activities.title: Il condizionale passato in uso
activities.intro: Practise regret, unrealised intentions and the future in the past.

questions.prompt: Choose the correct form: ___ dovuto chiamarti, scusami.
questions.explanation: Dovere takes avere, so the past conditional is avrei dovuto.

questions.prompt: Complete: Ci ha promesso che ___ a prenderci in stazione.
questions.explanation: After a past reporting verb Italian uses the past conditional for the future in the past.

questions.prompt: Complete with the past conditional of potere: ___ aiutarti, ma non me lo hai chiesto.
questions.explanation: Potere takes avere, giving avrei potuto for an unrealised possibility.

questions.prompt: Complete with the past conditional of tornare: Ero sicuro che lei ___ prima di cena.
questions.explanation: Tornare takes essere, so the auxiliary is sarebbe and the participle agrees with lei.

questions.prompt: Build the Italian for: 'He said that he would arrive late.'
questions.explanation: The future in the past is expressed with the past conditional sarebbe arrivato, not with the simple future.

activities.title: Un appuntamento mancato
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. You apologise for missing an appointment and report what people had said they would do.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Ti ho aspettato per un'ora.
lines.en: I waited for you for an hour.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Scusa, avrei dovuto avvisarti subito.
lines.en: Sorry, I should have let you know straight away.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Cosa è successo?
lines.en: What happened?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Il tecnico mi aveva detto che sarebbe passato alle tre.
lines.en: The technician had told me he would come by at three.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: E non è venuto?
lines.en: And he did not come?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: È arrivato alle cinque, altrimenti sarei uscito prima.
lines.en: He arrived at five, otherwise I would have left earlier.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Potevi mandarmi un messaggio.
lines.en: You could have sent me a message.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Hai ragione, avrei potuto scriverti dal telefono.
lines.en: You are right, I could have written to you from my phone.

days.title: Il futuro anteriore
days.objective: You can say that one future action will be finished before another, and you can use the same tense to guess about the present.
days.paretoFocus: It gives you the sequencing words quando, dopo che and appena in the future, plus the most natural way to speculate out loud.
drills: Write three sentences with Quando avrò finito about tomorrow.
Write two guesses about where somebody is right now using this tense.
Say aloud three steps of a plan in order, using appena.

items: How is the futuro anteriore formed?
Which conjunctions typically introduce it?
How do you guess that he has probably already left?

activities.title: Il futuro anteriore
activities.intro: The future perfect is the simple future of avere or essere plus the past participle: avrò finito, sarai arrivato. Its first job is sequencing, marking the action that will be over before another future action, usually after quando, dopo che or appena. Its second job is guessing, since Italian uses it to say that something has probably happened.

items.hr: Quando avrò finito il lavoro, ti chiamerò.
items.en: When I have finished work, I will call you.
items.note: English uses the present here, Italian uses the future perfect

items.hr: Appena saranno arrivati, ceneremo insieme.
items.en: As soon as they have arrived, we will have dinner together.
items.note: arrivare takes essere, so saranno arrivati

items.hr: Dopo che avrai letto il contratto, potrai firmarlo.
items.en: Once you have read the contract, you will be able to sign it.
items.note: potrai from potere, an irregular future

items.hr: Non risponde, sarà già uscito di casa.
items.en: He is not answering, he has probably already left the house.
items.note: the same tense used as a guess about the present

items.hr: Avranno avuto un problema con il treno.
items.en: They have probably had a problem with the train.
items.note: supposition rather than sequence

activities.title: Prima e dopo nel futuro
activities.intro: Practise the future perfect for sequencing and for supposition.

questions.prompt: Choose the correct form: Quando ___ i compiti, potrai guardare la televisione.
questions.explanation: The action that must be complete first goes into the future perfect after quando.

questions.prompt: Which sentence is a supposition about the present?
questions.explanation: The future perfect used on its own expresses a guess about something that has probably already happened.

questions.prompt: Complete with the future perfect of arrivare: Appena ___ a Roma, ti manderò un messaggio.
questions.explanation: Arrivare takes essere, so the future perfect is sarò arrivato, with the participle agreeing with the speaker.

questions.prompt: Complete with the future perfect of dimenticare: Non mi ha ancora risposto, ___ il mio messaggio.
questions.explanation: Here the future perfect expresses a probable explanation, formed with avrà plus the participle.

questions.prompt: Build the Italian for: 'When I have finished, I will call you.'
questions.explanation: Italian puts the earlier future action into the future perfect and keeps the simple future in the main clause.

activities.title: Organizzare la giornata
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. You describe the order of tomorrow's tasks and make a guess, using the future perfect.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: A che ora sei libero domani?
lines.en: What time are you free tomorrow?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Quando avrò finito la riunione, sarò libero.
lines.en: When I have finished the meeting, I will be free.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Passiamo in banca insieme?
lines.en: Shall we stop by the bank together?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Sì, appena avrò ritirato i documenti, andiamo.
lines.en: Yes, as soon as I have collected the documents, we will go.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Marco viene con noi?
lines.en: Is Marco coming with us?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Non risponde, avrà già spento il telefono.
lines.en: He is not answering, he has probably already turned off his phone.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: E la spesa?
lines.en: And the shopping?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Dopo che avremo mangiato, faremo anche quella.
lines.en: Once we have eaten, we will do that too.

days.title: Il discorso indiretto al presente
days.objective: You can report what somebody says, asks or orders when the reporting verb is in the present.
days.paretoFocus: Reporting other people's words is constant in conversation and at work, and starting with a present reporting verb keeps the tenses unchanged.
drills: Report three sentences a friend said to you today, using Dice che.
Turn two questions into indirect ones with Mi chiede se.
Say aloud two instructions someone gave you using Mi dice di.

items: Does the tense of the original sentence change when dice che introduces it?
How do you report a yes or no question?
How do you report an order?

activities.title: Il discorso indiretto
activities.intro: With dice, chiede or dice di in the present, the tense of the original sentence does not change: only the pronouns, the possessives and words like qui or oggi may need adjusting. A statement is introduced by dice che, a yes or no question by chiede se, a question word keeps its own word, and an order becomes dice di plus the infinitive.

items.hr: Anna dice che oggi non può venire.
items.en: Anna says that she cannot come today.
items.note: original: oggi non posso venire. The verb stays in the present

items.hr: Marco dice che ha finito il suo lavoro.
items.en: Marco says he has finished his work.
items.note: il mio becomes il suo

items.hr: Mi chiede se voglio un caffè.
items.en: He asks me whether I want a coffee.
items.note: a yes or no question becomes se

items.hr: Mi chiede dove abito e con chi vivo.
items.en: He asks me where I live and who I live with.
items.note: the question word stays, but the word order becomes that of a statement

items.hr: Il medico mi dice di bere più acqua.
items.en: The doctor tells me to drink more water.
items.note: an imperative becomes di plus the infinitive

activities.title: Riferire le parole degli altri
activities.intro: Practise reporting statements, questions and orders with a present reporting verb.

questions.prompt: Report 'Non ho tempo' said by Luca. Which is correct?
options: Luca dice che non ha tempo.
Luca dice che non ho tempo.
Luca dice di non avere tempo.
Luca chiede se non ha tempo.
questions.answer: Luca dice che non ha tempo.
questions.explanation: The person changes from io to lui, so the verb becomes ha, while the present tense itself stays.

questions.prompt: Report the question 'Vieni con noi?'. Which is correct?
options: Mi chiede che vengo con loro.
Mi chiede se vengo con loro.
Mi dice di venire se con loro.
questions.answer: Mi chiede se vengo con loro.
questions.explanation: A yes or no question is reported with se, and noi becomes loro from the reporter's point of view.

questions.prompt: Report the instruction 'Aspettami qui' said by Anna: Anna mi dice di ___ lì.
questions.explanation: An imperative in reported speech becomes di plus the infinitive, and the object pronoun attaches to the end of it.

questions.prompt: Complete the report of 'Il mio treno parte alle nove': Dice che il ___ treno parte alle nove.
questions.explanation: The possessive shifts from the speaker's point of view to the reporter's, so mio becomes suo.

questions.prompt: Build the Italian for: 'She says that she cannot come today.'
questions.explanation: With a present reporting verb the reported verb keeps its present tense, so può venire stays as it is.

activities.title: Un messaggio da riferire
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. You pass on what a third person has just told you, using reported speech.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Hai sentito Giulia?
lines.en: Have you heard from Giulia?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Sì, dice che stasera non può uscire.
lines.en: Yes, she says that she cannot go out tonight.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Perché?
lines.en: Why?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Dice che deve finire un lavoro urgente.
lines.en: She says she has to finish an urgent job.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Ha detto altro?
lines.en: Did she say anything else?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Mi chiede se possiamo spostare la cena a domani.
lines.en: She is asking whether we can move the dinner to tomorrow.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Per me va bene.
lines.en: That is fine by me.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Allora le scrivo, e mi dice di avvisare anche Paolo.
lines.en: Then I will write to her, and she is telling me to let Paolo know too.

days.title: Il discorso indiretto al passato
days.objective: Report what someone said in the past, shifting the tenses, the pronouns and the time words correctly.
days.paretoFocus: Almost every story you tell about work, family or news involves repeating what somebody else said, and the past reporting verb changes everything that follows.
drills: Take three text messages you received this week and report each one starting with 'Mi ha scritto che...'.
Report three commands you were given, using 'Mi ha detto di' plus the infinitive.
Say out loud three promises someone made to you, using the condizionale passato.

items: Which tense replaces the presente when the reporting verb is in the past?
How do you report an order or a command in Italian?
What happens to 'domani' and 'oggi' when you report a past conversation?

activities.title: Che cosa ha detto
activities.intro: When the reporting verb is in the past (ha detto, ha chiesto, ha risposto), everything inside the reported clause moves one step back. The presente becomes the imperfetto, the passato prossimo becomes the trapassato, and the futuro becomes the condizionale passato. Time words move too: oggi becomes quel giorno, domani becomes il giorno dopo.

items.hr: Marco ha detto che era stanco.
items.en: Marco said that he was tired.
items.note: sono stanco becomes era stanco, presente to imperfetto

items.hr: Mi ha chiesto se avevo finito il lavoro.
items.en: He asked me whether I had finished the work.
items.note: hai finito becomes avevo finito, passato prossimo to trapassato

items.hr: Ha risposto che sarebbe arrivato alle otto.
items.en: He answered that he would arrive at eight.
items.note: arriverò becomes sarebbe arrivato, the future in the past is the condizionale passato

items.hr: Le ho detto di chiudere la finestra.
items.en: I told her to close the window.
items.note: an order becomes di plus the infinitive, never a subjunctive

items.hr: Hanno spiegato che quel giorno non potevano venire.
items.en: They explained that they could not come that day.
items.note: oggi becomes quel giorno when you report later

activities.title: Riferire le parole degli altri
activities.intro: Practise shifting tenses and commands into reported speech after a past reporting verb.

questions.prompt: Anna ha detto: 'Ho comprato il pane.' Reported: Anna ha detto che ___ il pane.
questions.explanation: The original was a passato prossimo, and after a past reporting verb it moves back to the trapassato prossimo: aveva comprato.

questions.prompt: Luca ha promesso: 'Ti telefonerò domani.' Reported: Luca ha promesso che mi ___ il giorno dopo.
questions.explanation: A future seen from a point in the past is expressed with the condizionale passato, not the condizionale presente.

questions.prompt: Report the command: 'Aspettami!' becomes Mi ha detto ___ aspettarlo.
questions.explanation: Orders and requests are reported with the preposition plus the infinitive, so an imperative turns into a simple infinitive construction.

questions.prompt: The present becomes the imperfetto: 'Sto bene' reported as Ha detto che ___ bene.
questions.explanation: After a past reporting verb, a present tense in the original words shifts to the imperfetto: sto becomes stava.

questions.prompt: Build the Italian for: 'She said that she had already seen that movie.'
questions.explanation: The reported clause uses the trapassato prossimo, and già sits between the auxiliary and the participle.

activities.title: Che cosa ti ha detto il capo?
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. A colleague asks you to report a conversation you had earlier with your boss, so every answer uses past reported speech.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Allora, che cosa ti ha detto il capo stamattina?
lines.en: So, what did the boss tell you this morning?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Mi ha detto che il progetto era in ritardo di due settimane.
lines.en: He told me that the project was two weeks behind.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: E ti ha chiesto qualcosa su di noi?
lines.en: And did he ask you anything about us?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Mi ha chiesto se avevamo già parlato con il cliente.
lines.en: He asked me whether we had already spoken to the client.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Speriamo bene. Ha promesso qualcosa?
lines.en: Let us hope for the best. Did he promise anything?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Ha promesso che ci avrebbe dato una mano il giorno dopo.
lines.en: He promised that he would give us a hand the following day.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Perfetto. E a me non ha lasciato nessun messaggio?
lines.en: Perfect. And did he leave no message for me?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Sì, mi ha detto di avvisarti di preparare i documenti.
lines.en: Yes, he told me to let you know to prepare the documents.

days.title: La concordanza dei tempi
days.objective: Choose the right subjunctive tense automatically by looking at whether the main verb is present or past.
days.paretoFocus: This one grid turns the congiuntivo from four memorised tables into a single decision you can make while you are speaking.
drills: Write four sentences starting with 'Penso che' and then rewrite each one starting with 'Pensavo che'.
Say three things you did not know at the time, using 'Non sapevo che' plus the imperfetto or trapassato.
Take three opinions about the past and express them with 'Sono contento che' plus the congiuntivo passato.

items: Present trigger plus an action happening at the same time: which subjunctive tense?
Past trigger plus an action that happened earlier: which subjunctive tense?
Rewrite 'Credo che sia partito' with a past main verb.

activities.title: Il tempo giusto del congiuntivo
activities.intro: The tense of the subjunctive depends on two things: the tense of the main verb, and whether the second action is at the same time or earlier. A present main verb takes the congiuntivo presente for the same time and the congiuntivo passato for something earlier. A past main verb takes the imperfetto for the same time and the trapassato for something earlier.

items.hr: Penso che Marco sia in ritardo.
items.en: I think Marco is late.
items.note: present trigger, same time, congiuntivo presente

items.hr: Penso che Marco sia partito ieri.
items.en: I think Marco left yesterday.
items.note: present trigger, earlier action, congiuntivo passato

items.hr: Pensavo che Marco fosse in ritardo.
items.en: I thought Marco was late.
items.note: past trigger, same time, congiuntivo imperfetto

items.hr: Pensavo che Marco fosse già partito.
items.en: I thought Marco had already left.
items.note: past trigger, earlier action, congiuntivo trapassato

items.hr: Speravo che tu venissi alla festa con noi.
items.en: I was hoping you would come to the party with us.
items.note: a past hope about something later still takes the imperfetto

activities.title: Presente o passato, quale congiuntivo
activities.intro: Look at the main verb first, then decide which subjunctive tense the second clause needs.

questions.prompt: Credevo che voi ___ già a casa a quell'ora.
questions.explanation: Credevo is a past main verb, and the second action happens at the same time, so the congiuntivo imperfetto is required.

questions.prompt: Mi dispiace che tu non ___ venire stasera.
questions.explanation: Mi dispiace is in the present and the action is simultaneous, so the congiuntivo presente is the correct choice.

questions.prompt: Present trigger, earlier action: Sono contento che tu ___ superato l'esame.
questions.explanation: A present main verb plus an action already completed calls for the congiuntivo passato, formed with the present subjunctive of avere plus the participle.

questions.prompt: Past trigger, same time: Speravo che il tempo ___ bello.
questions.explanation: Speravo is past, so the subordinate clause drops to the congiuntivo imperfetto of essere.

questions.prompt: Build the Italian for: 'I thought that you were younger.'
questions.explanation: The past main verb pensavo forces the congiuntivo imperfetto fossi in the second clause.

activities.title: Un malinteso fra amici
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. You and a friend clear up a misunderstanding about a dinner, so both of you keep saying what you thought and what you think now.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Ieri sera ti ho aspettato per un'ora davanti al ristorante.
lines.en: Yesterday evening I waited for you for an hour in front of the restaurant.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Mi dispiace, credevo che l'appuntamento fosse per le nove.
lines.en: I am sorry, I thought the meeting was for nine o'clock.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Pensavo che tu avessi letto il mio messaggio.
lines.en: I thought you had read my message.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Non sapevo che me ne avessi mandato uno, avevo il telefono spento.
lines.en: I did not know you had sent me one, my phone was switched off.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Capisco. Adesso però penso che sia meglio riprovare sabato.
lines.en: I understand. Now, though, I think it is better to try again on Saturday.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Sono contento che tu non te la sia presa troppo.
lines.en: I am glad you did not take it too badly.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Figurati. Speravo solo che ci fosse una spiegazione.
lines.en: Not at all. I was only hoping there was an explanation.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: C'era, e questa volta credo che arriverò in anticipo.
lines.en: There was one, and this time I think I will arrive early.

days.title: I pronomi relativi che e cui
days.objective: Join two sentences with che for subject and object, and with cui after any preposition.
days.paretoFocus: Relative clauses are how you stop speaking in short choppy sentences and start sounding like an adult speaker.
drills: Describe five objects in your room, each with a relative clause introduced by che.
Say three sentences about people using a preposition plus cui, such as working with or writing to.
Take three short English sentences where the relative is dropped and put it back in Italian.

items: Does che change for gender or number?
What must always come immediately before cui?
Why is 'Il libro ho comprato' impossible in Italian?

activities.title: Unire due frasi
activities.intro: Che is invariable and covers both the subject and the direct object of the relative clause. After any preposition you must switch to cui: con cui, in cui, a cui, di cui, per cui. Remember that English can leave the relative out, while Italian never can.

items.hr: Il libro che sto leggendo è bellissimo.
items.en: The book I am reading is wonderful.
items.note: English drops the relative here, Italian keeps che

items.hr: La ragazza che abita qui sopra è francese.
items.en: The girl who lives upstairs is French.
items.note: che as subject, no change for gender

items.hr: La persona con cui lavoro si chiama Elena.
items.en: The person I work with is called Elena.
items.note: the preposition comes first in Italian, never at the end

items.hr: Il paese in cui sono nato è piccolissimo.
items.en: The village where I was born is tiny.
items.note: in cui also works as a place relative

items.hr: L'amico a cui ho prestato la macchina non risponde.
items.en: The friend I lent my car to is not answering.
items.note: a cui, because prestare takes a

activities.title: Che oppure cui
activities.intro: Decide whether the relative clause needs the invariable form or the one that follows a preposition.

questions.prompt: Questa è la ragione per ___ non sono venuto alla riunione.
questions.explanation: There is a preposition in front of the relative, and after any preposition Italian uses cui, never che.

questions.prompt: Il film ___ abbiamo visto ieri era davvero noioso.
questions.explanation: The relative is the direct object of abbiamo visto and there is no preposition, so che is correct.

questions.prompt: Fill in the relative: La casa in ___ ho vissuto da bambino non c'è più.
questions.explanation: The preposition in requires the form used after prepositions, giving the meaning of the place where something happened.

questions.prompt: Fill in the relative: Il ragazzo ___ hai conosciuto ieri è mio cugino.
questions.explanation: No preposition is involved and the relative is the direct object of hai conosciuto, so the invariable form is used.

questions.prompt: Build the Italian for: 'The colleague I work with is very kind.'
questions.explanation: Italian puts the preposition before the relative pronoun, so the English ending in 'with' becomes con cui in the middle of the sentence.

activities.title: La ragazza che abita qui sotto
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. You are describing neighbours and places to a friend who has just moved in, so every answer contains a relative clause.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Chi è la signora che ci ha salutato all'ingresso?
lines.en: Who is the lady who greeted us at the entrance?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: È la vicina con cui divido il posto auto.
lines.en: She is the neighbour I share the parking space with.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: E il bar all'angolo, quello che ha i tavolini fuori?
lines.en: And the bar on the corner, the one with the little tables outside?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: È il posto in cui faccio colazione quasi ogni mattina.
lines.en: It is the place where I have breakfast almost every morning.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Mi hanno parlato di un idraulico bravo, lo conosci?
lines.en: They told me about a good plumber, do you know him?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Sì, è l'uomo a cui ho telefonato quando si è rotto il termosifone.
lines.en: Yes, he is the man I phoned when the radiator broke.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Perfetto, mi dai il numero che hai salvato?
lines.en: Perfect, will you give me the number you saved?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Certo, è la persona di cui mi fido di più in questo palazzo.
lines.en: Of course, he is the person I trust most in this building.

days.title: Il quale e i relativi con preposizione
days.objective: Use il quale, la quale, i quali and le quali as the formal, agreeing alternative to cui, and combine them with prepositions.
days.paretoFocus: This is the relative you meet in contracts, official letters and the news, and it is the only one that removes ambiguity when two people are mentioned.
drills: Rewrite five sentences you built with cui, replacing it with the agreeing formal relative.
Write three sentences where two people are named and the relative must show which one you mean.
Practise the contracted forms aloud: with a, di, in and su plus the four agreeing forms.

items: What are the four agreeing forms of the formal relative?
Which relative would you choose in an official letter?
How does the formal relative solve an ambiguity that cui leaves open?

activities.title: Il relativo formale
activities.intro: This relative carries an article and comes in four shapes that agree with the noun: il quale and i quali for masculine, la quale and le quali for feminine. They are the formal alternative to cui, and after a preposition the article contracts in the normal way: a plus il quale gives al quale, di plus la quale gives della quale. Because they show gender and number, they can point clearly at one of two people.

items.hr: Il professore al quale ho scritto mi ha risposto subito.
items.en: The teacher I wrote to answered me straight away.
items.note: a plus il quale contracts to al quale, same meaning as a cui

items.hr: La collega della quale ti parlavo si è licenziata.
