items.en: The colleague I was telling you about has resigned.
items.note: feminine singular agreement, di plus la quale

items.hr: I documenti nei quali ho trovato l'errore sono questi.
items.en: The documents in which I found the mistake are these ones.
items.note: masculine plural, in plus i quali gives nei quali

items.hr: Le ragioni per le quali ho scelto questo lavoro sono personali.
items.en: The reasons why I chose this job are personal.
items.note: feminine plural, no contraction after per

items.hr: Ho parlato con il fratello di Anna, il quale vive a Torino.
items.en: I spoke to Anna's brother, who lives in Turin.
items.note: the masculine form makes clear that the brother lives there, not Anna

activities.title: La forma che concorda
activities.intro: Choose the agreeing formal relative and make it match the noun in gender and number.

questions.prompt: La ditta per ___ lavoro da tre anni ha sede a Milano.
questions.explanation: Ditta is feminine singular, so the formal relative takes the feminine singular form.

questions.prompt: Gli amici con ___ sono andato in vacanza sono molto simpatici.
questions.explanation: Amici is masculine plural, and the formal relative must agree with it.

questions.prompt: Make it formal and agreeing, feminine singular: La ragione per cui sono qui becomes La ragione per la ___ sono qui.
questions.explanation: The formal relative agrees with ragione, a feminine singular noun, so the singular feminine form is used after the article.

questions.prompt: Make it formal and agreeing, masculine plural: I motivi per cui ho deciso becomes I motivi per i ___ ho deciso.
questions.explanation: Motivi is masculine plural, so the formal relative takes its plural form after the plural article.

questions.prompt: Build the Italian for: 'The house in which we lived was very old.'
questions.explanation: In plus la contracts to nella, and the relative agrees with casa, which is feminine singular.

activities.title: Una telefonata formale
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. You are on the phone with an office clerk about a document, so both speakers use the formal agreeing relative.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Buongiorno, mi conferma il numero della pratica alla quale si riferisce?
lines.en: Good morning, can you confirm the number of the file you are referring to?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Certo, è la pratica per la quale ho già inviato due moduli.
lines.en: Of course, it is the file for which I have already sent two forms.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: E i documenti nei quali manca la firma sono arrivati?
lines.en: And have the documents in which the signature is missing arrived?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Sì, li ho consegnati all'impiegata con la quale avevo parlato lunedì.
lines.en: Yes, I handed them to the clerk I had spoken to on Monday.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Le invieremo una risposta all'indirizzo che risulta nel sistema.
lines.en: We will send you an answer at the address that appears in the system.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: La ringrazio. C'è un termine entro il quale devo rispondere?
lines.en: Thank you. Is there a deadline by which I must reply?

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Trenta giorni, dopo i quali la richiesta viene archiviata.
lines.en: Thirty days, after which the request is filed away.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Perfetto, allora invio subito la lettera della quale mi ha parlato.
lines.en: Perfect, then I will send the letter you told me about right away.

days.title: I pronomi combinati, ripasso e uso
days.objective: Combine an indirect and a direct pronoun in one word group and use them fluently in short answers.
days.paretoFocus: Native speakers almost never repeat a noun twice, so short natural replies depend entirely on stacking these two pronouns.
drills: Answer five yes questions about lending or giving things, replacing both nouns with pronouns.
Say three sentences with the form used for both him and her, and notice it never changes.
Practise the participle agreement out loud: dati, date, dato, data after the combined pronouns.

items: What happens to mi, ti, ci and vi when a direct pronoun follows?
Which single form covers both 'to him' and 'to her' plus a direct object?
Does the past participle agree with the direct pronoun in these structures?

activities.title: Due pronomi, una parola
activities.intro: When an indirect pronoun meets a direct one, the indirect comes first and its final vowel becomes e: mi lo becomes me lo. The forms for him and her merge into one invariable group written as a single word. The past participle agrees with the direct pronoun, not with the subject.

items.hr: Me lo presti un attimo, per favore?
items.en: Will you lend it to me for a moment, please?
items.note: mi plus lo gives me lo

items.hr: Te ne ho già parlato la settimana scorsa.
items.en: I already talked to you about it last week.
items.note: ti plus ne gives te ne

items.hr: Glielo dico io, non ti preoccupare.
items.en: I will tell him about it myself, do not worry.
items.note: the same form works for him and for her, written as one word

items.hr: Ce lo hanno promesso più volte.
items.en: They promised it to us several times.
items.note: ci plus lo gives ce lo

items.hr: Se ne sono andati senza salutare.
items.en: They left without saying goodbye.
items.note: si plus ne gives se ne

activities.title: Rispondere senza ripetere
activities.intro: Replace both objects with pronouns and keep the participle agreement right.

questions.prompt: Hai dato le chiavi a Luca? Sì, ___ date ieri sera.
questions.explanation: The indirect pronoun for Luca merges with the feminine plural direct pronoun, and the participle agrees, giving date.

questions.prompt: Mi porti il caffè? Sì, ___ porto subito.
questions.explanation: The answer comes from the other speaker, so the indirect pronoun refers to you, and ti plus lo becomes te lo.

questions.prompt: Replace both objects with pronouns: Ci hanno spiegato la regola becomes ___ hanno spiegata.
questions.explanation: Ci changes to ce before a direct pronoun, and la refers to regola, which is why the participle ends in a.

questions.prompt: Replace both objects with pronouns: Vi mando le foto becomes ___ mando subito.
questions.explanation: Vi becomes ve in front of a direct pronoun, and foto is feminine plural, so le follows.

questions.prompt: Build the Italian for: 'He brought them to us yesterday.'
questions.explanation: Ci becomes ce before li, and the participle agrees with the masculine plural direct pronoun.

activities.title: Chi ha preso il caricabatterie?
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. You and a flatmate are tracking down borrowed objects, so nearly every reply uses two pronouns together.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Senti, hai visto il mio caricabatterie?
lines.en: Listen, have you seen my charger?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: L'ho prestato a Giulia, gliel'ho dato sabato.
lines.en: I lent it to Giulia, I gave it to her on Saturday.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: E i miei auricolari? Me li restituisce qualcuno?
lines.en: And my earphones? Is anyone giving them back to me?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Te li riporto io stasera, li ho in borsa.
lines.en: I will bring them back to you tonight, they are in my bag.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Va bene. Puoi chiedere a Giulia quando torna?
lines.en: All right. Can you ask Giulia when she is coming back?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Gliene parlo domani, la vedo in ufficio.
lines.en: I will mention it to her tomorrow, I will see her at the office.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Grazie. E le chiavi di scorta, ce le hanno lasciate i proprietari?
lines.en: Thanks. And the spare keys, did the owners leave them for us?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Sì, ce le hanno consegnate il primo giorno.
lines.en: Yes, they handed them over to us on the first day.

days.title: Il passivo con essere
days.objective: Turn an active sentence into a passive one with essere plus the past participle, in any tense, with correct agreement.
days.paretoFocus: News, notices and workplace emails are full of the passive, and it lets you say what happened without naming who did it.
drills: Turn five active sentences about your week into passives with essere.
Write three passive sentences with the agent introduced by the right preposition.
Say the same passive sentence in the present, the passato prossimo and the future.

items: What does the past participle agree with in a passive sentence?
Which preposition introduces the person who does the action?
How do you form the passato prossimo passive?

activities.title: Essere più participio
activities.intro: The passive puts essere in front of a past participle, which then always agrees with the subject in gender and number. In compound tenses you get two participles, as in è stato consegnato. The person doing the action, when you mention it at all, is introduced by da.

items.hr: Questo dolce è preparato da mio nonno.
items.en: This dessert is made by my grandfather.
items.note: present passive, dolce is masculine singular

items.hr: Il pacco è stato consegnato ieri mattina.
items.en: The parcel was delivered yesterday morning.
items.note: passato prossimo passive, two participles

items.hr: Le lettere sono state spedite la settimana scorsa.
items.en: The letters were sent last week.
items.note: feminine plural, both participles agree

items.hr: Il ponte sarà inaugurato in primavera.
items.en: The bridge will be opened in spring.
items.note: future passive, essere carries the tense

items.hr: La riunione era stata rimandata due volte.
items.en: The meeting had been postponed twice.
items.note: trapassato passive, riunione is feminine singular

activities.title: Dalla forma attiva a quella passiva
activities.intro: Build passive sentences with essere and make every participle agree.

questions.prompt: Le finestre ___ state pulite ieri pomeriggio.
questions.explanation: The passato prossimo passive uses the present of essere plus stato plus the participle, and finestre is plural.

questions.prompt: Active: Il direttore ha firmato i contratti. Passive: I contratti ___ dal direttore.
questions.explanation: The active verb is in the passato prossimo, so the passive keeps that tense and adds stati before the participle.

questions.prompt: Make the participle agree: Le decisioni sono state ___ dal comitato.
questions.explanation: The subject decisioni is feminine plural, so the participle of prendere ends in e.

questions.prompt: Introduce the agent: Il quadro è stato dipinto ___ un artista locale.
questions.explanation: The person or thing performing the action in a passive sentence is introduced by this preposition.

questions.prompt: Build the Italian for: 'The letter was written by my grandfather.'
questions.explanation: Lettera is feminine singular, so both stata and scritta end in a, and the agent follows the preposition.

activities.title: I lavori in condominio
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. A neighbour asks what has been done in the building, so the answers report events in the passive.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Sai se il tetto è stato riparato?
lines.en: Do you know whether the roof has been repaired?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Sì, è stato sistemato la settimana scorsa da una ditta di fuori.
lines.en: Yes, it was fixed last week by a firm from out of town.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: E le scale? Erano piene di polvere.
lines.en: And the stairs? They were full of dust.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Sono state pulite ieri, adesso sono perfette.
lines.en: They were cleaned yesterday, now they are perfect.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: L'ascensore invece è ancora fermo.
lines.en: The lift, though, is still out of order.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Sarà controllato lunedì, me lo ha confermato l'amministratore.
lines.en: It will be checked on Monday, the building manager confirmed it to me.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: E la riunione? Era già stata fissata per giovedì.
lines.en: And the meeting? It had already been set for Thursday.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: È stata rimandata a lunedì prossimo, purtroppo.
lines.en: It has been postponed to next Monday, unfortunately.

days.title: Il passivo con venire e andare
days.objective: Use venire for a passive that stresses the action and andare for a passive that carries obligation.
days.paretoFocus: Instructions, rules and signs in Italy use these two verbs constantly, and no learner guesses that va fatto means it must be done.
drills: Rewrite three passive sentences with essere in the present, replacing it with the action passive.
Write four instructions for a household task, each using the obligation passive.
Say aloud why the action passive cannot appear in the passato prossimo.

items: In which tenses can the action passive be used?
What extra meaning does the obligation passive add?
Which passive would you choose on a sign giving a rule?

activities.title: Venire e andare al posto di essere
activities.intro: Venire can replace essere in the passive, but only in the simple tenses: present, imperfetto and future. It stresses the action itself rather than the resulting state, and it can never be used in compound tenses. Andare plus the participle is different again: it means that something must be done, so va fatto is equal to deve essere fatto.

items.hr: Il pacco viene consegnato ogni lunedì mattina.
items.en: The parcel is delivered every Monday morning.
items.note: action passive, present tense only, no compound forms exist

items.hr: I moduli verranno controllati domani in mattinata.
items.en: The forms will be checked tomorrow morning.
items.note: the future is a simple tense, so venire works

items.hr: La porta veniva chiusa a chiave ogni sera.
items.en: The door was locked every evening.
items.note: imperfetto, repeated action in the past

items.hr: Il modulo va compilato in stampatello.
items.en: The form must be filled in in block capitals.
items.note: va plus participle equals deve essere compilato

items.hr: Questi documenti vanno consegnati entro venerdì.
items.en: These documents must be handed in by Friday.
items.note: andare agrees with the subject, so documenti takes vanno

activities.title: Azione oppure obbligo
activities.intro: Decide whether the sentence describes an action in progress or a rule that must be followed.

questions.prompt: Questo piatto ___ servito freddo, è la regola della casa.
questions.explanation: The sentence states a rule, and the obligation passive with andare means that it must be served cold.

questions.prompt: Which sentence is impossible in Italian?
questions.explanation: The action passive exists only in simple tenses, so a compound form with venire is not possible.

questions.prompt: Obligation passive, third person plural: Le bottiglie ___ riciclate, non buttate.
questions.explanation: Andare in the passive means must be, and it agrees with the plural subject bottiglie.

questions.prompt: Action passive in the present, third person singular: La posta ___ ritirata alle nove.
questions.explanation: Venire replaces essere in the simple tenses and puts the emphasis on the action of collecting.

questions.prompt: Build the Italian for: 'The form must be signed by both parents.'
questions.explanation: The obligation passive uses andare plus the participle, and the agent still follows the same preposition as with essere.

activities.title: Allo sportello del comune
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. A clerk explains a procedure to you, using the obligation passive for rules and the action passive for what happens routinely.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Buongiorno, questa domanda va presentata di persona.
lines.en: Good morning, this application must be submitted in person.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Capisco. E i documenti vanno allegati tutti insieme?
lines.en: I see. And must the documents all be attached together?

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Sì, e le copie vengono controllate subito allo sportello.
lines.en: Yes, and the copies are checked immediately at the counter.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: La firma va messa in fondo alla seconda pagina?
lines.en: Must the signature be put at the bottom of the second page?

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Esatto. Le pratiche vengono registrate nello stesso giorno.
lines.en: Exactly. The files are registered on the same day.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: E la risposta verrà mandata per posta o per email?
lines.en: And will the answer be sent by post or by email?

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Per email, ma l'indirizzo va scritto in modo leggibile.
lines.en: By email, but the address must be written legibly.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Perfetto, allora compilo tutto e torno fra dieci minuti.
lines.en: Perfect, then I will fill everything in and come back in ten minutes.

days.title: Il si passivante
days.objective: Use si plus a verb to say what is done in general, and make the verb agree in number with the thing involved.
days.paretoFocus: Adverts, menus, signs and everyday generalisations all use this structure, and it is the fastest way to avoid naming who does something.
drills: Write five small ads with this structure, three singular and two plural.
Describe habits in your country with sentences that need no subject at all.
Take five active sentences and rewrite them without saying who does the action.

items: What decides whether the verb is singular or plural in this structure?
How is it different from the impersonal use with no object?
Rewrite 'Vendono appartamenti in centro' without naming the sellers.

activities.title: Si vende, si vendono
activities.intro: With si plus a verb you say what people in general do. When there is a thing involved, that thing is grammatically the subject, so the verb agrees with it: singular thing takes a singular verb, plural things take a plural verb. When there is no such thing at all, the verb simply stays singular.

items.hr: In Italia si cena piuttosto tardi.
items.en: In Italy people have dinner rather late.
items.note: no object at all, so the verb stays singular

items.hr: In questa zona si parla ancora il dialetto.
items.en: In this area people still speak the dialect.
items.note: dialetto is singular, so the verb is singular

items.hr: In quel negozio si vendono prodotti biologici.
items.en: In that shop they sell organic products.
items.note: prodotti is plural, so the verb becomes plural

items.hr: Non si accettano carte di credito.
items.en: Credit cards are not accepted.
items.note: a very common sign, plural noun and plural verb

items.hr: Si cerca un appartamento, si cercano due camere.
items.en: One flat wanted, two rooms wanted.
items.note: the same verb changes number with what follows

activities.title: Singolare o plurale
activities.intro: Make the verb agree with the noun that follows it in this impersonal structure.

questions.prompt: In questo ristorante ___ ottimi dolci fatti in casa.
questions.explanation: Dolci is plural and is the grammatical subject, so the verb has to be plural too.

questions.prompt: Which sentence is correct?
questions.explanation: The plural noun camere forces a plural verb, and the particle stays in front of it.

questions.prompt: Agree the verb with the plural noun: In quel mercato si ___ frutta e verdura freschissime.
questions.explanation: Two coordinated nouns count as plural, so the verb takes the third person plural ending.

questions.prompt: Complete the advert: ___ cerca una segretaria con esperienza.
questions.explanation: The impersonal particle goes before the verb, and since only one person is wanted the verb stays singular.

questions.prompt: Build the Italian for: 'In summer many houses by the sea are rented out.'
questions.explanation: Case is plural, so the verb is plural, and the particle stays immediately before the verb.

activities.title: Come funziona qui
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. A visitor asks how things are usually done in your town, so both speakers generalise with the impersonal structure.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: A che ora si mangia di solito la sera qui?
lines.en: What time do people usually eat in the evening here?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Si cena verso le otto e mezza, prima è difficile trovare posto.
lines.en: People have dinner around half past eight, earlier it is hard to find a table.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: E nei bar si possono pagare le consumazioni con la carta?
lines.en: And in the bars can you pay for drinks by card?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Quasi sempre, ma in alcuni posti non si accettano pagamenti sotto i cinque euro.
lines.en: Almost always, but in some places payments under five euros are not accepted.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Dove si comprano i biglietti dell'autobus?
lines.en: Where do you buy bus tickets?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Si comprano in tabaccheria oppure si fa il biglietto con il telefono.
lines.en: You buy them at the tobacconist or you get the ticket with your phone.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: E la domenica si trova qualcosa di aperto?
lines.en: And on Sundays is anything open?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Poco, però si organizzano sempre mercatini in piazza.
lines.en: Not much, but there are always little markets organised in the square.

days.title: I verbi pronominali complessi
days.objective: Use the high frequency pronominal verbs farcela, andarsene, cavarsela, prendersela, volerci and metterci in everyday speech.
days.paretoFocus: These six expressions cover complaining, coping, leaving and timing, and they appear in almost every informal conversation.
drills: Say three things you can and cannot cope with this week, using the verb of managing.
Describe how long three journeys take, once impersonally and once with yourself as the subject.
Write three sentences telling a friend not to take something badly.

items: Which verb means to leave a place, built from andare?
What is the difference between the impersonal time verb and the personal one?
How do you say 'I get by' about a language?

activities.title: Farcela, andarsene, cavarsela
activities.intro: Italian has a family of verbs where ci, la, ne and si are welded onto a normal verb and change its meaning completely. You cannot work them out from the parts, so learn them as whole units with the pronouns in place. They are extremely frequent in spoken Italian.

items.hr: Non ce la faccio più, sono troppo stanco.
items.en: I cannot cope any more, I am too tired.
items.note: farcela, to manage or to cope

items.hr: Me ne vado, ci vediamo domani.
items.en: I am off, see you tomorrow.
items.note: andarsene, to leave, from andare plus si plus ne

items.hr: Con l'inglese me la cavo abbastanza bene.
items.en: I get by fairly well in English.
items.note: cavarsela, to manage or to get by

items.hr: Non te la prendere, non era mia intenzione.
items.en: Do not take it badly, I did not mean it.
items.note: prendersela, to take offence

items.hr: Ci vogliono due ore, ma io ci metto di più.
items.en: It takes two hours, but I take longer.
items.note: volerci is impersonal, metterci has a personal subject

activities.title: Espressioni di ogni giorno
activities.intro: Pick the right pronominal verb and get its pronouns in the correct order.

questions.prompt: ___ tre ore per arrivare a Roma in treno.
questions.explanation: The impersonal verb of duration agrees with the amount of time, and tre ore is plural.

questions.prompt: È tardi, ___ vado a casa.
questions.explanation: In the first person the reflexive pronoun becomes me before ne, giving the standard way of announcing that you are leaving.

questions.prompt: Complete the verb of getting by: Con il francese non me la ___ molto bene.
questions.explanation: The infinitive is cavarsela, and in the first person singular the verb itself takes the normal are ending.

questions.prompt: Complete the expression of managing: Loro non ___ la fanno a finire entro stasera.
questions.explanation: The ci in farcela becomes ce in front of the direct pronoun la, and the order never changes.

questions.prompt: Build the Italian for: 'I cannot manage it on my own.'
questions.explanation: The two pronouns stay glued in front of the conjugated verb, and da solo expresses doing something alone.

activities.title: Una settimana pesante
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. You tell a friend how overloaded you are, and the whole conversation runs on pronominal verbs.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Hai una faccia stanchissima, tutto bene?
lines.en: You look exhausted, is everything all right?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Non ce la faccio più con questi turni, dormo cinque ore.
lines.en: I cannot cope with these shifts any more, I sleep five hours.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Quanto ci metti ad arrivare in ufficio la mattina?
lines.en: How long does it take you to get to the office in the morning?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Ci metto quasi un'ora, e ce ne vuole un'altra per tornare.
lines.en: It takes me almost an hour, and it takes another one to get back.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Hai provato a parlarne con il tuo responsabile?
lines.en: Have you tried talking to your manager about it?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Sì, ma se la cava sempre dicendo che non dipende da lui.
lines.en: Yes, but he always gets out of it by saying it does not depend on him.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Non te la prendere, prima o poi cambierà qualcosa.
lines.en: Do not take it badly, sooner or later something will change.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Speriamo, altrimenti me ne vado e cerco un altro posto.
lines.en: Let us hope so, otherwise I will leave and look for another job.

days.title: Il gerundio e le sue funzioni
days.objective: Use the gerund for actions in progress, for means, for cause and for concession, respecting the same subject rule.
days.paretoFocus: One short form replaces four different clauses, which makes your speech faster and much more compact.
drills: Describe what three people in your household are doing right now.
Explain how you learned three skills, using the gerund of means.
Write two concessive sentences with the short form meaning although.

items: What are the two gerund endings and which conjugations take them?
Which verb goes in front of the gerund to say an action is in progress?
Why must both verbs usually share the same subject?

activities.title: Sto studiando, imparando, uscendo
activities.intro: The gerund ends in ando for are verbs and in endo for ere and ire verbs. With stare it describes an action in progress, and on its own it expresses how, when or why something happens. The subject of the gerund is normally the same as the subject of the main verb.

items.hr: Sto preparando la cena, ti richiamo dopo.
items.en: I am making dinner, I will call you back later.
items.note: stare plus gerund, action in progress right now

items.hr: Ho imparato l'italiano guardando i film.
items.en: I learned Italian by watching films.
items.note: means, it answers the question how

items.hr: Uscendo di casa, ho incontrato il vicino.
items.en: As I was leaving home, I met the neighbour.
items.note: time, and the subject is the same in both clauses

items.hr: Essendo stanco, sono andato a letto presto.
items.en: Being tired, I went to bed early.
items.note: cause, equal to siccome ero stanco

items.hr: Pur avendo studiato, non ha superato l'esame.
items.en: Although he had studied, he did not pass the exam.
items.note: pur plus gerund expresses concession

activities.title: Una forma, quattro significati
activities.intro: Recognise which job the gerund is doing and form it correctly.

questions.prompt: ___ ogni giorno, si migliora molto in fretta.
questions.explanation: The sentence explains the means by which you improve, and that is exactly the job of the gerund.

questions.prompt: 'Anche se ha piovuto, siamo usciti' can be rewritten as:
questions.explanation: Concession needs pur in front of the gerund, and piovere in this use takes essere in the compound form.

questions.prompt: Form the gerund of the verb fare: Che cosa stai ___ in questo momento?
questions.explanation: Fare keeps its long stem fac before the ending, so the gerund is irregular in shape but regular in ending.

questions.prompt: Concessive form meaning although: ___ avendo poco tempo, mi ha aiutato lo stesso.
questions.explanation: This short word in front of the gerund is what turns a neutral clause into a concessive one.

questions.prompt: Build the Italian for: 'I learned a lot by travelling alone.'
questions.explanation: English uses by plus the ing form, while Italian uses the bare gerund with no preposition at all.

activities.title: Che cosa stai facendo?
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. A friend calls while you are busy, and the conversation keeps using the gerund for progress, means and cause.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Ciao, disturbo? Che cosa stai facendo?
lines.en: Hi, am I disturbing you? What are you doing?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Sto finendo di sistemare la cucina, ma dimmi pure.
lines.en: I am finishing tidying the kitchen, but go ahead.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Volevo sapere come hai imparato a cucinare così bene.
lines.en: I wanted to know how you learned to cook so well.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Guardando mia nonna e sbagliando tante volte.
lines.en: By watching my grandmother and by getting it wrong many times.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Anche io vorrei provare, ma ho pochissimo tempo.
lines.en: I would like to try too, but I have very little time.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Pur lavorando tutto il giorno, riesco a cucinare la sera.
lines.en: Even though I work all day, I manage to cook in the evening.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Essendo così stanca, di solito ordino qualcosa.
lines.en: Being so tired, I usually order something.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Vieni sabato, cucinando insieme impari più in fretta.
lines.en: Come on Saturday, by cooking together you learn faster.

days.title: L'infinito sostantivato e le frasi implicite
days.objective: Use the infinitive as a noun and build short implicit clauses with prima di, dopo, senza and invece di.
days.paretoFocus: When the subject is the same in both clauses, Italian strongly prefers these short forms, and using them makes your speech sound much less heavy.
drills: Write four general statements where the infinitive is the subject of the sentence.
Describe your morning in three sentences using before and after plus the infinitive.
Turn three sentences with the same subject twice into one short implicit clause.

items: Which form does Italian use where English uses the ing form as a subject?
What comes after dopo when both clauses share a subject?
When can you replace senza che plus the subjunctive with a shorter form?

activities.title: Mangiare bene, dopo aver mangiato
activities.intro: The infinitive can work as a noun, and with an article in front it becomes a full noun with a slightly formal flavour. It is also the form required after prima di, after dopo, after senza and after invece di whenever the two clauses share the same subject. After dopo you use the past infinitive, and avere usually loses its final vowel.

items.hr: Fumare fa male alla salute.
items.en: Smoking is bad for your health.
items.note: the infinitive is the subject, English uses the ing form

items.hr: Il suo continuo lamentarsi mi ha stancato.
items.en: His constant complaining has worn me out.
items.note: with an article the infinitive becomes a real noun

items.hr: Prima di uscire, controlla di avere le chiavi.
items.en: Before going out, check that you have your keys.
items.note: prima di plus infinitive, same subject in both parts

items.hr: Dopo aver mangiato, siamo andati a fare una passeggiata.
items.en: After eating, we went for a walk.
items.note: dopo takes the past infinitive, and avere drops its final e

items.hr: Sono uscito senza salutare nessuno.
items.en: I left without saying goodbye to anyone.
items.note: senza plus infinitive replaces senza che plus congiuntivo

activities.title: Frasi corte e naturali
activities.intro: Practise shortening clauses that share their subject with the main verb.

questions.prompt: ___ aver finito i compiti, è andato a giocare in cortile.
questions.explanation: Only this one is followed directly by the past infinitive, with no preposition in between.

questions.prompt: Same subject, so the short form is required: Penso ___ ragione.
questions.explanation: When the subject of both verbs is the same, Italian uses di plus the infinitive instead of a full clause.

questions.prompt: Use the past infinitive: Dopo ___ parlato con il capo, mi sono sentito meglio.
questions.explanation: The past infinitive is formed with the auxiliary plus the participle, and in this position the auxiliary normally loses its final vowel.

questions.prompt: Turn two clauses into one: Sono uscito e non ho detto niente becomes Sono uscito ___ dire niente.
questions.explanation: This preposition plus the infinitive expresses a negative accompanying action when the subject is the same.

questions.prompt: Build the Italian for: 'Reading every day helps a lot.'
questions.explanation: The English ing form used as a subject becomes a plain infinitive in Italian, and the verb stays singular.

activities.title: Buone abitudini
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. You and a friend compare daily routines, so the whole conversation uses infinitives and short implicit clauses.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Dormire poco è il mio problema principale.
lines.en: Sleeping little is my main problem.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Prima di andare a letto, prova a spegnere il telefono.
lines.en: Before going to bed, try switching off your phone.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Ci ho provato, ma finisco sempre per guardarlo lo stesso.
lines.en: I have tried, but I always end up looking at it anyway.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Invece di leggere le notizie, potresti leggere un romanzo.
lines.en: Instead of reading the news, you could read a novel.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: E la mattina? Esco sempre senza fare colazione.
lines.en: And in the morning? I always leave without having breakfast.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Dopo aver bevuto solo un caffè, è normale sentirsi stanchi.
lines.en: After drinking only a coffee, it is normal to feel tired.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Il suo continuo ripetermelo comincia a funzionare, sai?
lines.en: Your constantly repeating it to me is starting to work, you know?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Bene, perché cambiare abitudini richiede solo un po' di pazienza.
lines.en: Good, because changing habits only takes a little patience.

days.title: I connettivi del testo argomentativo
days.objective: Link your ideas with connectives that add, contrast, concede and conclude, so that an opinion sounds structured.
days.paretoFocus: In any discussion or written opinion, the connectives are what make a listener follow your reasoning instead of just hearing separate sentences.
drills: Take one opinion you hold and defend it in five sentences, each opening with a different connective.
Write two sentences that balance advantages against disadvantages of the same thing.
Practise conceding a point to someone before disagreeing with them.

items: Which connective adds a further argument on the same side?
Which one introduces a contrast in a fairly formal register?
How do you signal that you are drawing a conclusion?

activities.title: Collegare le idee
activities.intro: Connectives tell the listener what role each sentence plays in your argument. Some add, some contrast, some concede a point, and some announce the conclusion. Learning a small set well is far more useful than knowing many of them vaguely.

items.hr: Da un lato costa meno, dall'altro la qualità è peggiore.
items.en: On the one hand it costs less, on the other the quality is worse.
items.note: balances two sides of the same question

items.hr: Inoltre, il servizio è molto più veloce.
items.en: Moreover, the service is much faster.
items.note: adds a further point on the same side

items.hr: Tuttavia, non sono del tutto d'accordo con te.
items.en: However, I do not entirely agree with you.
items.note: contrast, a little more formal than però

items.hr: Quindi, direi che conviene aspettare ancora un po'.
items.en: So, I would say it is better to wait a little longer.
items.note: announces the conclusion of the argument

items.hr: In effetti, hai ragione su questo punto.
items.en: Actually, you are right on this point.
items.note: concedes a point, unlike infatti which confirms your own

activities.title: Aggiungere, contrastare, concludere
activities.intro: Choose the connective that matches the logical relation between the two sentences.

questions.prompt: Questo modello costa poco. ___, la qualità non è affatto male.
questions.explanation: The second sentence adds another advantage in the same direction, so an additive connective is needed.

questions.prompt: Ho studiato tantissimo. ___, non ho superato la prova orale.
questions.explanation: The result goes against what you would expect from the first sentence, so this calls for a contrastive connective.

questions.prompt: Connective introducing a conclusion: ___ possiamo dire che la proposta non convince.
questions.explanation: A conclusive connective signals that what follows is the result of everything said before.

questions.prompt: Presenting the first of two sides: Da un ___ è comodo, dall'altro è molto caro.
questions.explanation: The fixed expression uses this noun on both sides, and the second half normally leaves it out.

questions.prompt: Build the Italian for: 'However, I am not convinced by this solution.'
questions.explanation: The contrastive connective opens the sentence, and convinto is followed by di, not by da.

activities.title: Una discussione sul lavoro da casa
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. You and a colleague argue about working from home, and each turn opens with a connective that shows its role.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Secondo me lavorare da casa fa perdere il contatto con il gruppo.
lines.en: In my opinion working from home makes you lose contact with the team.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: In effetti hai ragione, tuttavia si guadagna moltissimo tempo.
lines.en: You are right in a way, however you gain a great deal of time.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Da un lato è vero, dall'altro le riunioni durano il doppio.
lines.en: On the one hand that is true, on the other the meetings last twice as long.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Inoltre non tutti hanno uno spazio adatto in casa, lo ammetto.
lines.en: Moreover not everyone has a suitable space at home, I admit it.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Quindi secondo te qual è la soluzione migliore?
lines.en: So in your opinion what is the best solution?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Direi due giorni in ufficio e tre da casa, cioè un sistema misto.
lines.en: I would say two days at the office and three at home, that is to say a mixed system.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Tuttavia bisognerebbe chiedere anche agli altri colleghi.
lines.en: However we should also ask the other colleagues.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Sono d'accordo, quindi propongo di parlarne alla prossima riunione.
lines.en: I agree, so I suggest talking about it at the next meeting.

days.title: La punteggiatura e i segnali del discorso
days.objective: Punctuate Italian sentences correctly and use the small spoken markers that open, correct and close a topic.
days.paretoFocus: These little words are the first thing that makes spoken Italian sound native, and correct punctuation is what makes your written Italian look native.
drills: Write five sentences with a subordinate clause and check that no comma appears before the linking word.
Record yourself asking three favours, each opening with an attention marker.
Rewrite three of your own sentences adding a correction marker in the middle.

items: Does Italian put a comma before a subordinate linking word?
Which marker do you use to correct or specify what you have just said?
Which marker closes a topic and moves the conversation on?

activities.title: Senti, insomma, cioè
activities.intro: Spoken Italian is full of short markers that organise the conversation, and each of them has a clear job. In writing, remember that Italian does not put a comma in front of a subordinate linking word, while a comma after an opening marker is normal. The colon introduces an explanation or a list.

items.hr: Senti, ti posso chiedere una cosa?
items.en: Listen, can I ask you something?
items.note: opens a request, always followed by a comma

items.hr: Insomma, non è andata benissimo.
items.en: All in all, it did not go very well.
items.note: sums up, often with a negative flavour

items.hr: Comunque, alla fine ha deciso lui.
items.en: Anyway, in the end he decided.
items.note: closes the topic or moves the conversation on

items.hr: Ho detto che verrò, cioè, se riesco a finire prima.
items.en: I said I will come, I mean, if I manage to finish earlier.
items.note: corrects or specifies what you have just said

items.hr: Allora, ricapitolando: siamo d'accordo sul prezzo.
items.en: Right, to sum up: we agree on the price.
items.note: no comma before che elsewhere, but a colon introduces the summary

activities.title: Segnali e virgole
activities.intro: Choose the right discourse marker and apply Italian comma rules.

questions.prompt: You want to correct or specify what you have just said. Which marker do you use?
questions.explanation: This marker introduces a reformulation, telling the listener that what follows makes your previous words more precise.

questions.prompt: Which sentence is punctuated correctly in Italian?
questions.explanation: Italian never separates the main clause from its subordinate with a comma before the linking word.

questions.prompt: Marker used to get attention before asking something: ___, sai che ora è?
questions.explanation: The informal imperative of sentire is the standard opener when you want someone to listen for a second.

questions.prompt: Marker that closes the topic and moves on: ___, ne parliamo domani con calma.
questions.explanation: This marker signals that the discussion is finished for now and something else follows.

questions.prompt: Build the Italian for: 'I mean, I am not sure that it is a good choice.'
questions.explanation: The marker takes a comma after it, while no comma appears before che, and essere sicuro triggers the congiuntivo.

activities.title: Mettiamoci d'accordo
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. You are organising a trip with a friend and the conversation is full of opening, correcting and closing markers.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Senti, hai deciso qualcosa per il fine settimana?
lines.en: Listen, have you decided anything about the weekend?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Allora, avevo pensato al mare, cioè, se il tempo regge.
lines.en: Right, I was thinking of the seaside, I mean, if the weather holds.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Insomma, non sei ancora sicuro.
lines.en: So basically, you are not sure yet.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Diciamo di sì, però guarda: le previsioni sono buone.
lines.en: Let us say I am, but look: the forecast is good.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Va bene. E il treno? Costa parecchio, no?
lines.en: All right. And the train? It is quite expensive, isn't it?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Comunque, prenotando adesso spendiamo la metà.
lines.en: Anyway, by booking now we spend half as much.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Perfetto, allora prenoto io e tu pensi all'albergo.
lines.en: Perfect, then I will book and you take care of the hotel.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Insomma, siamo d'accordo su tutto: ci sentiamo stasera.
lines.en: So we agree on everything: we will speak this evening.

days.title: La formazione delle parole, prefissi e suffissi
days.objective: Guess and build new words with the common Italian prefixes and suffixes instead of learning every word separately.
days.paretoFocus: One suffix learned well unlocks hundreds of words you have never seen, which is the cheapest vocabulary growth available.
drills: Take five nouns you know and make a small version and a negative version of each.
Turn five verbs into nouns and check which ending each one takes.
Find three words you already know that begin with the prefix meaning again.

items: Which suffix makes something small or minor?
Which suffix makes something unpleasant?
Which two endings turn a verb into an abstract noun?

activities.title: Costruire parole nuove
activities.intro: Italian builds most of its vocabulary from a small number of pieces. Suffixes such as etto and etta make things small, while accio makes them unpleasant. Prefixes such as s and mal reverse or worsen a meaning, and ri means again. Endings such as mento and zione turn verbs into abstract nouns.

items.hr: Ho trovato un lavoretto per l'estate.
items.en: I found a small job for the summer.
items.note: lavoro plus etto, something small or minor

items.hr: Che tempaccio oggi, piove da stamattina.
items.en: What awful weather today, it has been raining since this morning.
items.note: tempo plus accio, clearly negative

items.hr: Il suo comportamento è stato davvero scorretto.
items.en: His behaviour was really unfair.
items.note: corretto with the prefix s reversed into its opposite

items.hr: Il treno è ripartito con mezz'ora di ritardo.
items.en: The train left again half an hour late.
items.note: partire plus the prefix ri, meaning again

items.hr: Il cambiamento del clima preoccupa molti giovani.
items.en: Climate change worries many young people.
items.note: cambiare plus mento gives an abstract noun

activities.title: Prefissi e suffissi al lavoro
activities.intro: Work out meanings from the pieces, and build new words from a base word.

questions.prompt: 'Un ragazzaccio' is a boy who is:
questions.explanation: The ending accio is pejorative, so it describes character or quality negatively rather than size.

questions.prompt: Which is the correct noun formed from 'realizzare'?
questions.explanation: Verbs ending in izzare almost always form their abstract noun with the ending zione.

questions.prompt: Add the prefix meaning again: Ho dovuto ___fare la valigia.
questions.explanation: This two letter prefix attaches directly to the verb and adds the idea of doing something a second time.

questions.prompt: Add the diminutive suffix to the noun libro: Le ho regalato un ___ di poesie.
questions.explanation: The base noun drops its final vowel and takes etto, giving the meaning of a small book.

questions.prompt: Build the Italian for: 'We rented a little house in the mountains for the summer.'
questions.explanation: Casa takes the feminine diminutive etta, and the article stays feminine because the suffix does not change the gender here.

activities.title: Un weekend in montagna
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. You describe a short holiday to a friend, using diminutives, pejoratives and derived nouns naturally.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Allora, com'è andato il weekend in montagna?
lines.en: So, how did the weekend in the mountains go?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Bene, abbiamo preso una casetta piccolissima ma carina.
lines.en: Well, we took a tiny but nice little house.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: E il tempo? Qui ha piovuto tutto il giorno.
lines.en: And the weather? Here it rained all day.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Sabato c'era un ventaccio freddo, poi domenica è migliorato.
lines.en: On Saturday there was a nasty cold wind, then on Sunday it improved.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Avete fatto qualche passeggiata?
lines.en: Did you go for any walks?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Sì, una camminata lunga e poi un giretto in paese.
lines.en: Yes, a long walk and then a little stroll around the village.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Ci tornerete presto?
lines.en: Will you go back soon?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Penso di sì, il cambiamento d'aria mi ha fatto benissimo.
lines.en: I think so, the change of air did me a lot of good.

days.title: Il passato remoto, riconoscerlo nella lettura
days.objective: Recognise the most common third person forms of the passato remoto while reading, and say the same thing yourself in the passato prossimo.
days.paretoFocus: Novels, history books and a lot of southern speech use this tense constantly, so recognising it opens up written Italian without adding anything you have to produce.
drills: Read a page of any Italian novel and underline every verb that looks unfamiliar in the past.
Take five of those forms and say the passato prossimo equivalent out loud.
Retell a short story you have read, keeping every verb in the passato prossimo.

items: Which tense do you use yourself when you talk about the past?
Which everyday verbs do fu, ebbe and fece come from?
Where are you most likely to meet this tense?

activities.title: Leggere il passato remoto
activities.intro: This lesson is for reading only. You are not expected to produce this tense in this course, and in conversation you will always use the passato prossimo instead. What you do need is to recognise the common third person forms when they appear in books, in history and in speech from the south of Italy.

items.hr: L'anno dopo fu eletto sindaco della città.
items.en: The following year he was elected mayor of the city.
items.note: fu comes from essere, you would say è stato

items.hr: Ebbe molti amici e pochissimi nemici.
items.en: He had many friends and very few enemies.
items.note: ebbe comes from avere, you would say ha avuto

items.hr: Le disse la verità e poi se ne andò.
items.en: He told her the truth and then he left.
items.note: disse is ha detto, andò is è andato

items.hr: Fece le valigie e prese il primo treno.
items.en: He packed his bags and took the first train.
items.note: fece is ha fatto, prese is ha preso

items.hr: Venne a trovarci una sola volta.
items.en: He came to visit us only once.
items.note: venne is è venuto, which is what you say when speaking

activities.title: Dalla pagina alla conversazione
activities.intro: Recognise the reading forms and give the tense you would actually use when speaking.

questions.prompt: In a novel you read 'fece'. In conversation you would say:
questions.explanation: The reading form corresponds to a single completed action, and in speech that is expressed with the passato prossimo of fare.

questions.prompt: Which of these reading forms belongs to the verb essere?
questions.explanation: This is the third person singular reading form of essere, and in conversation it corresponds to è stato.

questions.prompt: Rewrite for speaking, using the passato prossimo: Venne a Roma nel 2019 becomes ___ venuto a Roma nel 2019.
questions.explanation: Venire takes essere as its auxiliary, so the spoken version uses the third person singular of essere plus the participle.

questions.prompt: Rewrite for speaking: Prese il treno delle sette becomes Ha ___ il treno delle sette.
questions.explanation: Prendere takes avere and has an irregular participle, which is the form you use in every spoken sentence.

questions.prompt: Build the Italian, in the tense you use for speaking, for: 'He told me the truth and then he left.'
questions.explanation: Both verbs go into the passato prossimo, and andarsene keeps its pronouns in front of the auxiliary.

activities.title: Il romanzo che sto leggendo
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. You tell a friend about a novel: the book uses the reading tense, but both of you speak in the passato prossimo.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Come va con il romanzo? È difficile la lingua?
lines.en: How is the novel going? Is the language difficult?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: All'inizio sì, perché i verbi sono tutti al passato remoto.
lines.en: At the beginning yes, because the verbs are all in the passato remoto.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Per esempio? Dammi una frase.
lines.en: For example? Give me a sentence.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: C'era scritto 'partì all'alba', cioè è partito all'alba.
lines.en: It said he set off at dawn, that is to say he left at dawn.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Ah, capito. E che cosa è successo al protagonista?
lines.en: Ah, I see. And what happened to the main character?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Ha perso il lavoro e si è trasferito in un paesino sul mare.
lines.en: He lost his job and moved to a small village by the sea.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Quindi quelle forme si trovano solo nei libri?
lines.en: So those forms are found only in books?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Nei libri e al sud, ma noi parlando usiamo sempre il passato prossimo.
lines.en: In books and in the south, but when we speak we always use the passato prossimo.

days.title: Il mercato immobiliare
days.objective: Talk about the housing market and state an opinion about prices, backing it up with a reason.
days.paretoFocus: Housing is the first big conversation newcomers have, and it forces opinion structures the speaking exam rewards.
drills: Say three sentences about housing prices in your area, each with 'penso che' plus a reason.
Describe two flats you have seen, using the past and agreeing the participle.
Write one sentence with 'benché' about a neighbourhood you know.

items: How do you form the present subjunctive after 'penso che'?
Which article goes before 'spazio' and why?
How do you build the unreal conditional about the past?

activities.title: Il mercato della casa
activities.intro: At this level an opinion is only worth saying if a reason follows it. These sentences pair a position with a justification, and most of them need the subjunctive after the opinion verb. Notice how the article changes shape before certain consonant clusters.

items.hr: Penso che il mercato immobiliare sia troppo caro, perché gli stipendi non crescono come i prezzi.
items.en: I think the property market is too expensive, because salaries are not growing like prices.
items.note: penso che + congiuntivo: essere -> sia

items.hr: Secondo me conviene visitare più di un appartamento, così puoi confrontare le spese condominiali.
items.en: In my opinion it is worth viewing more than one flat, so you can compare the service charges.
items.note: le spese condominiali: the monthly building costs

items.hr: Benché il quartiere sia lontano dal centro, i prezzi al metro quadro restano alti.
items.en: Although the area is far from the centre, prices per square metre stay high.
items.note: benché always takes the congiuntivo

items.hr: Chi cerca un monolocale in centro deve accettare uno spazio molto piccolo.
items.en: Anyone looking for a studio flat in the centre has to accept a very small space.
items.note: uno spazio: uno, not un, before s + consonant

items.hr: Se avessi saputo quanto costava il mutuo, avrei aspettato un anno.
items.en: If I had known how much the mortgage cost, I would have waited a year.
items.note: irreal past: se + congiuntivo trapassato, then condizionale passato

activities.title: Prezzi e opinioni
activities.intro: Practise opinion structures about housing, plus one point from earlier in the course.

questions.prompt: Penso che i prezzi delle case ___ troppo in fretta.
questions.explanation: 'Penso che' expresses an opinion, so the verb goes into the present subjunctive: salgano.

questions.prompt: Quelle case? Le abbiamo ___ la settimana scorsa.
questions.explanation: With avere, the participle agrees with a direct object pronoun placed before the verb. 'Le' is feminine plural, so viste.

questions.prompt: ___ il prezzo sia alto, l'appartamento mi piace molto.
questions.explanation: A concessive conjunction is needed before a subjunctive: benché or sebbene, both followed by 'sia'.

questions.prompt: Se avessimo cercato prima, ___ (trovare, noi) una casa migliore.
questions.explanation: The unreal past conditional pairs the congiuntivo trapassato in the 'se' clause with the condizionale passato in the main clause.

questions.prompt: Build the Italian for: 'I think that renting is more convenient at the start.'
questions.explanation: The infinitive 'affittare' works as the subject, and 'penso che' forces 'sia'.

activities.title: Aspettare o comprare adesso
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. Your friend wants to wait for prices to fall; you disagree and defend buying now with reasons.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Secondo me conviene aspettare: penso che i prezzi scendano il prossimo anno.
lines.en: In my opinion it is better to wait: I think prices will fall next year.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Non sono d'accordo. Aspettano tutti da anni, e i prezzi non sono scesi.
lines.en: I do not agree. Everyone has been waiting for years, and prices have not fallen.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Però adesso comprare significa pagare interessi altissimi.
lines.en: But buying now means paying very high interest.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Vero, ma se aspetto ancora, l'affitto che pago intanto non torna più indietro.
lines.en: True, but if I wait longer, the rent I pay in the meantime never comes back.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Benché tu abbia ragione sull'affitto, un mutuo lungo ti lega per vent'anni.
lines.en: Although you are right about the rent, a long mortgage ties you down for twenty years.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Lo so, per questo cerco qualcosa di piccolo, così le rate restano basse.
lines.en: I know, that is why I am looking for something small, so the instalments stay low.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Allora almeno vedi tre o quattro appartamenti prima di decidere.
lines.en: Then at least see three or four flats before deciding.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Certo, ne ho già visti due e le spese condominiali cambiano tantissimo.
lines.en: Of course, I have already seen two and the service charges vary enormously.

days.title: Comprare o affittare
days.objective: Weigh two options against each other and defend a preference using balancing connectives.
days.paretoFocus: Weighing two sides is the exact task the writing module sets, and the connectives transfer to any topic.
drills: Say 'da una parte ... dall'altra ...' about renting and buying.
Build three sentences with 'se' plus imperfect subjunctive about your own situation.
Turn one sentence into a past gerund opening, as in 'avendo firmato ...'.

items: Which two halves make the 'da una parte' structure?
How do you say what would happen if something were true now?
Which relative pronoun follows a preposition?

activities.title: Due strade possibili
activities.intro: To weigh options in Italian you need a small kit of balancing words: da una parte, dall'altra, invece, mentre, però. The second half of this lesson uses the past gerund, which packs a whole clause into two words.

items.hr: Da una parte comprare è un investimento, dall'altra ti lega a una città per molti anni.
items.en: On the one hand buying is an investment, on the other it ties you to one city for many years.
items.note: da una parte ... dall'altra: the standard balancing pair

items.hr: Preferirei affittare finché non sarò sicuro del lavoro.
items.en: I would rather rent until I am sure about my job.
items.note: finché non + futuro: 'until'

items.hr: Molti sostengono che l'affitto sia soldi buttati, ma io non sono d'accordo.
items.en: Many claim that rent is money thrown away, but I do not agree.
items.note: sostenere che + congiuntivo when reporting a contested claim

items.hr: Se comprassi adesso, dovrei rinunciare a tutti i miei risparmi.
items.en: If I bought now, I would have to give up all my savings.
items.note: se + congiuntivo imperfetto, then condizionale presente

items.hr: Avendo firmato il contratto, non puoi tornare indietro facilmente.
items.en: Having signed the contract, you cannot easily go back.
items.note: gerundio passato: avendo + participio

activities.title: Affitto contro acquisto
activities.intro: Practise balancing connectives and hypotheses, with one relative pronoun from earlier.

questions.prompt: ___ una parte l'affitto è flessibile, dall'altra non ti lascia niente.
questions.explanation: The fixed pair is 'da una parte ... dall'altra'. No other preposition works here.

questions.prompt: È probabile che loro ___ un mutuo lungo.
questions.explanation: Impersonal expressions of probability take the subjunctive: prendano.

questions.prompt: Se ___ (io, potere) scegliere, comprerei una casa piccola in periferia.
questions.explanation: A hypothesis about the present uses the congiuntivo imperfetto in the 'se' clause: potessi.

questions.prompt: L'agenzia con ___ abbiamo parlato non ci ha richiamato.
questions.explanation: After a preposition the relative pronoun is 'cui', never 'che'.

questions.prompt: Build the Italian for: 'Having signed the contract, you cannot change your mind.'
questions.explanation: The past gerund 'avendo firmato' expresses an action completed before the main verb.

activities.title: Meglio affittare
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. Your friend is sure buying always wins; you defend renting while admitting the weak points.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Io comprerei subito: l'affitto è denaro buttato.
lines.en: I would buy straight away: rent is money thrown away.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Da una parte ti capisco, dall'altra comprare mi lega a una città dove il lavoro non è sicuro.
lines.en: On the one hand I understand you, on the other buying ties me to a city where work is not secure.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Ma se restassi dieci anni, avresti già pagato mezza casa.
lines.en: But if you stayed ten years, you would already have paid for half a house.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: E se invece mi trasferissi dopo due anni? Rivendere costa tempo e soldi.
lines.en: And what if I moved after two years instead? Selling again costs time and money.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Nonostante questo, l'affitto sale ogni anno e tu non ottieni niente.
lines.en: In spite of that, rent goes up every year and you get nothing.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Preferisco pagare di più adesso ed essere libero di cambiare città.
lines.en: I prefer to pay more now and be free to change city.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Va bene, ma avendo firmato un affitto lungo non sei così libero.
lines.en: All right, but having signed a long lease you are not that free.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Giusto, infatti sto cercando un contratto più corto.
lines.en: Fair point, in fact I am looking for a shorter contract.

days.title: Vivere in città o in campagna
days.objective: Compare city and country life and defend a choice using concessive clauses and impersonal 'si'.
days.paretoFocus: Comparison plus concession is the backbone of any argued answer, and this topic comes up constantly.
drills: Give three advantages of the city, each followed by a drawback.
Say what one does in the countryside using the impersonal 'si'.
Tell the story of a move you made, using the past.

items: Which mood follows 'nonostante'?
How do you form a passive with 'venire'?
What does the impersonal 'si' add to a verb?

activities.title: Città o campagna
activities.intro: Concessive conjunctions let you admit the other side before making your point, which is exactly what an examiner listens for. Note that 'nonostante' and 'sebbene' take the subjunctive, while 'anche se' does not.

items.hr: Nonostante il traffico sia stressante, in città trovo lavoro molto più facilmente.
items.en: Even though the traffic is stressful, in the city I find work much more easily.
items.note: nonostante + congiuntivo

items.hr: In campagna si vive meglio, però bisogna avere per forza la macchina.
items.en: In the countryside one lives better, but you absolutely have to have a car.
items.note: si vive: impersonal si

items.hr: Non credo che la campagna sia noiosa: dipende da quello che cerchi.
items.en: I do not think the countryside is boring: it depends on what you are looking for.
items.note: non credo che + congiuntivo

items.hr: Le nuove case vengono costruite soprattutto fuori città.
items.en: The new houses are built mainly outside the city.
items.note: venire + participio: a plain descriptive passive

items.hr: Mi sono trasferito in periferia perché gli affitti erano diventati insostenibili.
items.en: I moved to the outskirts because rents had become unbearable.
items.note: trapassato prossimo for what happened before

activities.title: Due modi di vivere
activities.intro: Practise concession and impersonal forms, with one passive from the grammar block.

questions.prompt: ___ il traffico sia stressante, preferisco vivere in città.
questions.explanation: Only a concessive conjunction fits with 'sia'. The other three are causal and take the indicative.

questions.prompt: Le case nuove ___ costruite fuori città.
questions.explanation: The passive with venire agrees with the plural subject 'le case': vengono costruite.

questions.prompt: In campagna ___ vive bene, ma serve la macchina.
questions.explanation: The impersonal particle turns the verb into a general statement about people in general.

questions.prompt: Non credo che la vita di campagna ___ (essere) noiosa.
questions.explanation: A negated opinion verb triggers the subjunctive.

questions.prompt: Build the Italian for: 'I moved to the outskirts because rents had become unbearable.'
questions.explanation: Reflexive verbs take essere in the past, and the earlier action goes into the trapassato.

activities.title: Chi sta meglio
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. Your friend has moved to the country and defends it; you argue for the city without dismissing them.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Io in città non ci tornerei mai: rumore, traffico, aria pesante.
lines.en: I would never go back to the city: noise, traffic, heavy air.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Ti capisco, però in campagna senza macchina non fai niente, e questo mi pesa.
lines.en: I understand you, but in the countryside without a car you can do nothing, and that weighs on me.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Basta organizzarsi. Nonostante ci voglia più tempo, si vive molto meglio.
lines.en: You just have to organise yourself. Even though it takes more time, life is much better.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Non credo che sia sempre così: se ti ammali, l'ospedale è lontano.
lines.en: I do not think it is always like that: if you fall ill, the hospital is far away.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Anche in città aspetti, la differenza è solo nella coda.
lines.en: In the city you wait too, the difference is only in the queue.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Forse, ma in città trovo lavoro in un mese, in campagna ci metterei un anno.
lines.en: Maybe, but in the city I find work in a month, in the countryside it would take me a year.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Allora vieni a vivere fuori quando cambierai lavoro meno spesso.
lines.en: Then come and live outside when you change jobs less often.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Su questo hai ragione, mi sono trasferito in periferia proprio per questo.
lines.en: You are right about that, I moved to the outskirts for exactly that reason.

days.title: I trasporti pubblici e la mobilità
days.objective: Argue for or against public transport using impersonal proposals such as 'bisognerebbe' and denials with the subjunctive.
days.paretoFocus: Proposing improvements politely is a core B1 function, and 'bisognerebbe' is the fastest way to do it.
drills: Propose three improvements to transport where you live, each with 'bisognerebbe'.
Deny a common complaint using 'non è vero che' plus the subjunctive.
Report something a friend told you about a delay, in indirect speech.

items: Which mood follows 'non è vero che'?
Which article goes before 'sciopero'?
How does a reported future look in indirect speech?

activities.title: Muoversi in città
activities.intro: Two structures carry this lesson: 'bisognerebbe' plus infinitive, which proposes without ordering, and denial with 'non è vero che' plus subjunctive. Both let you take a position without sounding aggressive.

items.hr: Bisognerebbe potenziare i mezzi pubblici, altrimenti nessuno lascerà l'auto a casa.
items.en: Public transport should be strengthened, otherwise nobody will leave the car at home.
items.note: bisognerebbe + infinito: a soft proposal

items.hr: In molte città si sono aggiunte nuove piste ciclabili, e secondo me è la scelta giusta.
items.en: In many cities new cycle lanes have been added, and in my opinion it is the right choice.
items.note: si passivante in the past takes essere and agrees

items.hr: L'abbonamento mensile conviene solo se prendi l'autobus tutti i giorni.
items.en: The monthly pass is worth it only if you take the bus every day.
items.note: l'abbonamento: l' before a vowel

items.hr: Non è vero che il tram sia sempre in ritardo: dipende dalla linea.
items.en: It is not true that the tram is always late: it depends on the line.
items.note: non è vero che + congiuntivo

items.hr: Lo sciopero dei trasporti è stato annunciato con due giorni di anticipo.
items.en: The transport strike was announced two days in advance.
items.note: lo sciopero: lo before s + consonant

activities.title: Mezzi e proposte
activities.intro: Practise denials, articles and proposals, with one piece of reported speech from earlier.

questions.prompt: Non è vero che i mezzi ___ sempre affollati.
questions.explanation: Denying that something is the case takes the subjunctive: non è vero che ... siano.

questions.prompt: ___ sciopero è cominciato all'alba.
questions.explanation: Before s + consonant the masculine singular article is 'lo'.

questions.prompt: Se i biglietti costassero meno, molti ___ (lasciare) l'auto a casa.
questions.explanation: The main clause of a present hypothesis takes the condizionale presente.

questions.prompt: Ha detto che ___ (lui, arrivare) in ritardo per lo sciopero.
questions.explanation: In reported speech a future seen from the past becomes the condizionale passato.

questions.prompt: Build the Italian for: 'Public transport should be improved.'
questions.explanation: 'Bisognerebbe' is impersonal and is always followed directly by the infinitive.

activities.title: Autobus o macchina
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. Your friend has written off public transport; you push back with concrete reasons and admit one limit.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Secondo me i mezzi pubblici sono un disastro, meglio la macchina.
lines.en: In my opinion public transport is a disaster, the car is better.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Non è vero che siano sempre in ritardo: dipende molto dalla linea.
lines.en: It is not true that they are always late: it depends a lot on the line.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Sulla mia passa un autobus ogni quaranta minuti.
lines.en: On mine a bus comes every forty minutes.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Allora il problema è il servizio, non il mezzo. Bisognerebbe aumentare le corse.
lines.en: Then the problem is the service, not the mode of transport. The number of runs should be increased.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Con quali soldi? Le città non ne hanno.
lines.en: With what money? Cities do not have any.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Con quelli che si spendono per le strade nuove. Se costassero meno, in tanti lascerebbero l'auto a casa.
lines.en: With the money spent on new roads. If they cost less, a lot of people would leave the car at home.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Può darsi, ma io con due bambini l'autobus non lo prendo.
lines.en: Maybe, but with two children I am not taking the bus.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Questo lo capisco, però per andare al lavoro da solo l'abbonamento conviene.
lines.en: That I understand, but for going to work alone the pass is worth it.

days.title: L'auto, la patente e il traffico
days.objective: Handle driving in Italy in conversation, including the licence points system and limited traffic zones in historic centres.
days.paretoFocus: Limited traffic zones catch newcomers out constantly, and explaining them forces useful conditional structures.
drills: Explain in three sentences what a limited traffic zone is and who may enter.
Say what you would have done differently on a trip, using the past conditional.
Describe two habits that make driving safer, opening with a gerund.

items: Does 'anche se' take the indicative or the subjunctive?
When do you use the futuro anteriore?
How do you open a sentence with 'by doing something'?

activities.title: Guidare in Italia
activities.intro: Italian historic centres often have a zona a traffico limitato, where only residents and permit holders may drive. Licences work on a points system, so serious offences cost points. Note the contrast between 'anche se' with the indicative and 'benché' with the subjunctive.

items.hr: Per guidare in Italia serve la patente, e chi commette infrazioni gravi rischia di perdere punti.
items.en: To drive in Italy you need a licence, and anyone committing serious offences risks losing points.
items.note: la patente: driving licence

items.hr: Nei centri storici c'è spesso una zona a traffico limitato, dove possono entrare solo i residenti e chi ha un permesso.
items.en: In historic centres there is often a limited traffic zone, where only residents and permit holders may enter.
items.note: limitato agrees with traffico, not with zona

items.hr: Se non conosci la città, conviene lasciare la macchina fuori e prendere un mezzo pubblico.
items.en: If you do not know the city, it is best to leave the car outside and take public transport.
items.note: conviene + infinito: it is worth doing

items.hr: Anche se l'auto è comoda, penso che in centro sia più veloce andare a piedi.
items.en: Even though the car is comfortable, I think that in the centre walking is faster.
items.note: anche se + indicativo, but penso che + congiuntivo

items.hr: Guidando piano nei centri storici, si evitano molti problemi.
items.en: By driving slowly in historic centres, you avoid a lot of problems.
items.note: gerundio + si passivante with a plural subject

activities.title: Patente e centro storico
activities.intro: Practise driving vocabulary and concession, with one future perfect from earlier lessons.

questions.prompt: Anche se l'auto ___ comoda, in centro è più lenta.
questions.explanation: 'Anche se' takes the indicative, unlike 'benché' and 'sebbene', which take the subjunctive.

questions.prompt: Quando ___ preso la patente, potrò guidare da solo.
questions.explanation: The futuro anteriore marks the action completed before another future action.

questions.prompt: Chi guida senza rispettare i limiti rischia di perdere i ___ della patente.
questions.explanation: The Italian licence works on a points system, so you lose 'punti'.

questions.prompt: Se avessi saputo della zona a traffico limitato, non ___ (io, entrare) in centro.
questions.explanation: Entrare takes essere, so the past conditional is 'sarei entrato'.

questions.prompt: Build the Italian for: 'By driving slowly you avoid a lot of problems.'
questions.explanation: The gerund opens the sentence, and 'si evitano' agrees with the plural 'problemi'.

activities.title: In centro con la macchina
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. Your friend wants to drive into the historic centre; you explain why that is a bad idea.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Domani vengo in centro in macchina, così arriviamo prima.
lines.en: Tomorrow I will come into the centre by car, so we get there sooner.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Non farlo: in centro c'è la zona a traffico limitato e possono entrare solo i residenti.
lines.en: Do not do it: in the centre there is the limited traffic zone and only residents may enter.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: E come faccio? Il parcheggio fuori è lontanissimo.
lines.en: And what do I do? The car park outside is very far away.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Guidando fino al parcheggio di scambio e poi prendendo il tram, ci metti uguale.
lines.en: By driving to the park and ride and then taking the tram, it takes you the same time.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Sarà, ma anche se il tram è comodo, con le borse diventa scomodo.
lines.en: Maybe, but even if the tram is comfortable, with bags it becomes awkward.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Meglio le borse che il rischio di una multa e di perdere punti.
lines.en: Better the bags than the risk of a fine and of losing points.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Va bene, proviamo col tram. Se avessi saputo prima di questa zona, avrei organizzato meglio.
lines.en: All right, let us try the tram. If I had known about this zone earlier, I would have organised things better.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Tranquillo, la prima volta ci cascano quasi tutti.
lines.en: Do not worry, almost everyone falls for it the first time.

days.title: L'ambiente e il cambiamento climatico
days.objective: Discuss climate change and answer the objection that one person cannot make a difference.
days.paretoFocus: This debate recurs in exams and in real life, and it trains hypothesis plus subjunctive in one go.
drills: Say what would happen if everyone changed one habit, using the second conditional.
Answer the claim 'nothing can be done' with a counter-argument.
Describe two changes you have noticed in the seasons.

items: Which mood follows 'bisogna che'?
How do you make a passive with 'venire' inside a subjunctive clause?
Which relative form follows 'del'?

activities.title: Il clima che cambia
activities.intro: Talking about climate means saying what should be done and what would happen under other conditions. Note 'bisogna che' plus subjunctive, which is stronger than 'bisognerebbe', and the passive with venire inside it.

items.hr: Credo che il cambiamento climatico sia il problema più urgente della nostra generazione, perché tocca tutti.
items.en: I believe climate change is the most urgent problem of our generation, because it affects everyone.
items.note: credo che + congiuntivo

items.hr: C'è chi sostiene che non si possa fare niente da soli, ma io penso il contrario.
items.en: There are those who claim nothing can be done alone, but I think the opposite.
items.note: c'è chi + verbo singolare

items.hr: Se ognuno cambiasse una sola abitudine, l'effetto sarebbe enorme.
items.en: If everyone changed just one habit, the effect would be enormous.
items.note: ognuno takes a singular verb

items.hr: Le estati sono diventate più calde e le stagioni meno regolari.
items.en: Summers have become hotter and the seasons less regular.
items.note: diventare takes essere, so the participle agrees: diventate

items.hr: Non basta parlarne: bisogna che le città vengano ripensate.
items.en: Talking about it is not enough: cities need to be rethought.
items.note: bisogna che + congiuntivo, here with a passive

activities.title: Clima e responsabilità
activities.intro: Practise obligation and hypothesis, with one relative pronoun from the grammar block.

questions.prompt: Bisogna che le città ___ ripensate.
questions.explanation: 'Bisogna che' requires the subjunctive, and here it carries a passive: siano ripensate.

questions.prompt: C'è chi sostiene che non si ___ fare niente.
questions.explanation: 'Sostenere che' reporting a contested claim takes the subjunctive: possa.

questions.prompt: Se ognuno ___ (cambiare) una sola abitudine, l'effetto sarebbe grande.
questions.explanation: A present hypothesis uses the congiuntivo imperfetto after 'se'.

questions.prompt: L'aumento delle temperature, del ___ si parla da anni, è ormai visibile.
questions.explanation: 'Il quale' is the formal relative pronoun and combines with the preposition: del quale.

questions.prompt: Build the Italian for: 'I think that climate change concerns everyone.'
questions.explanation: After 'penso che' the verb takes the subjunctive ending: riguardi.

activities.title: Serve davvero a qualcosa
