activities.intro: Play 'Me'. Your friend thinks individual effort is pointless; you disagree without blaming them.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Sinceramente non credo che una persona sola possa cambiare qualcosa.
lines.en: Honestly I do not think one person alone can change anything.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Capisco il dubbio, ma se ognuno pensasse così, non cambierebbe mai niente.
lines.en: I understand the doubt, but if everyone thought like that, nothing would ever change.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Le grandi industrie inquinano molto più di me.
lines.en: Big industries pollute much more than I do.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: È vero, però le industrie producono quello che compriamo noi.
lines.en: That is true, but industries produce what we buy.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Quindi la colpa sarebbe mia?
lines.en: So it would be my fault?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: No, dico che serve tutto insieme: bisogna che le città vengano ripensate e che noi cambiamo abitudini.
lines.en: No, I am saying we need everything together: cities need to be rethought and we need to change habits.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Su questo sono d'accordo, purché le regole valgano per tutti.
lines.en: On that I agree, provided the rules apply to everyone.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Esatto, altrimenti chi si impegna si sente preso in giro.
lines.en: Exactly, otherwise those who make an effort feel mocked.

days.title: Vivere in modo sostenibile
days.objective: Defend everyday sustainable choices and answer the objection that they cost too much.
days.paretoFocus: Cost is the objection you will always meet, so having a prepared counter-argument makes you sound fluent fast.
drills: Name three habits you have changed and say what each one saved you.
Answer 'it is too expensive' with a reason and an example.
Turn one sentence into a gerund opening, as in 'comprando meno ...'.

items: Which subjunctive tense follows a conditional main verb?
What does the pronoun 'ne' replace?
How do you say 'by buying' at the start of a sentence?

activities.title: Scegliere meglio
activities.intro: When the main verb is a conditional, the subjunctive that follows moves back a tense: sarebbe utile che ... riducesse. This concordanza is one of the clearest markers of a solid B1 speaker.

items.hr: Vivere in modo sostenibile non significa rinunciare a tutto, significa scegliere meglio.
items.en: Living sustainably does not mean giving up everything, it means choosing better.
items.note: rinunciare a qualcosa

items.hr: Preferisco comprare meno cose ma di buona qualità, così durano più a lungo.
items.en: I prefer to buy fewer things but of good quality, so they last longer.
items.note: la qualità: accented final a, invariable in the plural

items.hr: Molti pensano che i prodotti sostenibili costino sempre di più, ma non è sempre vero.
items.en: Many think sustainable products always cost more, but that is not always true.
items.note: pensare che + congiuntivo: costino

items.hr: Da quando uso la borraccia, ho smesso di comprare bottiglie di plastica.
items.en: Since I started using a reusable bottle, I have stopped buying plastic bottles.
items.note: smettere di + infinito

items.hr: Sarebbe utile che ogni famiglia riducesse i consumi inutili.
items.en: It would be useful for every family to cut pointless consumption.
items.note: condizionale + congiuntivo imperfetto: the standard concordanza

activities.title: Abitudini e costi
activities.intro: Practise tense agreement and everyday choices, with one pronoun from earlier lessons.

questions.prompt: Sarebbe utile che ogni famiglia ___ i consumi.
questions.explanation: A conditional main verb pulls the subjunctive back to the imperfect: riducesse.

questions.prompt: Compri troppe bottiglie di plastica? ___ compro solo due alla settimana.
questions.explanation: 'Ne' replaces a partitive quantity, 'some of them', and is required before a number.

questions.prompt: Molti credono che i prodotti sostenibili ___ (costare) sempre di più.
questions.explanation: 'Credere che' triggers the present subjunctive: costino.

questions.prompt: ___ (comprare) meno cose, si risparmia e si inquina meno.
questions.explanation: The gerund expresses the means: by buying less.

questions.prompt: Build the Italian for: 'Living sustainably does not mean giving up everything.'
questions.explanation: The infinitive acts as the subject, and 'rinunciare' always keeps its preposition 'a'.

activities.title: Costa troppo
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. Your friend says sustainable living is a luxury; you answer with concrete numbers and concede one point.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Vivere in modo sostenibile costa troppo, non è per tutti.
lines.en: Living sustainably costs too much, it is not for everyone.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Dipende. Comprando meno cose ma migliori, alla fine spendo di meno.
lines.en: It depends. By buying fewer but better things, in the end I spend less.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Sì, però un prodotto di qualità costa il doppio subito.
lines.en: Yes, but a quality product costs twice as much straight away.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: E dura il triplo. Io ho smesso di comprare bottiglie e in un anno ho risparmiato parecchio.
lines.en: And it lasts three times as long. I stopped buying bottles and saved quite a lot in a year.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Questo è vero, l'acqua in bottiglia è una spesa inutile.
lines.en: That is true, bottled water is a pointless expense.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Sarebbe utile che ogni famiglia togliesse due o tre sprechi così.
lines.en: It would be useful if every family cut two or three wasteful habits like that.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: D'accordo, ma non chiedermi di rinunciare alla macchina.
lines.en: All right, but do not ask me to give up the car.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Nessuno te lo chiede, si tratta di scegliere meglio, non di rinunciare a tutto.
lines.en: Nobody is asking you to, it is about choosing better, not giving everything up.

days.title: I rifiuti e il riciclo, più a fondo
days.objective: Discuss waste sorting in detail and use the obligation passive with 'andare'.
days.paretoFocus: The 'va fatto' passive is everywhere in instructions and notices, and it replaces a whole clause of obligation.
drills: Say where three materials go, using 'va' or 'vanno' plus a participle.
Argue that recycling alone is not enough, and give a reason.
Report a question someone asked you about your habits at home.

items: What does 'va messo' mean and how is it different from 'viene messo'?
Which mood follows 'non credo che'?
How do you report an indirect question in the past?

activities.title: La raccolta differenziata
activities.intro: Italian has a second passive built with andare, and it carries obligation: 'il vetro va separato' means the glass must be separated. Compare it with the venire passive, which simply describes.

items.hr: La raccolta differenziata funziona solo se tutti la fanno bene.
items.en: Waste sorting only works if everyone does it properly.
items.note: la raccolta differenziata: separate waste collection

items.hr: Il vetro, la carta e la plastica vanno separati prima di essere buttati.
items.en: Glass, paper and plastic must be separated before being thrown away.
items.note: andare + participio expresses obligation, and the participle agrees

items.hr: Molti rifiuti potrebbero essere riutilizzati invece di finire in discarica.
items.en: Many kinds of waste could be reused instead of ending up in landfill.
items.note: invece di + infinito

items.hr: Non credo che riciclare basti: bisogna produrre meno rifiuti.
items.en: I do not think recycling is enough: we need to produce less waste.
items.note: bastare in the subjunctive: basti

items.hr: L'umido va messo in un sacchetto apposito, altrimenti non viene raccolto.
items.en: Food waste must be put in a special bag, otherwise it is not collected.
items.note: l'umido: the wet organic fraction

activities.title: Dove va ogni cosa
activities.intro: Practise the obligation passive and negated opinions, with one indirect question from earlier.

questions.prompt: Le bottiglie ___ messe nel contenitore giusto.
questions.explanation: In the andare passive the verb agrees with the plural subject: le bottiglie vanno messe.

questions.prompt: Non credo che riciclare ___ per risolvere il problema.
questions.explanation: A negated opinion verb takes the subjunctive: basti.

questions.prompt: Molti rifiuti ___ (potere) essere riutilizzati invece di finire in discarica.
questions.explanation: The conditional softens the statement into a possibility.

questions.prompt: Ci hanno chiesto se ___ (noi, fare) la raccolta differenziata a casa.
questions.explanation: An indirect question after a past main verb takes the congiuntivo imperfetto.

questions.prompt: Build the Italian for: 'We need to produce less waste and recycle better.'
questions.explanation: 'Bisogna' takes the infinitive, and both infinitives hang off it.

activities.title: Ma poi buttano tutto insieme
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. Your neighbour suspects sorting is pointless; you argue back and then concede a real limit.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Io la raccolta differenziata la faccio, ma sono sicuro che poi buttano tutto insieme.
lines.en: I do sort my waste, but I am sure they then throw it all together.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Non credo che sia così ovunque, e comunque se separiamo male diamo noi la scusa perfetta.
lines.en: I do not think it is like that everywhere, and anyway if we sort badly we hand them the perfect excuse.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Certe volte non capisco dove va un oggetto di plastica e carta insieme.
lines.en: Sometimes I do not understand where an object made of plastic and paper goes.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: In questi casi conviene dividerlo, altrimenti non viene riciclato niente.
lines.en: In those cases it is best to split it, otherwise nothing gets recycled.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: E l'umido? Il sacchetto si rompe sempre.
lines.en: And food waste? The bag always breaks.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: L'umido va messo in un sacchetto apposito e portato giù più spesso.
lines.en: Food waste must be put in a special bag and taken down more often.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Comunque riciclare non basta, ne parlano tutti ma i rifiuti aumentano.
lines.en: In any case recycling is not enough, everyone talks about it but waste keeps growing.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Su questo hai ragione: bisogna produrre meno rifiuti, non solo separarli meglio.
lines.en: You are right about that: we need to produce less waste, not just sort it better.

days.title: L'energia e i consumi
days.objective: Talk about energy bills and savings, and argue for an investment that pays off later.
days.paretoFocus: Bills and heating are constant household topics, and they naturally require hypotheses and the future perfect.
drills: Say three things that waste energy at home and what you would change.
Explain a saving that only appears after some time, using the futuro anteriore.
Report a promise someone made you about work on the house.

items: Which article goes before 'spreco'?
How do you say what someone promised would happen?
How do you build 'as soon as we have finished'?

activities.title: La bolletta e gli sprechi
activities.intro: This lesson pairs hypotheses about savings with the futuro anteriore, which marks an action finished before another future one. Watch the article before words starting with s plus consonant.

items.hr: Da quando ho cambiato le lampadine, la bolletta è scesa un po'.
items.en: Since I changed the light bulbs, the bill has gone down a little.
items.note: un po' with an apostrophe, short for un poco

items.hr: Se isolassimo meglio le case, consumeremmo molto meno per il riscaldamento.
items.en: If we insulated houses better, we would use much less for heating.
items.note: se + congiuntivo imperfetto, then condizionale

items.hr: Penso che le rinnovabili siano la strada giusta, anche se servono anni per costruirle.
items.en: I think renewables are the right path, even if it takes years to build them.
items.note: anche se + indicativo inside a subjunctive sentence

items.hr: Lo spreco di energia più grande è quello che non vediamo: gli apparecchi sempre accesi.
items.en: The biggest energy waste is the one we do not see: appliances left on all the time.
items.note: lo spreco: lo before s + consonant

items.hr: Appena avremo installato i pannelli, cominceremo a risparmiare.
items.en: As soon as we have installed the panels, we will start saving.
items.note: futuro anteriore after appena

activities.title: Risparmiare in casa
activities.intro: Practise hypotheses and the future perfect, with one reported promise from earlier.

questions.prompt: Se ___ meglio le case, consumeremmo molto meno.
questions.explanation: The 'se' clause of a present hypothesis takes the congiuntivo imperfetto.

questions.prompt: Mi aveva promesso che ___ i pannelli entro l'estate.
questions.explanation: A future seen from a past point of view becomes the condizionale passato.

questions.prompt: ___ spreco di energia è difficile da notare in casa.
questions.explanation: Before s + consonant the masculine article is 'lo'.

questions.prompt: Appena ___ (noi, finire) i lavori, la bolletta scenderà.
questions.explanation: The futuro anteriore marks completion before the other future event.

questions.prompt: Build the Italian for: 'I think renewables are the right path.'
questions.explanation: 'Penso che' forces the subjunctive 'siano', agreeing with the plural subject.

activities.title: Perché la bolletta è così alta
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. Your friend blames the bill on the supplier; you argue it is about habits and insulation.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Ho ricevuto la bolletta e non capisco perché sia così alta.
lines.en: I got the bill and I do not understand why it is so high.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Ti dico la mia: lo spreco più grande è quello che non si vede, gli apparecchi sempre accesi.
lines.en: Here is my view: the biggest waste is the one you do not see, appliances always left on.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Ma quelli consumano pochissimo.
lines.en: But those use very little.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Poco per volta, tutto l'anno. Se isolassi anche le finestre, vedresti la differenza.
lines.en: A little at a time, all year round. If you insulated the windows too, you would see the difference.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Isolare costa, e non so se recupero mai quei soldi.
lines.en: Insulating costs money, and I do not know if I will ever get it back.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Appena avrai finito i lavori, la bolletta scenderà ogni mese.
lines.en: As soon as you have finished the work, the bill will go down every month.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Mi avevano promesso la stessa cosa per le lampadine e non ho notato niente.
lines.en: They promised me the same thing about the light bulbs and I did not notice anything.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Le lampadine da sole fanno poco, è l'insieme che conta.
lines.en: Light bulbs alone do little, it is the whole set of changes that counts.

days.title: Il turismo sostenibile
days.objective: Argue for travelling off season and for slow travel, using 'invece di' and concessive clauses.
days.paretoFocus: Travel plans are a standard exam prompt, and negotiating them forces you to defend a preference.
drills: Give two reasons to travel off season and one drawback.
Contrast two plans using 'invece di' plus infinitive.
Describe what is found in historic centres, using the passive 'si'.

items: How do you say 'instead of doing something'?
Which mood follows 'nonostante'?
How does 'si trova' change with a plural noun?

activities.title: Viaggiare con calma
activities.intro: Sustainable travel talk lives on contrasts: off season against high season, staying against rushing. 'Invece di' plus an infinitive is the cleanest way to build that contrast in one sentence.

items.hr: Viaggiare fuori stagione costa meno e riduce la pressione sui luoghi più visitati.
items.en: Travelling off season costs less and reduces pressure on the most visited places.
items.note: fuori stagione: fixed expression, no article

items.hr: Nonostante i turisti portino soldi, molti centri storici si sono svuotati di abitanti.
items.en: Although tourists bring money, many historic centres have emptied of residents.
items.note: nonostante + congiuntivo: portino

items.hr: Preferisco restare più giorni in un posto solo, invece di correre da una città all'altra.
items.en: I prefer to stay several days in one place, instead of rushing from one city to another.
items.note: invece di + infinito

items.hr: Se ci fossero più regole, alcune zone non sarebbero così affollate.
items.en: If there were more rules, some areas would not be so crowded.
items.note: così with the accent on the final i

items.hr: Un turismo lento permette di conoscere davvero un posto.
items.en: Slow tourism lets you really get to know a place.
items.note: permettere di + infinito

activities.title: Alta e bassa stagione
activities.intro: Practise contrast and concession, plus one impersonal passive linked to driving in city centres.

questions.prompt: Preferisco restare in un posto solo, ___ correre da una città all'altra.
questions.explanation: Before an infinitive the fixed form is 'invece di'.

questions.prompt: Nonostante i turisti ___ soldi, molti abitanti se ne vanno.
questions.explanation: 'Nonostante' always takes the subjunctive: portino.

questions.prompt: Viaggiare fuori ___ costa meno ed evita la folla.
questions.explanation: The set phrase for travelling outside the busy months takes no article.

questions.prompt: Nei centri storici si ___ (trovare) spesso zone a traffico limitato.
questions.explanation: With the si passivante the verb agrees with the plural noun 'zone': si trovano.

questions.prompt: Build the Italian for: 'Slow tourism lets you really get to know a place.'
questions.explanation: 'Permettere' always keeps 'di' before the infinitive.

activities.title: Ad agosto o a maggio
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. Your friend wants a August seaside trip; you push for off season and they resist.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Ad agosto andiamo in quella città sul mare, è il periodo migliore.
lines.en: In August let us go to that city by the sea, it is the best time.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Io preferirei andarci a maggio: costa meno e non c'è la fila ovunque.
lines.en: I would rather go in May: it costs less and there is not a queue everywhere.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Ma a maggio il mare è freddo e mezzo paese è chiuso.
lines.en: But in May the sea is cold and half the town is closed.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Nonostante qualche locale sia chiuso, vedi il posto vero, non solo turisti.
lines.en: Even though a few places are closed, you see the real place, not just tourists.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Se ci fossero più regole d'estate, non ci sarebbe bisogno di scappare a maggio.
lines.en: If there were more rules in summer, there would be no need to escape to May.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: D'accordo, però intanto le regole non ci sono, e io scelgo di andare fuori stagione.
lines.en: Agreed, but for now the rules are not there, and I choose to go off season.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Allora restiamo più giorni in un posto solo, almeno non corriamo.
lines.en: Then let us stay more days in one place, at least we will not rush.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Perfetto, un turismo lento permette di conoscere davvero un posto.
lines.en: Perfect, slow tourism lets you really get to know a place.

days.title: Il cibo e gli sprechi
days.objective: Explain how food waste starts at the shop and distinguish causal from purpose 'perché'.
days.paretoFocus: 'Perché' with two different moods is a classic B1 trap and separates confident speakers from hesitant ones.
drills: Say why you buy what you buy, using causal 'perché' with the indicative.
Say what you do so that something happens, using purpose 'perché' with the subjunctive.
Describe what you would have done differently last week with the shopping.

items: When does 'perché' take the subjunctive?
How do you form the past conditional after an unreal 'se'?
Which subjunctive tense reports a completed action?

activities.title: Non buttare il cibo
activities.intro: 'Perché' means both 'because' and 'so that'. With 'because' it takes the indicative, with 'so that' it takes the subjunctive. The rest of the lesson recycles unreal past conditionals for regrets about the shopping.

items.hr: Buttiamo via troppo cibo, spesso solo perché abbiamo comprato più del necessario.
items.en: We throw away too much food, often just because we bought more than we needed.
items.note: perché = because, so the indicative follows

items.hr: Faccio la lista della spesa perché lo spreco sia minore.
items.en: I make a shopping list so that there is less waste.
items.note: perché = so that, so the congiuntivo follows

items.hr: Gli avanzi non sono un problema: con un po' di fantasia diventano un altro piatto.
items.en: Leftovers are not a problem: with a bit of imagination they become another dish.
items.note: gli avanzi: leftovers, always plural

items.hr: Se avessimo pianificato i pasti, non avremmo buttato niente.
items.en: If we had planned the meals, we would not have thrown anything away.
items.note: unreal past: congiuntivo trapassato plus condizionale passato

items.hr: Il pane raffermo va usato, non buttato: ci si fanno polpette e zuppe.
items.en: Stale bread should be used, not thrown away: you make meatballs and soups with it.
items.note: ci si fa: impersonal si combined with ci

activities.title: Spesa e avanzi
activities.intro: Practise the two values of 'perché', with one past subjunctive from earlier lessons.

questions.prompt: Compro poche cose perché il frigo ___ piccolo.
questions.explanation: Here 'perché' means 'because', so it takes the indicative. Only purpose 'perché' takes the subjunctive.

questions.prompt: Se avessimo pianificato i pasti, non ___ niente.
questions.explanation: The main clause of an unreal past hypothesis takes the condizionale passato.

questions.prompt: Con un po' di fantasia gli ___ diventano un altro piatto.
questions.explanation: The plural noun for what is left over after a meal.

questions.prompt: Credo che ieri ___ (loro, buttare) troppo cibo.
questions.explanation: A completed action inside an opinion clause takes the congiuntivo passato.

questions.prompt: Build the Italian for: 'We throw away too much food every week.'
questions.explanation: 'Buttare via' keeps its particle 'via' right after the verb.

activities.title: Mezzo frigo nel secchio
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. Your friend blames bad luck for wasted food; you argue the problem starts at the shop.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Ho buttato mezzo frigo, era tutto scaduto.
lines.en: I threw away half the fridge, everything had gone off.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Succede, però secondo me il problema nasce alla cassa, non nel frigo.
lines.en: It happens, but in my view the problem starts at the checkout, not in the fridge.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Cioè compro troppo?
lines.en: You mean I buy too much?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Esatto. Faccio la lista prima perché lo spreco sia minore, e funziona.
lines.en: Exactly. I make the list beforehand so that there is less waste, and it works.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Io la lista la faccio, poi le offerte mi fanno cambiare idea.
lines.en: I do make the list, then the special offers make me change my mind.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Le offerte convengono solo se mangi davvero quella roba.
lines.en: Offers are only worth it if you actually eat that stuff.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: E il pane vecchio? Quello lo butto sempre.
lines.en: And old bread? I always throw that away.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Il pane raffermo va usato, ci si fanno polpette e zuppe.
lines.en: Stale bread should be used, you make meatballs and soups with it.

days.title: L'alimentazione e le scelte
days.objective: Discuss dietary choices respectfully while defending a middle position with reasons.
days.paretoFocus: Disagreeing without offending is a marked skill in the speaking exam, and food is where it comes up first.
drills: Say you respect a choice and then give your own, in two sentences.
Use 'sebbene' plus subjunctive about your own eating habits.
Describe what one eats well and cheaply, using the impersonal 'si'.

items: Which mood follows 'sebbene'?
Which adjective ending follows an impersonal 'si è'?
How do you replace a whole predicate with the pronoun 'lo'?

activities.title: Mangiare bene
activities.intro: You will often need to disagree while respecting the other person. The pattern is: acknowledge, then state your position with 'ma' or 'però' and a reason. Note that after impersonal 'si è' the adjective goes into the masculine plural.

items.hr: Ognuno sceglie come mangiare, ma penso che valga la pena ridurre la carne almeno un po'.
items.en: Everyone chooses how to eat, but I think it is worth cutting down on meat at least a little.
items.note: valere la pena: valga in the subjunctive

items.hr: Chi è vegetariano non lo fa per moda: di solito ha ragioni precise.
items.en: People who are vegetarian do not do it as a fashion: they usually have specific reasons.
items.note: lo replaces the whole idea 'essere vegetariano'

items.hr: Mangiare bene non vuol dire spendere molto: le verdure di stagione costano poco.
items.en: Eating well does not mean spending a lot: seasonal vegetables are cheap.
items.note: vuol dire: shortened form of vuole dire

items.hr: Sebbene io mangi di tutto, cerco di variare durante la settimana.
items.en: Although I eat everything, I try to vary during the week.
items.note: sebbene + congiuntivo: mangi

items.hr: Quando si mangia bene, si è più contenti anche la sera.
items.en: When you eat well, you feel happier in the evening too.
items.note: after impersonal si è the adjective is masculine plural

activities.title: Scelte a tavola
activities.intro: Practise polite disagreement and concession, with one impersonal pattern from earlier lessons.

questions.prompt: Penso che ___ la pena ridurre la carne.
questions.explanation: 'Penso che' triggers the subjunctive of valere: valga.

questions.prompt: Quando si mangia bene, si è più ___.
questions.explanation: After the impersonal si with essere, a following adjective takes the masculine plural.

questions.prompt: Chi è vegetariano non ___ fa per moda.
questions.explanation: The neuter pronoun replaces the whole preceding idea, not a single noun.

questions.prompt: Sebbene io ___ (avere) poco tempo, cucino quasi ogni sera.
questions.explanation: 'Sebbene' takes the subjunctive, and avere is irregular: abbia.

questions.prompt: Build the Italian for: 'Eating well does not mean spending a lot.'
questions.explanation: 'Vuol dire' is followed directly by the infinitive with no preposition.

activities.title: Ridurre o smettere
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. Your friend has gone fully meat free and wants everyone to; you defend reducing instead.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Ho tolto la carne del tutto, secondo me tutti dovrebbero farlo.
lines.en: I have cut out meat completely, in my opinion everyone should do it.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Rispetto la tua scelta, ma non penso che valga per tutti allo stesso modo.
lines.en: I respect your choice, but I do not think it applies to everyone in the same way.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Perché no? Fa bene alla salute e al pianeta.
lines.en: Why not? It is good for your health and for the planet.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Sebbene io mangi di tutto, ho ridotto la carne a due volte a settimana, e mi basta.
lines.en: Although I eat everything, I have cut meat down to twice a week, and that is enough for me.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Ridurre non cambia niente, o smetti o non smetti.
lines.en: Reducing changes nothing, either you stop or you do not.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Non sono d'accordo: se molti riducessero un po', l'effetto sarebbe più grande di pochi che smettono.
lines.en: I disagree: if many people reduced a little, the effect would be bigger than a few people stopping.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Su questo forse hai ragione.
lines.en: On that you may be right.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: E poi mangiare bene non vuol dire spendere molto: le verdure di stagione costano poco.
lines.en: And besides, eating well does not mean spending a lot: seasonal vegetables are cheap.

days.title: Gli animali e la loro tutela
days.objective: Debate animal protection, conceding a point while defending habitat protection as the priority.
days.paretoFocus: Conceding part of an argument before restating your position is exactly how the speaking exam rewards nuance.
drills: Say what should be protected and why, using 'penso che' plus a modal.
Concede one point to an opponent and then restate your position.
Open a sentence with a gerund expressing means, as in 'riducendo ...'.

items: What is the subjunctive of 'dovere' for 'loro'?
How do you describe a state with the venire passive?
How do you say 'by reducing'?

activities.title: Proteggere gli animali
activities.intro: Modal verbs in the subjunctive are common in this debate: penso che debbano, credo che possano. Notice also the gerund used to say by what means something is achieved.

items.hr: Molte specie rischiano di sparire, e non solo per la caccia.
items.en: Many species risk disappearing, and not only because of hunting.
items.note: rischiare di + infinito

items.hr: Penso che gli animali debbano essere protetti anche quando non sono utili all'uomo.
items.en: I think animals should be protected even when they are not useful to humans.
items.note: dovere in the subjunctive: debbano

items.hr: Chi adotta un animale si prende un impegno per anni, non per un mese.
items.en: Anyone who adopts an animal takes on a commitment for years, not for a month.
items.note: prendersi un impegno: to take on a commitment

items.hr: Questi habitat vengono protetti da anni, ma non basta.
items.en: These habitats have been protected for years, but it is not enough.
items.note: venire + participio for an ongoing situation

items.hr: Se non proteggessimo questi habitat, molte specie sparirebbero in pochi anni.
items.en: If we did not protect these habitats, many species would disappear within a few years.
items.note: double consonant in sparirebbero, one r

activities.title: Tutela e habitat
activities.intro: Practise modal subjunctives and hypotheses, with one passive structure from earlier lessons.

questions.prompt: Penso che gli animali ___ essere protetti.
questions.explanation: 'Penso che' takes the subjunctive of dovere, which is irregular: debbano.

questions.prompt: Questi habitat ___ protetti da molti anni.
questions.explanation: The venire passive agrees with the plural subject: vengono protetti.

questions.prompt: Se non ___ (noi, proteggere) questi habitat, molte specie sparirebbero.
questions.explanation: The 'se' clause of a present hypothesis takes the congiuntivo imperfetto.

questions.prompt: ___ (ridurre) il consumo di suolo, si salvano molti habitat.
questions.explanation: The gerund of ridurre keeps the -uc- stem: riducendo.

questions.prompt: Build the Italian for: 'There are those who defend zoos and those who would close them.'
questions.explanation: 'C'è chi' stays singular even when it covers many people, and the object pronoun goes before the conditional.

activities.title: Gli zoo servono ancora
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. Your friend wants all zoos closed; you argue a more nuanced line and end by conceding the main point.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Gli zoo andrebbero chiusi tutti, senza discussioni.
lines.en: All zoos should be closed, no discussion.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Capisco l'idea, però penso che alcuni programmi salvino specie che fuori sparirebbero.
lines.en: I understand the idea, but I think some programmes save species that would disappear in the wild.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Un animale in gabbia non è salvo, è solo vivo.
lines.en: An animal in a cage is not saved, it is only alive.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Su questo hai ragione per i vecchi zoo, ma i centri seri lavorano per rimetterli in natura.
lines.en: You are right about the old zoos, but serious centres work to return them to the wild.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: E quanti tornano davvero in natura? Pochissimi.
lines.en: And how many really go back to the wild? Very few.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Pochi, è vero. Per questo dico che il lavoro vero è proteggere gli habitat.
lines.en: Few, that is true. That is why I say the real work is protecting habitats.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Allora siamo d'accordo: prima l'habitat, poi il resto.
lines.en: Then we agree: habitat first, then the rest.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Esatto: riducendo il consumo di suolo si salvano più animali di qualsiasi gabbia.
lines.en: Exactly: by reducing land consumption you save more animals than any cage does.

days.title: Il paesaggio e i beni comuni
days.objective: Argue that landscape is a shared good, using concessive clauses and passives of possibility.
days.paretoFocus: The idea of a bene comune underlies many Italian debates, and it gives you abstract vocabulary at low cost.
drills: Explain in two sentences why landscape belongs to everyone.
Concede that new housing is needed, then give your counter-proposal.
Describe a place looked after by residents, using a passive.

items: Which mood follows 'sebbene'?
How do you report a promise made in the past about the future?
How does the venire passive work in the present?

activities.title: Beni di tutti
activities.intro: A bene comune is something everyone shares and nobody may spoil. Arguing about it needs concession plus a counter-proposal, and passives that describe what could be done instead.

items.hr: Il paesaggio è un bene di tutti, quindi nessuno dovrebbe rovinarlo per un guadagno privato.
items.en: The landscape belongs to everyone, so nobody should ruin it for private gain.
items.note: nessuno takes a singular verb

items.hr: Sebbene servano case nuove, costruire ovunque non è la soluzione.
items.en: Although new houses are needed, building everywhere is not the solution.
items.note: servire agrees with the plural: servano case

items.hr: I beni comuni funzionano solo se chi li usa se ne prende cura.
items.en: Shared goods only work if those who use them take care of them.
items.note: prendersene cura: se + ne combined

items.hr: Molti edifici abbandonati potrebbero essere recuperati invece di costruirne altri.
items.en: Many abandoned buildings could be restored instead of building new ones.
items.note: costruirne: ne attached to the infinitive

items.hr: Quando un posto viene curato dagli abitanti, si vede subito.
items.en: When a place is looked after by its residents, you notice immediately.
items.note: da + article for the agent: dagli abitanti

activities.title: Costruire o recuperare
activities.intro: Practise concession and passives, with one reported future from earlier lessons.

questions.prompt: Sebbene ___ case nuove, non si può costruire ovunque.
questions.explanation: 'Sebbene' takes the subjunctive, and servire agrees with the plural 'case'.

questions.prompt: Ci avevano assicurato che il parco ___ entro l'estate.
questions.explanation: A future in the past becomes the condizionale passato, here in the passive.

questions.prompt: Il paesaggio è un bene di ___: nessuno può rovinarlo per interesse privato.
questions.explanation: A shared good belongs to everyone, hence the fixed phrase 'un bene di tutti'.

questions.prompt: Quando un posto ___ (venire) curato dagli abitanti, si nota subito.
questions.explanation: The venire passive in the present, agreeing with the singular subject.

questions.prompt: Build the Italian for: 'Many abandoned buildings could be restored.'
questions.explanation: The conditional of potere plus the passive infinitive expresses a possibility.

activities.title: Il terreno è mio
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. Your friend defends absolute property rights; you argue for shared goods and win a partial agreement.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Se uno compra un terreno, ci costruisce quello che vuole.
lines.en: If someone buys land, they build whatever they want on it.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Non credo: il paesaggio è un bene di tutti, non solo di chi ha il terreno.
lines.en: I do not think so: the landscape belongs to everyone, not just to the landowner.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Però servono case nuove, la gente deve pur abitare da qualche parte.
lines.en: But new houses are needed, people have to live somewhere.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Sebbene servano case, ci sono migliaia di edifici vuoti che potrebbero essere recuperati.
lines.en: Although houses are needed, there are thousands of empty buildings that could be restored.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Recuperare costa più che costruire.
lines.en: Restoring costs more than building.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: A breve termine sì, ma consumare altro suolo lo paghiamo tutti dopo.
lines.en: In the short term yes, but we all pay later for consuming more land.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Detta così ha senso. Il parco vicino a me lo curano gli abitanti e si vede.
lines.en: Put like that it makes sense. The park near me is looked after by residents and you can tell.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Ecco, quando un posto viene curato da chi ci vive, la differenza è enorme.
lines.en: Exactly, when a place is looked after by those who live there, the difference is huge.

days.title: La sicurezza stradale
days.objective: Discuss road safety, correcting a false belief and arguing that rules alone are not enough.
days.paretoFocus: Correcting someone politely with data is a high-value move in any discussion, not just this one.
drills: Correct a wrong belief about accidents, starting with 'non è così'.
Say what would happen if everyone kept their distance, using the second conditional.
State one rule using the obligation passive with 'va'.

items: Which mood follows purpose 'perché'?
Which subjunctive tense shows an action earlier than the main verb?
How do you say something must always be fastened?

activities.title: Guidare sicuri
activities.intro: Two structures do the work here: purpose 'perché' plus subjunctive, and the andare passive for rules. Both let you argue about safety without sounding like a lecture.

items.hr: La maggior parte degli incidenti succede a bassa velocità, vicino a casa.
items.en: Most accidents happen at low speed, close to home.
items.note: la maggior parte + verbo singolare

items.hr: Basta un attimo di distrazione perché tutto cambi.
items.en: One moment of distraction is enough for everything to change.
items.note: perché here means 'for ... to', so the congiuntivo follows

items.hr: Non credo che le multe da sole bastino: serve anche educazione.
items.en: I do not think fines alone are enough: education is needed too.
items.note: bastino: subjunctive after a negated opinion

items.hr: Se tutti rispettassero le distanze, ci sarebbero molti meno tamponamenti.
items.en: If everyone kept their distance, there would be far fewer rear end collisions.
items.note: ci sarebbero: conditional of esserci

items.hr: Il casco va allacciato sempre, anche per un tragitto breve.
items.en: The helmet must always be fastened, even for a short trip.
items.note: andare + participio for a rule

activities.title: Strade e regole
activities.intro: Practise purpose clauses and hypotheses, with one past subjunctive linked to travel.

questions.prompt: Basta un attimo di distrazione perché tutto ___.
questions.explanation: Here 'perché' introduces a consequence aimed at, so it takes the subjunctive: cambi.

questions.prompt: Nonostante ___ fuori stagione, abbiamo trovato traffico ovunque.
questions.explanation: The action is earlier than the main past verb, so the congiuntivo trapassato is required.

questions.prompt: Se tutti ___ (rispettare) le distanze, ci sarebbero meno incidenti.
questions.explanation: The congiuntivo imperfetto builds the 'se' half of a present hypothesis.

questions.prompt: Non credo che le multe da sole ___ (bastare) a risolvere il problema.
questions.explanation: A negated opinion verb takes the subjunctive: bastino.

questions.prompt: Build the Italian for: 'Most accidents happen close to home.'
questions.explanation: 'La maggior parte' takes a singular verb even though the noun after it is plural.

activities.title: Dove succedono gli incidenti
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. Your friend believes accidents only happen on motorways; you correct them and disagree about fines.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Io in città vado piano, gli incidenti veri succedono in autostrada.
lines.en: I drive slowly in town, real accidents happen on the motorway.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Non è così: la maggior parte degli incidenti succede vicino a casa, a bassa velocità.
lines.en: It is not like that: most accidents happen close to home, at low speed.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Sarà, ma a trenta all'ora non ti fai niente.
lines.en: Maybe, but at thirty an hour you do not get hurt.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: A un pedone basta molto meno. E poi conta la distrazione, non solo la velocità.
lines.en: For a pedestrian much less is enough. And distraction matters, not just speed.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Allora servono più multe, così la gente impara.
lines.en: Then we need more fines, that way people learn.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Non credo che le multe da sole bastino, serve anche educazione fin da piccoli.
lines.en: I do not think fines alone are enough, education from childhood is needed too.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Educazione è una parola grossa, intanto io il casco lo metto sempre.
lines.en: Education is a big word, meanwhile I always wear my helmet.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Bene, il casco va allacciato anche per un tragitto di due minuti.
lines.en: Good, the helmet must be fastened even for a two minute trip.

days.title: Il colloquio, le domande difficili
days.objective: Handle the awkward interview questions about weaknesses, gaps and past jobs, in a formal register.
days.paretoFocus: The hard questions are predictable, so a prepared structure turns the most stressful moment into a rehearsed one.
drills: Answer 'tell me a weakness' in two sentences, ending with what you do about it.
Explain why you left a previous job without criticising anyone.
Accept a condition using 'purché' plus subjunctive.

items: Which mood follows 'purché'?
Which subjunctive tense follows a past reporting verb?
How do you report a commitment you made in the past about the future?

activities.title: Domande scomode
activities.intro: Interviews use the formal Lei and a lot of indirect questions, which pull the subjunctive after a past reporting verb. 'Purché' lets you accept a condition without saying yes to everything.

items.hr: Se mi chiedessero il mio difetto principale, direi che tendo a controllare troppo i dettagli.
items.en: If they asked me my main weakness, I would say I tend to check details too much.
items.note: tendere a + infinito

items.hr: Non ho esperienza in questo settore, però imparo in fretta e non mi spavento.
items.en: I have no experience in this sector, but I learn quickly and I do not get scared.
items.note: in fretta: quickly

items.hr: Mi hanno chiesto perché avessi lasciato il lavoro precedente.
items.en: They asked me why I had left my previous job.
items.note: past reporting verb plus congiuntivo trapassato

items.hr: Preferisco dire la verità piuttosto che inventare una risposta perfetta.
items.en: I prefer to tell the truth rather than invent a perfect answer.
items.note: piuttosto che + infinito

items.hr: Sarei disponibile a spostarmi, purché il contratto sia stabile.
items.en: I would be willing to relocate, provided the contract is stable.
items.note: purché + congiuntivo

activities.title: Rispondere bene
activities.intro: Practise indirect questions and conditions, with one reported commitment from earlier lessons.

questions.prompt: Mi hanno chiesto perché ___ lasciato il lavoro precedente.
questions.explanation: An indirect question after a past verb takes the congiuntivo trapassato: avessi lasciato.

questions.prompt: Se mi ___ il mio difetto principale, risponderei con sincerità.
questions.explanation: A hypothetical question uses the congiuntivo imperfetto after 'se'.

questions.prompt: Sarei disponibile a spostarmi, ___ il contratto sia stabile.
questions.explanation: A conjunction of condition is needed before the subjunctive 'sia'.

questions.prompt: Avevo detto che ___ (io, accettare) qualsiasi turno pur di cominciare.
questions.explanation: A commitment made in the past about the future takes the condizionale passato.

questions.prompt: Build the Italian for: 'I prefer to tell the truth rather than invent an answer.'
questions.explanation: 'Piuttosto che' links two infinitives directly.

activities.title: Il colloquio
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. The interviewer presses you on weaknesses and missing experience, using the formal Lei.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Mi dica un suo difetto.
lines.en: Tell me one of your weaknesses.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Tendo a controllare troppo i dettagli, per questo ho imparato a darmi dei tempi.
lines.en: I tend to check details too much, so I have learned to set myself time limits.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: E perché ha lasciato il lavoro precedente?
lines.en: And why did you leave your previous job?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Cercavo un ruolo con più responsabilità, non ho avuto problemi con nessuno.
lines.en: I was looking for a role with more responsibility, I had no problems with anyone.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Non ha esperienza nel nostro settore. Non è un problema?
lines.en: You have no experience in our sector. Is that not a problem?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Non credo che lo sia: imparo in fretta e ho già cambiato settore una volta.
lines.en: I do not think it is: I learn quickly and I have already changed sector once.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Sarebbe disponibile a spostarsi in un'altra città?
lines.en: Would you be willing to relocate to another city?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Sarei disponibile, purché il contratto sia stabile.
lines.en: I would be willing, provided the contract is stable.

days.title: Il lavoro autonomo e la partita IVA
days.objective: Explain in general terms how self-employment works in Italy and weigh its freedom against its risks.
days.paretoFocus: Anyone working in Italy meets the partita IVA question sooner or later, and the vocabulary is fixed and reusable.
drills: Explain in three sentences what changes when you work for yourself.
List one advantage and one disadvantage in the same sentence.
Say what you would do if you had steady clients.

items: Which article goes before 'svantaggio'?
How does the participle agree in a passive with essere?
How do you say what someone pays for themselves?

activities.title: Lavorare in proprio
activities.intro: In Italy a self-employed person opens a partita IVA, invoices clients and handles their own social contributions rather than having them deducted by an employer. This lesson gives you the vocabulary and the balance structures to discuss it. Rates and thresholds change, so talk about how it works, not about numbers.

items.hr: Chi lavora in proprio apre una partita IVA e fattura i suoi clienti.
items.en: Anyone working for themselves opens a VAT number and invoices their clients.
items.note: lavorare in proprio: to be self-employed

items.hr: A differenza del dipendente, l'autonomo si paga da solo i contributi e la pensione.
items.en: Unlike an employee, a self-employed person pays their own contributions and pension.
items.note: a differenza di: unlike

items.hr: Il vantaggio è la libertà, lo svantaggio è che nessuno ti paga le ferie.
items.en: The advantage is freedom, the disadvantage is that nobody pays your holidays.
items.note: lo svantaggio: lo before s + consonant

items.hr: Le fatture vengono inviate di solito alla fine del mese.
items.en: Invoices are usually sent at the end of the month.
items.note: la fattura: invoice

items.hr: Se avessi clienti fissi, lascerei subito il posto da dipendente.
items.en: If I had steady clients, I would leave my employee position straight away.
items.note: il posto: here a permanent job

activities.title: Autonomo o dipendente
activities.intro: Practise self-employment vocabulary and hypotheses, with one passive agreement from earlier lessons.

questions.prompt: ___ svantaggio principale è l'assenza di ferie pagate.
questions.explanation: Before s + consonant the masculine singular article is 'lo'.

questions.prompt: Le fatture ___ inviate alla fine del mese.
questions.explanation: In a passive with essere the participle agrees with the feminine plural subject.

questions.prompt: Chi lavora in proprio apre una partita ___ per fatturare i clienti.
questions.explanation: The self-employed register a VAT number, called partita IVA in Italian.

questions.prompt: Molti pensano che il lavoro autonomo ___ (essere) più rischioso.
questions.explanation: 'Pensare che' triggers the present subjunctive.

questions.prompt: Build the Italian for: 'The self-employed person pays their own social security alone.'
questions.explanation: The reflexive 'si paga' plus 'da solo' shows the person does it themselves.

activities.title: Aprire la partita IVA
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. Your friend paints self-employment as easy money; you disagree while admitting its appeal.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Apri la partita IVA, guadagni il doppio.
lines.en: Open a VAT number, you will earn twice as much.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Non è così semplice: l'autonomo si paga da solo i contributi e la pensione.
lines.en: It is not that simple: a self-employed person pays their own contributions and pension.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Sì, però decidi tu quanto lavorare.
lines.en: Yes, but you decide how much to work.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Decidi tu se hai clienti. Senza clienti fissi decidi solo di stare fermo.
lines.en: You decide if you have clients. Without steady clients you only decide to sit still.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: All'inizio è dura per tutti, poi il lavoro arriva.
lines.en: At the start it is hard for everyone, then the work comes.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Lo svantaggio è che nessuno ti paga le ferie né i giorni di malattia.
lines.en: The disadvantage is that nobody pays you holidays or sick days.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Quindi non lo faresti mai?
lines.en: So you would never do it?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Se avessi tre o quattro clienti fissi, lascerei il posto da dipendente domani.
lines.en: If I had three or four steady clients, I would leave my employee job tomorrow.

days.title: I contratti e i diritti dei lavoratori
days.objective: Read a work contract critically in general terms and ask for clarification before signing.
days.paretoFocus: Knowing which three items to check in any contract, and how to ask about them, protects you in real life.
drills: Name the three things you check first in a contract and why.
Ask for a written explanation politely, in two different ways.
Open a sentence with a past gerund about something you read.

items: What follows 'prima di'?
How do you build the past gerund?
Which accent does the Italian verb meaning 'is' carry?

activities.title: Prima di firmare
activities.intro: This lesson gives you the language to check a contract and ask questions, in general terms only. Note 'prima di' always followed by an infinitive, and the accented 'è' meaning 'is', which is never written with an apostrophe.

items.hr: Prima di firmare un contratto conviene leggere tutto, anche le righe piccole.
items.en: Before signing a contract it is best to read everything, including the small print.
items.note: prima di + infinito

items.hr: In generale il contratto indica l'orario, la mansione e la durata.
items.en: In general a contract states the hours, the role and the duration.
items.note: la mansione: the duties or role

items.hr: Se qualcosa non è chiaro, è meglio chiedere una spiegazione scritta.
items.en: If something is not clear, it is better to ask for a written explanation.
items.note: è with a grave accent, never e with an apostrophe

items.hr: Penso che conoscere i propri diritti sia utile quanto conoscere il mestiere.
items.en: I think knowing your rights is as useful as knowing your trade.
items.note: utile quanto: as useful as

items.hr: Un contratto a tempo determinato finisce a una data precisa, uno a tempo indeterminato no.
items.en: A fixed term contract ends on a precise date, an open ended one does not.
items.note: uno here is a pronoun standing for 'un contratto'

activities.title: Leggere il contratto
activities.intro: Practise contract vocabulary and infinitive structures, with one gerund from earlier lessons.

questions.prompt: Prima di ___ conviene leggere tutto il contratto.
questions.explanation: 'Prima di' is always followed by the infinitive, never by a gerund.

questions.prompt: ___ letto il contratto, ho chiesto una copia.
questions.explanation: The past gerund is avendo plus the participle, showing an action completed first.

questions.prompt: Un contratto a tempo ___ finisce a una data precisa.
questions.explanation: A fixed term contract is 'a tempo determinato', as opposed to indeterminato.

questions.prompt: Se qualcosa non ___ (essere) chiaro, chiedi una spiegazione scritta.
questions.explanation: The third person of essere carries a grave accent and is never written with an apostrophe.

questions.prompt: Build the Italian for: 'It is better to ask for a written explanation.'
questions.explanation: 'È meglio' is followed directly by the infinitive, and the adjective agrees with spiegazione.

activities.title: Firmo e basta
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. Your friend wants to sign without reading; you push back and give them a short checklist.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Mi hanno mandato il contratto, firmo e basta, tanto è tutto standard.
lines.en: They sent me the contract, I will just sign, it is all standard anyway.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Aspetta, prima di firmare conviene leggere tutto, anche le righe piccole.
lines.en: Wait, before signing it is best to read everything, including the small print.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Sono due pagine di parole difficili, non ci capisco niente.
lines.en: It is two pages of difficult words, I do not understand any of it.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Guarda almeno tre cose: l'orario, la mansione e la durata.
lines.en: Look at least at three things: the hours, the role and the duration.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: La durata c'è: a tempo determinato, sei mesi.
lines.en: The duration is there: fixed term, six months.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Allora sai già che finisce a una data precisa, e questo cambia molti calcoli.
lines.en: Then you already know it ends on a precise date, and that changes a lot of calculations.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Se qualcosa non mi torna, chiedo direttamente il primo giorno.
lines.en: If something does not add up, I will ask on the first day.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Meglio prima, e se puoi chiedi una spiegazione scritta.
lines.en: Better beforehand, and if you can ask for a written explanation.

days.title: Il sindacato e la rappresentanza
days.objective: You can explain what workplace representation is and argue why a collective voice matters, using the congiuntivo after verbs of opinion.
days.paretoFocus: Saying what you think about how decisions get made at work, and backing it with a reason, is exactly what the speaking module rewards.
drills: Write three sentences starting with Penso che about how decisions are taken where you work or study.
Say aloud one advantage and one limit of being represented by someone else.
List five workplace nouns with their article and say each one twice.

items: Which verbs force the congiuntivo, and what does benché do to the verb after it?
How do you say that a decision concerns everyone, not just the people who voted?
Give one reason why collective bargaining can be stronger than an individual request.

activities.title: Parlare di rappresentanza
activities.intro: Verbs of opinion such as pensare, credere and sembrare take the congiuntivo in the clause that follows. So do concessive words such as benché and nonostante. In this lesson each sentence states a position and adds the reason behind it.

items.hr: Penso che ogni lavoratore abbia il diritto di essere rappresentato.
items.en: I think every worker has the right to be represented.
items.note: pensare che + congiuntivo presente: avere -> abbia

items.hr: Il sindacato è un'organizzazione che difende gli interessi di chi lavora.
items.en: A union is an organisation that defends the interests of those who work.
items.note: il sindacato, l'organizzazione

items.hr: Benché non tutti siano iscritti, le decisioni riguardano l'intera squadra.
items.en: Although not everyone is a member, the decisions concern the whole team.
items.note: benché + congiuntivo: essere -> siano

items.hr: Secondo me la contrattazione collettiva funziona meglio della trattativa individuale, perché una sola voce conta poco.
items.en: In my view collective bargaining works better than an individual negotiation, because a single voice counts for little.
items.note: position plus reason: secondo me ... perché ...

items.hr: Il rappresentante dei lavoratori raccoglie le richieste e le porta alla direzione.
items.en: The workers' representative collects the requests and takes them to management.
items.note: il rappresentante, la richiesta, la direzione

activities.title: Opinioni sulla rappresentanza
activities.intro: Work through opinion verbs and concessives; an older structure resurfaces near the end.

questions.prompt: Penso che il sindacato ___ un ruolo importante nella trattativa.
questions.explanation: Pensare che introduces an opinion, so the following verb goes into the congiuntivo presente: avere becomes abbia.

questions.prompt: Review from an earlier lesson: Il contratto d'affitto ___ ti ho parlato scade a giugno.
questions.explanation: Parlare takes the preposition di, so the relative must carry it: parlare di un contratto becomes il contratto di cui ti ho parlato.

questions.prompt: Complete with the right form of essere: Benché i colleghi ___ stanchi, partecipano sempre all'assemblea.
questions.explanation: Benché always governs the congiuntivo; with a plural subject in the present that gives siano.

questions.prompt: Complete with the correct definite article: Hanno annunciato ___ sciopero per venerdì.
questions.explanation: Masculine nouns beginning with s plus a consonant take lo in the singular: lo sciopero, lo studio, lo spazio.

questions.prompt: Build the Italian for: 'I think that the workers should have a voice.'
questions.explanation: Penso che triggers the congiuntivo of dovere, which is debbano in the third person plural.

activities.title: Vale la pena iscriversi?
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. A colleague doubts that representation changes anything, and you argue the opposite using opinion verbs plus the congiuntivo.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Sinceramente non credo che il sindacato serva a qualcosa, decidono sempre loro.
lines.en: Honestly I don't think the union is any use, they always decide anyway.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Non sono d'accordo. Penso che senza rappresentanza nessuno sappia nemmeno che cosa chiediamo.
lines.en: I disagree. I think that without representation nobody even knows what we are asking for.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Però l'anno scorso hanno ottenuto poco, benché ci siano state tante riunioni.
lines.en: But last year they got very little, although there were plenty of meetings.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: È vero, hanno ottenuto poco. Secondo me però poco insieme è più di niente da soli.
lines.en: That's true, they got little. In my view, though, a little together is more than nothing alone.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Non so, mi sembra che si perda solo tempo in assemblea.
lines.en: I don't know, it seems to me that we only waste time in the assembly.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Su questo ti do ragione, le riunioni sono lunghe. Ma è lì che si scrivono le richieste.
lines.en: There I agree with you, the meetings are long. But that's where the requests get written.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Va bene, vengo alla prossima e poi decido.
lines.en: All right, I'll come to the next one and then I'll decide.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Perfetto. Ascolta, fai una domanda e giudica dalla risposta.
lines.en: Perfect. Listen, ask a question and judge from the answer.

days.title: Un conflitto sul lavoro
days.objective: You can describe a disagreement at work and propose a solution using the second conditional with se plus the congiuntivo imperfetto.
days.paretoFocus: Hypothetical reasoning is the fastest way to sound reasonable instead of accusatory when you have to complain about something.
drills: Write three sentences of the type Se ci fosse ..., ... about a problem you have seen at work.
Say two ways of disagreeing politely, one soft and one firm.
Describe out loud a misunderstanding you had and what you would do differently.

items: Which tense follows se in an unreal condition, and which one goes in the main clause?
How do you say that nobody asked the opinion of the people who do the job?
Give two nouns for a misunderstanding and use one in a sentence.

activities.title: Gestire un disaccordo
activities.intro: For a situation you consider unreal or unlikely, put the congiuntivo imperfetto after se and the condizionale presente in the main clause. This softens a complaint into a proposal.

items.hr: Se ci fosse più dialogo, molti conflitti si risolverebbero prima.
items.en: If there were more dialogue, many conflicts would be resolved sooner.
items.note: se + congiuntivo imperfetto, poi condizionale presente

items.hr: Non sono d'accordo con questa decisione, perché nessuno ha chiesto il parere di chi fa il lavoro.
items.en: I don't agree with this decision, because nobody asked the opinion of the people who do the job.
items.note: il parere; chi fa il lavoro takes a singular verb

items.hr: Preferirei parlarne con calma piuttosto che scrivere un messaggio arrabbiato.
items.en: I would rather talk it over calmly than write an angry message.
items.note: piuttosto che introduces the rejected option

items.hr: Se io fossi al posto suo, chiederei un incontro con il responsabile.
items.en: If I were in his place, I would ask for a meeting with the manager.
items.note: essere -> fossi; il responsabile

items.hr: Il malinteso nasce spesso dal fatto che le regole non sono chiare per tutti.
items.en: The misunderstanding often comes from the fact that the rules are not clear to everyone.
items.note: il malinteso, il fraintendimento

activities.title: Ipotesi e disaccordo
activities.intro: The unreal conditional and polite disagreement, plus a question that reaches back a few lessons.

questions.prompt: Se il capo ___ più chiaro, non ci sarebbero tanti malintesi.
questions.explanation: An unreal condition takes the congiuntivo imperfetto after se, never the condizionale: se fosse, non ci sarebbero.

questions.prompt: Review from an earlier lesson: choose the word that makes this impersonal. ___ fa la raccolta differenziata in tutti i quartieri.
questions.explanation: The si passivante turns an active verb into an impersonal statement: si fa la raccolta, si fanno le raccolte.

questions.prompt: Complete with the conditional of parlare: Se avessi più tempo, ___ direttamente con il responsabile.
questions.explanation: After se plus congiuntivo imperfetto, the main clause takes the condizionale presente: io parlerei.

questions.prompt: Complete with the past participle of rimanere, agreeing correctly: Le colleghe sono ___ fino a tardi.
questions.explanation: With essere the participle agrees with the subject; a feminine plural subject gives rimaste.

questions.prompt: Build the Italian for: 'If there were more dialogue, we would work better.'
questions.explanation: Ci fosse is the impersonal there were, and lavoreremmo is the condizionale of lavorare in the first person plural.

activities.title: Due modi di reagire
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. Your colleague wants to react immediately and hard, and you argue for a different approach using unreal conditionals.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Scrivo subito una mail a tutti e dico come sono andate le cose.
lines.en: I'm writing an email to everyone right now and saying how things went.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Aspetta. Se scrivessi adesso, useresti parole che poi restano per iscritto.
lines.en: Wait. If you wrote now, you would use words that then stay in writing.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: E allora? Hanno deciso senza chiedere niente a noi.
lines.en: So what? They decided without asking us anything.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Su questo hai ragione, ed è proprio il punto da mettere sul tavolo.
lines.en: You're right about that, and it's exactly the point to put on the table.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Però se chiedo un incontro, mi risponderanno che è tutto già deciso.
lines.en: But if I ask for a meeting, they will tell me it's all decided already.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Forse. Se però ci andassimo in due, sarebbe più difficile liquidarci in due minuti.
lines.en: Maybe. But if the two of us went, it would be harder to brush us off in two minutes.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Va bene, proviamo così. Ma se non cambia niente, la mail la mando lo stesso.
lines.en: All right, let's try that. But if nothing changes, I'll send the email anyway.

days.title: La retribuzione e la busta paga
days.objective: You can read the main parts of an Italian payslip and explain the difference between gross and net pay, using passive and impersonal forms.
days.paretoFocus: Lordo against netto is the single biggest surprise for a newcomer, and the vocabulary comes back in every job conversation.
drills: Say aloud what is taken from the gross figure before the net one appears.
Write two sentences with vengono plus a participle about money and deductions.
Explain in three sentences what the tredicesima and the TFR are.

items: What is the difference between lo stipendio lordo and lo stipendio netto?
What are i contributi for, and why are they not simply lost money?
How do you build a passive sentence with venire instead of essere?

activities.title: Leggere la busta paga
activities.intro: The passive with venire plus a past participle is very common in administrative Italian, and the si passivante is used for general statements. No figures are given here, because amounts and rates change.

items.hr: Nella busta paga si distingue lo stipendio lordo dallo stipendio netto.
items.en: On the payslip you distinguish gross pay from net pay.
items.note: la busta paga, lo stipendio lordo, lo stipendio netto

items.hr: Dal lordo vengono tolti i contributi e le imposte, quindi il netto è la somma che arriva sul conto.
items.en: From the gross figure contributions and taxes are deducted, so the net figure is the sum that reaches the account.
items.note: venire + participio forms the passive: vengono tolti

items.hr: I contributi servono a finanziare la pensione e altre tutele, perciò non sono soldi persi.
items.en: Contributions serve to fund the pension and other protections, so they are not lost money.
items.note: il contributo, la tutela; position plus reason

items.hr: La tredicesima è una mensilità in più che di solito viene pagata verso la fine dell'anno.
items.en: The tredicesima is an extra month's pay that is usually paid towards the end of the year.
items.note: la tredicesima, la mensilità

items.hr: Il trattamento di fine rapporto è una somma che si accumula durante il lavoro e viene liquidata quando il rapporto finisce.
items.en: The end-of-employment payment is a sum that builds up during the job and is paid out when the employment ends.
items.note: il trattamento di fine rapporto, spesso abbreviato in TFR

activities.title: Lordo, netto e contributi
activities.intro: Payslip vocabulary and passive forms, and something learned before comes back for a second look.

questions.prompt: Quale espressione indica la somma che il lavoratore riceve davvero sul conto?
questions.explanation: Il netto is what remains after contributions and taxes have been deducted from the gross amount.

questions.prompt: Complete the passive with the right form of venire: I contributi ___ trattenuti ogni mese.
questions.explanation: Venire plus a participle makes a passive that stresses the regular process; essere would also be grammatical.

questions.prompt: Complete with the impersonal particle: In busta paga ___ leggono le voci una per una.
questions.explanation: The si passivante agrees with the noun, so with a plural noun the verb is plural: si leggono le voci.

questions.prompt: Review from an earlier lesson: Vorrei un chilo ___ mele, per favore.
questions.explanation: After a quantity such as un chilo, un litro or un pacco, Italian uses plain di with no article.

questions.prompt: Build the Italian for: 'The gross salary is not the money that arrives in the account.'
questions.explanation: Stipendio takes lo because it begins with s plus a consonant, and che introduces the relative clause.

activities.title: La prima busta paga
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. A friend is shocked by the difference between the agreed figure and the money received, and you weigh the two sides.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Mi avevano detto una cifra, e sul conto ne è arrivata un'altra molto più bassa.
lines.en: They told me one figure, and a much lower one arrived in my account.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Quella cifra era il lordo. Dal lordo vengono tolti i contributi e le imposte.
lines.en: That figure was the gross amount. Contributions and taxes are deducted from the gross.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Quindi ogni mese perdo una parte di quello che ho guadagnato.
lines.en: So every month I lose a part of what I earned.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Non la chiamerei una perdita. I contributi costruiscono la pensione e altre tutele.
lines.en: I wouldn't call it a loss. Contributions build your pension and other protections.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Sarà anche vero, però adesso l'affitto lo pago con il netto, non con la pensione.
lines.en: That may be true, but right now I pay the rent with the net figure, not with a pension.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Hai ragione, il problema di oggi è reale. Per questo conviene ragionare sempre sul netto quando si tratta lo stipendio.
lines.en: You're right, today's problem is real. That's why it's worth thinking in net terms when you negotiate pay.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: E la tredicesima quando arriva?
lines.en: And when does the tredicesima arrive?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Di solito verso la fine dell'anno, ma guarda il tuo contratto per esserne sicuro.
lines.en: Usually towards the end of the year, but check your contract to be sure.

days.title: Cambiare lavoro
days.objective: You can weigh up a job change and describe the steps of leaving, using nonostante with the congiuntivo passato and the futuro anteriore.
days.paretoFocus: Concessive sentences let you admit the good side of an option and still argue against it, which is the core move of a B1 argument.
drills: Write two sentences with nonostante about a decision you took anyway.
Say what you will do once you have signed something, using quando plus the futuro anteriore.
List three reasons that would justify leaving a job, each with perché.

items: Which mood follows nonostante, and how do you build its past form?
When do you use the futuro anteriore instead of the simple future?
What is il preavviso, and what is it for?

activities.title: Valutare un cambiamento
activities.intro: Nonostante and benché take the congiuntivo, and the congiuntivo passato is used when the concession refers to something already done. The futuro anteriore marks an action finished before another future action.

items.hr: Nonostante abbia trovato un posto più stabile, non è stata una scelta facile.
items.en: Although he found a more stable position, it was not an easy choice.
items.note: nonostante + congiuntivo passato: abbia trovato

items.hr: Quando avrò firmato il nuovo contratto, darò le dimissioni.
items.en: Once I have signed the new contract, I will hand in my resignation.
items.note: futuro anteriore: avrò firmato; dare le dimissioni

items.hr: Secondo me conviene cambiare solo se il nuovo lavoro offre qualcosa in più, non soltanto qualche euro.
items.en: In my view it is worth changing only if the new job offers something more, not just a few euros.
items.note: position plus condition

items.hr: Mi sembra che il colloquio sia andato bene, però preferisco aspettare la risposta.
items.en: It seems to me the interview went well, but I prefer to wait for the answer.
items.note: il colloquio; sembrare che + congiuntivo passato

items.hr: Il preavviso serve a dare all'azienda il tempo di organizzarsi.
items.en: The notice period serves to give the company time to get organised.
items.note: il preavviso, l'azienda

activities.title: Restare o andare via
activities.intro: Concessive clauses and the future perfect. One item revisits ground you have already covered.

questions.prompt: Nonostante ___ già firmato, ha continuato a valutare altre offerte.
questions.explanation: Nonostante requires the congiuntivo; with a present main verb reference the past form is abbia firmato.

questions.prompt: Quale frase esprime una posizione con la sua motivazione?
options: Cambio lavoro perché voglio più responsabilità.
Il nuovo ufficio è in centro.
questions.answer: Cambio lavoro perché voglio più responsabilità.
questions.explanation: Only this sentence pairs a decision with a reason introduced by perché; the others are plain statements of fact.

questions.prompt: Complete with the future auxiliary so the action is finished first: Quando ___ finito il periodo di prova, parlerò di stipendio.
questions.explanation: The futuro anteriore is avere or essere in the future plus the participle, and it marks the earlier of two future events.

questions.prompt: Review from an earlier lesson, complete with the preposition and article: Vado ___ lavoro in bicicletta ogni giorno.
questions.explanation: Andare a plus il lavoro contracts to al lavoro, like al mercato and al cinema.

questions.prompt: Build the Italian for: 'Although he has already accepted, he still has doubts.'
questions.explanation: Già sits between the auxiliary and the participle, and dei dubbi is the partitive plural of un dubbio.

activities.title: Un'offerta da valutare
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. Your friend wants to accept a new offer immediately, and you two genuinely weigh the two options.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Mi hanno offerto di più, quindi accetto e do le dimissioni domani.
lines.en: They offered me more, so I'm accepting and resigning tomorrow.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Nonostante l'offerta sia buona, aspetterei di avere tutto per iscritto.
lines.en: Although the offer is good, I would wait until you have everything in writing.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Mi fido, me l'hanno detto al colloquio davanti a due persone.
lines.en: I trust them, they told me at the interview in front of two people.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Ti credo, però quando avrai firmato saprai anche gli orari e il periodo di prova.
lines.en: I believe you, but once you have signed you will also know the hours and the trial period.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Secondo te allora dovrei restare qui?
lines.en: So do you think I should stay here?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: No, non dico questo. Dico solo che il preavviso lo dai una volta sola.
lines.en: No, I'm not saying that. I'm only saying that you hand in your notice only once.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Hai ragione. Chiedo il contratto e poi decido con calma.
lines.en: You're right. I'll ask for the contract and then decide calmly.

days.title: Il lavoro da remoto
days.objective: You can argue for or against remote work by presenting both sides, using da un lato and dall'altro and the congiuntivo after negative opinion verbs.
days.paretoFocus: Presenting two sides before choosing one is the structure the writing module expects in an opinion text.
drills: Write one sentence about your own week that opens with da un lato and answers itself with dall'altro.
Say two things that are easier at home and two that are easier in an office.
Turn three opinions into the negative form with non credo che plus the congiuntivo.

items: How do you introduce the opposite side of an argument in one sentence?
Which mood follows non credo che, and why?
Name two problems that appear when the desk is in the living room.

activities.title: Lavorare da casa
activities.intro: Connectives let you build a balanced argument: da un lato opens the first side, dall'altro answers it, and invece di and però mark the contrast. Negative opinion verbs such as non credo che make the congiuntivo even more likely.

items.hr: Da un lato il lavoro da remoto fa risparmiare tempo, dall'altro rende più difficile staccare la sera.
items.en: On the one hand remote work saves time, on the other it makes it harder to switch off in the evening.
items.note: da un lato ... dall'altro ... presents two sides

items.hr: Io lavoro meglio a casa, perché in ufficio mi interrompono di continuo.
items.en: I work better at home, because at the office they interrupt me constantly.
items.note: di continuo, in continuazione

items.hr: Invece di venire tutti i giorni, molti preferiscono un modello misto.
items.en: Instead of coming in every day, many prefer a mixed model.
items.note: invece di + infinito

items.hr: Non credo che il lavoro da remoto vada bene per tutti i mestieri.
items.en: I don't believe remote work is suitable for every trade.
items.note: credere che + congiuntivo: andare -> vada; il mestiere

items.hr: Il confine tra la vita privata e il lavoro si assottiglia quando la scrivania è in salotto.
items.en: The line between private life and work gets thinner when the desk is in the living room.
items.note: il confine, la scrivania, il salotto

activities.title: A casa o in ufficio
activities.intro: Balanced argument connectives and the congiuntivo, mixed with a question drawn from previous material.

questions.prompt: Non credo che questo modello ___ adatto a tutti.
questions.explanation: Negative verbs of opinion express doubt, so the dependent clause takes the congiuntivo presente sia.

questions.prompt: Quale frase pesa davvero i due lati della questione?
options: Il lavoro da remoto fa risparmiare tempo, però isola chi vive da solo.
Il lavoro da remoto è comodo.
Molti lavorano da casa il venerdì.
questions.answer: Il lavoro da remoto fa risparmiare tempo, però isola chi vive da solo.
questions.explanation: Only this option names an advantage and a disadvantage in the same sentence, joined by però.

questions.prompt: Complete with the connective that introduces the opposite side: Da un lato si risparmia tempo, ___ si lavora troppo la sera.
questions.explanation: Da un lato is answered by dall'altro; the pair signals that a counterweight is coming.
