lines.en: That seems an excellent first step to me, and if you want I will come with you.

days.title: Lo stress e il riposo
days.objective: You can describe what stresses you, what restores you, and argue about how work and rest should be balanced.
days.paretoFocus: Complaining about pressure and defending your time off are two of the most frequent conversations at work.
drills: Write three sentences with 'mi stressa' plus a noun and three with 'mi rilassa'.
Explain in thirty seconds how you switch off after a hard day.
Turn 'I work too much' into a sentence with 'finisco per' plus an infinitive.

items: How do you say 'to take some time for yourself'?
What does 'staccare la spina' mean and when do you use it?
Which construction expresses an unintended end result?

activities.title: Staccare la spina
activities.intro: Verbs of causing a feeling put the person in the object position: 'mi stressa il rumore' literally says the noise stresses me. 'Finire per' plus an infinitive describes an outcome you did not plan. 'Riuscire a' is the everyday verb for managing to do something.

items.hr: Mi stressano di più le riunioni inutili che il carico di lavoro.
items.en: Useless meetings stress me more than the workload does.
items.note: il soggetto è ciò che stressa

items.hr: Se non stacco la spina almeno un giorno, finisco per rendere meno.
items.en: If I do not switch off for at least one day, I end up performing worse.
items.note: staccare la spina, finire per + infinito

items.hr: Non riesco mai a prendermi mezz'ora per me durante la settimana.
items.en: I never manage to take half an hour for myself during the week.

items.hr: Sebbene il riposo sembri tempo perso, in realtà è un investimento.
items.en: Although rest seems like wasted time, in reality it is an investment.
items.note: sebbene + congiuntivo

items.hr: Da quando spengo il telefono la sera, dormo molto meglio.
items.en: Since I started switching my phone off in the evening, I sleep much better.
items.note: da quando + presente

activities.title: Pressione e recupero
activities.intro: Practise saying what wears you out and what puts you back together.

questions.prompt: Choose the correct sentence for 'Noise stresses me a lot.'
questions.explanation: The thing causing the feeling is the subject, so the verb agrees with 'il rumore' and the person appears as the pronoun 'mi'.

questions.prompt: What does 'staccare la spina' mean in everyday speech?
questions.explanation: Literally 'to unplug', the idiom means to cut off completely from work or obligations for a while.

questions.prompt: Complete the verb: 'Non ___ a dormire più di cinque ore per notte.'
questions.explanation: 'Riuscire a' plus an infinitive is the standard way to say you manage or fail to do something.

questions.prompt: Complete with the present subjunctive: 'Sebbene il riposo ___ tempo perso, migliora il rendimento.'
questions.explanation: 'Sebbene' governs the subjunctive; 'sembrare' gives 'sembri' in the third person singular.

questions.prompt: Build the Italian for: 'If I do not rest, I end up making mistakes.'
questions.explanation: A real condition uses 'se' plus the present indicative, and 'finire per' introduces the unintended outcome.

activities.title: Il weekend non basta
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. Your colleague thinks working weekends is normal; you push back and defend rest.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Ho lavorato anche sabato e domenica, ma ormai ci ho fatto l'abitudine.
lines.en: I worked on Saturday and Sunday too, but by now I have got used to it.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Sinceramente non credo che sia una cosa di cui andare fieri.
lines.en: Honestly I do not think it is something to be proud of.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Nel nostro settore funziona così. Chi si ferma resta indietro.
lines.en: In our sector that is how it works. Whoever stops falls behind.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Sebbene la pressione sia reale, dopo tre settimane senza pausa rendo molto meno.
lines.en: Although the pressure is real, after three weeks with no break I perform much worse.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Sarà, ma quando stacco mi sento in colpa. Mi sembra di rubare tempo.
lines.en: Maybe, but when I switch off I feel guilty. It feels like I am stealing time.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Quel senso di colpa lo conosco bene, però l'ho perso quando ho visto i risultati.
lines.en: I know that guilt well, but I lost it when I saw the results.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Cioè lavorando di meno hai concluso di più? Fatico a crederci.
lines.en: So by working less you got more done? I find that hard to believe.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Provaci per un mese e poi ne riparliamo. Se sbaglio, ti pago il caffè.
lines.en: Try it for a month and then we will talk about it again. If I am wrong, I will buy you a coffee.

days.title: Lo sport e le abitudini
days.objective: You can describe a habit you are trying to build and argue about what actually makes exercise stick.
days.paretoFocus: Habit language transfers to every other goal you will discuss in Italian, from study to money.
drills: Write three sentences with 'da quando' plus a change you made.
Describe your week of movement using 'un paio di volte', 'ogni tanto' and 'regolarmente'.
Write one sentence with 'basta che' plus the subjunctive.

items: How do you say 'to get into the habit of' something?
Which mood follows 'basta che' and 'purché'?
Give three frequency expressions between 'mai' and 'sempre'.

activities.title: Prendere l'abitudine
activities.intro: Habits are described with 'prendere l'abitudine di' and lost with 'perdere l'abitudine di'. Minimal conditions use 'basta che' or 'purché' with the subjunctive. 'Ci vuole' plus a noun says what something requires.

items.hr: Ho preso l'abitudine di camminare mezz'ora prima di cena.
items.en: I have got into the habit of walking for half an hour before dinner.
items.note: prendere l'abitudine di + infinito

items.hr: Basta che tu faccia venti minuti al giorno, non serve di più.
items.en: It is enough for you to do twenty minutes a day, there is no need for more.
items.note: basta che + congiuntivo

items.hr: Ci vuole costanza, non forza di volontà eroica.
items.en: What it takes is consistency, not heroic willpower.
items.note: ci vuole + nome singolare

items.hr: Mi sono iscritto in palestra a gennaio e ci vado ancora, incredibilmente.
items.en: I joined a gym in January and I still go, incredibly.
items.note: iscriversi in palestra

items.hr: Se lo sport fosse un obbligo, lo odierei come odiavo la ginnastica a scuola.
items.en: If sport were an obligation, I would hate it as I hated gym class at school.

activities.title: Costruire una routine
activities.intro: Practise talking about frequency, conditions and habits that last.

questions.prompt: Choose the correct form: 'Basta che tu ___ tre volte a settimana.'
questions.explanation: 'Basta che' requires the subjunctive; for 'allenarsi' the second person present subjunctive is 'ti alleni'.

questions.prompt: Which expression means 'it takes consistency'?
questions.explanation: 'Ci vuole' plus a singular noun expresses what is needed; with a plural noun it becomes 'ci vogliono'.

questions.prompt: Complete the noun: 'Ho preso l'___ di andare a piedi al lavoro.'
questions.explanation: 'Prendere l'abitudine di' is the fixed phrase; 'abitudine' is feminine, so the elided article is 'l'' from 'la'.

questions.prompt: Complete the verb: 'Mi sono ___ in palestra tre mesi fa e non ho ancora smesso.'
questions.explanation: 'Iscriversi' is reflexive and takes 'essere', with the irregular participle 'iscritto' agreeing with a masculine speaker.

questions.prompt: Build the Italian for: 'Twenty minutes a day are enough to feel better.'
questions.explanation: With a plural subject 'bastare' agrees in the plural, and 'per' plus the infinitive expresses purpose.

activities.title: Iniziare e non smettere
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. Your friend believes only intense training counts; you argue for small and sustainable habits.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Se vuoi risultati devi allenarti sul serio, almeno un'ora cinque volte a settimana.
lines.en: If you want results you have to train seriously, at least an hour five times a week.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Non sono d'accordo. Un programma così, io lo abbandono dopo due settimane.
lines.en: I do not agree. A programme like that, I abandon after two weeks.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Allora il problema è la motivazione, non il programma.
lines.en: Then the problem is motivation, not the programme.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Secondo me è il contrario. Basta che l'abitudine sia piccola perché duri.
lines.en: In my opinion it is the opposite. The habit only has to be small for it to last.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Venti minuti però non cambiano niente. È quasi tempo sprecato.
lines.en: Twenty minutes change nothing though. It is almost wasted time.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Cambiano tutto rispetto a zero, e da quando cammino ogni sera dormo meglio.
lines.en: They change everything compared to zero, and since I started walking every evening I sleep better.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Su questo ti do ragione, il confronto giusto non è con l'atleta.
lines.en: On that I agree with you, the right comparison is not with the athlete.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Esatto. Prima costruisci l'abitudine, poi semmai aumenti l'intensità.
lines.en: Exactly. First you build the habit, then if anything you increase the intensity.

days.title: L'invecchiamento e la cura degli anziani
days.objective: You can discuss ageing and elder care and weigh family care against professional care.
days.paretoFocus: Italy has one of the oldest populations in Europe, so this subject comes up in ordinary conversation constantly.
drills: Write three sentences about your grandparents using the imperfect for habits.
Say how you would organise care for a relative who can no longer live alone.
Write one sentence with 'occuparsi di' and one with 'prendersi cura di'.

items: What is the difference between 'occuparsi di' and 'prendersi cura di'?
How do you say 'to grow old' and 'to get by on your own'?
Which tense narrates what someone used to do regularly in the past?

activities.title: Invecchiare bene
activities.intro: 'Occuparsi di' means to handle the practical side, while 'prendersi cura di' carries warmth and responsibility. 'Cavarsela' means to manage or get by. Age is described with 'anziano', which is neutral and respectful, not with 'vecchio' for people.

items.hr: Mia nonna ha ottantasette anni e se la cava benissimo da sola.
items.en: My grandmother is eighty-seven and gets by perfectly well on her own.
items.note: cavarsela = riuscire a gestire

items.hr: Mi occupo io delle pratiche, mentre mia sorella si prende cura di lei ogni giorno.
items.en: I handle the paperwork, while my sister takes care of her every day.

items.hr: Da giovane mia madre lavorava e cresceva tre figli contemporaneamente.
items.en: When she was young my mother worked and raised three children at the same time.
items.note: imperfetto per abitudini passate

items.hr: Non è detto che una casa di riposo sia una scelta di comodo.
items.en: It is not necessarily true that a care home is a convenient way out.
items.note: non è detto che + congiuntivo

items.hr: Invecchiare non significa diventare inutili, significa cambiare ritmo.
items.en: Growing old does not mean becoming useless, it means changing pace.

activities.title: Chi si prende cura di chi
activities.intro: Practise the vocabulary of care and the tenses of remembered habits.

questions.prompt: Which verb expresses emotional responsibility for a person rather than practical tasks?
questions.explanation: 'Prendersi cura di' carries affection and ongoing responsibility; the others are more administrative or practical.

questions.prompt: Choose the correct form: 'Non è detto che tutti gli anziani ___ bisogno di assistenza.'
questions.explanation: 'Non è detto che' expresses uncertainty and therefore requires the subjunctive 'abbiano'.

questions.prompt: Complete the verb: 'Mio nonno ___ novant'anni e vive ancora nella sua casa.'
questions.explanation: Italian expresses age with 'avere' plus the number of years, unlike English which uses 'to be'.

questions.prompt: Complete with the imperfect: 'Quando ero piccolo, mia nonna mi ___ una favola ogni sera.'
questions.explanation: The imperfect describes a repeated habit in the past, in contrast with the passato prossimo for single completed events.

questions.prompt: Build the Italian for: 'Growing old does not mean giving up on your independence.'
questions.explanation: 'Rinunciare a' takes the preposition 'a', which contracts with 'la' to give 'alla'.

activities.title: Una decisione difficile in famiglia
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. Two siblings weigh home care against a care home, and neither option is presented as obviously right.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Papà non può più stare da solo. Secondo me dobbiamo pensare a una struttura.
lines.en: Dad cannot live alone any more. In my opinion we have to consider a facility.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Mi fa male sentirlo, però capisco che qualcosa vada deciso.
lines.en: It hurts me to hear it, but I understand that something has to be decided.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Io lavoro a duecento chilometri e non riesco a esserci ogni giorno.
lines.en: I work two hundred kilometres away and I cannot be there every day.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Nemmeno io. Ma prima di decidere, proviamo con qualcuno che venga a casa la mattina?
lines.en: Neither can I. But before deciding, shall we try with someone who comes to the house in the morning?

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: E la notte? Se cadesse alle tre, chi se ne accorgerebbe?
lines.en: And at night? If he fell at three in the morning, who would notice?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Hai ragione, è il punto più debole della mia proposta.
lines.en: You are right, that is the weakest point of my proposal.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Non è detto che una struttura sia un abbandono. Alcune sono ottime.
lines.en: It is not necessarily true that a facility means abandonment. Some are excellent.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Allora andiamo a vederne due insieme, e chiediamo a lui cosa preferisce.
lines.en: Then let us go and see two of them together, and ask him what he prefers.

days.title: Il sistema scolastico italiano
days.objective: You can describe how Italian schooling is organised from primary school to the final exam and give an opinion on it.
days.paretoFocus: If you have children or colleagues with children, these terms come up weekly and have no easy English equivalent.
drills: Write the stages of school from age six to age nineteen with their Italian names.
Explain in one minute the difference between a liceo and a technical or vocational school.
Write three sentences comparing this system with the one you grew up with.

items: What are 'la scuola primaria' and 'la scuola secondaria di primo grado'?
How do you say 'to enrol a child in a school'?
Which comparative structures do you use for two systems?

activities.title: Dalla primaria alla maturità
activities.intro: Italian schooling runs from 'la scuola dell'infanzia' through 'la scuola primaria', 'la secondaria di primo grado' and 'la secondaria di secondo grado', which ends with the state exam commonly called 'la maturità'. Comparisons use 'rispetto a' and 'a differenza di'.

items.hr: La scuola primaria dura cinque anni e comincia a sei anni di età.
items.en: Primary school lasts five years and begins at the age of six.
items.note: la scuola primaria, femminile

items.hr: Dopo la secondaria di primo grado si sceglie fra il liceo, l'istituto tecnico e quello professionale.
items.en: After lower secondary school you choose between the liceo, the technical institute and the vocational one.

items.hr: L'esame di Stato alla fine delle superiori viene chiamato ancora maturità da quasi tutti.
items.en: The state exam at the end of upper secondary is still called 'maturità' by almost everyone.
items.note: passivo: viene chiamato

items.hr: Rispetto ad altri paesi, qui si studiano più materie umanistiche.
items.en: Compared with other countries, more humanities subjects are studied here.
items.note: rispetto a + nome

items.hr: Ho iscritto mia figlia alla scuola del quartiere perché volevo che avesse i compagni vicino.
items.en: I enrolled my daughter at the local school because I wanted her to have her classmates nearby.
items.note: volevo che + congiuntivo imperfetto

activities.title: Orientarsi fra i cicli scolastici
activities.intro: Practise the names of the school stages and the language of comparison.

questions.prompt: Which stage comes immediately after 'la scuola primaria'?
options: la scuola secondaria di primo grado
questions.answer: la scuola secondaria di primo grado
questions.explanation: Lower secondary school follows the five years of primary school and lasts three years.

questions.prompt: Choose the correct comparison: '___ altri sistemi, qui si sceglie l'indirizzo a quattordici anni.'
questions.explanation: 'Rispetto a' is the standard comparative phrase, and 'a' becomes 'ad' before a word starting with a vowel.

questions.prompt: Complete with the imperfect subjunctive: 'Volevo che mio figlio ___ una scuola vicino a casa.'
questions.explanation: A past main verb of wishing takes the imperfect subjunctive in the subordinate clause.

questions.prompt: Complete the verb: 'Il mese scorso ho ___ mia figlia alla prima classe.'
questions.explanation: 'Iscrivere qualcuno a' means to enrol someone; the participle 'iscritto' is irregular and stays unchanged with 'avere' here.

questions.prompt: Build the Italian for: 'The final exam is still called maturity by everyone.'
questions.explanation: In a passive with 'venire' the agent is introduced by 'da', and the participle agrees with the masculine singular subject.

activities.title: Quale scuola dopo le medie?
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. A parent assumes the liceo is always the better choice; you argue that it depends on the student.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Mio figlio farà il liceo, ovviamente. Le altre scuole chiudono delle porte.
lines.en: My son will do the liceo, obviously. The other schools close doors.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Non ne sarei così sicuro. Dipende molto da cosa gli piace fare.
lines.en: I would not be so sure. It depends a lot on what he enjoys doing.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Ma con un istituto tecnico poi all'università fa più fatica.
lines.en: But with a technical institute he then struggles more at university.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Può succedere, però conosco ragazzi usciti dal tecnico che se la cavano benissimo.
lines.en: That can happen, but I know young people from technical schools who manage very well.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Sarà, ma se sbaglia scelta a quattordici anni poi è complicato tornare indietro.
lines.en: Maybe, but if he chooses wrong at fourteen it is then complicated to go back.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Questo è vero, ed è il difetto più serio del sistema secondo me.
lines.en: That is true, and it is the most serious flaw in the system in my opinion.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Quindi cosa faresti al posto mio?
lines.en: So what would you do in my place?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Chiederei a lui, e visiterei due scuole diverse prima di decidere.
lines.en: I would ask him, and I would visit two different schools before deciding.

days.title: L'università e gli studi
days.objective: You can talk about university life, degrees and exams, and defend a view on what a degree is really for.
days.paretoFocus: Study vocabulary is needed for recognising qualifications, writing applications and explaining your own background.
drills: Describe your own studies in five sentences using the passato prossimo.
Write three sentences with 'laurearsi', 'iscriversi' and 'dare un esame'.
Prepare one argument for and one against studying purely for employment.

items: What is the difference between 'la laurea triennale' and 'la laurea magistrale'?
How do you say 'to sit an exam' and 'to pass an exam'?
Which structure introduces a purpose clause with a different subject?

activities.title: La laurea serve ancora?
activities.intro: Italian says 'dare un esame' for sitting one and 'superare un esame' for passing it. Degrees are 'la laurea triennale' and then 'la laurea magistrale'. Purpose clauses with a different subject use 'affinché' or 'perché' plus the subjunctive.

items.hr: Mi sono laureato in economia dopo aver dato ventuno esami.
items.en: I graduated in economics after sitting twenty-one exams.
items.note: laurearsi in + materia

items.hr: La laurea triennale dura tre anni, poi si può proseguire con la magistrale.
items.en: The first degree lasts three years, then you can continue with the master's.

items.hr: Studio la sera affinché i miei figli mi vedano fare qualcosa di serio.
items.en: I study in the evening so that my children see me doing something serious.
items.note: affinché + congiuntivo

items.hr: Non credo che un titolo garantisca un buon lavoro, ma apre delle porte.
items.en: I do not believe a qualification guarantees a good job, but it opens doors.

items.hr: Ho superato l'esame più difficile al secondo tentativo, e non me ne vergogno.
items.en: I passed the hardest exam on the second attempt, and I am not ashamed of it.

activities.title: Esami, lauree e scelte
activities.intro: Practise the verbs and nouns of higher education.

questions.prompt: Which sentence correctly means 'I sat three exams in June'?
options: Ho dato tre esami a giugno.
Ho preso tre esami a giugno.
Ho fatto tre esami di giugno.
Ho passato per tre esami a giugno.
questions.answer: Ho dato tre esami a giugno.
questions.explanation: Italian uses 'dare un esame' for sitting one; 'superare' would mean passing it.

questions.prompt: Choose the correct purpose clause: 'Lavoro di notte ___ mia figlia possa studiare.'
questions.explanation: 'Affinché' introduces a purpose clause with a different subject and is followed by the subjunctive.

questions.prompt: Complete the verb: 'Mi sono ___ in ingegneria tre anni fa.'
questions.explanation: 'Laurearsi' is reflexive and takes 'essere', so the participle agrees with a masculine speaker.

questions.prompt: Complete with the present subjunctive: 'Non credo che un titolo ___ sufficiente da solo.'
questions.explanation: 'Non credo che' expresses doubt and therefore requires the subjunctive of 'essere'.

questions.prompt: Build the Italian for: 'I study in the evening so that my children see the effort.'
questions.explanation: The purpose clause needs the subjunctive 'vedano' because its subject differs from the subject of the main verb.

activities.title: Vale la pena laurearsi?
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. Your friend says university is a waste of years; you weigh both sides seriously.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Onestamente, cinque anni di università oggi non servono a niente.
lines.en: Honestly, five years of university today are of no use at all.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Dipende da cosa ci si aspetta. Se cerchi solo uno stipendio alto, forse hai ragione.
lines.en: It depends on what you expect. If you are only looking for a high salary, maybe you are right.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Conosco laureati che guadagnano meno di un tecnico specializzato.
lines.en: I know graduates who earn less than a specialised technician.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: È vero, però lo studio mi ha insegnato a leggere un testo difficile senza spaventarmi.
lines.en: That is true, but studying taught me to read a difficult text without getting scared.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Quello lo impari anche da solo, senza pagare le tasse universitarie.
lines.en: You can learn that on your own too, without paying tuition fees.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: In teoria sì, in pratica quasi nessuno lo fa con quella costanza.
lines.en: In theory yes, in practice almost nobody does it with that consistency.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Ammetto che una struttura aiuta. Ma il costo resta altissimo per molte famiglie.
lines.en: I admit that a structure helps. But the cost remains very high for many families.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Su questo sono d'accordo, e infatti il problema non è la laurea ma chi può permettersela.
lines.en: On that I agree, and in fact the problem is not the degree but who can afford it.

days.title: La formazione continua
days.objective: You can talk about learning new skills as an adult and argue about who should pay for retraining.
days.paretoFocus: Adult learners retrain constantly, and this is the language of courses, certificates and job changes.
drills: Write three sentences about a skill you learned after leaving school.
Say aloud what you would like to learn in the next two years and why.
Write one sentence with 'nel caso in cui' plus the subjunctive.

items: How do you say 'to retrain' and 'to update your skills'?
Which subjunctive follows 'nel caso in cui'?
Give two ways to say 'it is never too late'.

activities.title: Imparare da adulti
activities.intro: 'Aggiornarsi' means keeping your skills current, and 'riqualificarsi' means retraining for a different job. Hypothetical circumstances use 'nel caso in cui' with the subjunctive. Notice that 'un corso' is followed by 'di' plus the subject.

items.hr: Ho seguito un corso di contabilità mentre lavoravo a tempo pieno.
items.en: I took an accountancy course while I was working full time.
items.note: seguire un corso di

items.hr: Chi non si aggiorna rischia di trovarsi fuori dal mercato in pochi anni.
items.en: Anyone who does not keep up to date risks being out of the market within a few years.

items.hr: Nel caso in cui l'azienda chiudesse, vorrei avere già una seconda competenza.
items.en: In the event that the company closed, I would like to already have a second skill.
items.note: nel caso in cui + congiuntivo

items.hr: Non è mai troppo tardi per riqualificarsi, anche a cinquant'anni.
items.en: It is never too late to retrain, even at fifty.

items.hr: Penso che la formazione dovrebbe essere pagata almeno in parte dal datore di lavoro.
items.en: I think training should be paid for at least partly by the employer.
items.note: passivo con essere + da

activities.title: Corsi, competenze e certificati
activities.intro: Practise talking about training and hypothetical career changes.

questions.prompt: Which verb means to train for a different profession?
questions.explanation: 'Riqualificarsi' is a change of profession, while 'aggiornarsi' is keeping the same job current.

questions.prompt: Choose the correct form: 'Nel caso in cui il corso ___ troppo, chiederei un rimborso.'
questions.explanation: 'Nel caso in cui' takes the subjunctive, and the conditional main verb calls for the imperfect subjunctive.

questions.prompt: Complete the verb: 'L'anno scorso ho ___ un corso serale di informatica.'
questions.explanation: Italian says 'seguire un corso', not 'fare un corso' in careful usage; the participle with 'avere' stays unchanged.

questions.prompt: Complete the reflexive verb: 'Chi non si ___ resta indietro molto in fretta.'
questions.explanation: 'Aggiornarsi' in the third person present is 'si aggiorna', used here after the relative pronoun 'chi'.

questions.prompt: Build the Italian for: 'It is never too late to learn a new profession.'
questions.explanation: The double negative 'non ... mai' is obligatory in Italian, and 'per' plus the infinitive gives the purpose.

activities.title: Chi paga la formazione?
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. Your colleague says training is the worker's own problem; you argue that responsibility is shared.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Se vuoi imparare qualcosa di nuovo, paghi tu. L'azienda non è una scuola.
lines.en: If you want to learn something new, you pay. The company is not a school.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Capisco il ragionamento, però le competenze servono prima di tutto all'azienda.
lines.en: I understand the reasoning, but the skills are useful to the company first of all.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: E se poi te ne vai da un'altra parte? Hanno pagato per il concorrente.
lines.en: And what if you then leave for somewhere else? They have paid for the competitor.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: È un rischio reale, non lo nego. Ma chi non forma nessuno perde comunque le persone.
lines.en: That is a real risk, I do not deny it. But whoever trains nobody loses their people anyway.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Nel caso in cui l'azienda pagasse, almeno chiederei un impegno di due anni.
lines.en: In the event that the company paid, I would at least ask for a two-year commitment.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Questa mi sembra una via di mezzo ragionevole, ci avevo pensato anch'io.
lines.en: That seems a reasonable middle way to me, I had thought of it too.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Vedi che alla fine non siamo così lontani?
lines.en: You see that in the end we are not so far apart?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: No, discutiamo su chi paga, non sul fatto che aggiornarsi sia necessario.
lines.en: No, we are arguing about who pays, not about the fact that keeping up to date is necessary.

days.title: Le disuguaglianze nell'istruzione
days.objective: You can describe why children start school from different places and argue about what would reduce the gap.
days.paretoFocus: Talking about fairness and opportunity is a core exam topic and a frequent subject among parents and colleagues.
drills: Write three sentences with 'avere accesso a' about different resources.
Explain in a minute one concrete measure that could help pupils who fall behind.
Write one sentence with 'se avessi' plus the conditional.

items: How do you say 'to drop out of school' in Italian?
Which tense pair builds the second conditional?
Give two nouns for the gap between people's starting points.

activities.title: Partire da posizioni diverse
activities.intro: 'Il divario' and 'la disuguaglianza' both name a gap, the first more neutral and statistical. 'Abbandonare la scuola' is the standard phrase for dropping out. The second conditional pairs 'se' plus the imperfect subjunctive with the present conditional.

items.hr: Non tutti i bambini partono dallo stesso punto, e la scuola lo sa bene.
items.en: Not all children start from the same point, and schools know this well.

items.hr: Il divario fra le zone del paese si vede già alla scuola primaria.
items.en: The gap between areas of the country is already visible in primary school.

items.hr: Se le famiglie avessero più sostegno, meno ragazzi abbandonerebbero la scuola.
items.en: If families had more support, fewer young people would drop out of school.

items.hr: Chi ha in casa dei libri e qualcuno che lo aiuta con i compiti parte avvantaggiato.
items.en: Someone who has books at home and somebody to help with homework starts at an advantage.

items.hr: Credo che il tempo pieno sia lo strumento più efficace contro la dispersione scolastica.
items.en: I believe that full-time schooling is the most effective tool against early school leaving.
items.note: la dispersione scolastica = abbandono precoce

activities.title: Opportunità e ostacoli
activities.intro: Practise describing gaps and imagining what would change things.

questions.prompt: Which phrase means 'to leave school before finishing'?
questions.explanation: 'Abbandonare la scuola' is the fixed expression for dropping out; the others describe simply leaving the building.

questions.prompt: Choose the correct pairing: 'Se le scuole ___ più fondi, offrirebbero più attività.'
questions.explanation: In a second conditional the 'se' clause takes the imperfect subjunctive and the main clause the present conditional.

questions.prompt: Complete the noun: 'Il ___ tra nord e sud si nota già nei primi anni di scuola.'
questions.explanation: 'Il divario' is the usual noun for a measurable gap between groups or areas.

questions.prompt: Complete with the present conditional: 'Con più insegnanti di sostegno, molti alunni ___ risultati migliori.'
questions.explanation: 'Ottenere' forms its conditional on the stem 'otterr-', giving 'otterrebbero' in the third person plural.

questions.prompt: Build the Italian for: 'Not all pupils start from the same point.'
questions.explanation: 'Partire da' contracts with the article to give 'dallo' before a masculine noun beginning with s plus consonant.

activities.title: Merito o fortuna?
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. Your friend believes results depend only on effort; you argue that starting conditions matter too.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Alla fine chi studia ce la fa. Il resto sono scuse.
lines.en: In the end whoever studies makes it. The rest are excuses.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Lo studio conta moltissimo, però non tutti studiano nelle stesse condizioni.
lines.en: Studying counts enormously, but not everyone studies in the same conditions.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Io venivo da una famiglia semplice e me la sono cavata lo stesso.
lines.en: I came from a simple family and I managed all the same.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Ti faccio i complimenti sinceramente. Ma un caso non basta a descrivere un sistema.
lines.en: I sincerely congratulate you. But one case is not enough to describe a system.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Allora secondo te il merito non esiste?
lines.en: So in your opinion merit does not exist?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Esiste, ma pesa di più quando tutti hanno accesso alle stesse risorse.
lines.en: It exists, but it counts for more when everyone has access to the same resources.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Su questo posso essere d'accordo. Servirebbero più aiuti nei primi anni.
lines.en: On that I can agree. More help would be needed in the early years.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Esatto, perché se un bambino resta indietro a otto anni, difficilmente recupera dopo.
lines.en: Exactly, because if a child falls behind at eight, they rarely catch up later.

days.title: L'immigrazione e l'integrazione
days.objective: You can tell your own story of arriving, settling and building a life, and say plainly what has been hard and what has been good.
days.paretoFocus: This is your own biography, and being able to tell it fluently is worth more than any other single conversation in Italian.
drills: Tell the story of your arrival in five sentences using the passato prossimo.
Write three things that felt strange at first and now feel normal.
Write one sentence about something you miss and one about something you have gained.

items: How do you say 'to settle in' and 'to get used to' something?
Which preposition follows 'abituarsi'?
Give two ways to say 'I have built a life here'.

activities.title: Costruirsi una vita qui
activities.intro: 'Trasferirsi' is to move to another place, 'ambientarsi' is to settle in and 'abituarsi a' is to get used to something. Talking about your own path, the passato prossimo narrates the events and the imperfect describes how things felt.

items.hr: Mi sono trasferito qui sei anni fa e all'inizio non capivo quasi niente.
items.en: I moved here six years ago and at the beginning I understood almost nothing.
items.note: passato prossimo per l'evento, imperfetto per lo stato

items.hr: Mi sono ambientato prima al lavoro che nel quartiere, stranamente.
items.en: I settled in at work before I did in the neighbourhood, strangely enough.

items.hr: Mi sono abituato agli orari, ma alla burocrazia non mi abituerò mai.
items.en: I have got used to the timetables, but I will never get used to the bureaucracy.
items.note: abituarsi a + nome

items.hr: Mi manca il rumore della mia città, però qui ho costruito qualcosa di mio.
items.en: I miss the noise of my city, but here I have built something of my own.
items.note: mi manca + soggetto

items.hr: Sono stanco di dover spiegare da dove vengo prima di poter dire cosa faccio.
items.en: I am tired of having to explain where I come from before I can say what I do.

activities.title: Raccontare il proprio percorso
activities.intro: Practise narrating your own move and the adjustments that followed.

questions.prompt: Choose the correct preposition: 'Mi sono abituato ___ orari dei negozi.'
questions.explanation: 'Abituarsi' governs 'a', which contracts with the plural masculine article 'gli' to give 'agli'.

questions.prompt: Which verb means 'to settle in' to a new place?
questions.explanation: 'Trasferirsi' is the physical move; 'ambientarsi' is the process of feeling at home afterwards.

questions.prompt: Complete the verb: 'Mi ___ molto i sapori della mia infanzia.' (the verb agrees with 'i sapori')
questions.explanation: In Italian the thing missed is the subject, so with a plural subject like 'i sapori' the verb takes the plural form.

questions.prompt: Complete with the passato prossimo: 'Cinque anni fa mi sono ___ in questa città con due valigie.'
questions.explanation: 'Trasferirsi' is reflexive and takes 'essere', so the participle agrees with a masculine speaker.

questions.prompt: Build the Italian for: 'Here I have built something of my own.'
questions.explanation: 'Qualcosa' is followed by 'di' plus a masculine singular adjective or possessive.

activities.title: Da quanto tempo sei qui?
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. A neighbour asks the usual questions; you answer warmly but also say what wears you down.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Parli benissimo. Da quanto tempo vivi in Italia?
lines.en: You speak really well. How long have you been living in Italy?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Da sei anni. Grazie, anche se ormai non lo sento più come un complimento.
lines.en: For six years. Thank you, although by now I no longer feel it as a compliment.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Scusa, non volevo offenderti. Lo dicevo con affetto.
lines.en: Sorry, I did not mean to offend you. I said it affectionately.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Lo so, e ti ringrazio. È solo che dopo sei anni vorrei parlare del mio lavoro, non della mia pronuncia.
lines.en: I know, and I thank you. It is just that after six years I would like to talk about my work, not my pronunciation.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Hai ragione. Allora dimmi, ti sei ambientato qui in quartiere?
lines.en: You are right. So tell me, have you settled in here in the neighbourhood?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Abbastanza. Mi sono abituato ai ritmi, agli uffici un po' meno.
lines.en: Fairly well. I have got used to the rhythms, to the offices rather less.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Su quello ci lamentiamo tutti, credimi. Non è una questione di documenti.
lines.en: We all complain about that, believe me. It is not a question of documents.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Mi fa piacere sentirlo. Alla fine questa città è anche mia, e la difendo quando la criticano.
lines.en: I am glad to hear it. In the end this city is mine too, and I defend it when people criticise it.

days.title: Le generazioni a confronto
days.objective: You can compare how different generations live and work, and argue against easy stereotypes about each of them.
days.paretoFocus: Generational comparison is a favourite topic of Italian conversation and a standard exam prompt.
drills: Write three sentences contrasting 'una volta' with 'oggi'.
Say aloud one thing your parents' generation did better and one thing yours does better.
Write one sentence with 'mentre' expressing contrast rather than time.

items: How do you say 'the previous generation' and 'young people today'?
Which connective introduces a contrast between two groups?
How does the imperfect help you describe how life used to be?

activities.title: Una volta e adesso
activities.intro: Contrast between eras uses the imperfect for the past state and the present for now, joined by 'mentre' or 'invece'. 'Una volta' means 'in the past' rather than 'once'. Be careful with generalisations: soften them with 'in genere' or 'tendenzialmente'.

items.hr: Una volta si restava nella stessa azienda trent'anni, mentre oggi si cambia spesso.
items.en: In the past people stayed in the same company for thirty years, whereas today people change often.

items.hr: I miei genitori compravano casa a trent'anni, io a trentacinque pago ancora l'affitto.
items.en: My parents bought a house at thirty, at thirty-five I am still paying rent.

items.hr: Tendenzialmente i più giovani danno più peso al tempo libero, e non li biasimo.
items.en: Younger people generally give more weight to free time, and I do not blame them.

items.hr: Non sopporto quando si dice che una generazione intera sia pigra.
items.en: I cannot stand it when people say that an entire generation is lazy.
items.note: si dice che + congiuntivo

items.hr: Ogni generazione ha avuto le sue difficoltà, solo che erano di tipo diverso.
items.en: Every generation has had its difficulties, it is just that they were of a different kind.

activities.title: Confrontare epoche diverse
activities.intro: Practise contrasting past and present without falling into clichés.

questions.prompt: Which connective sets two contrasting situations side by side?
questions.explanation: Besides its time meaning, 'mentre' introduces a contrast between two simultaneous states of affairs.

questions.prompt: Choose the correct tense: 'Una volta ___ molto meno e si viveva più lentamente.'
questions.explanation: 'Una volta' introduces a habitual past situation, which Italian expresses with the imperfect.

questions.prompt: Complete with the present subjunctive: 'Non sopporto quando si dice che i giovani ___ pigri.'
questions.explanation: The impersonal 'si dice che' reports an unverified opinion and takes the subjunctive.

questions.prompt: Complete the adverb: 'I miei nonni parlavano ___ meno di sentimenti rispetto a noi.'
questions.explanation: 'Molto' intensifies the comparative 'meno' and is invariable when used as an adverb.

questions.prompt: Build the Italian for: 'Every generation has had its own difficulties.'
questions.explanation: 'Ogni' is always followed by a singular noun, while the possessive agrees with the plural 'difficoltà'.

activities.title: I giovani di oggi
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. An older colleague generalises about young workers; you disagree with evidence rather than slogans.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: I ragazzi di oggi non hanno voglia di lavorare, ai miei tempi era diverso.
lines.en: Young people today do not feel like working, in my day it was different.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Sinceramente non credo che sia una questione di voglia.
lines.en: Honestly I do not think it is a question of willingness.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: E allora come lo spieghi? Cambiano posto ogni due anni.
lines.en: So how do you explain it? They change jobs every two years.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Perché con quegli stipendi restare fedeli a un'azienda non conviene più.
lines.en: Because with those salaries staying loyal to a company is no longer worth it.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Noi però abbiamo aspettato, e alla fine il posto fisso è arrivato.
lines.en: But we waited, and in the end the permanent job arrived.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Mentre voi aspettavate, la casa costava tre stipendi annui. Oggi ne costa dieci.
lines.en: While you were waiting, a house cost three annual salaries. Today it costs ten.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Questo devo ammetterlo, il confronto sui prezzi non regge.
lines.en: I have to admit that, the comparison on prices does not hold up.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Ogni generazione ha le sue difficoltà, solo che non sono le stesse.
lines.en: Every generation has its difficulties, it is just that they are not the same ones.

days.title: La famiglia oggi
days.objective: You can describe different family arrangements in neutral language and argue about what actually makes a family work.
days.paretoFocus: Family words are used constantly, and knowing the neutral modern ones avoids awkwardness.
drills: Describe your household in four sentences without using the word 'normale'.
Write three sentences with 'convivere', 'sposarsi' and 'separarsi'.
Write one sentence with 'nonostante' plus the subjunctive.

items: How do you say 'to live together' without being married?
Which mood follows 'nonostante'?
Give the neutral word for a partner regardless of gender or marriage.

activities.title: Famiglie di ogni forma
activities.intro: 'Il compagno' and 'la compagna' are the neutral words for a long-term partner outside marriage, and 'il partner' is also common. 'Convivere' means to live together as a couple. 'Nonostante' concedes a fact and takes the subjunctive.

items.hr: Conviviamo da otto anni e non sentiamo il bisogno di sposarci.
items.en: We have been living together for eight years and we do not feel the need to get married.
items.note: convivere da + periodo

items.hr: Mia sorella cresce due figli da sola e ci riesce benissimo.
items.en: My sister is raising two children on her own and she is doing it very well.

items.hr: Nonostante siano separati, hanno mantenuto un rapporto civile per i bambini.
items.en: Although they are separated, they have kept a civil relationship for the children.
items.note: nonostante + congiuntivo

items.hr: Ti presento la mia compagna, viviamo insieme in centro.
items.en: Let me introduce my partner, we live together in the city centre.

items.hr: Secondo me una famiglia si riconosce da come si tratta, non da come è composta.
items.en: In my opinion you recognise a family by how its members treat each other, not by who it is made of.

activities.title: Parlare della propria famiglia
activities.intro: Practise describing households in modern, neutral Italian.

questions.prompt: Which verb means 'to live together as a couple without being married'?
questions.explanation: 'Convivere' specifically describes a couple sharing a home; 'abitare' is simply where you live.

questions.prompt: Choose the correct form: 'Nonostante ___ separati, vanno d'accordo.'
questions.explanation: 'Nonostante' introduces a concession and requires the subjunctive, here 'siano'.

questions.prompt: Complete the noun: 'Ti presento la mia ___, stiamo insieme da sei anni.'
questions.explanation: 'La compagna' is the neutral term for a female long-term partner outside marriage.

questions.prompt: Complete the verb: 'Mia zia ___ tre figli da sola dopo la separazione.' (present tense of raising children)
questions.explanation: 'Crescere' is used transitively for raising children as well as intransitively for growing up.

questions.prompt: Build the Italian for: 'We have been living together for eight years.'
questions.explanation: For an action still going on, Italian uses the present tense with 'da', where English uses a perfect.

activities.title: Ma quando vi sposate?
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. A relative presses you about marriage; you hold your position politely but firmly.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Allora, dopo otto anni insieme, quando vi decidete a sposarvi?
lines.en: So, after eight years together, when are you going to decide to get married?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Probabilmente mai, e per noi va benissimo così.
lines.en: Probably never, and for us that is perfectly fine.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Però una famiglia vera ha bisogno di qualcosa di ufficiale, no?
lines.en: But a real family needs something official, does it not?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Non sono d'accordo. Nonostante non ci sia un documento, le responsabilità sono le stesse.
lines.en: I do not agree. Although there is no document, the responsibilities are the same.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: E se un giorno uno dei due sta male? Ci sono questioni pratiche.
lines.en: And what if one day one of you falls ill? There are practical matters.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Questa è un'obiezione seria e ci abbiamo pensato, infatti ci siamo tutelati.
lines.en: That is a serious objection and we have thought about it, in fact we have protected ourselves.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Va bene, allora è una scelta ragionata e non pigrizia.
lines.en: All right, so it is a considered choice and not laziness.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Esatto. Una famiglia si vede da come ci si prende cura l'uno dell'altro.
lines.en: Exactly. You can tell a family by how the two of you look after each other.

days.title: Le amicizie e le relazioni
days.objective: You can describe how friendships form and change, and argue about what you owe a friend.
days.paretoFocus: Friendship language lets you deepen relationships beyond transactions, which is where real fluency begins.
drills: Write three sentences about how you met three different people.
Explain in a minute what you expect from a close friend.
Write one sentence with 'volerci' about how long something takes.

items: What is the difference between 'un conoscente' and 'un amico'?
How do you say 'we lost touch' and 'we got back in touch'?
Which verb means to fall out with someone?

activities.title: Amici, conoscenti e altro
activities.intro: Italians distinguish sharply between 'un conoscente' and 'un amico', and calling someone a friend too quickly can sound odd. 'Perdersi di vista' means to lose touch and 'litigare' means to fall out. 'Ci vuole' plus a time expression says how long something takes.

items.hr: Ci siamo conosciuti al corso di italiano e siamo diventati amici col tempo.
items.en: We met at the Italian course and we became friends over time.
items.note: conoscersi = incontrarsi la prima volta

items.hr: Ci vuole tempo per fidarsi di qualcuno, almeno per me.
items.en: It takes time to trust someone, at least for me.

items.hr: Dopo il trasloco ci siamo persi di vista, ma l'anno scorso ci siamo risentiti.
items.en: After the move we lost touch, but last year we got back in contact.

items.hr: Abbiamo litigato per una sciocchezza e non ci siamo parlati per mesi.
items.en: We fell out over something silly and did not speak for months.

items.hr: Un vero amico è quello che ti dice la verità anche quando non ti fa piacere.
items.en: A real friend is the one who tells you the truth even when you do not like it.
items.note: quello che = colui che

activities.title: Come nascono le amicizie
activities.intro: Practise reciprocal verbs and the vocabulary of closeness and distance.

questions.prompt: Which expression means 'we lost touch'?
questions.explanation: 'Perdersi di vista' is the fixed reciprocal idiom for losing contact with someone.

questions.prompt: Choose the correct auxiliary: 'Noi due ci ___ conosciuti in palestra.'
questions.explanation: Reciprocal verbs with 'ci' always take 'essere' in compound tenses, and the participle agrees in the plural.

questions.prompt: Complete the noun: 'Non è un amico, è solo un ___ di lavoro.'
questions.explanation: 'Un conoscente' is someone you know without real closeness, a distinction Italian keeps clearly.

questions.prompt: Complete the verb: 'Ci ___ anni per costruire una vera fiducia.' (impersonal expression of duration)
questions.explanation: 'Volerci' agrees with what is needed, so a plural noun like 'anni' requires 'vogliono'.

questions.prompt: Build the Italian for: 'We fell out over something silly.'
questions.explanation: 'Litigare' takes 'avere' and the cause is introduced by 'per'.

activities.title: Che cosa si deve a un amico
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. Your friend thinks honesty always comes first; you argue that timing matters as much as truth.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Gliel'ho detto in faccia che stava sbagliando tutto. Un amico deve essere sincero.
lines.en: I told him to his face that he was getting everything wrong. A friend has to be honest.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Sono d'accordo sul principio, però il momento che hai scelto era pessimo.
lines.en: I agree on the principle, but the moment you chose was terrible.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Quindi avrei dovuto mentire per farlo sentire meglio?
lines.en: So should I have lied to make him feel better?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: No, ma tacere per un giorno non è mentire. È aspettare.
lines.en: No, but keeping quiet for a day is not lying. It is waiting.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Se aspetti troppo poi la verità non serve più a niente.
lines.en: If you wait too long the truth is then of no use at all.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Su questo hai ragione, e ammetto che io a volte rimando troppo.
lines.en: You are right about that, and I admit that sometimes I put it off too long.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Allora forse la differenza è come lo dici, non se lo dici.
lines.en: Then maybe the difference is how you say it, not whether you say it.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Esattamente. Ci vuole sincerità, ma anche un po' di delicatezza.
lines.en: Exactly. It takes honesty, but also a little tact.

days.title: La cortesia e la distanza sociale
days.objective: You can judge when to use Lei and when to use tu, and handle the moment when someone offers the switch.
days.paretoFocus: Getting this wrong is the fastest way to sound rude or distant, and no dictionary tells you the rules.
drills: Rewrite three tu sentences into the Lei form, changing the verb and the pronouns.
Practise saying 'shall we use tu?' and accepting when someone else offers.
List five people in your week and decide which form you would use with each.

items: Which grammatical person does the Lei form actually use?
Who normally offers the switch to tu?
How do you politely stay on Lei if you prefer to?

activities.title: Dare del Lei o del tu
activities.intro: The polite form uses third person singular verbs, so 'Lei come sta?' is literally 'how is she'. The switch to tu is normally offered by the older person, the woman, or whoever holds more authority, and is proposed with 'possiamo darci del tu?'. Formality is stronger in offices and in the south, and looser among younger people and in informal workplaces.

items.hr: Buongiorno, scusi, Lei sa se lo sportello è ancora aperto?
items.en: Good morning, excuse me, do you know if the counter is still open?
items.note: Lei + terza persona singolare

items.hr: Possiamo darci del tu? Mi sembra più comodo.
items.en: Shall we use tu with each other? It seems easier to me.
items.note: darsi del tu

items.hr: Volentieri, ma se preferisce continuo a darLe del Lei.
items.en: Gladly, but if you prefer I will go on using the polite form with you.

items.hr: Con i colleghi ci diamo del tu, con i clienti no.
items.en: With colleagues we use tu, with clients we do not.

items.hr: Anche se molti giovani danno subito del tu, con una persona anziana conviene aspettare.
items.en: Even though many young people use tu straight away, with an older person it is better to wait.

activities.title: Il registro giusto
activities.intro: Practise switching between the polite and familiar forms.

questions.prompt: Which sentence correctly addresses a stranger politely?
questions.explanation: The polite form needs both the imperative 'scusi' and the third person verb 'sa'; mixing the two registers is the classic mistake.

questions.prompt: Who normally proposes moving to the familiar form?
options: la persona più anziana o di grado superiore
questions.answer: la persona più anziana o di grado superiore
questions.explanation: The offer conventionally comes from the person with more age or authority, so the other side waits.

questions.prompt: Complete the polite form: 'Buonasera, ___ ha bisogno di aiuto?'
questions.explanation: The polite subject pronoun is capitalised in careful writing and takes third person singular agreement.

questions.prompt: Complete the phrase: 'Possiamo ___ del tu, se per te va bene?'
questions.explanation: 'Darsi del tu' is reciprocal, so the infinitive carries the pronoun 'ci' after the modal 'possiamo'.

questions.prompt: Build the Italian for: 'With clients we always use the polite form.'
questions.explanation: The fixed expression is 'dare del Lei a qualcuno', with the article 'del' before the pronoun.

activities.title: Diamoci del tu
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. A new senior colleague offers the familiar form; you negotiate the register naturally.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Buongiorno, mi hanno detto che lavoreremo insieme al nuovo progetto.
lines.en: Good morning, I have been told we will be working together on the new project.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Buongiorno a Lei. Sì, comincio lunedì prossimo.
lines.en: Good morning to you. Yes, I start next Monday.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Senta, possiamo darci del tu? Qui in ufficio lo facciamo quasi tutti.
lines.en: Listen, shall we use tu with each other? Here in the office almost all of us do.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Volentieri, grazie. All'inizio però mi scapperà ancora qualche Lei.
lines.en: Gladly, thank you. At the beginning though the polite form will still slip out sometimes.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Nessun problema, capita a tutti. E con i clienti invece restiamo sul formale.
lines.en: No problem, it happens to everyone. With clients, on the other hand, we stay formal.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Mi sembra giusto. Non tutti apprezzano la confidenza immediata.
lines.en: That seems right to me. Not everyone appreciates immediate familiarity.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Esatto, e dipende anche dalla città. Nella mia regione si è più formali.
lines.en: Exactly, and it also depends on the city. In my region people are more formal.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Lo avevo notato. Nel dubbio comincio sempre con il Lei e aspetto.
lines.en: I had noticed. When in doubt I always start with the polite form and wait.

days.title: Le feste e i riti di passaggio
days.objective: You can describe celebrations and life milestones, and say honestly which traditions you keep and which you drop.
days.paretoFocus: Invitations and celebrations are where you get included, so knowing what to say and bring matters.
drills: Write three sentences about a celebration you attended, using the passato prossimo.
Say what you would bring to a lunch, a wedding and a housewarming.
Write one sentence with 'si usa' describing a custom.

items: How do you say 'it is customary to' in Italian?
How do you congratulate someone at a wedding and at a graduation?
Which verb means 'to celebrate' an anniversary?

activities.title: Come si festeggia
activities.intro: Customs are described impersonally with 'si usa' plus an infinitive. Congratulations differ by occasion: 'auguri' is general, 'congratulazioni' rewards an achievement, and 'complimenti' praises the person. 'Festeggiare' takes a direct object without a preposition.

items.hr: Da noi si usa portare un dolce quando si è invitati a pranzo.
items.en: Where I am from it is customary to bring a dessert when you are invited to lunch.
items.note: si usa + infinito

items.hr: Abbiamo festeggiato il diploma di mio figlio con tutta la famiglia.
items.en: We celebrated my son's diploma with the whole family.
items.note: festeggiare qualcosa, senza preposizione

items.hr: A un matrimonio si dice auguri agli sposi, non congratulazioni.
items.en: At a wedding you say 'auguri' to the couple, not 'congratulazioni'.

items.hr: Benché non sia credente, partecipo volentieri alle feste del paese.
items.en: Although I am not a believer, I gladly take part in the town's festivals.

items.hr: Alcune tradizioni le ho lasciate, altre le ho portate con me e le tengo strette.
items.en: Some traditions I have let go, others I brought with me and I hold on to them tightly.

activities.title: Auguri e tradizioni
activities.intro: Practise customs, congratulations and the impersonal si.

questions.prompt: What do you say to someone who has just graduated?
questions.explanation: 'Congratulazioni' rewards a personal achievement, while 'auguri' is for wishes and occasions.

questions.prompt: Choose the correct impersonal form: 'In molte case ___ togliere le scarpe all'ingresso.'
questions.explanation: With a following infinitive the impersonal 'si usa' stays singular regardless of what comes after.

questions.prompt: Complete the verb: 'Ieri abbiamo ___ il compleanno di mia madre in giardino.'
questions.explanation: 'Festeggiare' is a regular are verb taking 'avere', and the participle stays unchanged.

questions.prompt: Complete with the present subjunctive: 'Benché non ___ credente, vado alla processione con i vicini.'
questions.explanation: 'Benché' governs the subjunctive; the first person present of 'essere' is 'sia'.

questions.prompt: Build the Italian for: 'It is customary to bring a dessert.'
questions.explanation: The impersonal construction 'si usa' plus an infinitive describes a general custom.

activities.title: Quali tradizioni tenere
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. A friend thinks big family celebrations are outdated; you defend keeping some of them.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Sinceramente questi pranzi infiniti con venti parenti non li sopporto più.
lines.en: Honestly I cannot stand these endless lunches with twenty relatives any more.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Ti capisco, sono faticosi. Però per me sono l'unico momento in cui ci vediamo tutti.
lines.en: I understand you, they are tiring. But for me they are the only time we all see each other.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Potremmo vederci lo stesso senza cucinare per tre giorni.
lines.en: We could see each other all the same without cooking for three days.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: In teoria sì, in pratica senza un'occasione precisa nessuno si organizza.
lines.en: In theory yes, in practice without a specific occasion nobody organises anything.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Allora inventiamo un'occasione più leggera, senza tutti quei riti.
lines.en: Then let us invent a lighter occasion, without all those rituals.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Su questo posso venirti incontro. Alcune tradizioni le ho lasciate anch'io.
lines.en: On that I can meet you halfway. I have let some traditions go too.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Quali hai tenuto, allora?
lines.en: Which ones have you kept, then?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Quelle in cui si cucina insieme. Il resto si può cambiare senza perdere niente.
lines.en: The ones where we cook together. The rest can be changed without losing anything.

days.title: La storia d'Italia, l'unità e la Repubblica
days.objective: You can give a short factual account of how Italy became one state and then a republic, using past tenses correctly.
days.paretoFocus: These few dates and words come up in citizenship materials, in the news and in everyday references.
drills: Write four sentences about the timeline, each with a year and the passato prossimo.
Say aloud, in one minute, what happened in 1861 and in 1946.
Write one sentence with 'da allora' plus the present tense.

items: What happened in 1861 and what happened in 1946?
How do you say 'to become a republic' and 'to be unified'?
How do you read a year aloud in Italian?

activities.title: Dall'unità alla Repubblica
activities.intro: Before 1861 the peninsula was divided into several states. The Kingdom of Italy was proclaimed in 1861 and Rome became the capital in 1871. After the Second World War, a referendum in 1946 replaced the monarchy with the republic, and the Constitution came into force in 1948. Use the passato prossimo to narrate these events in speech.

items.hr: Prima del 1861 la penisola era divisa in diversi stati.
items.en: Before 1861 the peninsula was divided into several states.
items.note: imperfetto per la situazione

items.hr: Nel 1861 è stato proclamato il Regno d'Italia.
items.en: In 1861 the Kingdom of Italy was proclaimed.
items.note: passivo al passato prossimo

items.hr: Roma è diventata capitale nel 1871, dieci anni dopo l'unità.
items.en: Rome became the capital in 1871, ten years after unification.

items.hr: Nel 1946 gli italiani hanno scelto la repubblica con un referendum.
items.en: In 1946 Italians chose the republic in a referendum.

items.hr: La Costituzione è entrata in vigore nel 1948 e da allora è la legge fondamentale.
items.en: The Constitution came into force in 1948 and since then it has been the fundamental law.
items.note: entrare in vigore

activities.title: Date e passaggi storici
activities.intro: Practise narrating historical events with the right tenses.

questions.prompt: What did the 1946 referendum decide?
options: il passaggio dalla monarchia alla repubblica
questions.answer: il passaggio dalla monarchia alla repubblica
questions.explanation: The 1946 institutional referendum replaced the monarchy with the republic.

questions.prompt: Choose the correct tense: 'Prima dell'unità la penisola ___ divisa in vari stati.'
questions.explanation: A lasting background situation in the past takes the imperfect, not the passato prossimo.

questions.prompt: Complete the participle: 'Roma è ___ capitale nel 1871.'
questions.explanation: 'Diventare' takes 'essere', so the participle agrees with the feminine subject 'Roma'.

questions.prompt: Complete the phrase: 'La Costituzione è entrata in ___ il primo gennaio 1948.'
questions.explanation: 'Entrare in vigore' is the fixed legal expression for a law taking effect.

questions.prompt: Build the Italian for: 'In 1946 Italians chose the republic.'
questions.explanation: Years take the article, giving 'nel 1946', and 'scegliere' has the irregular participle 'scelto'.

activities.title: Quanto conta la storia
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. Your friend thinks history is irrelevant today; you argue that it explains present differences.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Onestamente, cosa ci importa oggi di quello che è successo nell'Ottocento?
lines.en: Honestly, why do we care today about what happened in the nineteenth century?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Perché molte differenze fra le regioni vengono proprio da lì.
lines.en: Because many differences between the regions come precisely from there.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Mi sembra una spiegazione un po' comoda per ogni problema attuale.
lines.en: That seems a rather convenient explanation for every current problem to me.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Hai ragione, non spiega tutto. Però l'unità è arrivata tardi e a pezzi.
lines.en: You are right, it does not explain everything. But unification arrived late and in pieces.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: E la repubblica? Quella è recente, del dopoguerra.
lines.en: And the republic? That is recent, from the postwar period.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Esatto, gli italiani l'hanno scelta nel 1946 dopo anni durissimi.
lines.en: Exactly, Italians chose it in 1946 after extremely hard years.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Quindi il paese come lo conosciamo ha meno di ottant'anni.
lines.en: So the country as we know it is less than eighty years old.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Proprio così, ed è per questo che alcune cose sono ancora in costruzione.
lines.en: Precisely, and that is why some things are still being built.

days.title: La Costituzione e i diritti
days.objective: You can describe what the Constitution is, what its founding articles establish, and talk about rights and duties in neutral language.
days.paretoFocus: Rights vocabulary is needed for citizenship materials, for work situations and for understanding official letters.
drills: Write three sentences with 'avere diritto a' about concrete situations.
Explain in a minute the difference between a right and a duty, with an example of each.
Write one sentence with 'garantire' in the passive.

items: What is the difference between 'un diritto' and 'un dovere'?
Which preposition follows 'avere diritto'?
How do you say 'everyone is equal before the law'?

activities.title: Diritti e doveri
activities.intro: The Constitution is the fundamental law of the Republic. Its opening articles state that Italy is a democratic republic founded on work, that sovereignty belongs to the people, that all citizens have equal social dignity and are equal before the law, and that the Republic recognises the right to work. Rights language uses 'avere diritto a' and passives with 'essere garantito'.

items.hr: La Costituzione è la legge fondamentale della Repubblica italiana.
items.en: The Constitution is the fundamental law of the Italian Republic.

items.hr: Il primo articolo dice che l'Italia è una repubblica democratica fondata sul lavoro.
items.en: The first article says that Italy is a democratic republic founded on work.

items.hr: Tutti i cittadini sono uguali davanti alla legge, senza distinzione di sesso o di religione.
items.en: All citizens are equal before the law, without distinction of sex or religion.

items.hr: Ogni lavoratore ha diritto a un riposo settimanale e a ferie retribuite.
items.en: Every worker has the right to a weekly rest and to paid holidays.
items.note: avere diritto a + nome

items.hr: La libertà di espressione è garantita, ma comporta anche delle responsabilità.
items.en: Freedom of expression is guaranteed, but it also carries responsibilities.
items.note: passivo con essere

activities.title: La legge fondamentale
activities.intro: Practise the language of rights, duties and guarantees.

questions.prompt: What is 'la Costituzione'?
questions.explanation: The Constitution sits above ordinary laws and sets the framework all other laws must respect.

questions.prompt: Choose the correct preposition: 'Ogni dipendente ha diritto ___ ferie retribuite.'
questions.explanation: 'Avere diritto' is always followed by 'a' before a noun, and by 'di' only before an infinitive.

questions.prompt: Complete the noun: 'Votare è un diritto, pagare le tasse è un ___.'
questions.explanation: 'Il dovere' is the obligation that balances 'il diritto' in constitutional language.

questions.prompt: Complete the participle: 'La libertà di associazione è ___ dalla legge.'
questions.explanation: In a passive with 'essere' the participle agrees with the feminine singular subject 'la libertà'.

questions.prompt: Build the Italian for: 'All citizens are equal before the law.'
questions.explanation: 'Davanti a' contracts with the feminine article to give 'alla', and 'uguali' agrees with the plural subject.

activities.title: Un diritto o un privilegio?
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. A colleague treats paid leave as a favour from the employer; you argue it is a right, staying on the level of principles.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Il capo mi ha concesso le ferie ad agosto, sono stato fortunato.
lines.en: The boss granted me holidays in August, I was lucky.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Le ferie non sono una gentilezza, sono un diritto riconosciuto per legge.
lines.en: Holidays are not a kindness, they are a right recognised by law.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Sulla carta sì, ma nella pratica dipende sempre da chi comanda.
lines.en: On paper yes, but in practice it always depends on whoever is in charge.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Il periodo si concorda, questo è vero. Il diritto in sé però non si discute.
lines.en: The period is agreed on, that is true. The right in itself, though, is not up for discussion.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Prova a dirlo a chi ha un contratto precario e ha paura di perderlo.
lines.en: Try saying that to someone on an insecure contract who is afraid of losing it.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Hai toccato il punto vero. Un diritto che non si riesce a esercitare vale poco.
lines.en: You have touched on the real point. A right you cannot exercise is worth little.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Ecco, allora siamo d'accordo più di quanto sembrasse.
lines.en: There you are, so we agree more than it seemed.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Sì, la differenza è fra quello che la legge garantisce e quello che si ottiene davvero.
lines.en: Yes, the difference is between what the law guarantees and what you actually get.

days.title: Le regioni e le differenze locali
days.objective: You can describe how Italy is divided administratively and talk about regional differences without stereotyping.
days.paretoFocus: Many public services are run at regional or municipal level, so knowing who does what saves real time.
drills: Write three sentences naming a region, its capital city and one thing it is known for.
Explain in a minute which office you would go to for a residence certificate.
Write one sentence with 'a seconda di' about variation between places.

items: How many regions does Italy have and what is 'un comune'?
Which phrase expresses 'depending on'?
How do you say 'it varies from region to region'?

activities.title: Regioni, province e comuni
activities.intro: Italy is divided into twenty regions, five of which have a special statute with wider autonomy, and then into provinces and municipalities. Many services, including health, are organised regionally, so rules genuinely differ. 'A seconda di' and 'variare da ... a ...' express that variation.

items.hr: L'Italia è divisa in venti regioni, cinque delle quali a statuto speciale.
items.en: Italy is divided into twenty regions, five of which have a special statute.
items.note: delle quali = pronome relativo

items.hr: Il comune rilascia i certificati di residenza e gestisce i servizi locali.
items.en: The municipality issues residence certificates and runs local services.

items.hr: Le liste di attesa variano da regione a regione, perché la sanità è organizzata a livello regionale.
items.en: Waiting lists vary from region to region, because healthcare is organised at regional level.

items.hr: A seconda di dove vivi, cambiano le tariffe dei trasporti e alcune agevolazioni.
items.en: Depending on where you live, transport fares and some concessions change.
items.note: a seconda di + nome o frase

items.hr: Ogni zona ha la sua cucina e il suo modo di parlare, ed è una ricchezza.
items.en: Every area has its own food and its own way of speaking, and that is a richness.

activities.title: Chi fa che cosa sul territorio
activities.intro: Practise the administrative levels and the language of local variation.

questions.prompt: Which body issues a residence certificate?
questions.explanation: Municipal offices keep the population register, so residence documents are issued there.

questions.prompt: Choose the correct phrase: 'Le tariffe cambiano ___ della zona in cui abiti.'
questions.explanation: The fixed phrase is 'a seconda di', where 'seconda' is feminine and invariable.

questions.prompt: Complete the number: 'L'Italia è divisa in ___ regioni.'
questions.explanation: The number twenty is written 'venti' and stays unchanged before a plural noun.

questions.prompt: Complete the relative pronoun: 'Ci sono cinque regioni, alcune ___ quali hanno un'autonomia particolare.'
questions.explanation: In the formal relative structure 'delle quali' the preposition 'di' combines with the feminine plural article.

questions.prompt: Build the Italian for: 'The rules vary from region to region.'
questions.explanation: The idiom 'da ... a ...' with a bare noun expresses variation across units.

activities.title: Nord, centro e sud
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. A friend reaches for regional clichés; you accept real differences but reject the stereotypes.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Si sa come funziona: al nord si lavora, al sud si vive.
lines.en: Everyone knows how it works: in the north people work, in the south people live.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Questa frase la sento spesso, ma non regge appena guardi i dati.
lines.en: I hear that sentence often, but it does not hold up as soon as you look at the data.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Però qualche differenza esiste davvero, non puoi negarlo.
lines.en: But some differences really do exist, you cannot deny it.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Certo che esistono, soprattutto nei servizi e nelle opportunità di lavoro.
lines.en: Of course they exist, especially in services and in job opportunities.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: E allora dove sta la differenza fra quello che dico io e quello che dici tu?
lines.en: So where is the difference between what I say and what you say?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Tu parli delle persone, io parlo delle condizioni in cui vivono.
lines.en: You are talking about the people, I am talking about the conditions they live in.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Detta così cambia parecchio, lo ammetto.
lines.en: Put like that it changes quite a lot, I admit it.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Le regole variano da regione a regione, e questo spiega molto più del carattere.
lines.en: The rules vary from region to region, and that explains far more than character does.

days.title: La politica e il voto, le basi
days.objective: You can name the main institutions of the Republic, say what each one does, and describe how voting works in practical terms.
days.paretoFocus: Understanding who decides what makes the news comprehensible and is part of the citizenship syllabus.
drills: Write one sentence for each institution saying what it does.
Explain in a minute the difference between the head of state and the head of government.
Write three sentences about the practical steps of voting on election day.

items: What are the two chambers of the Parliament called?
Who is the head of state and who leads the government?
What is 'la scheda elettorale' and what is 'il seggio'?

activities.title: Le istituzioni della Repubblica
activities.intro: The Parliament has two chambers, the Chamber of Deputies and the Senate, and both must approve a law. The President of the Republic is the head of state, elected by the Parliament, with a role of guarantee. The President of the Council of Ministers, commonly called the Prime Minister, leads the government and its ministers.

items.hr: Il Parlamento è composto dalla Camera dei deputati e dal Senato della Repubblica.
items.en: The Parliament is made up of the Chamber of Deputies and the Senate of the Republic.
items.note: essere composto da

items.hr: Una legge deve essere approvata da entrambe le camere per entrare in vigore.
items.en: A law must be approved by both chambers in order to come into force.

items.hr: Il Presidente della Repubblica è il capo dello Stato e ha un ruolo di garanzia.
items.en: The President of the Republic is the head of state and has a role of guarantee.

items.hr: Il Presidente del Consiglio guida il governo, mentre il capo dello Stato non governa.
items.en: The President of the Council leads the government, whereas the head of state does not govern.

items.hr: Il giorno del voto si va al seggio con un documento e la tessera elettorale.
items.en: On voting day you go to the polling station with an identity document and the electoral card.

activities.title: Istituzioni e seggi
activities.intro: Practise naming the institutions and describing the act of voting.

questions.prompt: Which two bodies together form the Parliament?
options: la Camera dei deputati e il Senato
il Senato e il Consiglio dei ministri
la Camera dei deputati e i comuni
questions.answer: la Camera dei deputati e il Senato
questions.explanation: Italy has a bicameral parliament, and both chambers must pass a bill in the same text.

questions.prompt: Who is the head of state?
questions.explanation: The head of state is distinct from the head of government, who is the President of the Council of Ministers.

questions.prompt: Complete the noun: 'La domenica del voto si va al ___ con un documento valido.'
questions.explanation: 'Il seggio elettorale' is the polling station where voting physically takes place.

questions.prompt: Complete the participle: 'Una legge deve essere ___ da entrambe le camere.'
questions.explanation: In the passive the participle agrees with the feminine singular subject 'una legge'.
