questions.prompt: Build the Italian for: 'The head of state does not lead the government.'
questions.explanation: 'Di' plus 'lo' before a masculine noun beginning with s plus consonant gives the contraction 'dello'.

activities.title: Vale la pena votare?
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. A friend says voting changes nothing; you argue for participation without naming any party or side.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Domenica non vado a votare. Tanto non cambia niente.
lines.en: On Sunday I am not going to vote. Nothing changes anyway.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Capisco la delusione, però se non voti decidono gli altri anche per te.
lines.en: I understand the disappointment, but if you do not vote others decide for you as well.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Decidono comunque. Il mio voto è uno su milioni.
lines.en: They decide anyway. My vote is one among millions.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: È vero singolarmente, ma alle comunali qui hanno vinto per poche centinaia di voti.
lines.en: That is true individually, but in the local elections here they won by a few hundred votes.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Ammetto che a livello locale si vede di più il risultato.
lines.en: I admit that at local level you see the result more clearly.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: E poi il comune decide su scuole, trasporti e rifiuti, cose che tocchi ogni giorno.
lines.en: And besides the municipality decides on schools, transport and waste, things you touch every day.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Su questo hai ragione. Forse guardo troppo alla politica nazionale.
lines.en: You are right about that. Maybe I look too much at national politics.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Io la vedo così: votare è il minimo, poi si può fare anche altro.
lines.en: I see it this way: voting is the minimum, then you can do other things too.

days.title: I dialetti e l'italiano standard
days.objective: You can explain what Italian dialects are, recognise that they are separate historical languages rather than bad Italian, and stay confident using standard Italian everywhere.
days.paretoFocus: You are about to meet real speakers who do not sound like a course book, and knowing why protects your confidence.
drills: Write three sentences explaining to a friend back home what a dialect is.
Practise asking someone politely to repeat something in standard Italian.
Note down two local words you have heard and find their standard equivalents.

items: Why is a dialect not simply an accent?
How do you politely ask someone to switch to standard Italian?
Which variety is understood everywhere in the country?

activities.title: Il dialetto non è italiano sbagliato
activities.intro: Italian dialects are not corrupted Italian: they developed in parallel from Latin and several have their own literature. The main families are the northern ones, the central ones, the southern ones and the island ones, and there are also recognised minority languages. Standard Italian, the variety this course teaches, is understood everywhere, which is exactly why we chose it. Everything you say should stay standard, even when you understand something local.

items.hr: Il dialetto non è italiano parlato male, è una lingua con una storia propria.
items.en: A dialect is not badly spoken Italian, it is a language with a history of its own.

items.hr: Nel nord, nel centro, nel sud e nelle isole i dialetti appartengono a famiglie diverse.
items.en: In the north, in the centre, in the south and on the islands the dialects belong to different families.

items.hr: Molte persone usano il dialetto in famiglia e l'italiano standard al lavoro.
items.en: Many people use dialect at home and standard Italian at work.

items.hr: Se non capisco, chiedo con garbo: scusi, me lo può ripetere in italiano?
items.en: If I do not understand, I ask politely: excuse me, could you repeat that in Italian?

items.hr: Ho sentito dire guaglione al posto di ragazzo, e ho capito solo dal contesto.
items.en: I heard 'guaglione' instead of 'ragazzo', and I only understood from the context.
items.note: forma locale citata, equivalente standard: il ragazzo

activities.title: Capire senza scoraggiarsi
activities.intro: Practise talking about linguistic variety while producing standard Italian yourself.

questions.prompt: Which statement about Italian dialects is accurate?
options: Sono lingue sviluppate dal latino accanto all'italiano.
Sono lingue straniere importate di recente.
questions.answer: Sono lingue sviluppate dal latino accanto all'italiano.
questions.explanation: Dialects descend directly from Latin in parallel with the Tuscan variety that became standard Italian, so they are not deviations from it.

questions.prompt: Which variety is understood across the whole country?
questions.explanation: Standard Italian is taught in schools and used in the media nationwide, which is why it is the safe choice everywhere.

questions.prompt: Complete the polite request: 'Scusi, me lo ___ ripetere in italiano, per favore?'
questions.explanation: The polite form uses the third person singular of 'potere', which is 'può' with a grave accent.

questions.prompt: Give the standard equivalent: the local form 'guaglione' corresponds to 'il ___' in standard Italian.
questions.explanation: The standard noun is 'il ragazzo'; quoting a local form is fine, but your own production should stay standard.

questions.prompt: Build the Italian for: 'Many people speak dialect at home and Italian at work.'
questions.explanation: 'Parlare in dialetto' takes 'in' before the name of a variety when the manner of speaking is meant.

activities.title: Non capisco quando parlate così
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. A neighbour slips into dialect and then dismisses it as worthless; you ask for standard Italian and defend the dialect at the same time.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Ieri al mercato ho comprato le verdure e poi, uè, ho incontrato tuo cugino.
lines.en: Yesterday at the market I bought the vegetables and then, hey, I met your cousin.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Scusi, l'ultima parte non l'ho capita. Me la può ripetere più lentamente?
lines.en: Sorry, I did not catch the last part. Could you repeat it more slowly?

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Colpa mia, mi scappa il dialetto. Tanto è una parlata che non serve a niente.
lines.en: My fault, the dialect slips out. It is a way of speaking that is useless anyway.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Non sono d'accordo. Il dialetto è una lingua vera, con secoli di storia dietro.
lines.en: I do not agree. Dialect is a real language, with centuries of history behind it.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: A scuola però ci dicevano che parlarlo era un difetto.
lines.en: At school though they used to tell us that speaking it was a defect.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Lo so, e mi sembra un peccato. Io intanto studio l'italiano standard perché mi capiscono ovunque.
lines.en: I know, and it seems a shame to me. Meanwhile I study standard Italian because people understand me everywhere.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Su questo hai ragione, per lavorare in tutto il paese serve quello.
lines.en: You are right about that, to work all over the country that is what you need.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Esatto, le due cose non sono in conflitto. Anzi, mi piacerebbe imparare qualche parola della sua.
lines.en: Exactly, the two things are not in conflict. On the contrary, I would like to learn a few words of yours.

days.title: L'italiano formale e informale
days.objective: You can choose between colloquial and formal vocabulary and recognise which words belong to spoken speech only.
days.paretoFocus: Register mistakes in word choice are less obvious than grammar mistakes but leave a stronger impression.
drills: Rewrite three colloquial sentences into formal ones, changing only the vocabulary.
List five spoken fillers you should avoid in a written request.
Write the same request twice, once for a friend and once for an office.

items: Give the formal equivalents of 'un sacco di' and 'roba'.
Which verb replaces 'dare' in formal contexts of documents?
Why is 'penso che' softer than 'ritengo che'?

activities.title: Scegliere le parole giuste
activities.intro: Italian marks register mostly through vocabulary. Spoken speech uses 'un sacco di', 'roba', 'un attimo' and 'tipo', while formal Italian prefers 'una grande quantità di', 'materiale' and 'un momento'. Formal verbs replace common ones: 'rilasciare' rather than 'dare' a document, 'ritenere' rather than 'pensare'.

items.hr: Nel parlato si dice un sacco di gente, ma per iscritto conviene scrivere molte persone.
items.en: In speech people say 'un sacco di gente', but in writing it is better to write 'molte persone'.

items.hr: L'ufficio rilascia il certificato entro dieci giorni lavorativi.
items.en: The office issues the certificate within ten working days.
items.note: rilasciare, verbo formale

items.hr: Ritengo che la proposta sia interessante, benché richieda qualche modifica.
items.en: I consider the proposal interesting, although it requires some changes.
items.note: ritenere che + congiuntivo

items.hr: Fra amici dico che è una roba complicata, in una riunione dico che è una questione complessa.
items.en: Among friends I say it is a complicated thing, in a meeting I say it is a complex matter.

items.hr: Le parole tipo e cioè sono normali parlando, ma appesantiscono un testo scritto.
items.en: Words like 'tipo' and 'cioè' are normal in speech, but they weigh down a written text.

activities.title: Alzare e abbassare il registro
activities.intro: Practise swapping colloquial vocabulary for formal equivalents.

questions.prompt: Which is the formal equivalent of 'un sacco di problemi'?
questions.explanation: 'Numerosi' belongs to written and formal Italian, while the other options are colloquial or spoken.

questions.prompt: Which verb does an office use for issuing a document?
questions.explanation: 'Rilasciare un documento' is the administrative verb; 'dare' would sound out of place on a form.

questions.prompt: Complete the formal verb: '___ che la sua richiesta sia fondata.' (formal alternative to 'penso')
questions.explanation: 'Ritenere che' is the formal counterpart of 'pensare che' and likewise governs the subjunctive.

questions.prompt: Replace the colloquial noun: 'Ho lasciato tutta quella ___ in magazzino.' Use the informal word for unspecified stuff.
questions.explanation: 'La roba' is the everyday spoken word for unspecified things; in a formal text you would write 'il materiale'.

questions.prompt: Build the Italian for: 'The office issues the certificate within ten days.'
questions.explanation: 'Entro' expresses a deadline by which something happens, unlike 'fra' which counts forward from now.

activities.title: Troppo formale o troppo diretto?
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. A colleague thinks formal Italian is pointless; you argue that register is information, not decoration.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Ho scritto al comune così: ciao, mi servirebbe il certificato. Tanto il contenuto è chiaro, no?
lines.en: I wrote to the municipality like this: hi, I would need the certificate. The content is clear anyway, is it not?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Il contenuto sì, però il tono dice anche quanto prendi sul serio la richiesta.
lines.en: The content yes, but the tone also says how seriously you take the request.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Secondo me sono formalità inutili che fanno perdere tempo.
lines.en: In my opinion they are useless formalities that waste time.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Non del tutto. Con le persone giuste il registro alto ottiene risposte più rapide.
lines.en: Not entirely. With the right people a formal register gets faster replies.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Sarà, ma io non voglio scrivere come un avvocato per chiedere un foglio.
lines.en: Maybe, but I do not want to write like a lawyer to ask for a piece of paper.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Non serve. Basta togliere le parole del parlato e restare gentile.
lines.en: There is no need. It is enough to remove the spoken words and stay polite.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Detta così è meno faticosa di quanto pensassi.
lines.en: Put like that it is less tiring than I thought.

days.title: Il registro nelle email di lavoro
days.objective: You can write a work email with the right opening, body and closing, and adjust the level of formality to the recipient.
days.paretoFocus: A single badly opened email can decide whether you get an answer, and the formulas are fixed and learnable.
drills: Write three different openings for three different recipients.
Write a four-line email asking for a document, with a formal closing.
Rewrite a demanding sentence as a polite request with the conditional.

items: How do you open an email to someone whose name you do not know?
Which closing formula suits a formal email?
Why does the conditional soften a request?

activities.title: Scrivere una email di lavoro
activities.intro: Formal emails open with 'Gentile' plus the surname or 'Spettabile' plus the company name, and close with 'Cordiali saluti' or the warmer 'Un cordiale saluto'. Requests are softened with the conditional, and 'Le scrivo per' states the purpose immediately. Keep 'Ciao' for colleagues you already use tu with.

items.hr: Gentile dottoressa Rossi, Le scrivo per chiedere un chiarimento sul contratto.
items.en: Dear Doctor Rossi, I am writing to you to ask for a clarification about the contract.
items.note: Gentile + titolo + cognome

items.hr: Le sarei grato se potesse inviarmi il documento entro venerdì.
items.en: I would be grateful if you could send me the document by Friday.
items.note: condizionale + congiuntivo imperfetto

items.hr: Resto a disposizione per qualsiasi ulteriore informazione.
items.en: I remain available for any further information.

items.hr: In attesa di un Suo riscontro, La ringrazio e Le porgo cordiali saluti.
items.en: Awaiting your reply, I thank you and send you kind regards.

items.hr: Con un collega scrivo semplicemente: ciao Marco, mi mandi il file quando puoi?
items.en: With a colleague I simply write: hi Marco, will you send me the file when you can?

activities.title: Aperture e chiusure
activities.intro: Practise the fixed formulas of professional correspondence.

questions.prompt: How do you open a formal email to a woman whose surname you know?
questions.explanation: 'Gentile' plus title and surname is the neutral formal opening; 'Salve' and 'Ciao' belong to informal messages.

questions.prompt: Which closing is appropriate in a formal work email?
questions.explanation: 'Cordiali saluti' is the standard professional sign-off; the others are personal and would sound inappropriate.

questions.prompt: Complete with the imperfect subjunctive: 'Le sarei grato se ___ confermarmi l'appuntamento.'
questions.explanation: A polite request pairs the conditional 'sarei' with 'se' plus the imperfect subjunctive of 'potere'.

questions.prompt: Complete the formula: 'Resto a ___ per ogni chiarimento.'
questions.explanation: 'Restare a disposizione' is the fixed phrase offering further availability at the end of a message.

questions.prompt: Build the Italian for: 'I am writing to you to request an appointment.'
questions.explanation: The polite indirect pronoun 'Le' precedes the verb, and 'per' plus the infinitive states the purpose.

activities.title: Rileggiamo questa email
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. A colleague wants to send a blunt email; you propose changes and they push back.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Ho scritto al fornitore: mandate il preventivo subito, siamo in ritardo.
lines.en: I wrote to the supplier: send the quote immediately, we are behind schedule.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Il messaggio è chiaro, però suona come un ordine. Rischi di irrigidire il rapporto.
lines.en: The message is clear, but it sounds like an order. You risk making the relationship tense.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Siamo davvero in ritardo. Non ho tempo per i giri di parole.
lines.en: We really are behind. I do not have time for roundabout phrasing.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Non servono giri di parole, basta il condizionale: vi sarei grato se poteste inviarlo entro domani.
lines.en: You do not need roundabout phrasing, the conditional is enough: I would be grateful if you could send it by tomorrow.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: E se poi non lo mandano lo stesso? La gentilezza non accelera niente.
lines.en: And what if they still do not send it? Politeness does not speed anything up.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: A volte non basta, è vero. Però una scadenza precisa e un tono cortese funzionano meglio insieme.
lines.en: Sometimes it is not enough, that is true. But a precise deadline and a courteous tone work better together.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Va bene, aggiungo la data e cambio l'apertura. Il resto lo lascio così.
lines.en: All right, I will add the date and change the opening. The rest I will leave as it is.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Mi sembra un buon compromesso, e chiudi con cordiali saluti.
lines.en: That seems a good compromise to me, and close with kind regards.

days.title: Le espressioni idiomatiche
days.objective: You can understand and use a set of very frequent Italian idioms in the right situations.
days.paretoFocus: Idioms are the part of speech that dictionaries fail you on, and a handful of them unlock hours of real conversation.
drills: Write a short situation for each of three idioms you learn here.
Say aloud two sentences where an idiom replaces a plain phrase.
Note two idioms you have heard this week and guess their meaning from context.

items: What does 'non vedo l'ora' express?
When would you say 'in bocca al lupo' and how do you reply?
What does 'costare un occhio della testa' mean?

activities.title: Modi di dire quotidiani
activities.intro: Idioms cannot be translated word for word, and using them at the wrong moment sounds strange. These five are extremely frequent and completely neutral in register. Note that 'non vedere l'ora di' is always followed by an infinitive.

items.hr: Non vedo l'ora di finire questo progetto e riposarmi un po'.
items.en: I cannot wait to finish this project and rest a little.
items.note: non vedere l'ora di + infinito

items.hr: In bocca al lupo per l'esame! Si risponde crepi il lupo.
items.en: Good luck with the exam! The reply is 'crepi il lupo'.

items.hr: Quell'appartamento costa un occhio della testa, non possiamo permettercelo.
items.en: That flat costs an arm and a leg, we cannot afford it.

items.hr: Ho perso il filo del discorso, mi puoi ripetere l'ultima parte?
items.en: I lost the thread of what was being said, can you repeat the last part?

items.hr: Alla fine abbiamo fatto due chiacchiere e la tensione è sparita.
items.en: In the end we had a chat and the tension disappeared.
items.note: fare due chiacchiere = parlare informalmente

activities.title: Usare i modi di dire
activities.intro: Practise choosing the right idiom for the right situation.

questions.prompt: A friend has an interview tomorrow. What do you say?
questions.explanation: 'In bocca al lupo' is the wish; 'crepi il lupo' is the answer given by the person receiving it.

questions.prompt: What does 'costare un occhio della testa' mean?
questions.explanation: The idiom exaggerates the price by naming a body part as the cost.

questions.prompt: Complete the idiom: 'Non ___ l'ora di rivedere la mia famiglia.'
questions.explanation: The fixed expression is 'non vedere l'ora di', conjugated normally for the person speaking.

questions.prompt: Complete the idiom: 'Scusa, ho perso il ___ del discorso.'
questions.explanation: 'Perdere il filo' pictures a conversation as a thread you were following.

questions.prompt: Build the Italian for: 'We had a chat over a coffee.'
questions.explanation: 'Fare due chiacchiere' always uses the number two idiomatically, whatever the length of the conversation.

activities.title: Le espressioni si imparano vivendo
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. Your friend says idioms should be avoided by learners; you argue they should be learned early.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Secondo me chi impara una lingua dovrebbe evitare i modi di dire.
lines.en: In my opinion someone learning a language should avoid idioms.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Perché mai? Senza quelli non capisco metà di quello che si dice al bar.
lines.en: Why on earth? Without them I do not understand half of what is said in a bar.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Perché usati male fanno un effetto strano, quasi comico.
lines.en: Because used badly they create a strange, almost comical effect.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Questo è vero, mi è successo. Però capirli e usarli sono due cose diverse.
lines.en: That is true, it has happened to me. But understanding them and using them are two different things.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Ammetto la distinzione. Impararli per capire ha senso.
lines.en: I admit the distinction. Learning them in order to understand makes sense.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: E poi qualcuno è talmente comune che evitarlo suona innaturale.
lines.en: And besides some are so common that avoiding them sounds unnatural.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Su questo non discuto. Nessuno dice sono impaziente invece di non vedo l'ora.
lines.en: I will not argue about that. Nobody says 'sono impaziente' instead of 'non vedo l'ora'.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Esatto. Non vedo l'ora di provarne qualcuno in una conversazione vera.
lines.en: Exactly. I cannot wait to try some of them in a real conversation.

days.title: I proverbi e i modi di dire
days.objective: You can recognise common Italian proverbs, understand the practical advice inside them, and use one appropriately.
days.paretoFocus: Proverbs are quoted constantly by older speakers, and recognising them saves you from guessing at whole sentences.
drills: Write the situation in which you would quote each of three proverbs.
Find an equivalent proverb in your own language for two of them.
Write one sentence with 'chi' as an indefinite subject.

items: What does 'chi' mean at the start of a proverb?
What advice does 'meglio tardi che mai' give?
Which proverb warns against judging by appearances?

activities.title: La saggezza in una riga
activities.intro: Many proverbs begin with 'chi', meaning 'whoever', and take a third person singular verb. They are quoted whole and never rephrased. Using one at the right moment sounds warm; using three in a row sounds like a parody.

items.hr: Chi dorme non piglia pesci, quindi conviene muoversi presto.
items.en: Whoever sleeps catches no fish, so it is better to act early.
items.note: chi = colui che, verbo alla terza persona

items.hr: Meglio tardi che mai, ho cominciato a studiare a quarant'anni.
items.en: Better late than never, I started studying at forty.

items.hr: L'abito non fa il monaco, non giudicare dalle apparenze.
items.en: The habit does not make the monk, do not judge by appearances.

items.hr: Chi va piano va sano e va lontano, mi ripeteva sempre mio nonno.
items.en: Whoever goes slowly goes safely and goes far, my grandfather always used to say to me.

items.hr: Tra il dire e il fare c'è di mezzo il mare, le promesse sono facili.
items.en: Between saying and doing there is the sea in between, promises are easy.

activities.title: Riconoscere i proverbi
activities.intro: Practise matching proverbs to the situations that call for them.

questions.prompt: Which proverb warns against judging by appearances?
options: Chi va piano va sano e va lontano.
questions.explanation: The image contrasts the outward garment with what the person really is.

questions.prompt: What does 'chi' mean at the start of a proverb?
questions.explanation: Proverbial 'chi' is an indefinite relative pronoun meaning 'whoever' and takes a third person singular verb.

questions.prompt: Complete the proverb: 'Chi va ___ va sano e va lontano.'
questions.explanation: 'Piano' here is an adverb meaning slowly and carefully, and it is invariable.

questions.prompt: Complete the proverb: 'Tra il dire e il ___ c'è di mezzo il mare.'
questions.explanation: Both infinitives work as nouns here, which is why each takes the article 'il'.

questions.prompt: Build the Italian for: 'Better late than never, I said to myself.'
questions.explanation: The comparative 'meglio ... che' compares two adverbs directly, without any article.

activities.title: I proverbi hanno sempre ragione?
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. A relative quotes proverbs as proof; you argue that they are memorable but not always right.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Te lo dico io: chi va piano va sano e va lontano. Non cambiare lavoro adesso.
lines.en: I am telling you: slow and steady goes far. Do not change job now.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: I proverbi mi piacciono, però non dimostrano niente da soli.
lines.en: I like proverbs, but on their own they prove nothing.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Sono secoli di esperienza concentrati in una frase.
lines.en: They are centuries of experience concentrated into one sentence.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Anche chi dorme non piglia pesci lo è, e dice il contrario del tuo.
lines.en: 'Whoever sleeps catches no fish' is too, and it says the opposite of yours.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Vero, ce n'è sempre uno per ogni situazione.
lines.en: True, there is always one for every situation.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Ecco il punto. Servono a spiegare una scelta, non a farla al posto tuo.
lines.en: That is the point. They serve to explain a choice, not to make it for you.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Però ammetti che rendono un discorso più vivo.
lines.en: But admit that they make a conversation livelier.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Certo, e infatti li imparo volentieri. Meglio tardi che mai.
lines.en: Certainly, and in fact I learn them gladly. Better late than never.

days.title: L'ironia e l'umorismo
days.objective: You can recognise when an Italian is being ironic, respond in kind, and check politely when you are not sure.
days.paretoFocus: Missing irony makes you look offended or naive, and it is the single hardest thing to catch in a second language.
drills: Write three sentences that mean the opposite of what they say, with the context that makes it clear.
Practise two ways of asking whether someone is joking.
Write one self-deprecating sentence about your own Italian.

items: How do you ask 'are you joking?' in two registers?
Which words often signal irony in spoken Italian?
What does 'prendere in giro' mean?

activities.title: Capire quando si scherza
activities.intro: Italian irony often relies on exaggeration and on tone rather than on special words, but 'ma va', 'figurati' and 'certo, come no' are frequent signals. 'Prendere in giro' means to tease, and it can be affectionate or unkind depending on tone. If you are unsure, asking directly is normal and not rude.

items.hr: Certo, come no, ci arrivo in cinque minuti con questo traffico.
items.en: Sure, of course, I will get there in five minutes with this traffic.
items.note: ironia: significa il contrario

items.hr: Scusa, stai scherzando o dici sul serio? Non ho capito il tono.
items.en: Sorry, are you joking or are you serious? I did not catch the tone.

items.hr: Mi prendi in giro perché sbaglio le doppie, ma ti perdono.
items.en: You tease me because I get the double consonants wrong, but I forgive you.

items.hr: Figurati se arriva puntuale, non è mai successo in dieci anni.
items.en: As if he will arrive on time, it has never happened in ten years.
items.note: figurati se = è impossibile che

items.hr: Il mio accento fa ridere, e ormai rido anch'io quando succede.
items.en: My accent makes people laugh, and by now I laugh too when it happens.

activities.title: Ironia e tono
activities.intro: Practise reading intention behind the literal words.

questions.prompt: Someone says 'Figurati se mi risponde.' What do they mean?
questions.explanation: 'Figurati se' introduces something the speaker considers extremely unlikely, so the literal meaning is reversed.

questions.prompt: What does 'prendere in giro qualcuno' mean?
questions.explanation: The idiom means teasing, and the tone decides whether it is friendly or hurtful.

questions.prompt: Complete the question: 'Aspetta, stai ___ o parli sul serio?'
questions.explanation: The gerund with 'stare' forms the progressive, used here for something happening right now.

questions.prompt: Complete the ironic marker: '___, come no, sarà pronto per stasera.'
questions.explanation: 'Certo, come no' is a set ironic formula whose apparent agreement signals the opposite.

questions.prompt: Build the Italian for: 'You are teasing me and I know it.'
questions.explanation: The object pronoun 'mi' precedes 'stare' in the progressive construction rather than attaching to the gerund.

activities.title: Non era una critica
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. A joke lands badly; you say so and the two of you work out where the line is.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Bravo, con quell'accento potresti fare il doppiatore in un film comico.
lines.en: Well done, with that accent you could dub a comedy film.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Capisco che sia una battuta, però oggi mi ha dato un po' fastidio.
lines.en: I understand it is a joke, but today it bothered me a little.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Scusami davvero, ti prendevo in giro come faccio con tutti.
lines.en: I am really sorry, I was teasing you the way I do with everyone.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Lo so, e in generale ci rido sopra. Dipende dalla giornata.
lines.en: I know, and generally I laugh about it. It depends on the day.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Allora dimmelo, perché io da fuori non me ne accorgo.
lines.en: Then tell me, because from the outside I do not notice.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Sto imparando anch'io. All'inizio non distinguevo l'ironia dalla cattiveria.
lines.en: I am learning too. At the beginning I could not tell irony from unkindness.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Immagino sia complicato, qui si scherza su tutto per abitudine.
lines.en: I imagine it is complicated, here we joke about everything out of habit.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Infatti ormai rispondo anch'io. Certo, come no, sono l'unico straniero della città.
lines.en: In fact by now I answer back too. Sure, of course, I am the only foreigner in the city.

days.title: I giornali e le notizie
days.objective: You can talk about how you follow the news, report what you have read, and describe the parts of a news story.
days.paretoFocus: Reporting what someone else said is one of the most frequent things you do in any conversation about the world.
drills: Report three pieces of news you read this week using 'ho letto che'.
Describe the difference between a fact and a comment, with an example.
Write one sentence with 'secondo quanto riportato'.

items: How do you say 'according to the press' without naming anyone?
What is 'un titolo' and what is 'un articolo di commento'?
Which tense follows 'ha detto che' for a later event?

activities.title: Riportare una notizia
activities.intro: Reported speech uses 'ha detto che' plus a tense shifted back: a present becomes an imperfect and a future becomes a conditional perfect. 'Secondo quanto riportato' introduces information you are not vouching for. Distinguish 'la cronaca', the factual report, from 'il commento', the opinion piece.

items.hr: Ho letto che il prezzo dei trasporti aumenterà dal mese prossimo.
items.en: I read that transport prices will rise from next month.

items.hr: Il sindaco ha detto che i lavori sarebbero finiti entro l'estate.
items.en: The mayor said the works would be finished by the summer.
items.note: futuro nel passato: condizionale composto

items.hr: Secondo quanto riportato dalla stampa, la decisione arriverà a settembre.
items.en: According to press reports, the decision will come in September.

items.hr: Il titolo era allarmante, ma l'articolo diceva una cosa molto più sfumata.
items.en: The headline was alarming, but the article said something far more nuanced.

items.hr: Preferisco leggere la cronaca e farmi un'opinione da solo.
items.en: I prefer to read the factual reporting and form an opinion myself.

activities.title: Dalla cronaca al commento
activities.intro: Practise reporting what others have said and separating fact from comment.

questions.prompt: Choose the correct reported form: 'Ha annunciato che il cantiere ___ a giugno.'
questions.explanation: A future seen from a past reporting verb becomes the compound conditional in Italian, unlike English which keeps 'would'.

questions.prompt: Which word names the factual reporting section rather than opinion?
questions.explanation: 'La cronaca' reports events, while the other three carry the writer's judgement.

questions.prompt: Complete the noun: 'Il ___ era esagerato rispetto al contenuto del pezzo.' (the line printed at the top)
questions.explanation: 'Il titolo' is the headline, often written to attract attention rather than to summarise.

questions.prompt: Complete with the imperfect: 'Mi ha detto che ___ già tutto e non voleva parlarne.'
questions.explanation: A present tense in the original words becomes the imperfect when reported after a past verb.

questions.prompt: Build the Italian for: 'I read that the decision will arrive in September.'
questions.explanation: When the reporting verb is a passato prossimo with present relevance, the future can be kept unchanged.

activities.title: Ma lo hai letto tutto?
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. Your friend reacts to a headline; you argue for reading past it, and they answer back.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Hai visto? Dicono che aumenteranno tutte le tariffe del cinquanta per cento.
lines.en: Have you seen? They say all the fares will go up by fifty per cent.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Quello era il titolo. Nell'articolo si parlava di un aumento su una sola linea.
lines.en: That was the headline. In the article they were talking about an increase on one line only.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: E allora perché scrivono così? È una presa in giro.
lines.en: So why do they write it like that? It is a joke.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Perché un titolo forte viene letto di più, purtroppo funziona così.
lines.en: Because a strong headline gets read more, unfortunately that is how it works.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Io però non ho tempo di leggere tremila parole ogni mattina.
lines.en: But I do not have time to read three thousand words every morning.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Questo lo capisco benissimo, e infatti non chiedo di leggere tutto.
lines.en: I understand that perfectly well, and in fact I am not asking you to read everything.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Allora cosa proponi, in concreto?
lines.en: So what do you suggest, concretely?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: I primi tre paragrafi. Lì c'è il fatto, il resto spesso è commento.
lines.en: The first three paragraphs. That is where the fact is, the rest is often comment.

days.title: Le notizie false e come verificarle
days.objective: You can express doubt about a claim, ask where information comes from, and describe simple checking steps.
days.paretoFocus: Expressing calibrated doubt is a high level skill that makes you sound thoughtful rather than dismissive.
drills: Write three sentences expressing doubt at three different strengths.
Practise asking politely where someone got a piece of information.
Write one sentence with 'dubito che' plus the subjunctive.

items: Which mood follows 'dubito che' and 'mi sembra strano che'?
How do you ask 'where does this information come from?' politely?
Give three degrees of doubt from mild to strong.

activities.title: Dubitare con garbo
activities.intro: Doubt is graded in Italian: 'non ne sono sicuro' is mild, 'mi sembra strano che' is stronger, and 'dubito che' is firm. All the expressions of doubt take the subjunctive. Asking for the source is done with 'da dove viene' or 'chi lo dice'.

items.hr: Dubito che sia vero, ma controllo prima di dire qualcosa.
items.en: I doubt it is true, but I will check before saying anything.
items.note: dubitare che + congiuntivo

items.hr: Mi sembra strano che una cosa così grave non sia uscita da nessuna altra parte.
items.en: It seems strange to me that something so serious has not come out anywhere else.

items.hr: Posso chiederti da dove viene questa informazione?
items.en: Can I ask you where this information comes from?

items.hr: Prima di condividere qualcosa, cerco la stessa notizia in due posti diversi.
items.en: Before sharing something, I look for the same story in two different places.

items.hr: Se una notizia ti fa arrabbiare subito, spesso è scritta apposta per questo.
items.en: If a piece of news makes you angry immediately, it is often written on purpose for that.

activities.title: Verificare prima di condividere
activities.intro: Practise the grammar of doubt and the questions that check a claim.

questions.prompt: Choose the correct form: 'Dubito che quella cifra ___ corretta.'
questions.explanation: Verbs of doubt govern the subjunctive, so 'essere' becomes 'sia'.

questions.prompt: Which expression conveys the strongest doubt?
options: non ne sono del tutto sicuro
questions.explanation: 'Dubitare' states an active disbelief, whereas the other three only suspend judgement.

questions.prompt: Complete with the present subjunctive: 'Mi sembra strano che nessuno ne ___ parlato prima.'
questions.explanation: 'Mi sembra strano che' takes the subjunctive, and a completed action requires the perfect subjunctive 'abbia parlato'.

questions.prompt: Complete the question: 'Da ___ viene questa notizia, scusa?'
questions.explanation: 'Da dove' asks about origin, unlike 'dove' alone which asks about location.

questions.prompt: Build the Italian for: 'Before sharing it, I check the source.'
questions.explanation: 'Prima di' plus an infinitive is used when both clauses share the same subject, and the pronoun attaches to the infinitive.

activities.title: Me lo ha mandato mia cugina
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. A relative forwards an alarming message; you question it without humiliating them.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Guarda qua, dicono che da gennaio cambiano tutte le regole per i documenti.
lines.en: Look at this, they say that from January all the rules for documents are changing.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Mi sembra strano che una cosa così non sia stata annunciata ufficialmente.
lines.en: It seems strange to me that something like that has not been officially announced.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Me lo ha mandato mia cugina, e lei lavora in un ufficio.
lines.en: My cousin sent it to me, and she works in an office.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Non dubito di lei, ma anche lei l'avrà ricevuto da qualcun altro.
lines.en: I do not doubt her, but she will have received it from someone else too.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Quindi secondo te è tutto falso? A volte le cose vere escono prima così.
lines.en: So in your opinion it is all false? Sometimes true things come out this way first.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Hai ragione, capita. Per questo non dico che sia falso, dico che va verificato.
lines.en: You are right, it happens. That is why I am not saying it is false, I am saying it needs checking.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: E come si fa, in pratica?
lines.en: And how do you do that, in practice?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Cerchiamo la stessa notizia altrove. Se nessuno ne parla, aspettiamo prima di allarmarci.
lines.en: Let us look for the same story elsewhere. If nobody is talking about it, let us wait before getting alarmed.

days.title: I social e la vita online
days.objective: You can describe your online habits and argue about what social networks do to attention and relationships.
days.paretoFocus: Online life is a constant conversation topic and brings a whole layer of recent vocabulary.
drills: Write three sentences about how much time you spend online and on what.
Explain in a minute one rule you have set for yourself about your phone.
Write one sentence with 'passare il tempo a' plus an infinitive.

items: How do you say 'to scroll' and 'to post' in ordinary Italian?
Which construction says 'I spend hours doing something'?
How do you say 'I deactivated my account'?

activities.title: Il tempo che passiamo online
activities.intro: Italian has absorbed some English verbs and adapted others: 'postare' and 'scrollare' are common in speech, while 'pubblicare' and 'scorrere' are the standard forms you should prefer in writing. 'Passare il tempo a' plus an infinitive says what you spend time doing.

items.hr: Passo troppo tempo a scorrere il telefono la sera, e me ne accorgo.
items.en: I spend too much time scrolling on my phone in the evening, and I notice it.
items.note: passare il tempo a + infinito

items.hr: Ho pubblicato una foto del viaggio e sono arrivati subito i commenti.
items.en: I posted a photo of the trip and the comments arrived immediately.

items.hr: Ho disattivato un profilo e non mi è mancato per niente.
items.en: I deactivated one profile and I did not miss it at all.

items.hr: Benché serva per lavoro, cerco di non tenerlo aperto tutto il giorno.
items.en: Although I need it for work, I try not to keep it open all day.

items.hr: Mi sembra che le conversazioni online si irrigidiscano più in fretta.
items.en: It seems to me that online conversations harden more quickly.
items.note: mi sembra che + congiuntivo

activities.title: Abitudini digitali
activities.intro: Practise describing online habits in standard Italian.

questions.prompt: Which is the standard Italian verb for putting content online?
questions.explanation: 'Postare' is common in speech, but 'pubblicare' is the standard form that works in every register.

questions.prompt: Choose the correct construction: 'Passo le serate ___ video senza motivo.'
questions.explanation: 'Passare il tempo' governs 'a' before an infinitive when saying what the time is spent on.

questions.prompt: Complete the verb: 'Ho ___ il profilo a marzo e non l'ho più riattivato.'
questions.explanation: 'Disattivare' is a regular are verb taking 'avere', so the participle stays unchanged.

questions.prompt: Complete with the present subjunctive: 'Mi sembra che online la gente ___ più aggressiva.'
questions.explanation: 'Mi sembra che' introduces a personal impression and therefore requires the subjunctive.

questions.prompt: Build the Italian for: 'In the evening I try not to open it.'
questions.explanation: 'Cercare di' takes an infinitive, the negation precedes it, and the object pronoun attaches to the end.

activities.title: Staccare o restare connessi
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. Your friend defends being always online for work; you weigh the cost honestly.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Io non posso disattivare niente, i clienti mi scrivono anche di domenica.
lines.en: I cannot deactivate anything, clients write to me on Sundays too.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Capisco il vincolo, però rispondere sempre insegna loro che sei sempre disponibile.
lines.en: I understand the constraint, but always answering teaches them that you are always available.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Se non rispondo io risponde un altro, è semplice.
lines.en: If I do not answer someone else will, it is simple.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: In alcuni settori è vero. Nel mio ho provato a rispondere solo in orario e non ho perso nessuno.
lines.en: In some sectors that is true. In mine I tried answering only during working hours and I lost nobody.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Sarai stato fortunato. E poi io comunque il telefono lo guarderei lo stesso.
lines.en: You must have been lucky. And anyway I would look at my phone all the same.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Questa è la parte più onesta di tutto il discorso, e vale anche per me.
lines.en: That is the most honest part of the whole conversation, and it applies to me too.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Quindi il problema non è il lavoro, è l'abitudine.
lines.en: So the problem is not work, it is the habit.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Esatto. Passo le serate a scorrere e poi mi lamento di non avere tempo.
lines.en: Exactly. I spend my evenings scrolling and then I complain about having no time.

days.title: La privacy e i dati personali
days.objective: You can talk about personal data, consent and privacy, and argue about the trade between convenience and control.
days.paretoFocus: You sign consent forms constantly, at the doctor, at work and in shops, and these are the words on them.
drills: Write three sentences with 'i dati personali' in different situations.
Explain in a minute what you agree to when you accept a privacy notice.
Write one sentence with 'a patto che' plus the subjunctive.

items: What is 'il consenso' and what does 'trattamento dei dati' mean?
Which mood follows 'a patto che'?
How do you say 'to give your consent' and 'to withdraw it'?

activities.title: Dati, consenso e fiducia
activities.intro: 'Il trattamento dei dati' is the fixed administrative phrase for how your information is handled, and you 'dare il consenso' or 'revocare il consenso'. Conditions are set with 'a patto che' or 'purché' plus the subjunctive. 'La riservatezza' is the formal word for privacy.

items.hr: Ho firmato il consenso al trattamento dei dati senza leggerlo, e non è una buona idea.
items.en: I signed the consent to data processing without reading it, and that is not a good idea.

items.hr: Do i miei dati a patto che servano davvero al servizio che ho chiesto.
items.en: I give my data provided that it is genuinely needed for the service I asked for.
items.note: a patto che + congiuntivo

items.hr: Si può revocare il consenso in qualsiasi momento, anche se pochi lo sanno.
items.en: You can withdraw consent at any time, although few people know it.

items.hr: La riservatezza dei dati sanitari è protetta in modo particolare.
items.en: The confidentiality of health data is protected in a particular way.

items.hr: Se un servizio è gratuito, spesso il prezzo lo paghi con le tue informazioni.
items.en: If a service is free, you often pay the price with your information.

activities.title: Firmare sapendo cosa si firma
activities.intro: Practise the vocabulary of consent and the grammar of conditions.

questions.prompt: What does 'il trattamento dei dati' refer to?
options: il modo in cui le informazioni vengono raccolte e usate
questions.answer: il modo in cui le informazioni vengono raccolte e usate
questions.explanation: 'Trattamento' covers every operation performed on personal information, from collection to storage.

questions.prompt: Choose the correct form: 'Accetto, a patto che i dati non ___ ceduti ad altri.'
questions.explanation: 'A patto che' takes the subjunctive, here the present subjunctive of 'venire' used to build the passive.

questions.prompt: Complete the verb: 'Si può ___ il consenso in qualunque momento.' (to take it back)
questions.explanation: 'Revocare' is the legal verb for withdrawing something previously granted.

questions.prompt: Complete the noun: 'Ho firmato il ___ al trattamento dei dati sanitari.'
questions.explanation: 'Il consenso' is the formal noun on every privacy form, distinct from the everyday 'permesso'.

questions.prompt: Build the Italian for: 'I read the form before signing it.'
questions.explanation: With one subject in both clauses, 'prima di' plus the infinitive is used and the pronoun attaches to it.

activities.title: Tanto non ho niente da nascondere
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. Your colleague shrugs off data collection; you disagree without exaggerating the danger.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Firmo tutto senza guardare. Tanto io non ho niente da nascondere.
lines.en: I sign everything without looking. I have nothing to hide anyway.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Non è questione di nascondere, è questione di sapere chi usa cosa.
lines.en: It is not a question of hiding, it is a question of knowing who uses what.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: E cosa cambia? Prendono l'età e le abitudini di acquisto, non i segreti.
lines.en: And what difference does it make? They take your age and shopping habits, not your secrets.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Cambia quando quelle informazioni decidono il prezzo che ti offrono.
lines.en: It makes a difference when that information decides the price you are offered.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Questo non me lo aspettavo. Però leggere venti pagine è impossibile.
lines.en: I did not expect that. But reading twenty pages is impossible.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Sono d'accordo, sono scritte male apposta. Io guardo solo due righe.
lines.en: I agree, they are badly written on purpose. I only look at two lines.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Quali due righe?
lines.en: Which two lines?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Quelle sulla cessione a terzi. Accetto a patto che i dati restino lì.
lines.en: The ones about transfer to third parties. I accept provided that the data stays there.

days.title: L'intelligenza artificiale nella vita quotidiana
days.objective: You can say what automatic language tools are good at, where they fail, and defend that opinion with a concession and a hypothesis.
days.paretoFocus: Everyone now has an opinion about these tools, so this is one of the few brand new topics that comes up in real conversation and in exam speaking tasks alike.
drills: Say out loud three tasks you would trust an automatic tool with, and three you would not, each with because.
Take one of your no answers and turn it into a concession: 'Benché sia comodo, ...'.
Describe one time a tool gave you wrong information, using the past tense and then a third conditional about what you should have done.

items: How do you introduce a concession with benché, and which mood follows it?
How do you say 'people say that' impersonally, and what happens to the verb after it?
Give the full third conditional pattern for 'if I had checked, I would not have made that mistake'.

activities.title: Comodi ma non affidabili
activities.intro: These sentences all take a position and then qualify it. Notice that a concession (benché, pur) and an impersonal report (si dice che) both push the verb into the congiuntivo. Use them to sound measured rather than absolute.

items.hr: Benché questi programmi traducano in pochi secondi, spesso non capiscono il contesto di una frase.
items.en: Although these programs translate in a few seconds, they often do not understand the context of a sentence.
items.note: benché + congiuntivo presente: tradurre -> che traducano

items.hr: Si dice che l'intelligenza artificiale sappia tutto, ma in realtà inventa i dati con grande sicurezza.
items.en: People say that artificial intelligence knows everything, but in fact it invents data with great confidence.
items.note: si dice che + congiuntivo: sapere -> che sappia

items.hr: Lo strumento con cui ho riassunto la relazione ha tagliato proprio la parte che mi interessava di più.
items.en: The tool with which I summarised the report cut out exactly the part that interested me most.
items.note: con cui after a preposition, che as subject of the second clause

items.hr: Se avessi controllato le fonti, non avrei ripetuto quell'errore davanti a tutti.
items.en: If I had checked the sources, I would not have repeated that mistake in front of everyone.
items.note: third conditional: se + congiuntivo trapassato, then condizionale passato

items.hr: Molti testi vengono corretti da un programma, tuttavia la responsabilità di quello che si pubblica resta nostra.
items.en: Many texts are corrected by a program, however the responsibility for what gets published remains ours.
items.note: passive with venire, then the si passivante in si pubblica

activities.title: Prendere posizione sugli strumenti automatici
activities.intro: Practise conceding a point, reporting impersonally and reasoning about the past.

questions.prompt: Scegli la forma giusta: 'Bisogna che tu ___ le fonti prima di pubblicare qualcosa.'
questions.explanation: Bisogna che is an impersonal expression of necessity, so it takes the congiuntivo presente: che tu controlli.

questions.prompt: Quale frase descrive meglio un uso sensato di questi strumenti?
options: Servono a scrivere una prima versione, non a verificare i fatti.
Servono a verificare i fatti, non a scrivere una prima versione.
Servono solo a chi non sa scrivere.
questions.answer: Servono a scrivere una prima versione, non a verificare i fatti.
questions.explanation: The realistic position is that they help you draft and rephrase, while facts still need checking elsewhere. Note servire a + infinito.

questions.prompt: Completa con la congiunzione concessiva: 'Uso questi programmi ___ siano imprecisi, perché mi fanno risparmiare tempo.'
questions.explanation: A concession before a congiuntivo (siano) calls for benché or sebbene. Both are standard in writing and in careful speech.

questions.prompt: Completa con il si passivante del verbo tradurre: 'In quell'ufficio ___ molti documenti ogni giorno.'
questions.explanation: With the si passivante the verb agrees with the thing affected: documenti is plural, so si traducono, not si traduce.

questions.prompt: Build the Italian for: 'If I had checked the sources, I would not have made that mistake.'
questions.explanation: The unreal past pattern is se + congiuntivo trapassato (avessi controllato) followed by the condizionale passato (avrei fatto).

activities.title: Vale la pena fidarsi?
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. A colleague trusts automatic tools far more than you do; concede what is true, then push back with benché and a hypothesis.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Ho preparato tutta la presentazione in venti minuti, mi sono fatto aiutare da un programma.
lines.en: I prepared the whole presentation in twenty minutes, I got a program to help me.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Per la forma sono d'accordo, è utilissimo. Benché sia veloce, però, non mi fiderei dei numeri che ti ha dato.
lines.en: As far as the wording goes I agree, it is really useful. Although it is fast, though, I would not trust the figures it gave you.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Si dice che sbagli, ma a me non è mai capitato di trovare un errore grave.
lines.en: People say it makes mistakes, but I have never happened to find a serious error.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Forse non te ne sei accorto: inventa le fonti in modo molto convincente. Se non le controlli, non te ne rendi conto.
lines.en: Maybe you did not notice: it invents sources in a very convincing way. If you do not check them, you do not realise it.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Quindi secondo te dovrei riscrivere tutto a mano? Ci metterei tre giorni.
lines.en: So in your opinion I should rewrite everything by hand? It would take me three days.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: No, non è questo il punto. Lascia il testo, verifica solo i dati. Lo strumento scrive bene, ma la responsabilità della presentazione resta tua.
lines.en: No, that is not the point. Keep the text, just verify the data. The tool writes well, but the responsibility for the presentation stays yours.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: E poi mi chiedo una cosa: se scrive lui, il lavoro è ancora mio?
lines.en: And then I wonder one thing: if it does the writing, is the work still mine?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Secondo me sì, purché tu decida che cosa dire. Le idee restano tue, le parole sono solo uno strumento.
lines.en: In my view yes, provided that you decide what to say. The ideas stay yours, the words are only a tool.

days.title: Il cinema e le serie
days.objective: You can review a film or a series out loud: summarise the plot, judge the acting and the directing, and recommend it to a specific kind of viewer.
days.paretoFocus: Giving an opinion about something you have watched is one of the most common speaking tasks there is, and the same four or five phrases carry every review you will ever give.
drills: Describe the plot of the last thing you watched in three sentences, without naming it.
Say what struck you most about it using 'Quello che mi ha colpito di più è ...'.
Finish this twice, positively and negatively: 'Lo consiglio a chi ... , invece chi ... resterà deluso.'

items: Name the Italian words for the plot, the lead character and the directing.
How do you say 'it is worth watching it' and what form follows vale la pena?
How do you recommend something to a type of person rather than to a named person?

activities.title: Come si recensisce un film
activities.intro: A review has three moves: describe, judge, recommend. Italian does the judging with mi ha colpito and vale la pena, and the recommending with consigliare a chi plus a present tense. Notice how a criticism and a compliment sit in the same sentence with però.

items.hr: La trama è abbastanza prevedibile, però la regia è così curata che vale la pena vederlo lo stesso.
items.en: The plot is fairly predictable, but the directing is so careful that it is worth watching it anyway.
items.note: vale la pena + infinito; la trama, la regia

items.hr: Quello che mi ha colpito di più è il modo in cui il protagonista cambia idea alla fine.
items.en: What struck me most is the way in which the main character changes his mind at the end.
items.note: quello che = what; il modo in cui = the way in which

items.hr: Lo consiglio a chi ama le storie lente, mentre chi cerca l'azione resterà deluso.
items.en: I recommend it to those who love slow stories, while anyone looking for action will be disappointed.
items.note: chi takes a singular verb: chi ama, chi cerca

items.hr: Non credo che una serie debba durare otto stagioni per essere bella.
items.en: I do not think a series has to last eight seasons in order to be good.
items.note: non credo che + congiuntivo: dovere -> che debba

items.hr: Se la seconda stagione fosse stata all'altezza della prima, ne parlerebbero ancora tutti.
items.en: If the second season had lived up to the first, everyone would still be talking about it.
items.note: essere all'altezza di = to live up to; ne parlerebbero = they would talk about it

activities.title: Giudicare e consigliare
activities.intro: Practise the vocabulary of a review together with the passive you met earlier in the course.

questions.prompt: Completa la recensione: 'La trama è debole, ___ la fotografia è splendida.'
questions.explanation: You are contrasting a flaw with a merit, so you need the adversative però. Benché and affinché would both require the congiuntivo and change the meaning.

questions.prompt: Scegli il passivo corretto: 'La serie ___ girata in un paese di montagna.'
questions.explanation: The passive uses essere plus the past participle, and the participle agrees with the subject: la serie is feminine singular, so è stata girata.

questions.prompt: Completa con il participio del verbo che indica una forte impressione: 'Il finale mi ha ___ moltissimo.'
questions.explanation: Colpire means to strike, and mi ha colpito is the standard way to say something made a strong impression. With avere the participle stays colpito here.

questions.prompt: Completa con l'infinito adatto: 'Vale la pena ___ almeno i primi due episodi prima di giudicare.'
questions.explanation: Vale la pena is always followed by an infinitive. Both vedere and guardare work for watching episodes.

questions.prompt: Build the Italian for: 'I recommend it to anyone who loves a slow pace.'
questions.explanation: The object pronoun lo comes before the verb, and chi introduces the type of person, followed by a singular verb: chi ama.

activities.title: Non siamo d'accordo sul finale
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. Your friend loved a series that you found disappointing; defend your judgement with concrete reasons instead of just saying you did not like it.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Allora, l'hai finita? Secondo me il finale è geniale.
lines.en: So, did you finish it? In my opinion the ending is brilliant.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: L'ho finita, sì, ma non sono d'accordo. Quello che mi ha colpito è la prima stagione, non l'ultima.
lines.en: I finished it, yes, but I do not agree. What struck me is the first season, not the last one.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Però la regia resta bellissima anche alla fine, non puoi negarlo.
lines.en: But the directing stays beautiful right to the end, you cannot deny that.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Su quello ti do ragione. Il problema è la trama: se avessero tagliato tre episodi, il finale funzionerebbe molto meglio.
lines.en: On that I agree with you. The problem is the plot: if they had cut three episodes, the ending would work much better.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Forse hai ragione sui tempi, ma il protagonista è scritto benissimo.
lines.en: Maybe you are right about the pacing, but the main character is written really well.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Il protagonista sì, gli altri no. Non credo che i personaggi secondari abbiano una storia vera.
lines.en: The main character yes, the others no. I do not think the secondary characters have a real story.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Quindi non la consiglieresti a nessuno?
lines.en: So you would not recommend it to anyone?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: La consiglio a chi ha pazienza. Vale la pena arrivare alla fine, anche se io mi aspettavo altro.
lines.en: I recommend it to people who have patience. It is worth getting to the end, even if I was expecting something else.

days.title: La musica e i concerti
days.objective: You can separate the parts of a song and of a live show in Italian, and argue about whether a concert was worth the money.
days.paretoFocus: Music is the safest topic with strangers of any age, and it forces you to justify taste, which is exactly what an oral exam asks you to do.
drills: Describe a song you like without naming it: say what the lyrics are about and what the chorus does.
Say one artist you prefer live and one you prefer recorded, each with a reason using pur + gerundio.
Argue for and against paying a lot for a ticket, two sentences each side.

items: Give the Italian for the lyrics, the chorus and the acoustics of a venue.
How does pur plus the gerundio work, and what does it replace?
How do you say tickets get sold out in minutes using a passive with venire?

activities.title: Dal vivo o registrato
activities.intro: Talking about music needs a small set of precise nouns and one useful concessive shortcut: pur plus the gerundio, which packs 'although they are' into two words. Notice that a compliment about one part of a song can sit next to a criticism of another.

items.hr: Ammetto che il testo sia banale, eppure il ritornello mi resta in testa per giorni.
items.en: I admit the lyrics are banal, and yet the chorus stays in my head for days.
items.note: il testo = lyrics; il ritornello = chorus; ammettere che + congiuntivo

items.hr: Pur essendo bravissimi in studio, dal vivo mi hanno deluso parecchio.
items.en: Even though they are excellent in the studio, live they disappointed me quite a lot.
items.note: pur + gerundio = benché siano; dal vivo = live

items.hr: Vale la pena spendere tanto solo se l'acustica del locale è decente.
items.en: It is worth spending a lot only if the acoustics of the venue are decent.
items.note: l'acustica, il locale; note the article l' before a

items.hr: La canzone che mi ha emozionato di più è quella che hanno suonato senza microfoni.
items.en: The song that moved me most is the one they played without microphones.
items.note: quella che = the one that, referring back to la canzone

items.hr: I biglietti vengono venduti in pochi minuti, per questo finiscono spesso nelle mani di chi li rivende.
items.en: The tickets get sold in a few minutes, which is why they often end up in the hands of people who resell them.
items.note: passive with venire; per questo = which is why

activities.title: Parlare di canzoni e di serate
activities.intro: Practise the concessive gerund and the music vocabulary, plus one hypothesis from the earlier grammar block.

questions.prompt: Completa: 'Pur ___ bravissimi in studio, dal vivo non mi hanno convinto.'
questions.explanation: Pur is followed by the gerundio, so essendo. The whole phrase is a compact way of saying benché siano.

questions.prompt: Scegli la forma corretta: 'Se il locale ___ più piccolo, l'acustica sarebbe migliore.'
questions.explanation: An unreal present hypothesis takes se + congiuntivo imperfetto in the first half and the condizionale presente in the second: se fosse, sarebbe.

questions.prompt: Completa con il nome della parte di una canzone che si ripete: 'Il ___ è facilissimo da ricordare.'
questions.explanation: Il ritornello is the part that comes back after each verse. The verses themselves are le strofe.

questions.prompt: Completa con il nome delle parole di una canzone: 'Il ___ di questo pezzo parla di una città che cambia.'
questions.explanation: Italian uses il testo for the words of a song. Do not use le parole in a review, it sounds vague.

questions.prompt: Build the Italian for: 'The tickets get sold in a few minutes.'
questions.explanation: Venire plus the past participle is a common passive in Italian for actions that happen repeatedly, and venduti agrees with i biglietti.

activities.title: Il biglietto costa troppo?
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. Your friend thinks a big concert is never worth the price; weigh the two sides honestly instead of simply insisting.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Ottanta euro per due ore di musica? Con quei soldi ci mangio fuori tre volte.
lines.en: Eighty euros for two hours of music? With that money I can eat out three times.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Capisco l'obiezione, ma per me vale la pena una volta all'anno. Dal vivo si sente una cosa che a casa non arriva.
lines.en: I understand the objection, but for me it is worth it once a year. Live you hear something that does not come across at home.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Sì, però negli stadi l'acustica è terribile e la gente parla per tutto il concerto.
lines.en: Yes, but in stadiums the acoustics are terrible and people talk through the whole concert.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Su questo hai ragione, infatti io preferisco i locali piccoli. Se suonassero sempre in sale da cinquecento posti, andrei molto più spesso.
lines.en: On that you are right, in fact I prefer small venues. If they always played in five hundred seat halls, I would go much more often.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: E poi i biglietti spariscono in due minuti, non si riesce mai a comprarli.
lines.en: And then the tickets disappear in two minutes, you can never manage to buy them.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Vengono comprati da chi poi li rivende al doppio, questo è il vero problema.
lines.en: They get bought by people who then resell them at double the price, that is the real problem.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Allora siamo d'accordo: conviene ascoltare a casa.
lines.en: So we agree: it is better to listen at home.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Non proprio. Pur costando troppo, un concerto ti resta addosso per mesi. Una registrazione no.
lines.en: Not exactly. Even though it costs too much, a concert stays with you for months. A recording does not.

days.title: I libri e la lettura
days.objective: You can explain why you abandoned or finished a book, describe its style and pacing, and recommend it to a precise kind of reader.
days.paretoFocus: Books give you the vocabulary of style and structure, which transfers directly to talking about articles, reports and anything else you read for work.
drills: Say in three sentences what a book you have read is about, without naming it or its author.
Explain one book you gave up on and why, using benché plus a congiuntivo trapassato.
Write two lines that recommend it to one reader and warn off another.

items: Say the Italian for the plot, the style and the pace of a book.
How do you say 'although everyone had recommended it to me' with the right mood?
How do you deny a generalisation politely: 'it is not true that a long book is always boring'?

activities.title: Perché l'ho lasciato a metà
activities.intro: Judging a book means talking about the writing, not only the story: lo stile, il ritmo, la struttura. Two structures do most of the work here, benché with a past congiuntivo for a concession about something already done, and non è vero che for pushing back on a generalisation.

items.hr: Ho lasciato il romanzo a metà, benché tutti me lo avessero consigliato con entusiasmo.
items.en: I gave up on the novel halfway through, although everyone had recommended it to me enthusiastically.
items.note: benché + congiuntivo trapassato for an earlier action: avessero consigliato

items.hr: Quello che mi ha convinto è lo stile, non la trama, che in fondo è piuttosto semplice.
items.en: What won me over is the style, not the plot, which is actually rather simple.
items.note: lo stile takes lo because of the s + consonant

items.hr: Lo consiglio a chi ha voglia di un libro impegnativo, non a chi legge cinque pagine prima di dormire.
items.en: I recommend it to people who want a demanding book, not to people who read five pages before going to sleep.
items.note: impegnativo = demanding, of a book or a job

items.hr: Non è vero che un libro lungo sia sempre noioso: dipende tutto dal ritmo.
items.en: It is not true that a long book is always boring: it all depends on the pace.
items.note: non è vero che + congiuntivo; il ritmo = the pace

items.hr: Leggendo la prefazione, ho capito perché l'autrice ha scelto una struttura così strana.
items.en: By reading the preface, I understood why the author chose such a strange structure.
items.note: gerundio for the means: leggendo = by reading; l'autrice, la prefazione

activities.title: Recensire un romanzo
activities.intro: Practise concessions and the language of style, with one relative pronoun from the earlier grammar block.

questions.prompt: Completa: 'Ho smesso di leggerlo, ___ tutti me lo avessero consigliato.'
questions.explanation: The second half contradicts the first, so it is a concession, and the congiuntivo avessero consigliato confirms that benché is required.

questions.prompt: Scegli il pronome relativo giusto: 'Il libro ___ ti parlavo è appena uscito in edizione economica.'
questions.explanation: Parlare governs di, and that preposition stays attached to the relative pronoun: parlare di un libro becomes il libro di cui ti parlavo.

questions.prompt: Completa con il nome che indica come è costruita una storia: 'Mi interessa più lo stile che la ___.'
questions.explanation: La trama is the sequence of events. Lo stile is how it is written, and the two are judged separately in a review.

questions.prompt: Completa con il gerundio del verbo che significa 'to read': '___ la prefazione, ho capito la struttura del romanzo.'
questions.explanation: The gerundio here expresses the means: it answers how you understood something. Verbs in ere form it with endo.

questions.prompt: Build the Italian for: 'It is not true that a long book is always boring.'
questions.explanation: Non è vero che denies something, so the verb that follows goes into the congiuntivo: sia, not è.

activities.title: Vale la pena finirlo?
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. Your friend insists that a book must always be finished; take the opposite position and defend it with reasons.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: L'hai lasciato a pagina cento? Un libro va finito, altrimenti non puoi giudicarlo.
lines.en: You left it at page one hundred? A book should be finished, otherwise you cannot judge it.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Non sono d'accordo. Dopo cento pagine lo stile lo conosci già, e a me non diceva niente.
lines.en: I do not agree. After a hundred pages you already know the style, and it was saying nothing to me.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Benché l'inizio sia lento, la seconda parte cambia completamente, te lo assicuro.
lines.en: Although the beginning is slow, the second part changes completely, I assure you.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Può darsi, ma se un autore ha bisogno di duecento pagine per partire, forse il problema è la struttura.
lines.en: That may be, but if an author needs two hundred pages to get going, maybe the problem is the structure.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Con questo criterio non finiresti mai niente di impegnativo.
lines.en: With that criterion you would never finish anything demanding.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Al contrario: lascio i libri deboli proprio per avere tempo per quelli difficili. Il tempo di lettura non è infinito.
lines.en: On the contrary: I drop weak books precisely so as to have time for the difficult ones. Reading time is not infinite.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Allora a chi lo consiglieresti, questo romanzo?
lines.en: So who would you recommend this novel to?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: A chi ama le descrizioni lunghe. Io preferisco un ritmo più veloce, quindi lo lascio volentieri a qualcun altro.
lines.en: To people who love long descriptions. I prefer a faster pace, so I am happy to leave it to someone else.

days.title: L'arte e i musei
days.objective: You can describe an exhibition, say what struck you in a work of art, and take a reasoned position in the argument about contemporary art and museum prices.
days.paretoFocus: A visit to an exhibition is a standard conversation and a standard exam prompt, and the words for describing what you saw double as the words for describing any image.
drills: Describe one work you remember: the subject, the colours and one detail, without naming the work.
Give one argument for free museums and one against, in two sentences each.
Say what time of day you would visit and why, using vale la pena.

items: Give the Italian for an exhibition, a painting, a room in a museum and the lighting.
How do you say 'what struck me is not the subject but the use of the light'?
How do you set up a hypothesis about free museums with se plus the imperfect subjunctive?

activities.title: Che cosa mi ha colpito alla mostra
activities.intro: Describing art needs two things: precise nouns (la mostra, il quadro, la sala, la luce) and a way to separate what a work shows from how it is made. Ciò che and quello che both mean what, and both let you put the important part at the end of the sentence.

items.hr: La mostra è stata allestita benissimo, però i quadri più importanti erano tutti in fondo.
items.en: The exhibition was put together very well, but the most important paintings were all at the back.
items.note: allestire una mostra = to set up an exhibition; il quadro = painting

items.hr: Ciò che mi ha colpito non è il soggetto del quadro, ma l'uso della luce.
items.en: What struck me is not the subject of the painting, but the use of the light.
items.note: ciò che = quello che; il soggetto, l'uso, la luce

items.hr: Non credo che l'arte contemporanea sia una presa in giro: semplicemente chiede più tempo.
items.en: I do not think contemporary art is a joke: it simply asks for more time.
items.note: una presa in giro = a joke at someone's expense

items.hr: Vale la pena visitare il museo di mattina presto, quando le sale sono ancora vuote.
items.en: It is worth visiting the museum early in the morning, when the rooms are still empty.
items.note: la sala, le sale = the rooms of a museum

items.hr: Se i musei fossero gratuiti, ci andrebbe molta più gente, però resterebbero meno soldi per il restauro.
items.en: If museums were free, many more people would go there, but less money would be left for restoration.
items.note: ci = there; il restauro

activities.title: Descrivere e discutere una mostra
activities.intro: Practise museum vocabulary and opinion structures, with one connective from the earlier argument block.

questions.prompt: Completa: 'Non credo che l'arte contemporanea ___ una presa in giro.'
questions.explanation: Non credo che expresses doubt, and doubt takes the congiuntivo presente: che sia.

questions.prompt: Scegli il connettivo adatto: 'Il biglietto costa parecchio; ___, la mostra chiude fra due settimane.'
questions.explanation: The second clause adds a further drawback rather than contradicting the first, so you need an additive connective. Tuttavia and invece would signal a contrast.

questions.prompt: Completa con il nome di un'esposizione temporanea: 'La ___ resta aperta fino alla fine di giugno.'
questions.explanation: La mostra is a temporary exhibition, while il museo is the permanent institution that hosts it.

questions.prompt: Completa con l'espressione che significa 'it is worth it': '___ visitare le sale piccole, non solo quelle famose.'
questions.explanation: Vale la pena is impersonal and is always followed straight by an infinitive, with no preposition in between.

questions.prompt: Build the Italian for: 'What struck me is the use of the light.'
questions.explanation: Ciò che opens the sentence as the subject, and the thing you want to highlight comes after è. Della is di plus la.

activities.title: Musei gratuiti: sì o no?
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. Your friend wants all museums to be free; you see the other side, so weigh both arguments before landing on a position.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Venti euro per due sale? I musei dovrebbero essere gratuiti per tutti, punto.
lines.en: Twenty euros for two rooms? Museums should be free for everyone, full stop.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Capisco il principio, e in parte lo condivido. Però se fossero gratuiti, chi pagherebbe il restauro dei quadri?
lines.en: I understand the principle, and I partly share it. But if they were free, who would pay for the restoration of the paintings?

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Lo Stato, come fa per le scuole. La cultura non è un lusso.
lines.en: The state, as it does for schools. Culture is not a luxury.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: D'accordo, ma i soldi pubblici non bastano mai. Forse una via di mezzo funzionerebbe meglio: gratis per i giovani, a pagamento per gli altri.
lines.en: Agreed, but public money is never enough. Maybe a middle way would work better: free for young people, paid for everyone else.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Sì, però così ci vanno sempre le stesse persone. Ciò che mi dà fastidio è proprio questo.
lines.en: Yes, but that way it is always the same people who go. That is exactly what bothers me.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Questo è vero, e infatti secondo me il problema non è solo il prezzo: sono le sale piene e le spiegazioni scritte male.
lines.en: That is true, and in fact in my view the problem is not only the price: it is the crowded rooms and the badly written explanations.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Quindi non cambieresti niente sui biglietti?
lines.en: So you would not change anything about the tickets?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Li abbasserei, sì, ma investirei anche sull'orario. Vale la pena aprire di sera, quando la gente esce dal lavoro.
lines.en: I would lower them, yes, but I would also invest in the opening hours. It is worth opening in the evening, when people finish work.

days.title: Raccontare un imprevisto: un furto, uno smarrimento, un incidente
days.objective: Report a theft, a loss or an accident to the authorities, narrate the sequence of events with the past tenses, and handle the officer's questions.
days.paretoFocus: This is the one situation where you must be understood precisely under stress, and it forces two structures that appear everywhere in adult speech: indirect questions and pronouns attached to the end of the verb.
