drills: Narrate a real or invented theft in six sentences, alternating imperfetto for the background and passato prossimo for the events.
Turn five direct questions into indirect ones, three with se and two with come.
Rewrite five commands and infinitives with the pronouns attached, for example 'give it to me' and 'to bring it to me'.

items: Which word introduces an indirect question that has a yes or no answer?
Where do the pronouns go with a tu imperative, and where with an infinitive?
How do you say that you want to file a report at the police station?

activities.title: Sporgere denuncia
activities.intro: To report an incident you need la denuncia and the verb sporgere denuncia, plus smarrire (to lose), rubare (to steal), il testimone and il verbale, the written statement you sign. Two structures carry the rest. Le interrogative indirette are questions embedded in another sentence, introduced by se for yes or no questions and by come, dove, quando or perché otherwise. L'enclisi dei pronomi means the pronoun is attached to the END of the verb: this happens with the tu imperative and with the infinitive, giving dammelo, diglielo and portarmelo.

items.hr: Ieri sera mi hanno rubato il portafoglio in metropolitana, così sono andato a sporgere denuncia.
items.en: Last night they stole my wallet on the underground, so I went to file a report.
items.note: rubare qualcosa a qualcuno: mi hanno rubato, not sono stato rubato

items.hr: Ho smarrito i documenti e devo presentare una denuncia di smarrimento.
items.en: I have lost my documents and I have to file a loss report.
items.note: smarrire is the formal verb for perdere in official language

items.hr: L'agente mi ha chiesto se avevo visto qualcosa e non so come sia successo.
items.en: The officer asked me whether I had seen anything, and I do not know how it happened.
items.note: interrogative indirette: se for yes or no, come for manner

items.hr: Mentre aspettavo l'autobus, un ragazzo mi ha strappato la borsa, ma per fortuna c'era un testimone.
items.en: While I was waiting for the bus, a boy snatched my bag, but luckily there was a witness.
items.note: imperfetto for the background, passato prossimo for the single event

items.hr: Se ti chiedono il verbale, mostraglielo; se serve una copia, possono mandarmela domani.
items.en: If they ask you for the report, show it to them; if a copy is needed, they can send it to me tomorrow.
items.note: enclisi: mostra + glielo -> mostraglielo; mandare + me la -> mandarmela

activities.title: Il verbale
activities.intro: Practise reporting an incident, indirect questions and attached pronouns.

questions.prompt: Completa: Mentre ___ in centro, mi hanno rubato il telefono.
questions.explanation: The imperfetto sets the ongoing background against which a single completed event in the passato prossimo happens.

questions.prompt: Quale forma è corretta per dire 'give it to me' a un amico?
questions.explanation: With the tu imperative the pronouns attach to the end of the verb and mi becomes me before lo, giving dammelo with a double m.

questions.prompt: Completa l'interrogativa indiretta di modo: Non so ___ sia successo l'incidente.
questions.explanation: An embedded question about the manner of an event needs the same word you would use to ask it directly, and here it is followed by the congiuntivo.

questions.prompt: Completa: Sono andato in questura a ___ denuncia.
questions.explanation: The fixed collocation for filing an official report uses this verb, not fare or dare.

questions.prompt: Build the Italian for: 'He asked me whether I had seen anything.'
questions.explanation: An indirect yes or no question uses se, and the earlier action takes the trapassato prossimo avevo visto.

activities.title: In questura
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. You are reporting a stolen backpack and the officer pushes you for details you may not have.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Buongiorno, mi dica che cosa è successo.
lines.en: Good morning, tell me what happened.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Mi hanno rubato lo zaino sul treno, vorrei sporgere denuncia.
lines.en: They stole my backpack on the train, I would like to file a report.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Ricorda a che ora è successo e se c'era qualcuno vicino a lei?
lines.en: Do you remember what time it happened and whether there was anyone near you?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Erano circa le otto. Non so come sia successo, purtroppo dormivo.
lines.en: It was around eight. I do not know how it happened, unfortunately I was asleep.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Senza una descrizione precisa è difficile ritrovarlo. C'era un testimone?
lines.en: Without a precise description it is hard to recover it. Was there a witness?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Sì, una signora ha visto tutto. Se le serve il suo numero, glielo do subito.
lines.en: Yes, a lady saw everything. If you need her number, I will give it to you right away.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Me lo dia, per favore. Adesso preparo il verbale: lo legga con calma e lo firmi.
lines.en: Give it to me, please. Now I will prepare the report: read it carefully and sign it.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: D'accordo. E se ritrovate lo zaino, potete avvisarmi per telefono?
lines.en: All right. And if you find the backpack, can you let me know by phone?

days.title: Dare e sostenere un'opinione
days.objective: You can state an opinion in Italian and back it up with a reason and a concrete example.
days.paretoFocus: A bare opinion scores almost nothing in a spoken test or a real argument; the reason plus the example is what makes it count.
drills: Pick a topic you care about and say your position out loud in one sentence beginning with Secondo me.
Add a reason with Il motivo è che, then an example with Per esempio, without pausing in between.
Say the same opinion again starting with Penso che and check that the verb moves to the subjunctive.

items: Which opinion phrases take the subjunctive, and which keep the indicative?
Give three ways to introduce the reason behind your position.
How do you signal that a concrete example is coming?

activities.title: La mia opinione e il mio motivo
activities.intro: A supported opinion in Italian has three moving parts: the position, the reason, the example. Phrases built on secondo me and per me keep the indicative, while penso che, credo che and sono convinto che pull the following verb into the subjunctive. Knowing both routes means you can always say what you think.

items.hr: Secondo me il trasporto pubblico dovrebbe essere gratuito in centro.
items.en: In my opinion public transport should be free in the city centre.
items.note: secondo me takes the indicative or conditional, never the subjunctive

items.hr: Penso che sia una decisione giusta.
items.en: I think it is a fair decision.
items.note: penso che, essere becomes sia

items.hr: Sono convinta che valga la pena provare.
items.en: I am convinced it is worth trying.
items.note: valere becomes valga; convinta agrees with a female speaker

items.hr: Il motivo principale è che molte famiglie non hanno l'automobile.
items.en: The main reason is that many families do not have a car.
items.note: il motivo, l'automobile

items.hr: Per esempio, mia sorella va al lavoro in autobus tutti i giorni.
items.en: For example, my sister goes to work by bus every day.
items.note: l'esempio turns a general claim into something concrete

activities.title: Sostenere quello che dici
activities.intro: Practise the opinion frame and the verb form that follows it.

questions.prompt: Penso che questa proposta ___ molto utile per il quartiere.
questions.explanation: Penso che expresses a personal view, so the verb takes the present subjunctive: sia.

questions.prompt: Which phrase introduces the reason that supports your position?
questions.explanation: Il motivo è che announces the justification. Al contrario introduces an opposing idea, not a supporting one.

questions.prompt: Make the claim concrete: 'Molti ragazzi lavorano già a diciotto anni. Per ___, mio cugino fa il cameriere la sera.'
questions.explanation: Per esempio introduces the concrete case that backs up a general statement.

questions.prompt: Sono convinta che questa soluzione ___ (funzionare) meglio della precedente.
questions.explanation: Sono convinto che and sono convinta che take the subjunctive: funzionare becomes funzioni.

questions.prompt: Build the Italian for: 'I think that this idea is interesting.'
questions.explanation: Penso che comes first, the subject follows, and sia is the subjunctive of essere.

activities.title: Il lavoro da casa
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. A colleague asks what you think about working from home, and you answer with a position, a reason and an example.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Tu che ne pensi del lavoro da casa?
lines.en: What do you think about working from home?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Secondo me è una buona cosa, almeno due giorni alla settimana.
lines.en: In my opinion it is a good thing, at least two days a week.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Perché proprio due giorni?
lines.en: Why two days exactly?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Il motivo principale è che si risparmia molto tempo negli spostamenti.
lines.en: The main reason is that you save a lot of time commuting.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Non so, io in ufficio mi concentro di più.
lines.en: I do not know, I concentrate better in the office.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Per esempio io, il martedì, finisco la relazione in metà tempo.
lines.en: For example on Tuesdays I finish the report in half the time.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Quindi non torneresti mai in ufficio tutti i giorni?
lines.en: So you would never go back to the office every day?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Penso che sia meglio alternare, così vediamo anche i colleghi.
lines.en: I think it is better to alternate, that way we also see our colleagues.

days.title: Ribattere e concedere
days.objective: You can concede part of the other person's argument and then answer it, instead of simply repeating your own view.
days.paretoFocus: Granting a point before countering it is the single move that makes a learner sound like a real debater rather than a list of sentences.
drills: Take any opinion you disagree with and concede one true part of it out loud with È vero che.
Follow each concession immediately with però or tuttavia and your counterpoint.
Build three sentences with benché and check that the verb after it is subjunctive.

items: Name three ways to concede a point before disagreeing.
Which connectors introduce the counter-move after a concession?
What mood follows benché, and why?

activities.title: Sono d'accordo, però
activities.intro: The strongest reply has two beats: first you admit what is true in the other view, then you turn it round. È vero che and Certo, si può dire che open the concession; però, tuttavia and va detto che open the answer. Benché packs both beats into one sentence and always takes the subjunctive.

items.hr: È vero che i prezzi sono aumentati, però gli stipendi sono rimasti uguali.
items.en: It is true that prices have gone up, but wages have stayed the same.
items.note: è vero che + indicativo, then però

items.hr: Certo, si può dire che il centro sia più comodo, tuttavia costa troppo.
items.en: Of course, you could say the centre is more convenient, however it costs too much.
items.note: si può dire che takes the subjunctive: sia

items.hr: Capisco il tuo punto di vista, ma non sono del tutto d'accordo.
items.en: I understand your point of view, but I do not entirely agree.
items.note: il punto di vista; non del tutto softens the disagreement

items.hr: Benché la città sia rumorosa, io non la lascerei mai.
items.en: Although the city is noisy, I would never leave it.
items.note: benché always takes the subjunctive

items.hr: D'accordo in parte, va detto però che non tutti hanno la stessa possibilità.
items.en: I agree in part, but it has to be said that not everyone has the same opportunity.
items.note: va detto però che introduces the objection

activities.title: Concedere e poi rispondere
activities.intro: Practise the two-beat move: admit something true, then counter it.

questions.prompt: Benché il corso ___ caro, molti studenti si iscrivono lo stesso.
questions.explanation: Benché is a concessive conjunction and always requires the subjunctive, so essere becomes sia.

questions.prompt: Your friend says the flat is too small. Which reply concedes first and then counters?
options: È vero che è piccolo, però la posizione è ottima.
Hai proprio ragione, allora lasciamo perdere.
Secondo me è piccolo anche il tuo.
questions.answer: È vero che è piccolo, però la posizione è ottima.
questions.explanation: Only this reply admits the true part with È vero che and then turns it round with però.

questions.prompt: Benché ___ (piovere) da tre giorni, la partita non è stata rinviata.
questions.explanation: After benché the verb is subjunctive: piovere becomes piova.

questions.prompt: Certo, si può dire che il servizio ___ (essere) migliorato, ma i tempi restano lunghi.
questions.explanation: Si può dire che introduces something presented as arguable, so the verb takes the subjunctive.

questions.prompt: Build the Italian for: 'It is true that it costs a lot but it lasts for years.'
questions.explanation: È vero che opens the concession and ma introduces the counterpoint.

activities.title: L'automobile in città
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. Your friend defends driving in the city; you concede a point each time before answering it.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: In macchina arrivo ovunque in dieci minuti.
lines.en: By car I get anywhere in ten minutes.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: È vero che è comoda, però trovare parcheggio è un incubo.
lines.en: It is true that it is convenient, but finding a parking space is a nightmare.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Con l'autobus però perdi un sacco di tempo.
lines.en: With the bus, though, you waste a lot of time.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Certo, si può dire che sia più lento, tuttavia leggo mentre viaggio.
lines.en: Certainly, you could say it is slower, however I read while I travel.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: E quando piove? Alla fermata ti bagni.
lines.en: And when it rains? You get wet at the stop.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Benché piova spesso, in dieci anni mi sarò bagnato tre volte.
lines.en: Although it rains often, in ten years I have got wet maybe three times.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Quindi non useresti mai la macchina in centro?
lines.en: So you would never use the car in the centre?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Capisco il tuo punto di vista, ma va detto che il centro è piccolo.
lines.en: I understand your point of view, but it has to be said that the centre is small.

days.title: Negoziare e trovare un compromesso
days.objective: You can propose a compromise in Italian and attach a condition to what you are offering.
days.paretoFocus: Real conversations end in an agreement, not in two opinions; the language of conditions is what gets you there.
drills: Think of something you want and offer a trade out loud using Se tu, io.
Attach a condition to an offer three times using a patto che plus a subjunctive verb.
Close a negotiation with Facciamo così and two concrete tasks, one for each person.

items: How do you propose meeting halfway in Italian?
Which mood follows a patto che?
Say a full offer and its condition in one sentence.

activities.title: Una via di mezzo
activities.intro: Negotiating in Italian runs on offers with conditions attached. Se plus the present indicative makes a simple trade, while a patto che and purché make the condition explicit and take the subjunctive. Facciamo così is the everyday way of sealing the deal and dividing the tasks.

items.hr: Potremmo trovare una via di mezzo.
items.en: We could find a middle ground.
items.note: la via di mezzo; potremmo softens the proposal

items.hr: Se tu accetti di aspettare una settimana, io ti faccio uno sconto.
items.en: If you agree to wait a week, I will give you a discount.
items.note: lo sconto; se + presente for a real trade

items.hr: Che ne dici se ci incontriamo a metà strada?
items.en: What do you say if we meet halfway?
items.note: che ne dici se invites a counter-offer

items.hr: Sarei disposto a pagare di più, a patto che la consegna sia rapida.
items.en: I would be willing to pay more, provided the delivery is quick.
items.note: a patto che + congiuntivo; la consegna

items.hr: Facciamo così: tu prenoti il ristorante e io porto il dolce.
items.en: Let's do this: you book the restaurant and I will bring the dessert.
items.note: il dolce; facciamo così closes the deal

activities.title: Trovare un accordo
activities.intro: Practise offers, conditions and the phrases that close a deal.

questions.prompt: Accetto il tuo prezzo, a patto che tu me lo ___ entro venerdì.
questions.explanation: A patto che introduces a condition and takes the subjunctive: consegnare becomes tu consegni.

questions.prompt: Which sentence actually proposes a compromise rather than restating a position?
options: Che ne dici se ci incontriamo a metà strada?
Ne ho abbastanza di questa storia.
questions.answer: Che ne dici se ci incontriamo a metà strada?
questions.explanation: Che ne dici se opens a concrete proposal and hands the turn to the other person.

questions.prompt: Se tu ___ (accettare) di cambiare data, io pago la differenza.
questions.explanation: A real, likely condition uses se plus the present indicative: tu accetti.

questions.prompt: Vengo volentieri alla riunione, a patto che ___ (finire) prima delle sette.
questions.explanation: After a patto che the verb is subjunctive, and finire takes the isc form: finisca.

questions.prompt: Build the Italian for: 'We could find a middle ground.'
questions.explanation: The conditional potremmo plus the infinitive makes the suggestion polite.

activities.title: La bicicletta usata
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. You are buying a second-hand bicycle and want a lower price; offer conditions until you reach a deal.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: La bicicletta costa duecento euro, è quasi nuova.
lines.en: The bicycle costs two hundred euros, it is almost new.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Potremmo trovare una via di mezzo? Io pensavo a centosessanta.
lines.en: Could we find a middle ground? I was thinking of a hundred and sixty.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Centosessanta mi sembra davvero poco.
lines.en: A hundred and sixty seems really low to me.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Sarei disposto a darti centottanta, a patto che tu cambi le gomme.
lines.en: I would be willing to give you a hundred and eighty, provided you change the tyres.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Le gomme costano, però ci posso pensare.
lines.en: The tyres are expensive, but I can think about it.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Se tu accetti, io vengo a ritirarla domani e ti pago subito.
lines.en: If you agree, I will come and collect it tomorrow and pay you straight away.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Va bene, ma il lucchetto non è incluso.
lines.en: All right, but the lock is not included.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Facciamo così: tu metti le gomme nuove e io il lucchetto lo compro da solo.
lines.en: Let's do this: you put on the new tyres and I will buy the lock myself.

days.title: Fare una presentazione breve
days.objective: You can structure a two minute spoken presentation with an opening, signposts between parts, and a closing.
days.paretoFocus: Listeners forgive mistakes but not confusion; announcing the structure and signposting each move makes any talk sound prepared.
drills: Announce a topic and its three parts out loud in two sentences.
Move between parts three times using Passiamo ora and In secondo luogo.
Close a talk with a summary sentence and a thank you, without trailing off.

items: How do you announce the structure of your talk in one sentence?
Name two phrases that move the listener from one part to the next.
Which phrases signal that you are about to finish?

activities.title: Il mio intervento in tre parti
activities.intro: A short talk needs an opening, visible joints and a clear ending. Italian uses a small set of fixed signposts: prima di tutto, in secondo luogo, passiamo ora a, per concludere. Say the structure at the start so the listener knows how many parts to expect.

items.hr: Buongiorno a tutti, oggi vi parlo del riciclo nel mio quartiere.
items.en: Good morning everyone, today I am going to talk to you about recycling in my neighbourhood.
items.note: il riciclo, il quartiere

items.hr: Il mio intervento si divide in tre parti.
items.en: My talk is divided into three parts.
items.note: l'intervento is the usual word for a short talk

items.hr: Prima di tutto vorrei spiegare qual è il problema.
items.en: First of all I would like to explain what the problem is.
items.note: vorrei plus infinitive sounds measured, not abrupt

items.hr: Passiamo ora al secondo punto, cioè le possibili soluzioni.
items.en: Let's now move on to the second point, that is the possible solutions.
items.note: passiamo ora a; cioè introduces the explanation

items.hr: Per concludere, vi ringrazio dell'attenzione.
items.en: To conclude, thank you for your attention.
items.note: ringraziare di; l'attenzione

activities.title: Le tappe di un discorso
activities.intro: Practise the fixed phrases that hold a short talk together.

questions.prompt: Which phrase tells the listener that the talk is about to end?
questions.explanation: Per concludere signals the final move; the other three all point to parts still to come.

questions.prompt: Buongiorno a tutti, oggi ___ parlo del volontariato nella mia città.
questions.explanation: Parlare takes an indirect object, and the audience is voi, so the pronoun is vi.

questions.prompt: Announce the shape of your talk: 'Il mio intervento si ___ in tre parti.'
questions.explanation: Dividersi in is the standard verb for splitting a talk into sections.

questions.prompt: Move the listener forward: 'Passiamo ___ al secondo punto.'
questions.explanation: Passiamo ora a marks the joint between one section and the next.

questions.prompt: Build the Italian for: 'Today I am going to talk to you about my work.'
questions.explanation: The pronoun vi goes before the verb, and parlare di contracts with the article: del.

activities.title: Prima di parlare in pubblico
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. A colleague checks how you have organised your short talk, and you describe its parts.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Allora, come hai organizzato l'intervento di domani?
lines.en: So, how have you organised tomorrow's talk?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Il mio intervento si divide in tre parti, dura circa cinque minuti.
lines.en: My talk is divided into three parts, it lasts about five minutes.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: E come cominci?
lines.en: And how do you start?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Prima di tutto spiego qual è il problema, con due numeri chiari.
lines.en: First of all I explain what the problem is, with two clear figures.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Poi passi alle soluzioni?
lines.en: Then you move on to the solutions?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Sì, passo ora al secondo punto e propongo tre soluzioni concrete.
lines.en: Yes, I then move on to the second point and propose three concrete solutions.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: E la fine? Spesso è la parte più debole.
lines.en: And the ending? It is often the weakest part.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Per concludere ripeto l'idea principale e ringrazio dell'attenzione.
lines.en: To conclude I repeat the main idea and thank them for their attention.

days.title: Descrivere dati e grafici
days.objective: You can describe a trend or a proportion in Italian using the right verb and the right preposition.
days.paretoFocus: Numbers appear in news, at work and in reading tasks, and one wrong preposition after aumentare changes the whole meaning.
drills: Describe a rise and a fall out loud using aumentare di and diminuire di with percentages.
Say three proportions using la maggior parte di, un quarto di and quasi la metà di.
Compare two years in one sentence using rispetto a.

items: Which preposition follows aumentare when you give a percentage?
How do you say that most of the people interviewed think something?
How do you compare this year with last year in one phrase?

activities.title: Il grafico dice che
activities.intro: Talking about data needs three tools: a verb of movement, a quantity with the right preposition, and a point of comparison. Aumentare and diminuire take di before the size of the change, and rispetto a introduces what you are comparing with. Adverbs like leggermente and notevolmente say how big the change was.

items.hr: Il grafico mostra l'andamento delle iscrizioni negli ultimi cinque anni.
items.en: The chart shows the trend in enrolments over the last five years.
items.note: il grafico, l'andamento, l'iscrizione

items.hr: Il numero degli studenti è aumentato del venti per cento.
items.en: The number of students has risen by twenty per cent.
items.note: aumentare di, contracted with the article: del

items.hr: Le vendite sono diminuite leggermente rispetto all'anno scorso.
items.en: Sales have fallen slightly compared with last year.
items.note: diminuite agrees with le vendite; rispetto a

items.hr: La maggior parte degli intervistati preferisce lavorare da casa.
items.en: Most of the people interviewed prefer working from home.
items.note: la maggior parte takes a singular verb

items.hr: Solo un quarto delle persone usa ancora il contante.
items.en: Only a quarter of people still use cash.
items.note: il contante; un quarto di, contracted: delle

activities.title: Numeri e percentuali
activities.intro: Practise the verbs, prepositions and proportions used to describe data.

questions.prompt: Il prezzo del biglietto è aumentato ___ dieci per cento.
questions.explanation: Aumentare takes di before the size of the change, and di plus il contracts to del.

questions.prompt: La maggior parte dei ragazzi ___ lo smartphone anche a tavola.
questions.explanation: La maggior parte is grammatically singular, so the verb stays in the third person singular.

questions.prompt: Compare the two years: 'Le spese sono diminuite ___ a quelle dell'anno scorso.'
questions.explanation: Rispetto a is the fixed phrase for comparing one figure with another.

questions.prompt: Il grafico ___ l'andamento dei prezzi negli ultimi tre anni.
questions.explanation: Mostrare is the standard verb for what a chart displays.

questions.prompt: Build the Italian for: 'Only a quarter of people still use cash.'
questions.explanation: Un quarto di contracts to delle, and the verb stays singular because the subject is un quarto.

activities.title: I dati del sondaggio
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. You are showing a colleague the results of a survey and describing the numbers.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Hai visto i risultati del sondaggio?
lines.en: Have you seen the survey results?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Sì, il grafico mostra l'andamento degli ultimi tre anni.
lines.en: Yes, the chart shows the trend of the last three years.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: E come sono andate le iscrizioni?
lines.en: And how did enrolments go?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Sono aumentate del quindici per cento rispetto all'anno scorso.
lines.en: They have risen by fifteen per cent compared with last year.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Ottimo. E che cosa chiedeva la seconda domanda?
lines.en: Excellent. And what did the second question ask?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: La maggior parte degli intervistati preferisce i corsi della sera.
lines.en: Most of the people interviewed prefer evening courses.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Quindi quelli del mattino non interessano più a nessuno?
lines.en: So nobody is interested in the morning ones any more?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Sono calati leggermente, ma un quarto delle persone li sceglie ancora.
lines.en: They have dropped slightly, but a quarter of people still choose them.

days.title: Riassumere con parole proprie
days.objective: You can keep talking when the exact word will not come, by defining it, giving a synonym, or naming its opposite.
days.paretoFocus: Every conversation eventually hits a word you do not know; paraphrasing is what turns a dead stop into a small detour.
drills: Choose five objects around you and define each one with È quella cosa che si usa per.
Describe three words using only their opposite plus non.
Retell something you read today in two sentences beginning with In poche parole.

items: Give two phrases that buy you time while you search for a word.
How do you define an object by its function?
How do you introduce a two sentence summary of a longer text?

activities.title: Quando la parola non viene
activities.intro: When a word escapes you, Italian gives you four ways round it: a synonym, a definition by function, an example, or the opposite with non. Two fillers buy you the seconds you need, Non mi viene la parola and Come si dice. Saying something approximate always beats saying nothing.

items.hr: Non mi viene la parola, intendo una cosa tipo un cassetto ma più grande.
items.en: The word will not come to me, I mean something like a drawer but bigger.
items.note: il cassetto; una cosa tipo buys time

items.hr: È quell'oggetto che si usa per aprire le bottiglie.
items.en: It is that object you use for opening bottles.
items.note: l'oggetto; definition by function with si usa per

items.hr: Non è economico, cioè costa parecchio.
items.en: It is not cheap, that is, it costs quite a lot.
items.note: the opposite plus non, then cioè to clarify

items.hr: In poche parole, il testo dice che i giovani leggono meno di prima.
items.en: In short, the text says that young people read less than before.
items.note: in poche parole opens a summary

items.hr: Come si dice quella persona che ripara i tubi? Ecco, l'idraulico.
items.en: How do you say that person who fixes pipes? There we are, the plumber.
items.note: il tubo, l'idraulico; come si dice keeps the turn

activities.title: Girare intorno alla parola
activities.intro: Practise the strategies and the fillers that keep you talking.

questions.prompt: You cannot remember the word for a corkscrew. Which reply keeps the conversation moving?
options: È quell'oggetto che si usa per aprire le bottiglie di vino.
questions.answer: È quell'oggetto che si usa per aprire le bottiglie di vino.
questions.explanation: Defining the object by its function gets the meaning across without the missing noun.

questions.prompt: Which sentence explains a word by using its opposite?
options: Non è pulito, cioè è sporchissimo.
Si usa in cucina ogni giorno.
questions.answer: Non è pulito, cioè è sporchissimo.
questions.explanation: The speaker negates the opposite quality, pulito, to arrive at the meaning.

questions.prompt: Define it by function: 'È l'oggetto ___ si usa per tagliare il pane.'
questions.explanation: The relative pronoun che links the object to what it is used for and never changes form.

questions.prompt: Open your summary: 'In poche ___, l'articolo parla del costo delle case.'
questions.explanation: In poche parole is the fixed phrase for compressing a long text into a short statement.

questions.prompt: Build the Italian for: 'I mean something like a big bag.'
questions.explanation: Una cosa tipo introduces an approximation, and the adjective follows the noun.

activities.title: Quell'affare per il pane
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. You want to buy a toaster but cannot recall the word, so you describe it until you get there.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Buongiorno, mi dica, che cosa cerca?
lines.en: Good morning, tell me, what are you looking for?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Allora, non mi viene la parola, cerco una cosa per la cucina.
lines.en: Right, the word will not come to me, I am looking for something for the kitchen.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Una pentola? Un piatto?
lines.en: A pot? A plate?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: No, è quell'oggetto che si usa per scaldare il pane la mattina.
lines.en: No, it is that object you use to heat bread in the morning.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Ah, intende un forno piccolo?
lines.en: Ah, do you mean a small oven?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Non è un forno, cioè è più piccolo e ha due fessure sopra.
lines.en: It is not an oven, I mean it is smaller and has two slots on top.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Ho capito, il tostapane! Ne abbiamo tre modelli.
lines.en: I see, the toaster! We have three models.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Esatto, il tostapane. In poche parole, cerco il più semplice che avete.
lines.en: Exactly, the toaster. In short, I am looking for the simplest one you have.

days.title: Chiarire un malinteso
days.objective: You can repair a misunderstanding in Italian, restate what you meant, and confirm the corrected version.
days.paretoFocus: Misunderstandings are constant in a second language, and the repair phrases are short, fixed and endlessly reusable.
drills: Say three times, out loud, that you did not express yourself well, using different phrases.
Correct a wrong assumption using Non intendevo dire, volevo dire.
Close three repairs with Allora siamo d'accordo and the corrected detail.

items: How do you take the blame for a misunderstanding politely?
Which tense follows pensavo che, and why?
How do you confirm the corrected arrangement at the end?

activities.title: Forse non mi sono spiegato
activities.intro: A repair has three beats: take the blame, restate, confirm. Italians usually take the blame themselves with non mi sono spiegato bene, which keeps the tone friendly. Pensavo che and credevo che refer to a past belief, so they take the imperfect subjunctive, fosse or avesse.

items.hr: Scusa, forse non mi sono spiegato bene.
items.en: Sorry, maybe I did not explain myself well.
items.note: spiegato agrees with a male speaker; a woman says spiegata

items.hr: Non intendevo dire questo, volevo dire un'altra cosa.
items.en: I did not mean to say that, I meant something else.
items.note: intendevo and volevo, imperfect for what was in your mind

items.hr: Credo che ci sia stato un malinteso.
items.en: I think there has been a misunderstanding.
items.note: il malinteso; credo che takes the subjunctive

items.hr: Fammi capire: tu pensavi che la riunione fosse domani?
items.en: Let me get this straight: you thought the meeting was tomorrow?
items.note: pensavi che + congiuntivo imperfetto: fosse

items.hr: Allora siamo d'accordo: ci vediamo alle sei, non alle sette.
items.en: So we agree: we are meeting at six, not at seven.
items.note: confirming the corrected detail prevents the next mix-up

activities.title: Rimettere le cose a posto
activities.intro: Practise the phrases that repair a misunderstanding and confirm the correction.

questions.prompt: Credo che ci ___ stato un malinteso sull'orario.
questions.explanation: Credo che takes the subjunctive, and the perfect subjunctive of esserci is ci sia stato.

questions.prompt: Your friend understood the opposite of what you said. Which reply repairs it most politely?
options: Scusa, forse non mi sono spiegata bene.
questions.answer: Scusa, forse non mi sono spiegata bene.
questions.explanation: Taking the blame yourself repairs the exchange without accusing the other person.

questions.prompt: Pensavo che il negozio ___ (essere) aperto anche la domenica.
questions.explanation: A past belief with pensavo che takes the imperfect subjunctive: fosse.

questions.prompt: Correct the assumption: 'Non ___ dire che era colpa tua, volevo dire un'altra cosa.'
questions.explanation: Intendere in the imperfect describes what you had in mind at the time you spoke.

questions.prompt: Build the Italian for: 'I think there has been a misunderstanding.'
questions.explanation: The particle ci comes before the subjunctive sia, and stato agrees with the masculine malinteso.

activities.title: L'appuntamento sbagliato
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. Your friend turned up on the wrong day; repair the misunderstanding and confirm the new arrangement.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Ti ho aspettato al bar per un'ora, dov'eri?
lines.en: I waited for you at the cafe for an hour, where were you?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Scusa, credo che ci sia stato un malinteso.
lines.en: Sorry, I think there has been a misunderstanding.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Ma avevi scritto giovedì pomeriggio.
lines.en: But you wrote Thursday afternoon.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Forse non mi sono spiegato bene, intendevo giovedì prossimo.
lines.en: Maybe I did not explain myself well, I meant next Thursday.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Ah, io pensavo che fosse questa settimana.
lines.en: Ah, I thought it was this week.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Fammi capire: tu pensavi che avessimo già deciso tutto?
lines.en: Let me get this straight: you thought we had already decided everything?

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Sì, per me era una cosa fatta.
lines.en: Yes, for me it was settled.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Mi dispiace davvero. Allora siamo d'accordo: giovedì prossimo alle quattro.
lines.en: I am really sorry. So we agree: next Thursday at four.

days.title: Fare un reclamo a voce
days.objective: You can complain in person about a faulty product or a poor service, state the facts, and ask for a specific remedy.
days.paretoFocus: A complaint only works if it names the facts and the remedy; the conditional is what keeps it firm without becoming rude.
drills: Open a complaint three times with Avrei un problema con, changing the object each time.
State one problem in two facts: what happened and when.
Ask for a refund, a replacement and a manager, each in one polite sentence.

items: How do you open a complaint politely rather than aggressively?
Which conditional forms keep a firm request courteous?
How do you say that nobody called you back?

activities.title: Ho un problema con l'ordine
activities.intro: An effective spoken complaint moves from problem to facts to request. The conditional forms avrei, vorrei and preferirei make the request firm but polite, which is exactly the register Italians expect at a counter. Say what you want to happen, otherwise the other person decides for you.

items.hr: Buongiorno, avrei un problema con l'ordine che ho ricevuto ieri.
items.en: Good morning, I have a problem with the order I received yesterday.
items.note: avrei is softer than ho and opens the complaint politely

items.hr: Il pacco è arrivato con tre giorni di ritardo e la scatola era rotta.
items.en: The parcel arrived three days late and the box was broken.
items.note: il pacco, il ritardo, la scatola

items.hr: Vorrei sapere se è possibile avere un rimborso.
items.en: I would like to know whether it is possible to get a refund.
items.note: il rimborso; vorrei sapere se introduces an indirect question

items.hr: Ho già chiamato la settimana scorsa, ma nessuno mi ha richiamato.
items.en: I already called last week, but nobody called me back.
items.note: richiamare; nessuno takes a singular verb

items.hr: Preferirei parlare con il responsabile, per favore.
items.en: I would prefer to speak to the manager, please.
items.note: il responsabile is the usual word for the person in charge

activities.title: Protestare con garbo
activities.intro: Practise stating the problem and asking for a specific remedy.

questions.prompt: ___ un problema con la mia prenotazione: la camera non è quella che avevo scelto.
questions.explanation: The conditional avrei opens the complaint in the polite register expected at a desk.

questions.prompt: Which sentence asks for a concrete remedy rather than only describing the problem?
options: Vorrei sapere se è possibile avere un rimborso.
Sono arrivato alle otto e non c'era nessuno.
questions.answer: Vorrei sapere se è possibile avere un rimborso.
questions.explanation: Only this one names what the speaker wants to happen next; the others stop at the facts.

questions.prompt: State the fact: 'Il pacco è arrivato con due giorni di ___ e mancava un pezzo.'
questions.explanation: Con due giorni di ritardo is the fixed way of saying how late something was.

questions.prompt: Ask for the person in charge: 'Preferirei ___ con il responsabile, per cortesia.'
questions.explanation: After the conditional preferirei the second verb stays in the infinitive.

questions.prompt: Build the Italian for: 'I would like to know if I can get a refund.'
questions.explanation: Vorrei sapere se opens an indirect question, so the word order stays that of a statement.

activities.title: Alla cassa del negozio
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. A lamp you bought arrived damaged; complain at the counter and ask for a replacement or a refund.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Buongiorno, come posso aiutarla?
lines.en: Good morning, how can I help you?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Buongiorno, avrei un problema con la lampada che ho comprato sabato.
lines.en: Good morning, I have a problem with the lamp I bought on Saturday.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Mi dica pure, che cosa è successo?
lines.en: Go ahead, what happened?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: È arrivata con la scatola rotta e il vetro è danneggiato.
lines.en: It arrived with the box broken and the glass is damaged.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Ha lo scontrino con sé?
lines.en: Do you have the receipt with you?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Sì, eccolo. Ho già chiamato lunedì, ma nessuno mi ha richiamato.
lines.en: Yes, here it is. I already called on Monday, but nobody called me back.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Mi dispiace, possiamo sostituirla entro una settimana.
lines.en: I am sorry, we can replace it within a week.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Vorrei sapere se è possibile avere un rimborso, mi serviva subito.
lines.en: I would like to know if I can have a refund, I needed it straight away.

days.title: Telefonare in ambito professionale
days.objective: You can handle a professional phone call in Italian: announce yourself, ask to be put through, cope with a bad line, and agree a follow up.
days.paretoFocus: On the phone you lose gestures and lip reading, so the fixed formulas and the formal Lei do all the work.
drills: Announce yourself and your reason for calling in two sentences.
Say three times that the line is bad and that you will call back.
Turn five informal phone sentences into the formal Lei form.

items: How do you introduce yourself on the phone in Italian?
How do you ask someone to hold the line formally?
How do you propose calling back later without sounding rude?

activities.title: Pronto, sono Marco
activities.intro: Italian phone calls have their own formulas, starting with Pronto and sono plus your name, never mi chiamo. In the professional register everything runs on Lei, so the object pronoun is La and the indirect one is Le. The formal imperative uses the subjunctive form: resti, scusi, mi dica.

items.hr: Pronto, buongiorno, sono Marco Rossi. La disturbo?
items.en: Hello, good morning, this is Marco Rossi. Am I disturbing you?
items.note: sono plus your name on the phone; La disturbo uses the formal object pronoun

items.hr: Le passo la collega, resti in linea un attimo.
items.en: I will put you through to my colleague, please hold the line a moment.
items.note: la linea; resti is the formal imperative of restare

items.hr: Purtroppo la linea è disturbata, La sento male.
items.en: Unfortunately the line is bad, I can hardly hear you.
items.note: sentire male is the standard way of saying you cannot hear

items.hr: Le dispiace se La richiamo tra dieci minuti?
items.en: Do you mind if I call you back in ten minutes?
items.note: Le dispiace se is the polite way of asking permission

items.hr: La ringrazio, Le mando una email di conferma entro stasera.
items.en: Thank you, I will send you a confirmation email by this evening.
items.note: la conferma; entro means by a deadline, not within a duration

activities.title: Al telefono con il cliente
activities.intro: Practise the fixed phone formulas and the formal Lei forms.

questions.prompt: Signora Bianchi, ___ passo il direttore, resti in linea.
questions.explanation: Passare qualcosa a qualcuno takes an indirect object, so the formal pronoun is Le, not La.

questions.prompt: How do you announce yourself when someone answers the phone?
questions.explanation: On the telephone Italians identify themselves with sono plus their name.

questions.prompt: Ask the caller to wait: 'Un attimo per favore, resti in ___.'
questions.explanation: Restare in linea is the fixed phrase for holding on during a call.

questions.prompt: Signora, ___ (scusare) il disturbo, chiamo solo per un'informazione.
questions.explanation: The formal imperative uses the subjunctive form of the verb: scusare becomes scusi.

questions.prompt: Build the Italian for: 'May I call you back in ten minutes?' (formal)
questions.explanation: The formal object pronoun La attaches to the end of the infinitive: richiamarLa.

activities.title: Una chiamata di lavoro
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. You call a company to speak to a manager, struggle with the connection, then settle on a next step.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Pronto, buongiorno, ufficio acquisti.
lines.en: Hello, good morning, purchasing department.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Buongiorno, sono Elena Conti. Potrei parlare con la dottoressa Marini?
lines.en: Good morning, this is Elena Conti. Could I speak to Doctor Marini?

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Un attimo, Le passo il suo ufficio. Resti in linea.
lines.en: One moment, I will put you through to her office. Please hold the line.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Grazie. Scusi, la linea è disturbata, La sento male.
lines.en: Thank you. Sorry, the line is bad, I can hardly hear you.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Ora mi sente meglio? La dottoressa è in riunione fino alle cinque.
lines.en: Can you hear me better now? Doctor Marini is in a meeting until five.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Le dispiace se richiamo verso le cinque e mezza?
lines.en: Do you mind if I call back around half past five?

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Nessun problema, le lascio un messaggio sulla scrivania.
lines.en: No problem, I will leave her a message on her desk.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: La ringrazio, intanto Le mando una email di conferma entro stasera.
lines.en: Thank you, meanwhile I will send you a confirmation email by this evening.

days.title: Le sfumature dei sentimenti
days.objective: You can describe how you feel with precise adjectives and the preposition each one takes.
days.paretoFocus: Bene and male cover almost nothing; six precise feeling adjectives make your Italian sound adult instead of elementary.
drills: Describe three recent situations using deluso, sollevato and seccato, with the right agreement.
Say what you are proud of and what you are worried about, watching the prepositions.
Turn five sentences with sono contento into a more precise adjective.

items: Which preposition follows deluso, and which follows orgoglioso?
What is the difference in strength between seccato and arrabbiato?
How do the endings change when a woman describes herself?

activities.title: Non solo bene o male
activities.intro: Italian feeling adjectives each carry their own preposition, and getting it wrong is more noticeable than getting the adjective wrong. Deluso goes with da, orgoglioso with di, preoccupato and seccato with per. All of them agree with the person speaking, so a woman says delusa, sollevata, commossa.

items.hr: Sono rimasto deluso dal risultato dell'esame.
items.en: I was disappointed by the exam result.
items.note: deluso da; rimanere deluso is more idiomatic than essere deluso

items.hr: Mi sento sollevata: alla fine è andato tutto bene.
items.en: I feel relieved: in the end everything went well.
items.note: sollevato means relieved after worry, not lifted

items.hr: Sono un po' seccato per il ritardo del treno.
items.en: I am a bit annoyed about the train delay.
items.note: seccato per; milder than arrabbiato

items.hr: Siamo molto orgogliosi di nostra figlia.
items.en: We are very proud of our daughter.
items.note: orgoglioso di; no article before nostra figlia

items.hr: Ero preoccupata per lui, ma il suo discorso mi ha commossa.
items.en: I was worried about him, but his speech moved me.
items.note: preoccupato per; commosso means moved to tears

activities.title: Dire come ti senti davvero
activities.intro: Practise precise feeling adjectives with their prepositions and agreements.

questions.prompt: I miei genitori sono orgogliosi ___ me.
questions.explanation: Orgoglioso always takes di before the person or thing you are proud of.

questions.prompt: The exam result was much worse than you hoped. Which sentence fits best?
options: Mi sento sollevato per il voto.
questions.explanation: Deluso describes the gap between what you hoped for and what happened.

questions.prompt: A woman speaking: 'Sono rimasta ___ dalla sua risposta, mi aspettavo molto di più.'
questions.explanation: Deluso agrees with a female speaker, giving delusa, and takes da.

questions.prompt: Mia madre era molto ___ per il viaggio di mio fratello in montagna.
questions.explanation: Preoccupato per expresses worry about something, and here it agrees with mia madre.

questions.prompt: Build the Italian for: 'I am relieved because everything went well.'
questions.explanation: Andare forms the past with essere, so the participle andato agrees with tutto.

activities.title: Dopo il colloquio
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. A friend asks how your job interview went, and you describe your feelings precisely.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Allora, come è andato il colloquio?
lines.en: So, how did the interview go?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Meglio del previsto, mi sento sollevato.
lines.en: Better than expected, I feel relieved.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Eri agitato prima di entrare?
lines.en: Were you nervous before going in?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Ero preoccupato per le domande tecniche, ma erano semplici.
lines.en: I was worried about the technical questions, but they were easy.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: E la parte sullo stipendio?
lines.en: And the part about the salary?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Sinceramente sono rimasto un po' deluso dalla loro offerta.
lines.en: Honestly I was a bit disappointed by their offer.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Però hai passato tre selezioni su cento candidati.
lines.en: But you got through three rounds out of a hundred candidates.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Hai ragione, di questo sono davvero orgoglioso.
lines.en: You are right, I am really proud of that.

days.title: Raccontare un ricordo
days.objective: You can tell a childhood memory in Italian, using the imperfect for the background and the passato prossimo for the events.
days.paretoFocus: Narration is half of every real conversation, and the imperfect against the passato prossimo is what makes a story sound like a story.
drills: Describe three habits from your childhood using Da bambino and the imperfect.
Interrupt a background scene with a single event using Mentre plus the imperfect.
Tell a two sentence memory: one line of scene, one line of what happened.

items: Which tense carries repeated actions and which carries the single event?
What tense follows mentre in a narrative?
How do you say you remember something as if it were yesterday?

activities.title: Da bambino, ogni estate
activities.intro: Italian narration works on a split: the imperfect paints the background and the repeated habits, the passato prossimo delivers the events that move the story on. Mentre always introduces a background action, so it takes the imperfect. Come se pushes the verb into the imperfect subjunctive.

items.hr: Da bambino passavo tutte le estati dai nonni, in campagna.
items.en: As a child I spent every summer at my grandparents' place, in the countryside.
items.note: la campagna; dai nonni means at their home

items.hr: Ogni mattina mi svegliavo presto e andavo a prendere il pane.
items.en: Every morning I woke up early and went to get the bread.
items.note: repeated habit, so the imperfect throughout

items.hr: Un giorno ho trovato un gattino sotto il portone.
items.en: One day I found a kitten under the front door.
items.note: il gattino, il portone; single event, passato prossimo

items.hr: Mentre giocavamo in giardino, è arrivato un temporale.
items.en: While we were playing in the garden, a storm arrived.
items.note: il temporale; mentre + imperfetto, then the event

items.hr: Me lo ricordo come se fosse ieri.
items.en: I remember it as if it were yesterday.
items.note: come se + congiuntivo imperfetto, always

activities.title: Sfondo ed evento
activities.intro: Practise choosing between the imperfect and the passato prossimo in a story.

questions.prompt: Mentre ___ la cena, mio figlio è tornato da scuola.
questions.explanation: Mentre introduces the ongoing background action, which always takes the imperfect.

questions.prompt: Which sentence describes a repeated childhood habit rather than one event?
options: Un giorno ho perso le chiavi di casa.
Ieri sera abbiamo mangiato una pizza.
D'estate andavamo al fiume ogni pomeriggio.
Sono nato in una piccola città.
questions.answer: D'estate andavamo al fiume ogni pomeriggio.
questions.explanation: Ogni pomeriggio plus the imperfect andavamo marks a habit repeated over time.

questions.prompt: Mentre ___ (leggere, io) in salotto, è suonato il telefono.
questions.explanation: The background action after mentre goes into the imperfect: leggevo.

questions.prompt: Un pomeriggio d'inverno ___ (trovare, io) una vecchia foto dei miei genitori.
questions.explanation: A single completed event in a story takes the passato prossimo: ho trovato.

questions.prompt: Build the Italian for: 'I remember it as if it were yesterday.'
questions.explanation: The double pronoun me lo comes before the verb, and come se requires fosse.

activities.title: Le estati dai nonni
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. A friend asks about your childhood summers; keep the habits in the imperfect and switch tense for the one thing that happened.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Dove passavi le vacanze da bambino?
lines.en: Where did you spend your holidays as a child?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Da bambino passavo tutte le estati dai nonni, in campagna.
lines.en: As a child I spent every summer at my grandparents' place, in the countryside.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: E che cosa facevate tutto il giorno?
lines.en: And what did you do all day?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Ogni mattina mi svegliavo presto e andavo a prendere il pane.
lines.en: Every morning I woke up early and went to get the bread.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Ti ricordi qualcosa di particolare?
lines.en: Do you remember anything special?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Sì, un giorno ho trovato un gattino sotto il portone.
lines.en: Yes, one day I found a kitten under the front door.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: E l'avete tenuto?
lines.en: And did you keep it?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Certo, si chiamava Nuvola. Me lo ricordo come se fosse ieri.
lines.en: Of course, it was called Nuvola. I remember it as if it were yesterday.

days.title: Parlare di progetti e desideri
days.objective: You can talk about firm plans, vague wishes, and unreal situations, keeping the three apart.
days.paretoFocus: Mixing up a plan and a wish confuses your listener; the conditional plus the imperfect subjunctive marks the difference cleanly.
drills: State two firm plans using ho intenzione di and penso di.
Say three wishes with mi piacerebbe plus an infinitive.
Build three unreal sentences with Se avessi and a conditional main clause.

items: How do you announce a firm plan as opposed to a wish?
Which two verb forms make up the unreal conditional?
How do you say you hope to manage to do something?

activities.title: Progetti, sogni e ipotesi
activities.intro: Italian separates what you have decided from what you would like and from what is not real. Ho intenzione di marks a decision, mi piacerebbe marks a wish, and the pair se plus imperfect subjunctive with a conditional marks a situation that is not the case. Notice that sperare and riuscire both take di before an infinitive.

items.hr: L'anno prossimo ho intenzione di cambiare lavoro.
items.en: Next year I intend to change job.
items.note: avere intenzione di, a decision already taken

items.hr: Mi piacerebbe vivere qualche mese all'estero.
items.en: I would like to live abroad for a few months.
items.note: l'estero; qualche is always followed by a singular noun

items.hr: Se vivessi al mare, nuoterei ogni mattina.
items.en: If I lived by the sea, I would swim every morning.
items.note: se + congiuntivo imperfetto, then the conditional

items.hr: Spero di riuscire a finire il corso entro giugno.
items.en: I hope to manage to finish the course by June.
items.note: sperare di, riuscire a

items.hr: Il mio sogno sarebbe aprire un piccolo ristorante.
items.en: My dream would be to open a small restaurant.
items.note: il sogno; the conditional sarebbe keeps it modest

activities.title: Se avessi tempo
activities.intro: Practise plans, wishes and the unreal conditional.

questions.prompt: Se avessi più soldi, ___ un mese in Sudamerica.
questions.explanation: In an unreal condition the se clause takes the imperfect subjunctive and the main clause the conditional.

questions.prompt: Which sentence describes a decision already taken rather than a wish?
options: Il mio sogno sarebbe vivere al mare.
L'anno prossimo ho intenzione di cambiare casa.
questions.answer: L'anno prossimo ho intenzione di cambiare casa.
questions.explanation: Avere intenzione di reports a plan the speaker has already settled on.

questions.prompt: Se ___ (potere, io) scegliere, lavorerei solo quattro giorni alla settimana.
questions.explanation: After se in an unreal condition the verb takes the imperfect subjunctive: potessi.

questions.prompt: State the plan: 'Ho ___ di iscrivermi a un corso di nuoto a settembre.'
questions.explanation: Avere intenzione di plus an infinitive is the standard way of announcing a plan.

questions.prompt: Build the Italian for: 'I would like to live abroad for a few months.'
questions.explanation: Piacerebbe takes an indirect pronoun and an infinitive, and qualche stays with a singular noun.

activities.title: I piani per l'anno prossimo
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. A friend asks about your plans; separate what you have decided from what you only wish for.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Hai già deciso che cosa farai l'anno prossimo?
lines.en: Have you already decided what you will do next year?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Sì, ho intenzione di cambiare lavoro entro la primavera.
lines.en: Yes, I intend to change job by the spring.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Bello. E hai qualche sogno nel cassetto?
lines.en: Nice. And do you have a secret dream?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Mi piacerebbe vivere qualche mese all'estero, magari in Spagna.
lines.en: I would like to live abroad for a few months, maybe in Spain.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: E perché non lo fai?
lines.en: And why don't you do it?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Se avessi più tempo libero, partirei anche domani.
lines.en: If I had more free time, I would leave tomorrow.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Intanto stai finendo il corso di spagnolo, no?
lines.en: In the meantime you are finishing the Spanish course, aren't you?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Sì, spero di riuscire a finirlo entro giugno.
lines.en: Yes, I hope to manage to finish it by June.

days.title: Dare consigli e riceverli
days.objective: You can give advice in Italian at three levels of directness and respond gracefully when advice is given to you.
days.paretoFocus: Advice comes up constantly with friends, colleagues and doctors, and the wrong level of directness sounds either rude or evasive.
drills: Give the same piece of advice three ways: Se fossi in te, Dovresti, Perché non provi a.
Use ti conviene twice with a practical reason attached.
Accept advice out loud without simply saying grazie.

items: Which verb form follows Se fossi in te in the main clause?
What does ti conviene add that dovresti does not?
How do you accept advice without committing to it?

activities.title: Se fossi in te
activities.intro: Italian grades advice by how directly it tells you what to do. Dovresti is the plain conditional, Se fossi in te frames it as what you would do, and Perché non provi a is the gentlest of the three. Ti conviene is different again: it says the advice is in your practical interest.

items.hr: Se fossi in te, ne parlerei subito con il tuo capo.
items.en: If I were you, I would talk to your boss about it straight away.
items.note: se fossi in te + condizionale; ne replaces di questo

items.hr: Ti conviene prenotare adesso, dopo i prezzi salgono.
items.en: You would be better off booking now, prices go up later.
items.note: convenire is about practical advantage

items.hr: Perché non provi a scrivergli una email?
items.en: Why don't you try writing him an email?
items.note: provare a; the pronoun gli attaches to the infinitive

items.hr: Dovresti riposarti un po' di più, hai una faccia stanca.
items.en: You should rest a bit more, you look tired.
items.note: dovresti softens dovere into advice

items.hr: Grazie del consiglio, ci penserò con calma.
items.en: Thanks for the advice, I will think it over calmly.
items.note: il consiglio; pensarci means to think it over

activities.title: Consigli fra amici
activities.intro: Practise the three levels of advice and the ways of accepting it.

questions.prompt: Se fossi in te, ___ subito il medico.
questions.explanation: Se fossi in te sets up an unreal condition, so the main clause takes the conditional.

questions.prompt: Which sentence gives advice by pointing at your practical advantage?
options: Ti conviene comprare il biglietto oggi.
Non so proprio che cosa dirti.
Io al posto tuo sarei già partito.
questions.answer: Ti conviene comprare il biglietto oggi.
questions.explanation: Convenire says the action is in the listener's own interest, usually for cost or time.

questions.prompt: Point at the advantage: 'Ti ___ partire presto, alle otto c'è già traffico.'
questions.explanation: Convenire is used impersonally with an indirect pronoun: ti conviene.

questions.prompt: ___ (dovere, tu) riposarti di più, lavori troppe ore.
questions.explanation: The conditional dovresti turns an obligation into a piece of advice.

questions.prompt: Build the Italian for: 'If I were you I would speak to the teacher.'
questions.explanation: Se fossi in te is fixed, and the advice itself goes into the conditional parlerei.

activities.title: Un problema al lavoro
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. Your friend has a problem with a colleague; give advice at different levels of directness.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Il mio collega mi lascia sempre il lavoro più pesante.
lines.en: My colleague always leaves me the hardest work.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Se fossi in te, ne parlerei subito con lui, senza aspettare.
lines.en: If I were you, I would talk to him about it straight away, without waiting.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Ho paura di litigare, è un tipo permaloso.
lines.en: I am afraid of arguing, he is a touchy sort.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Allora perché non provi a scrivergli un messaggio tranquillo?
lines.en: Then why don't you try writing him a calm message?

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: E se non risponde?
lines.en: And if he doesn't reply?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Ti conviene parlarne con la responsabile, così resta scritto.
lines.en: You would be better off talking to the manager about it, that way there is a record.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Forse hai ragione, ci sto pensando da settimane.
lines.en: Maybe you are right, I have been thinking about it for weeks.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Dovresti farlo presto, altrimenti la situazione peggiora.
lines.en: You should do it soon, otherwise the situation gets worse.

days.title: La conversazione informale e il suo avvio
days.objective: You can start a conversation with a stranger in Italian, keep it going for a few turns, and close it naturally.
days.paretoFocus: The opening line is the hardest part of any real conversation, and Italians use a very small set of them.
drills: Open three conversations with a question about the shared situation, not about the person.
Add a tag with vero to three statements and hear how it invites a reply.
Close a chat twice without simply saying ciao.

items: What is the safest opening line with a stranger at a bus stop?
How does Senti change the direction of a conversation?
How do you close a friendly chat and leave the door open?

activities.title: Attaccare bottone
activities.intro: Italians open conversations with strangers by commenting on the shared situation, not by introducing themselves. Sai per caso softens a question into a small favour, and a statement plus vero invites agreement. Senti and Dai are the two little words that steer an informal chat.

items.hr: Ciao, scusa, sai per caso a che ora apre la biblioteca?
items.en: Hi, sorry, do you happen to know what time the library opens?
items.note: la biblioteca; sai per caso softens the request

items.hr: Anche tu aspetti l'autobus delle otto?
items.en: Are you waiting for the eight o'clock bus too?
items.note: anche tu turns a shared situation into an opening

items.hr: Che caldo oggi, vero?
items.en: It's so hot today, isn't it?
items.note: vero invites agreement, like a question tag

items.hr: Senti, ma tu di dove sei?
items.en: Listen, where are you from?
items.note: senti introduces a change of subject

items.hr: Dai, ci sentiamo uno di questi giorni.
items.en: Come on, let's be in touch one of these days.
items.note: ci sentiamo closes a chat and leaves it open

activities.title: Rompere il ghiaccio
activities.intro: Practise the openings, tags and closings of an informal chat.

questions.prompt: You are waiting at a bus stop next to someone you do not know. Which opening sounds most natural?
options: Come ti chiami e dove abiti?
Anche tu aspetti l'autobus delle otto?
Vorrei parlare con te di una cosa importante.
Ti dispiace se ti faccio molte domande?
questions.answer: Anche tu aspetti l'autobus delle otto?
questions.explanation: Italians open with the situation both people share, which is much less intrusive than a personal question.

questions.prompt: Che freddo stasera, ___?
questions.explanation: Vero works as a question tag and invites the other person to agree and keep talking.

questions.prompt: Soften the question: 'Scusa, sai per ___ dove si trova la farmacia più vicina?'
questions.explanation: Sai per caso turns a direct question into a small, easily refused favour.

questions.prompt: Close the chat warmly: 'Dai, ci ___ uno di questi giorni.'
questions.explanation: Sentirsi means to be in touch, and ci sentiamo is the standard friendly sign off.

questions.prompt: Build the Italian for: 'Do you happen to know what time the shop closes?'
questions.explanation: Sai per caso stays together, and a che ora introduces the indirect question.

activities.title: Alla fermata dell'autobus
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. Start a chat with the person waiting beside you, keep it going, then close it.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Uff, questo autobus è sempre in ritardo.
lines.en: Ugh, this bus is always late.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Anche tu aspetti quello delle otto? Che caldo oggi, vero?
lines.en: Are you waiting for the eight o'clock one too? It's so hot today, isn't it?

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Da morire. E domani dicono che farà anche peggio.
lines.en: Unbearable. And tomorrow they say it will be even worse.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Senti, sai per caso se questo passa vicino alla biblioteca?
lines.en: Listen, do you happen to know if this one goes near the library?

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Sì, scendi alla terza fermata, quella dopo il ponte.
lines.en: Yes, get off at the third stop, the one after the bridge.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Grazie mille, non lo sapevo. Tu vai sempre da quella parte?
lines.en: Thanks a lot, I did not know that. Do you always go that way?

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Ogni mattina, lavoro proprio lì accanto.
lines.en: Every morning, I work right next door.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Allora ci vedremo spesso. Dai, ci sentiamo uno di questi giorni.
lines.en: Then we will see each other often. Come on, let's be in touch one of these days.

days.title: Gestire il tempo e le priorità
days.objective: You can explain in Italian how you organise your time, what comes first, and what you have postponed.
days.paretoFocus: Talking about priorities is a favourite topic in interviews and in exam prompts, and it reuses sequencing language you need everywhere.
drills: Describe your morning in three sentences using Prima, poi and Infine.
Say twice what you have postponed and to when, using rimandare a.
Explain one habit using Cerco di plus an infinitive.

items: How do you order two actions in one sentence?
Which verb means to postpone, and which preposition follows it?
How do you say something is urgent and something else can wait?

activities.title: Prima le cose urgenti
activities.intro: Organising time in Italian uses a small set of sequencing words and two key verbs: rimandare, to postpone, and dedicare, to devote. Cerco di plus an infinitive is how you describe an intention you do not always keep. Urgente and può aspettare are the two poles of any priority list.

items.hr: Prima finisco questa relazione, poi rispondo alle email.
items.en: First I finish this report, then I answer the emails.
items.note: la relazione; prima and poi order the actions

items.hr: Questa faccenda è urgente, il resto può aspettare.
items.en: This matter is urgent, the rest can wait.
items.note: la faccenda is an everyday word for a matter to deal with

items.hr: Cerco di dedicare mezz'ora al giorno allo studio.
items.en: I try to devote half an hour a day to studying.
items.note: cercare di, dedicare qualcosa a qualcosa

items.hr: Ho rimandato la telefonata a domani mattina.
items.en: I have put the phone call off until tomorrow morning.
items.note: rimandare a; la telefonata

items.hr: Meglio fare poco ma con costanza che tutto in un giorno solo.
items.en: Better to do a little but consistently than everything in a single day.
items.note: la costanza; meglio A che B compares two options

activities.title: Prima, poi, infine
activities.intro: Practise sequencing, postponing and describing your routines.

questions.prompt: Ho rimandato la visita ___ settimana prossima.
questions.explanation: Rimandare takes a before the new date or time to which something is moved.

questions.prompt: Which sentence says you try to do something regularly without claiming you always manage?
options: Studio sempre due ore al giorno.
Cerco di studiare due ore al giorno.
Devo studiare due ore al giorno.
questions.answer: Cerco di studiare due ore al giorno.
questions.explanation: Cercare di states the intention and quietly admits it does not always happen.

questions.prompt: Say what you moved: 'Ho ___ la riunione a lunedì perché mancava metà squadra.'
questions.explanation: Rimandare means to postpone, and here it forms the passato prossimo with avere.

questions.prompt: Cerco di ___ mezz'ora al giorno alla lettura, di solito la sera.
questions.explanation: Dedicare qualcosa a qualcosa is the natural verb for giving time to an activity.

questions.prompt: Build the Italian for: 'First I finish this, then I answer the emails.'
questions.explanation: Prima and poi frame the two actions, and rispondere takes a, contracted to alle.

activities.title: Una giornata piena
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. A colleague wants your time today; explain your priorities and which tasks slipped to next week.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Hai dieci minuti adesso per il nuovo progetto?
lines.en: Do you have ten minutes now for the new project?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Adesso no, prima finisco questa relazione, poi rispondo alle email.
lines.en: Not right now, first I finish this report, then I answer the emails.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Ma il progetto è importante.
lines.en: But the project is important.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Lo so, però questa faccenda è urgente, il resto può aspettare.
lines.en: I know, but this matter is urgent, the rest can wait.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: E la riunione di stamattina come è andata?
lines.en: And how did this morning's meeting go?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: L'ho rimandata a lunedì, mancava metà squadra.
lines.en: I postponed it until Monday, half the team was missing.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Riesci a trovare tempo per il progetto questa settimana?
lines.en: Can you find time for the project this week?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Cerco di dedicargli un'ora al giorno, meglio poco ma con costanza.
lines.en: I try to devote an hour a day to it, better a little but consistently.

days.title: Ascolto, cogliere l'essenziale
days.objective: You can follow the gist and the speaker's attitude in a recording without understanding every word.
days.paretoFocus: In the listening module you hear each recording a limited number of times, so chasing individual words loses you the whole answer.
drills: Listen to two minutes of Italian audio and write one sentence saying what it was about.
Note three words you did not know, then continue listening without stopping.
For each speaker, decide in one word whether their attitude was positive, negative or neutral.

items: What should you listen for first: the topic, the details, or the numbers?
Which phrases signal that a speaker is unenthusiastic while sounding polite?
What do you do when you miss a sentence completely?

activities.title: Capire il senso generale
activities.intro: The strategy for this task is to listen for the speaker's overall position first and the details second. Italian understates disagreement, so poteva andare meglio and diciamo che are quiet ways of saying something was bad. If you miss a sentence, let it go and keep following the thread, because the answer usually depends on the whole turn, not one word.

items.hr: Alla fine ho deciso di non iscrivermi al corso: costava troppo.
items.en: In the end I decided not to enrol on the course: it was too expensive.
items.note: the decision is the answer, the price is only the reason

items.hr: Il tono era chiaro: non era per niente convinta.
items.en: The tone was clear: she was not at all convinced.
items.note: non per niente is a strong negative

items.hr: Sinceramente, non me l'aspettavo così bello.
items.en: Honestly, I was not expecting it to be so good.
items.note: aspettarsi; a negative form carrying a positive surprise

items.hr: Diciamo che poteva andare meglio.
items.en: Let's say it could have gone better.
items.note: understatement, so the real judgement is negative

items.hr: Non ho capito ogni parola, ma ho afferrato il senso generale.
items.en: I did not understand every word, but I got the general sense.
items.note: afferrare il senso is exactly what this task asks for

activities.title: Il tono di chi parla
activities.intro: Practise reading attitude and gist rather than isolated words.

questions.prompt: A speaker says: 'Diciamo che poteva andare meglio.' What is their attitude?
questions.explanation: Poteva andare meglio is polite understatement: the speaker is disappointed but avoids saying so directly.

questions.prompt: You miss one whole sentence of a recording. What is the best thing to do?
options: Fermarsi e ripensare a quella frase.
Continuare ad ascoltare e seguire il filo del discorso.
Scrivere tutte le parole che si riescono a sentire.
Tradurre mentalmente ogni parola nella propria lingua.
questions.answer: Continuare ad ascoltare e seguire il filo del discorso.
questions.explanation: Stopping to recover one sentence costs you the next three; the overall thread carries the answer.

questions.prompt: Describe what you managed to follow: 'Non ho capito ogni parola, ma ho afferrato il ___ generale.'
questions.explanation: Il senso generale is the gist, which is what this task type is testing.

questions.prompt: Report a strong negative reaction: 'Si vedeva dalla faccia che non era per ___ convinta.'
questions.explanation: Non per niente after a negative verb intensifies it: not at all convinced.

questions.prompt: Build the Italian for: 'Honestly, I was not expecting it.'
questions.explanation: Aspettarsi is reflexive, so the pronouns me and l' both come before the verb.

activities.title: Che cosa hai capito?
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. A study partner asks what you understood from a recording; report the gist and the attitude, not the words.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Allora, di che cosa parlava la registrazione?
lines.en: So, what was the recording about?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: In poche parole, una ragazza spiegava perché ha lasciato il corso.
lines.en: In short, a girl was explaining why she left the course.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Hai capito tutte le parole?
lines.en: Did you understand every word?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: No, ma non serviva: ho afferrato il senso generale.
lines.en: No, but it was not necessary: I got the general sense.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: E lei era contenta della sua decisione?
lines.en: And was she happy with her decision?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Dal tono direi di no, ha detto che poteva andare meglio.
lines.en: From her tone I would say no, she said it could have gone better.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Io invece avevo capito che era entusiasta.
lines.en: I had understood that she was enthusiastic.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Attenzione al tono: non era per niente convinta.
lines.en: Watch the tone: she was not at all convinced.

days.title: Ascolto, annunci e messaggi
days.objective: You can extract the changed detail from a public announcement or a voice message: the new time, the new platform, the reason.
days.paretoFocus: Announcements are built around a correction, and the word that signals the correction is worth more than the whole rest of the sentence.
drills: Say three announcements out loud that change a platform, a time and a gate.
Listen for anziché, invece di and non più in any recording and note what they replace.
Leave a thirty second voice message that changes an arrangement and gives a reason.

items: Which words signal that a detail has been changed?
How do you say a departure has been delayed by forty minutes?
Which verb form do automated messages use to give instructions?

activities.title: Attenzione, annuncio
activities.intro: An announcement almost always corrects an earlier piece of information, and anziché, invece di and non più are the words that flag the correction. Times, platforms and gates are the details worth catching. Automated systems address you formally, so their instructions use the formal imperative: prema, attenda, digiti.

items.hr: Il treno regionale delle nove e venti partirà dal binario sette, anziché dal binario tre.
items.en: The regional train at nine twenty will depart from platform seven, instead of platform three.
items.note: il binario; anziché flags the change

items.hr: Attenzione: la partenza è posticipata di quaranta minuti.
items.en: Attention: the departure has been put back by forty minutes.
items.note: la partenza; posticipare di plus the amount of delay
