items.hr: Ciao, sono Luca. Non riesco a venire alle otto, arrivo verso le nove.
items.en: Hi, it's Luca. I can't make it at eight, I will arrive around nine.
items.note: riuscire a; verso means approximately with times

items.hr: Il volo per Napoli è stato cancellato per motivi tecnici.
items.en: The flight to Naples has been cancelled for technical reasons.
items.note: il volo; per motivi plus adjective gives the reason

items.hr: Per parlare con un operatore, prema il tasto due.
items.en: To speak to an operator, press button two.
items.note: il tasto, l'operatore; prema is the formal imperative

activities.title: Cogliere il dettaglio che cambia
activities.intro: Practise catching the corrected detail and the instruction in an announcement.

questions.prompt: You hear: 'Il treno delle dieci partirà dal binario otto, anziché dal binario due.' Where should you go?
questions.explanation: Anziché introduces what is being replaced, so the platform named before it is the new one.

questions.prompt: Per prenotare un appuntamento, ___ il tasto uno.
questions.explanation: Automated messages use the formal Lei, whose imperative takes the subjunctive form prema.

questions.prompt: Announce the delay: 'La partenza del volo è ___ di venti minuti.'
questions.explanation: Posticipare means to push back to a later time, and the participle agrees with la partenza.

questions.prompt: Give the reason: 'Il collegamento è stato ___ per il forte maltempo, nessuno partirà oggi.'
questions.explanation: Cancellare in the passive with essere gives è stato cancellato, agreeing with il collegamento.

questions.prompt: Build the Italian for: 'The train leaves from platform seven.'
questions.explanation: Partire da contracts with the article to give dal binario.

activities.title: In stazione
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. You heard the announcement and your friend did not; report the changed details exactly.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Scusa, hai sentito l'annuncio? Io ero al bar.
lines.en: Sorry, did you hear the announcement? I was at the cafe.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Sì, il nostro treno partirà dal binario sette, anziché dal tre.
lines.en: Yes, our train will leave from platform seven, instead of platform three.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Ma allora dobbiamo attraversare tutta la stazione.
lines.en: But then we have to cross the whole station.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Con calma, la partenza è posticipata di quaranta minuti.
lines.en: Take it easy, the departure has been put back by forty minutes.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Quaranta minuti? Hanno detto perché?
lines.en: Forty minutes? Did they say why?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Per motivi tecnici, e un altro treno è stato cancellato.
lines.en: For technical reasons, and another train has been cancelled.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Provo a chiamare il numero verde per il rimborso.
lines.en: I will try to call the freephone number about the refund.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Ricorda: per parlare con un operatore devi premere il tasto due.
lines.en: Remember: to speak to an operator you have to press button two.

days.title: Lettura, trovare l'idea principale
days.objective: You can identify the main idea of an Italian text quickly and tell it apart from the examples that support it.
days.paretoFocus: Most wrong answers in a reading task come from picking a true detail instead of the idea the whole text is built around.
drills: Read the questions before the text, then read the text once without stopping.
For any Italian article, write the main idea in one sentence of your own words.
Underline the first and last sentence of each paragraph and check whether the idea lives there.

items: Where in a paragraph does the main idea usually sit?
Which phrases announce that a summary of the argument is coming?
How do you tell an example apart from the claim it supports?

activities.title: Qual è il punto del testo
activities.intro: Read the questions first, then read the text once for the shape of the argument. The main idea usually sits in the first or last sentence of a paragraph, and phrases like in sintesi and l'autore sostiene che point straight at it. Everything introduced by per esempio or infatti is support, never the claim itself.

items.hr: In sintesi, l'articolo sostiene che le città piccole stanno tornando attraenti.
items.en: In short, the article argues that small towns are becoming attractive again.
items.note: sostenere che introduces the central claim

items.hr: L'autore vuole soprattutto spiegare perché i giovani si trasferiscono.
items.en: The author mainly wants to explain why young people move away.
items.note: l'autore, trasferirsi; soprattutto marks the priority

items.hr: Il resto del testo serve solo a dare esempi concreti.
items.en: The rest of the text only serves to give concrete examples.
items.note: servire a plus infinitive

items.hr: Il titolo e la prima frase bastano spesso per capire l'argomento.
items.en: The title and the first sentence are often enough to understand the topic.
items.note: il titolo, l'argomento; bastare per

items.hr: Attenzione ai connettivi come infatti, invece e al contrario.
items.en: Pay attention to connectives such as in fact, instead and on the contrary.
items.note: il connettivo; these words show how the argument turns

activities.title: Idea centrale o dettaglio
activities.intro: Practise telling a writer's central claim apart from the illustrations underneath it.

questions.prompt: A text explains that many families are leaving big cities, then gives three family stories. Which is the main idea?
options: Una famiglia si è trasferita in Umbria nel duemilaventi.
Sempre più famiglie lasciano le grandi città.
Gli affitti a Milano sono molto alti.
Una madre lavora da casa tre giorni alla settimana.
questions.answer: Sempre più famiglie lasciano le grandi città.
questions.explanation: The three stories are examples; the claim they all support is the general statement about families leaving.

questions.prompt: What should you do before reading the text in a reading task?
options: Leggere le domande, per sapere che cosa cercare.
Tradurre il titolo parola per parola.
Leggere il testo tre volte lentamente.
questions.answer: Leggere le domande, per sapere che cosa cercare.
questions.explanation: Reading the questions first turns the reading into a targeted search instead of a general effort.

questions.prompt: Open your one sentence summary: 'In ___, l'articolo parla del costo degli affitti in centro.'
questions.explanation: In sintesi introduces a compressed statement of the whole text.

questions.prompt: Name who is making the claim: 'L'___ sostiene che il lavoro da casa conviene alle aziende.'
questions.explanation: L'autore takes the elided article because autore begins with a vowel.

questions.prompt: Build the Italian for: 'The author wants to explain why prices are rising.'
questions.explanation: Volere is followed directly by the infinitive, with no preposition in between.

activities.title: Di che cosa parla l'articolo
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. A study partner has read the same article; agree on the main idea and set the details aside.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Hai letto l'articolo sulle città piccole?
lines.en: Have you read the article about small towns?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Sì, in sintesi sostiene che stanno tornando attraenti per i giovani.
lines.en: Yes, in short it argues that they are becoming attractive again for young people.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Io avevo capito che parlasse degli affitti di Milano.
lines.en: I had understood that it was about rents in Milan.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Quello è solo un esempio, il resto del testo serve a sostenere l'idea.
lines.en: That is only an example, the rest of the text serves to support the idea.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Come fai a distinguere l'idea principale dai dettagli?
lines.en: How do you tell the main idea from the details?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Guardo il titolo e la prima frase di ogni paragrafo.
lines.en: I look at the title and the first sentence of each paragraph.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: E le parole che non conosci?
lines.en: And the words you do not know?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Le salto, però seguo i connettivi come invece e al contrario.
lines.en: I skip them, but I follow the connectives like instead and on the contrary.

days.title: Lettura, abbinare testi e informazioni
days.objective: You can match short adverts or profiles to people's needs by hunting for the detail that rules an option out.
days.paretoFocus: In matching tasks two options always look right; only the disqualifying detail separates them.
drills: Read four short adverts and write, for each, the one condition that could rule it out.
For any set of needs, list the non negotiable requirement before you start matching.
Practise the phrases tranne, a parte and però on three real adverts.

items: Should you scan for what matches or for what excludes?
Which words introduce an exception in Italian?
What do you do when two options both seem to fit?

activities.title: Il dettaglio che esclude
activities.intro: In a matching task, start from the requirement the person cannot give up, then scan each text for anything that breaks it. Italian marks these exceptions with tranne, a parte, senza and però, and they are usually placed at the end of the text. If two options match, the difference is always one small excluded condition.

items.hr: Cerco una casa con giardino, ma senza scale.
items.en: I am looking for a house with a garden, but without stairs.
items.note: le scale; senza marks the non negotiable condition

items.hr: Questo appartamento ha il giardino, però è al terzo piano senza ascensore.
items.en: This flat has a garden, but it is on the third floor with no lift.
items.note: l'ascensore, il piano; the detail that rules it out

items.hr: Il corso sarebbe perfetto, tranne per l'orario: si tiene solo di mattina.
items.en: The course would be perfect, except for the timetable: it is only held in the morning.
items.note: l'orario; tenersi means to be held

items.hr: Va bene tutto, a parte il prezzo.
items.en: Everything is fine, apart from the price.
items.note: a parte introduces the single exception

items.hr: Conviene sottolineare la condizione che esclude, non quella che coincide.
items.en: It is worth underlining the condition that excludes, not the one that matches.
items.note: sottolineare, coincidere

activities.title: Chi va con che cosa
activities.intro: Practise finding the detail that eliminates an option.

questions.prompt: Giulia works mornings and wants an evening course near her home. Which advert must be excluded?
options: Corso serale in centro, due volte alla settimana.
Corso di gruppo il martedì alle diciannove, vicino alla stazione.
Corso intensivo dal lunedì al venerdì, dalle nove alle dodici.
Corso serale con lezioni brevi, a dieci minuti a piedi.
questions.answer: Corso intensivo dal lunedì al venerdì, dalle nove alle dodici.
questions.explanation: Only this one runs in the morning, which is precisely when Giulia cannot attend.

questions.prompt: Two adverts both match a person's needs. What should you check first?
options: Quale annuncio contiene una condizione che la persona non accetta.
Quale annuncio usa parole più difficili.
Quale annuncio viene prima nel testo.
questions.answer: Quale annuncio contiene una condizione che la persona non accetta.
questions.explanation: Matching tasks are decided by exclusion: the extra condition is what makes one of the two wrong.

questions.prompt: Name the single exception: 'L'appartamento mi piace tutto, a ___ il prezzo troppo alto.'
questions.explanation: A parte introduces the one element that does not fit, leaving everything else intact.

questions.prompt: Flag the problem: 'La casa ha il giardino, ___ è al terzo piano e non c'è ascensore.'
questions.explanation: Però introduces the drawback that makes the option unsuitable.

questions.prompt: Build the Italian for: 'I am looking for a flat with a garden but without stairs.'
questions.explanation: Con and senza both come before their noun, and no article is needed after them here.

activities.title: Scegliere l'annuncio giusto
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. You are helping a friend choose between adverts; eliminate the ones with a disqualifying detail.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Ho trovato quattro annunci, ma non so quale scegliere.
lines.en: I have found four adverts, but I do not know which to choose.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Partiamo da quello che non puoi accettare: tu cerchi senza scale, giusto?
lines.en: Let's start from what you cannot accept: you are looking for somewhere without stairs, right?

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Esatto, e possibilmente con un piccolo giardino.
lines.en: Exactly, and if possible with a small garden.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Allora il secondo lo escludiamo: ha il giardino, però è al terzo piano.
lines.en: Then we rule out the second one: it has a garden, but it is on the third floor.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: E il terzo? Mi sembrava perfetto.
lines.en: And the third? It seemed perfect to me.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Va bene tutto, a parte il prezzo: costa duecento euro in più.
lines.en: Everything is fine, apart from the price: it costs two hundred euros more.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Quindi restano il primo e il quarto.
lines.en: So the first and the fourth are left.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Sì, e il quarto sarebbe perfetto, tranne per la distanza dal lavoro.
lines.en: Yes, and the fourth would be perfect, except for the distance from work.

days.title: Lettura, riconoscere le opinioni
days.objective: You can tell a reported fact from the writer's opinion, and spot the words that mark the difference.
days.paretoFocus: Reading questions often ask what the writer thinks, not what happened, and the answer hides in a handful of signal words.
drills: Take any Italian article and mark each sentence as fact or opinion.
List every hedging word you find: sembrerebbe, pare, a quanto pare.
Rewrite two factual sentences as opinions using A mio avviso.

items: Which phrases show that a statement is the writer's own view?
What does the conditional do to a claim in a news text?
How can you tell a fact from an opinion when neither is signposted?

activities.title: Fatto o opinione
activities.intro: Italian texts flag opinions with secondo, a mio avviso and evaluative adjectives like discutibile. Facts arrive with figures and neutral verbs such as riporta and dice. The conditional, sembrerebbe or sarebbe, is how a writer reports something without taking responsibility for it.

items.hr: Secondo l'autore, la riforma non ha dato i risultati sperati.
items.en: According to the author, the reform has not produced the hoped for results.
items.note: la riforma; secondo marks whose view it is

items.hr: I dati dicono che le iscrizioni sono calate del dieci per cento.
items.en: The figures say that enrolments have fallen by ten per cent.
items.note: i dati; a fact, with a number attached

items.hr: A mio avviso si tratta di una scelta discutibile.
items.en: In my view it is a questionable choice.
items.note: a mio avviso; discutibile is an evaluation, not a fact

items.hr: Sembrerebbe che la situazione stia migliorando.
items.en: It would seem that the situation is improving.
items.note: the conditional keeps the writer at a distance; stia is subjunctive

items.hr: L'articolo riporta i fatti, ma il commento finale è chiaramente personale.
items.en: The article reports the facts, but the closing comment is clearly personal.
items.note: riportare, il commento

activities.title: Chi lo dice e quanto ci crede
activities.intro: Practise separating reported facts from the writer's own judgements.

questions.prompt: Which sentence expresses an opinion rather than a fact?
options: Le iscrizioni sono calate del dieci per cento.
Il corso è iniziato a settembre.
Si tratta di una scelta davvero discutibile.
questions.answer: Si tratta di una scelta davvero discutibile.
questions.explanation: Discutibile is an evaluation; the other three report verifiable information.

questions.prompt: A text says 'Il provvedimento sarebbe già in vigore.' What does the conditional signal?
options: Che chi scrive è sicuro del fatto.
Che chi scrive riporta la notizia senza garantirla.
Che il fatto avverrà in futuro.
Che il fatto è già stato smentito.
questions.answer: Che chi scrive riporta la notizia senza garantirla.
questions.explanation: In news writing the conditional distances the writer from a claim that is not yet confirmed.

questions.prompt: Mark it clearly as your own view: 'A mio ___ la decisione è stata sbagliata.'
questions.explanation: A mio avviso is a slightly formal fixed phrase for in my view.

questions.prompt: ___ (sembrare) che la situazione stia migliorando, ma mancano ancora i dati. Usa il condizionale.
questions.explanation: The conditional of sembrare presents the claim as reported rather than established.

questions.prompt: Build the Italian for: 'According to the author, the reform did not work.'
questions.explanation: Secondo takes no preposition before the noun, and the negation sits before the auxiliary.

activities.title: Il commento in fondo al testo
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. Your study partner has mixed up a fact with the writer's opinion; sort the two out.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Il testo dice che la riforma è stata un fallimento.
lines.en: The text says the reform was a failure.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Attenzione: quella è l'opinione dell'autore, non un dato.
lines.en: Careful: that is the author's opinion, not a fact.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Come lo capisci?
lines.en: How can you tell?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Perché scrive a mio avviso e usa l'aggettivo discutibile.
lines.en: Because he writes in my view and uses the adjective questionable.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: E i numeri sulle iscrizioni?
lines.en: And the figures on enrolments?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Quelli sono fatti: i dati dicono che sono calate del dieci per cento.
lines.en: Those are facts: the figures say they have fallen by ten per cent.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Alla fine però scrive sembrerebbe che le cose migliorino.
lines.en: At the end though he writes it would seem that things are improving.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Esatto, con il condizionale prende le distanze da quella notizia.
lines.en: Exactly, with the conditional he distances himself from that piece of news.

days.title: Scrittura, una email formale
days.objective: You can write a formal Italian email with the correct greeting, a clear reason for writing, every required point, and a proper closing.
days.paretoFocus: Formal emails are graded on structure and register, and both are fixed formulas you can learn once and reuse forever.
drills: Write the greeting and closing for three different recipients, keeping the register consistent.
Turn one informal message into a formal email without changing the content.
Before writing, list the points the prompt asks for and tick each one off in your draft.

items: Which greeting goes with Cordiali saluti, and which with A presto?
How do you state the reason for writing in one sentence?
How do you ask for a reply without sounding demanding?

activities.title: Gentile Dottoressa
activities.intro: A formal email has five fixed parts: greeting, reason for writing, body covering every required point, request, closing. Register must match at both ends: Gentile pairs with Cordiali saluti, while an informal Ciao pairs with A presto. The polite Lei is capitalised in careful writing: la Sua risposta.

items.hr: Gentile Dottoressa Bianchi,
items.en: Dear Doctor Bianchi,
items.note: gentile plus title and surname is the standard formal opening

items.hr: Le scrivo in merito al corso di italiano che inizierà a settembre.
items.en: I am writing to you regarding the Italian course starting in September.
items.note: in merito a states the reason for writing

items.hr: Vorrei sapere se sono ancora disponibili dei posti e quanto costa l'iscrizione.
items.en: I would like to know whether there are still places available and how much enrolment costs.
items.note: one sentence can cover two of the required points

items.hr: Resto in attesa di una Sua risposta.
items.en: I look forward to your reply.
items.note: restare in attesa di; Sua is capitalised out of courtesy

items.hr: Cordiali saluti, Marta Neri
items.en: Kind regards, Marta Neri
items.note: cordiali saluti closes a formal email; a friend would get A presto

activities.title: Il registro giusto
activities.intro: Practise the fixed parts of a formal email and the register that holds them together.

questions.prompt: Your email begins with Gentile Dottoressa Bianchi. Which closing matches?
questions.explanation: The opening and closing must share the same register; Gentile requires a formal sign off.

questions.prompt: Which sentence states the reason for writing in the formal register?
options: Ti scrivo due righe per il corso.
Le scrivo in merito al corso di italiano.
Volevo solo chiederti una cosa al volo.
questions.answer: Le scrivo in merito al corso di italiano.
questions.explanation: Le scrivo uses the formal indirect pronoun, and in merito a is the formal way to state the subject.

questions.prompt: State the subject formally: 'Le scrivo in ___ alla Sua email del cinque marzo.'
questions.explanation: In merito a means regarding, and it belongs to the written formal register.

questions.prompt: Ask for a reply politely: 'Resto in ___ di una Sua risposta.'
questions.explanation: Restare in attesa di is the standard closing request in a formal email.

questions.prompt: Build the Italian for: 'I am writing to you to ask for some information.' (formal)
questions.explanation: The formal pronoun Le precedes the verb, and per plus infinitive gives the purpose.

activities.title: Controlliamo l'email
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. A friend checks your draft email against the points you were asked to cover.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Fammi vedere l'email. Come l'hai aperta?
lines.en: Let me see the email. How did you open it?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Con Gentile Dottoressa Bianchi, perché non la conosco.
lines.en: With Dear Doctor Bianchi, because I do not know her.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Bene. E si capisce subito perché scrivi?
lines.en: Good. And is it immediately clear why you are writing?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Sì, nella prima riga: Le scrivo in merito al corso di settembre.
lines.en: Yes, in the first line: I am writing regarding the September course.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Ti chiedevano tre cose: posti, prezzo e orario.
lines.en: They asked you three things: places, price and timetable.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Ci sono tutte e tre, ogni punto ha la sua frase.
lines.en: All three are there, each point has its own sentence.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: E come chiudi?
lines.en: And how do you close?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Resto in attesa di una Sua risposta, poi Cordiali saluti e il mio nome.
lines.en: I look forward to your reply, then Kind regards and my name.

days.title: Scrittura, un testo di opinione
days.objective: You can plan and write a short opinion text in Italian with an introduction, two sides, your position and a conclusion.
days.paretoFocus: Two minutes of planning is what separates a text that argues from a text that wanders, and the connectors do the rest.
drills: Spend two minutes writing only a plan: introduction, side one, side two, position, conclusion.
Write one paragraph using Da una parte and Dall'altra with no repetition of ideas.
Close three different texts with In conclusione and a sentence that adds something new.

items: What are the five parts of a short opinion text?
Which pair of connectors introduces the two sides of a question?
How do you make a conclusion do more than repeat the introduction?

activities.title: Da una parte, dall'altra
activities.intro: Plan before you write: introduction, one side, the other side, your position, conclusion. Da una parte and Dall'altra hold the two sides apart, while Personalmente ritengo che marks the moment your own voice enters and takes the subjunctive. Non A bensì B is a compact way of correcting a common assumption in the conclusion.

items.hr: Negli ultimi anni si discute molto se i compiti a casa siano davvero utili.
items.en: In recent years there has been a lot of debate about whether homework is really useful.
items.note: i compiti; se plus subjunctive in an indirect question about opinion

items.hr: Da una parte, i compiti aiutano a fissare quello che si impara in classe.
items.en: On the one hand, homework helps to fix what you learn in class.
items.note: aiutare a; fissare here means to consolidate

items.hr: Dall'altra, molti ragazzi passano il pomeriggio chiusi in casa.
items.en: On the other hand, many young people spend the afternoon shut up at home.
items.note: dall'altra picks up da una parte and needs no repetition

items.hr: Personalmente ritengo che ne servano meno, ma fatti meglio.
items.en: Personally I believe fewer are needed, but done better.
items.note: ritenere che takes the subjunctive; ne replaces di compiti

items.hr: In conclusione, il problema non è la quantità bensì la qualità.
items.en: In conclusion, the problem is not the quantity but rather the quality.
items.note: la quantità, la qualità; non A bensì B corrects an assumption

activities.title: Costruire un testo argomentativo
activities.intro: Practise the plan, the two sided connectors and the conclusion.

questions.prompt: You have written Da una parte in the second paragraph. How should the third begin?
questions.explanation: Da una parte and dall'altra are a fixed pair, and the reader is waiting for the second half.

questions.prompt: What should you do in the first two minutes of a written opinion task?
options: Scrivere subito l'introduzione per non perdere tempo.
Fare un piano con i punti e l'ordine dei paragrafi.
questions.answer: Fare un piano con i punti e l'ordine dei paragrafi.
questions.explanation: A two minute plan guarantees the structure and stops you from repeating yourself later.

questions.prompt: Complete the pair: 'Da una parte lavorare da casa è comodo, ___ si perde il contatto con i colleghi.'
questions.explanation: Dall'altra completes the pair and introduces the opposing consideration.

questions.prompt: Personalmente ritengo che ___ (essere) una scelta sbagliata, per due motivi.
questions.explanation: Ritenere che expresses a personal judgement and therefore takes the subjunctive.

questions.prompt: Build the Italian for: 'In conclusion, in my opinion it is worth trying.'
questions.explanation: Valere la pena is a fixed expression followed directly by the infinitive.

activities.title: Il piano del testo
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. A study partner asks how you organised your opinion text before writing it.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Hai cominciato subito a scrivere?
lines.en: Did you start writing straight away?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: No, ho fatto due minuti di piano: introduzione, due lati, conclusione.
lines.en: No, I did two minutes of planning: introduction, two sides, conclusion.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: E come hai aperto?
lines.en: And how did you open?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Ho scritto che negli ultimi anni si discute molto di questo tema.
lines.en: I wrote that in recent years this topic has been much debated.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: I due lati come li hai divisi?
lines.en: How did you divide the two sides?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Da una parte i vantaggi, dall'altra i problemi, un paragrafo ciascuno.
lines.en: On the one hand the advantages, on the other the problems, one paragraph each.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: E la tua opinione dove sta?
lines.en: And where is your own opinion?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Nel quarto paragrafo: personalmente ritengo che ne servano meno.
lines.en: In the fourth paragraph: personally I believe fewer are needed.

days.title: Scrittura, un reclamo
days.objective: You can write a written complaint in Italian that states the facts, names the remedy you want, and stays formal throughout.
days.paretoFocus: A written complaint that only expresses annoyance gets no result; naming the remedy is what makes it a request.
drills: Write the facts of one real problem in two sentences: what happened and when.
Ask for a replacement, then rewrite the same sentence asking for a refund.
Check a draft for any informal word that has slipped in and replace it.

items: Which greeting do you use when you do not know the person's name?
How do you introduce the remedy you are requesting?
Which closing formula is more formal than Cordiali saluti?

activities.title: Segnalare un problema per iscritto
activities.intro: A written complaint follows the same five part shape as any formal letter, but the body must contain facts with dates and the request must be explicit. Pertanto and di conseguenza mark the move from problem to request. Distinti saluti is a notch more formal and more distant than Cordiali saluti, which suits a complaint.

items.hr: Gentile Servizio Clienti,
items.en: Dear Customer Service,
items.note: used when you do not have a name; il servizio clienti

items.hr: Scrivo per segnalare un problema con il mio ordine del tre aprile.
items.en: I am writing to report a problem with my order of the third of April.
items.note: segnalare; the date makes the complaint traceable

items.hr: Il prodotto è arrivato danneggiato e mancava un pezzo.
items.en: The product arrived damaged and a part was missing.
items.note: danneggiato, il pezzo; facts, with no adjectives of anger

items.hr: Chiedo pertanto la sostituzione dell'articolo o il rimborso della somma pagata.
items.en: I therefore request the replacement of the item or a refund of the amount paid.
items.note: la sostituzione, la somma; pertanto introduces the request

items.hr: In attesa di un Vostro riscontro, porgo distinti saluti.
items.en: Awaiting your reply, I send my best regards.
items.note: il riscontro; Vostro because you address a company

activities.title: Fatti, richiesta, chiusura
activities.intro: Practise the formal complaint structure and its fixed phrases.

questions.prompt: You are writing to a company whose staff you do not know by name. Which opening is correct?
questions.explanation: When there is no named recipient, you address the department in the formal register.

questions.prompt: Which sentence turns a description of the problem into an actual request?
options: Sono davvero molto arrabbiato per questa situazione.
Il pacco è arrivato dopo dieci giorni.
Non è la prima volta che succede.
questions.explanation: Only this sentence names the remedy, which is what the company needs in order to act.

questions.prompt: State the fact plainly: 'Il prodotto è arrivato ___ e non è utilizzabile.'
questions.explanation: Danneggiato agrees with il prodotto and states the fact without expressing anger.

questions.prompt: Move from problem to request: 'Chiedo ___ la sostituzione dell'articolo entro dieci giorni.'
questions.explanation: Pertanto means therefore and belongs to the formal written register.

questions.prompt: Build the Italian for: 'I am writing to report a problem with my order.'
questions.explanation: Per plus the infinitive states the purpose, and segnalare is the formal verb for reporting a problem.

activities.title: Rileggere il reclamo
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. A colleague checks your written complaint before you send it.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Hai scritto a chi? Conosci il nome della persona?
lines.en: Who did you write to? Do you know the person's name?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: No, quindi ho aperto con Gentile Servizio Clienti.
lines.en: No, so I opened with Dear Customer Service.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Si capisce subito qual è il problema?
lines.en: Is it immediately clear what the problem is?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Sì: scrivo per segnalare un problema con l'ordine del tre aprile.
lines.en: Yes: I am writing to report a problem with the order of the third of April.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Hai scritto che sei arrabbiato?
lines.en: Did you write that you are angry?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: No, solo i fatti: è arrivato danneggiato e mancava un pezzo.
lines.en: No, just the facts: it arrived damaged and a part was missing.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: E che cosa chiedi esattamente?
lines.en: And what exactly are you asking for?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Chiedo pertanto la sostituzione o il rimborso, poi distinti saluti.
lines.en: I therefore request a replacement or a refund, then best regards.

days.title: Parlato, la presentazione
days.objective: You can deliver the spoken monologue by covering every point on the prompt card, in order, with an example for each.
days.paretoFocus: The marks are attached to the bullet points, so a fluent talk that misses one point scores below a simple talk that covers them all.
drills: Take any three part prompt and spend two minutes planning one sentence per point.
Deliver a two minute talk and check afterwards that no point was skipped.
Add one concrete example to each point, taken from your own life.

items: What is the first thing to do with a prompt card?
How do you announce which point you are answering now?
How do you signal that you have covered everything and finished?

activities.title: Rispondere a tutti i punti
activities.intro: The prompt card lists the points you must cover, and each of them carries marks. Plan for two minutes, one sentence per point, then speak. Announcing which point you are on keeps you and the listener oriented, and one concrete example per point is what lifts the talk above a list.

items.hr: Il tema che mi è stato assegnato riguarda il tempo libero dei giovani.
items.en: The topic I have been given concerns young people's free time.
items.note: assegnare; riguardare means to concern

items.hr: Rispondo prima alla domanda su quanto tempo libero ho, poi dico come lo passo.
items.en: I will answer first the question about how much free time I have, then say how I spend it.
items.note: announcing the order keeps the structure visible

items.hr: Un esempio concreto: il sabato mattina gioco a calcetto con gli amici.
items.en: A concrete example: on Saturday mornings I play five a side football with friends.
items.note: il calcetto; giocare a plus the sport

items.hr: Non ho ancora parlato dell'ultimo punto, cioè di come vorrei cambiarlo.
items.en: I have not yet spoken about the last point, that is how I would like to change it.
items.note: a useful self correction when you notice a gap

items.hr: Ho detto tutto quello che mi era stato chiesto, grazie.
items.en: I have said everything I was asked, thank you.
items.note: a clear ending is better than trailing off

activities.title: Coprire ogni punto
activities.intro: Practise the planning and the signposting the spoken task requires.

questions.prompt: The prompt card lists three points. What is the best use of your preparation time?
options: Scrivere il discorso parola per parola.
Preparare una frase per ogni punto, con un esempio.
Imparare a memoria una frase di apertura elegante.
questions.answer: Preparare una frase per ogni punto, con un esempio.
questions.explanation: Marks are attached to the points, so a sentence for each guarantees full coverage in the time available.

questions.prompt: You realise you have skipped a point halfway through. What is the best move?
options: Ignorarlo e continuare fino alla fine.
Dire che non si è ancora parlato di quel punto e affrontarlo.
questions.answer: Dire che non si è ancora parlato di quel punto e affrontarlo.
questions.explanation: Naming the gap and filling it recovers the marks; starting again wastes the time you have left.

questions.prompt: Introduce your topic: 'Il tema che mi è stato ___ riguarda lo sport nella vita quotidiana.'
questions.explanation: Assegnare in the passive gives mi è stato assegnato, agreeing with il tema.

questions.prompt: Make the point concrete: 'Un ___ concreto: il sabato pomeriggio vado sempre in piscina.'
questions.explanation: Naming the example explicitly signals to the listener that support is coming.

questions.prompt: Build the Italian for: 'First I answer the first question, then the second.'
questions.explanation: Rispondere takes a, and the article is not repeated with domanda in the second half.

activities.title: Prepariamo il monologo
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. A study partner reads you the points on a prompt card and checks your plan covers them all.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Il tema è il tempo libero. Ci sono tre punti da coprire.
lines.en: The topic is free time. There are three points to cover.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Bene, mi prendo due minuti e preparo una frase per ogni punto.
lines.en: Good, I will take two minutes and prepare one sentence for each point.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Il primo chiede quanto tempo libero hai.
lines.en: The first asks how much free time you have.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Rispondo prima a quello, poi dico come lo passo.
lines.en: I will answer that one first, then say how I spend it.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: E l'ultimo punto chiede come vorresti cambiarlo.
lines.en: And the last point asks how you would like to change it.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Giusto, non avevo ancora parlato di quello. Lo metto alla fine.
lines.en: Right, I had not spoken about that yet. I will put it at the end.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Hai un esempio concreto per il secondo punto?
lines.en: Do you have a concrete example for the second point?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Sì: il sabato mattina gioco a calcetto con gli amici da dieci anni.
lines.en: Yes: on Saturday mornings I have played five a side football with friends for ten years.

days.title: Parlato, il dialogo con l'esaminatore
days.objective: You can hold a two way discussion in Italian: make a point, invite the other person in, react to their answer, and reach a joint decision.
days.paretoFocus: Learners lose marks in the paired task by monologuing; the marks are for interaction, so handing over the turn is the skill being tested.
drills: Follow every opinion you state with a question that hands over the turn.
React to three statements before adding your own idea, using Hai ragione or Non ci avevo pensato.
Finish a discussion with a joint decision starting Allora siamo d'accordo.

items: Name three short questions that hand the turn to the other person.
How do you react to someone's idea before adding your own?
How do you close a discussion with a shared decision?

activities.title: Parlare in due
activities.intro: This task rewards interaction, not speeches. Every time you make a point, hand the turn over with a short question: Tu che ne pensi, E tu, Sei d'accordo. React to what you hear before adding your own idea, and finish by putting the shared decision into words so the conversation has an ending.

items.hr: Io comincerei dal budget. Tu che ne pensi?
items.en: I would start with the budget. What do you think?
items.note: il budget; the question hands over the turn immediately

items.hr: Hai ragione, non ci avevo pensato. Allora potremmo ridurre le spese del cibo.
items.en: You are right, I had not thought of that. So we could cut the food costs.
items.note: pensarci; react first, then add

items.hr: E tu, preferiresti il sabato o la domenica?
items.en: And you, would you prefer Saturday or Sunday?
items.note: offering two options makes the reply easy

items.hr: Su questo punto sono d'accordo con te, però aggiungerei una cosa.
items.en: On this point I agree with you, but I would add one thing.
items.note: agreeing and extending keeps the exchange moving

items.hr: Allora siamo d'accordo: decidiamo per il sabato pomeriggio.
items.en: So we agree: we will go for Saturday afternoon.
items.note: stating the joint decision closes the task properly

activities.title: Dare e prendere la parola
activities.intro: Practise handing over the turn, reacting, and closing with a shared decision.

questions.prompt: You have just given your opinion in a paired discussion. What should you do next?
options: Fare una domanda che passa la parola all'altra persona.
Aspettare in silenzio la domanda dell'esaminatore.
questions.answer: Fare una domanda che passa la parola all'altra persona.
questions.explanation: This task assesses interaction, so handing over the turn is worth more than adding another argument.

questions.prompt: Which reply reacts to the other person before adding a new idea?
options: Comunque io la penso in un altro modo.
Hai ragione, non ci avevo pensato, e allora potremmo cambiare data.
Adesso spiego la mia idea dall'inizio.
Come dicevo prima, per me è impossibile.
questions.answer: Hai ragione, non ci avevo pensato, e allora potremmo cambiare data.
questions.explanation: It acknowledges the other contribution first and then builds on it, which is what interaction means.

questions.prompt: Hand over the turn: 'Io comincerei dalla data. Tu che ne ___?'
questions.explanation: Che ne pensi is the short fixed question that passes the turn to your partner.

questions.prompt: React to a good idea: 'Hai ragione, non ci avevo ___ per niente.'
questions.explanation: Pensarci forms the pluperfect with avere, giving non ci avevo pensato.

questions.prompt: Build the Italian for: 'So we agree, we will do it on Saturday.'
questions.explanation: The object pronoun lo comes before the verb, and days of the week take no preposition.

activities.title: Organizziamo la festa insieme
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. Plan an end of course party with your partner: make a point, hand over the turn, react, and decide together.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Dobbiamo organizzare la festa di fine corso. Da dove cominciamo?
lines.en: We have to organise the end of course party. Where do we start?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Io comincerei dal budget. Tu che ne pensi?
lines.en: I would start with the budget. What do you think?

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Secondo me prima bisogna scegliere il giorno, altrimenti non viene nessuno.
lines.en: In my opinion we should choose the day first, otherwise nobody will come.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Hai ragione, non ci avevo pensato. E tu, preferiresti il sabato o la domenica?
lines.en: You are right, I had not thought of that. And you, would you prefer Saturday or Sunday?

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Sabato pomeriggio, così chi abita fuori città riesce a tornare.
lines.en: Saturday afternoon, that way people who live out of town can get back.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Su questo sono d'accordo con te, però aggiungerei di finire presto.
lines.en: On this I agree with you, but I would add that we should finish early.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Va bene. E per il cibo, ognuno porta qualcosa?
lines.en: All right. And for the food, does everyone bring something?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Ottima idea. Allora siamo d'accordo: sabato pomeriggio e cibo condiviso.
lines.en: Great idea. So we agree: Saturday afternoon and shared food.
